Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/152/155/157/157a/159/161): `master`==`origin/master`==`32155f3a` verified FIRSTHAND so no push-master-first; PR diff Slice-1-only (3 commits: S186 feat `2ec4c329` + S186 close-out `81319c10` + S187 NEWS `7e4404e8`); all 10 CI checks PASS re-queried FRESH non-watch (Learning 157b -- never trust the `--watch` exit); `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge with a fresh OPEN/MERGEABLE/CLEAN + `headRefOid`==local re-check; ancestor-gated `reset --hard` (both old-master AND branch tip asserted ancestors of merge commit `54b11740`, Learning 146); verified-merged-before-delete (`gh api` 404); the S187 1B stub stash-carried OUT of the PR and popped clean onto master. **Issue #73 stays OPEN** -- Slice 1 (GVA tab) is live; Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) remains, and its PR will use "Closes #73". (S187, publish issue #73 Part 2 Slice 1)

#### Learning 176 -- When you add an argument to a Shiny `moduleServer` whose name collides with an existing local reactive in that module, the local binding SHADOWS the argument inside every closure created after it, so references resolve to the reactive object (a function), not the argument value -- a SILENT bug (no error; the value just goes wrong). Implementing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2 (the user-configurable gestation override for the Potential Parents tab): `modPotentialParentsServer` already had a local `gestationDefault <- reactive({...})` that is ALSO its public returned key (`list(..., gestationDefault = gestationDefault)`, pinned by `test_modPotentialParents.R`). Adding a same-named `gestationDefault = NULL` argument would have made `pedigreeGestationDefault(ped, gestationDefault = gestationDefault)` inside that reactive pass the REACTIVE OBJECT, not the argument -- `getSpeciesGestation` would receive a function as `default`. **Fix:** rename the INTERNAL reactive (`gestationDefaultReactive`) and keep the public returned key `gestationDefault = gestationDefaultReactive`; the argument is now unshadowed and resolves correctly. **Do NOT instead dodge the shadow by capturing the arg into a local at module setup** (`gd <- gestationDefault` at the top of the moduleServer body): that EAGERLY forces the argument promise during module CONSTRUCTION, and for a boot-loaded override (`appServer` passes `shared$speciesOverrides$gestationDefault`, populated by an `observe` that has NOT run when the module is built) the forced value is the pre-boot `NULL`. The argument MUST stay a lazily-forced promise -- first forced inside the prefill reactive, which fires on pedigree load (`observeEvent(pedigree(), ...)` has `ignoreNULL = TRUE`, so it does not fire at boot), well after the boot `observe` has populated `shared$speciesOverrides`. So: rename the reactive, do not pre-capture the arg. R2 reused unchanged -- thread the override `default` to `getSpeciesGestation` via omit-when-NULL (`if (is.null(gestationDefault)) accessor(species, gestationTable = ...) else accessor(species, gestationTable = ..., default = gestationDefault)`), never a bare `NULL`. **Phase-3E config-completeness gotcha (cost me one smoke run):** a config file that EXISTS but lacks the fixed site-config keys (center/baseUrl/...) crashes `appServer` boot -- NOT via the Slice 2 wiring but via the PRE-EXISTING ORIP-tab line `getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)` (`appServer.R:294`), which strict-parses the file and STOPs on a missing `center` (the tolerant `loadSiteConfig()` at boot `:70` is a separate, `tryCatch`-wrapped call). An override-only config is NOT a supported file; a real config is the full bundled example site config PLUS the appended override keys (exactly how S186 ran Slice 1's boot smoke). Run the appServer Phase-3E boot with `file.copy(system.file("extdata","example_nprcgenekeepr_config",package="nprcgenekeepr"))` + appended `speciesOverridesPath`/`gestationDefault`, not a minimal stub. The plain-param wiring (owner-chosen over a Slice-1-style reactive param, to match the existing `gestationTable` seam and preserve the #46 tests) works precisely because the override arg is forced lazily AFTER boot: verified firsthand -- `shared$speciesOverrides` populated at boot (RHESUS gestation 999, gestationDefault 300, unlisted CYNOMOLGUS kept bundled 170), module prefill 999 (override) / 300 (species-less fallback), and no-config boot 210 (byte-identical to today). D5 prefill-only scope was already structurally true (the module forces `maxGestationalPeriod` non-NULL, so `getPotentialParents` never consults the table for the computed window) -- Slice 2 touched only the prefill, never `R/modPotentialParents.R:242`. (S188, implement issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2)

#### Learning 177 -- `codecov/patch` is PATCH-COMPOSITION-sensitive: a small publish patch can FAIL `codecov/patch` even when the identical wiring PASSED in a larger earlier slice, because patch coverage is the percentage of *changed executable lines* hit -- a handful of structurally-uncovered glue lines dominates a small diff but is diluted in a large one. Publishing issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2 (S189): the FRESH non-watch `gh pr checks 79` (Learning 157b) returned exit 1 with `codecov/patch` red -- **76.93% of diff hit vs auto target 90.01%** -- while all 9 `R CMD check`/lint/pkgdown/test-coverage jobs passed and `codecov/project` passed (89.99%, -0.02%, flat). The shortfall was the 2 new `appServer` boot-wiring lines (`gestationTable`/`gestationDefault` passed to `modPotentialParentsServer`), which `covr` CANNOT execute -- `appServer` is never run under coverage; S188 verified that wiring with a `deparse`-match test (source inspection, not execution) + the Phase-3E runtime smoke, neither of which `covr` counts. The module LOGIC (both `pedigreeGestationDefault` branches, the `gestationDefaultReactive`) IS covered by the executing module tests. Slice 1's PR #78 had the SAME appServer-wiring blind spot yet `codecov/patch` passed at **94.24%** -- because Slice 1's large, reader-heavy patch (19-test `loadSpeciesOverrides` reader) diluted the few uncovered lines; Slice 2's ~13-line patch could not. So a red `codecov/patch` on a small publish patch is a composition artifact, NOT necessarily a deliverable defect -- but PROVE it: get the codecov check-run summary (`gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha>/check-runs -q '.check_runs[]|select(.name|test("codecov"))|.output.summary'` gives the "X% of diff hit (target Y%)" line), read the R diff, and attribute every uncovered line to either structural-non-execution (boot wiring, verified by other means) or a real test gap. THREE handling rules. **(a) `codecov/patch` is not a merge gate here** -- `master` has no branch protection, so `mergeStateStatus` is UNSTABLE (not BLOCKED) and `mergeable` stays MERGEABLE; UNSTABLE = mergeable-with-non-passing-non-required-checks. Confirm via `gh api repos/<o>/<r>/branches/master/protection/required_status_checks` (404 = no required checks). **(b) A publish session must NOT fix a red patch by adding test code** -- writing a test that boots `appServer` under coverage is implementation work (and reopens the fiddly Phase-3E `getSiteInfo` boot trap, Learning 176); bundling it into a publish is FM #18/#25. The right response is to SURFACE the red check + its diagnosed cause to the owner via `AskUserQuestion` (never merge a red check silently, never paper it over) and let them decide merge-now vs defer-for-coverage. **(c)** `gh pr view` has NO `baseRefOid` or (for issues) `stateReason` field -- an invalid `--json` field makes the WHOLE query return empty, which a guard reads as a failure; this actually HELPED (my guarded merge refused to run on the empty values), but use only valid fields (`state`/`mergeable`/`headRefOid` for PRs; verify `origin/master` via `git rev-parse` separately) and verify a closed issue with `state`/`closed`/`closedAt`. Publish mechanics otherwise textbook (146/155/157/157a/171): NEWS *Changes* bullet self-framed to supersede Slice 1's "remaining part of issue #73" line (Learning 171's prediction, now fulfilled -- "This completes issue #73"), 0-delta spelling, confined pure-insertion; `master`==`origin/master`==`c512771a` firsthand (no push-master-first); PR diff Slice-2-only 3 commits (S188 feat `e012e6ad` + S188 close-out `866b0a7f` + S189 NEWS `8ce3f937`); `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge `gh pr merge 79 --merge` -> `0980a028`; **issue #73 verified CLOSED** (`closedAt` == merge time, "Closes #73" worked); ancestor-gated `reset --hard` (old-master `c512771a` AND tip `8ce3f937` both ancestors of `0980a028`); verified-merged-before-delete (`gh api` 404). **Issue #73 is now CLOSED** -- Part 1 (data) + Slice 1 (GVA tab) + Slice 2 (Potential Parents tab) all live on `master`. (S189, publish issue #73 Part 2 Slice 2)

#### Learning 178 -- A backlog item / handoff that asserts a change is invasive ("reverses the documented stance", "breaks golden invariants", "needs replacement golden values") states a HYPOTHESIS about blast radius, not a fact -- verify it firsthand during ratification, because the clean realization of the *ratified behavior* is often far shallower than the issue assumed. Ratifying issue #76 Reading A (de-inflate genome uniqueness `gu` for both-unknown founders): the issue body AND the S189 handoff both said Reading A must change `calcGU`/`calcA`/gene-drop, reverse the `R/calcGU.R:10-34` stance, and supply replacement golden values (issue acceptance criterion #1). A 10-agent research + adversarial-critique workflow (`wf_67d4c94a-691`) plus firsthand pipeline reads showed the owner-ratified "decline-to-credit" (report unknown-origin founders' `gu` as 0) has a REPORT-LAYER realization in `reportGV` (~3-5 lines after `classifyParentage` at `reportGV.R:184`, before the `cbind`/`$gu`) that changes NONE of those functions or their golden tests and does NOT reverse the documented stance -- same displayed numbers, far lower risk. The ratification deliverable legitimately concluded the issue OVER-SCOPED the change: criterion #1's "replacement gene-drop goldens" became "zero gene-drop changes + new report-layer tests." Treat "this is invasive" as the question the ratification answers, not a given. **(a) The targeting trap, generalized -- the classification set is NOT the gene-drop founder set.** For unknown-parent work the U-id-aware "both unknown" classification set (`classifyParentage.R:21`: `is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)`) differs from the gene-drop `isFounder`/`getFounders` set: after `addParents` synthesizes U-stub rows with `NA` parents, a U-id-parented proband is "both unknown" by classification but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (it inherits from its U-stub parents), while a literal-`NA` animal IS a gene-drop founder. Target the classification/report set where `origin` is known (= `orderReport`'s `noParentage` bucket, `orderReport.R:38-62`), NOT the gene-drop set. Mis-targeting the gene-drop set was the fatal flaw that made candidate A2 (tag phantom alleles at the mint point) unsound -- it marks EVERY founder, not the Undetermined subset. **(b) Decline-to-credit resolves to a single value.** Because the target animals have BOTH parents unknown, all their gene-drop alleles trace to phantom mints (`assignAlleles.R:33-36`), so "exclude the artifact alleles" = `gu` -> 0 (no formula to tune). Apply to BOTH the report `gu` column and the `$gu` element for one coherent reported statistic; never write `NA` (it poisons `rank()`/`order()`); the Slice-3 demotion (flag-driven on `value=="Undetermined"`, `rankSubjects.R:37-44`) composes on top unchanged, and imports (both-unknown WITH a recorded `origin`) are preserved. **(c) Adversarial-candidate review earns its keep on a stance-reversal ratification.** The workflow killed 3 of 4 candidates with VERIFIED fatal flaws -- A4 rescales the wrong term (the inflation is in the numerator `rowSums(rare)` at `calcGU.R:91`, not the `/(2L*iterations)` denominator); A3 injects per-iteration randomness and breaks the seeded gene-drop goldens; A2 mis-targets all founders -- and refined the survivor A1 (target `noParentage`, not the full "both unknown" set). Ratification is a docs-only DESIGN deliverable -> TDD code-phases N/A, Phase-3E N/A; recorded in `docs/planning/issue76-gu-deinflation-ratification.md` in the §8-E house style (provenance/authority header, load-bearing finding, authoritative rule, RED/GREEN charter, rejected candidates, firsthand anchors), with a one-line cross-ref added to the issue-9 plan §8-F; committed direct to local `master` then pushed to origin ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]); do NOT bundle with the implementation (FM #18/#25 -- the ratify -> implement -> publish arc is 3 sessions min). (S190, ratify issue #76 Reading A)

#### Learning 179 -- A gene-drop NON-founder's genome uniqueness (`gu`) is structurally 0 at the default `guThresh = 1`, because every allele it carries is inherited from a parent that is ALSO in the analysed population, so no allele it holds is held by only one animal. This matters when writing a RED test that a U-id-PARENTED both-unknown animal (sire/dam are generated U-ids like "U0001", not literal `NA`) gets de-inflated to `gu = 0` (issue #76 Reading A -- proving the predicate's U-id-awareness): such a proband is "both unknown" by `classifyParentage` (`is.na(x) | isGeneratedUnknownId(x)`) but a NON-founder at the gene-drop level (Learning 178a -- after `addParents` it inherits from its U-stub parents), so at `guThresh = 1` its `gu` is ALREADY 0 and de-inflating it is INVISIBLE -- the assertion `expect_equal(gu["P1"], 0)` would pass on the UNCHANGED code (not a real RED). Use `guThresh = 2` (an allele held by <= 2 animals counts as rare): each of the proband's two inherited alleles is then held by exactly {proband, parent} = 2 animals, so it counts as rare and the proband carries a strictly-positive `gu` (verified firsthand = 100 on the pre-change `reportGV`), making the de-inflation observable and the test RED-meaningful. Verified the threshold firsthand by running `reportGV(fixture, guIter = 1000L, guThresh = thr)` for thr in 1/2/3 BEFORE writing assertions (FM #11/Learning 6): at thr=1 the U-id proband is 0 (literal-NA founders 26-50), at thr=2 founders AND the U-id proband are all > 0 (the sweet spot), at thr=3 everything saturates to 100. **General test-design rule:** when a RED test must observe a value being DRIVEN to 0, first confirm the value is NON-zero on the unchanged code under the exact fixture+params, or the "failing" assertion silently passes. **Robustness:** assert only the DISCRIMINATING properties -- `== 0` for de-inflation targets (RNG-independent), `> 0` for preserved imports/known animals (the guard that proves targeting), and `$report` gu == `$gu` element (same-run internal consistency) -- NEVER exact seeded gu magnitudes, so the test survives RNG / R-version differences; a fixture WITH an `origin` column is REQUIRED to exercise the import-preservation branch (qcPed and `makeValidTestPed` both lack `origin`, so they only cover the no-origin -> all-Undetermined path). The GREEN realization was exactly the ratified ~12 lines (origin if/else mirroring `orderReport.R:43-47`, the `undetermined` mask, `gu$gu[undetermined] <- 0.0`) after `reportGV.R:184` before the `cbind`; mutating the ONE `gu` data.frame covered BOTH the report column and `$gu`; all existing goldens held UNMODIFIED exactly as S190 predicted (verified: full regression 1225/0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` 0/0/0, `calcGU`/`calcA`/`geneDrop` untouched). Re-read `modGeneticValue.R:224-231` firsthand (S190 gotcha #8) and confirmed the Slice-3 demotion keys on `value == "Undetermined"` NOT on `gu` magnitude, so de-inflating `gu` to 0 leaves the displayed ranking unchanged -- the two corrections COMPOSE (Phase-3E: Undetermined ranked last 6-10/10 with gu 0, imports preserved at top). REFACTOR fixed only the 2 style lints GREEN introduced (line-wrap; `0` -> `0.0` for the double `gu` column, `implicit_integer_linter`); the 2 `object_usage_linter` warnings on `classifyParentage`/`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` are pre-existing single-file false positives that resolve under `lint_package`. (S191, implement issue #76 Reading A)

#### Learning 180 -- To clear a Shiny `fileInput` (reset BOTH the re-read behavior AND the displayed file name) WITHOUT adding a client-side dependency (`shinyjs`), combine three server-side moves: **(1)** render the file input dynamically -- `output$xUI <- renderUI({ key(); fileInput(session$ns("x"), ...) })` keyed by a `reactiveVal` counter, and bump the counter on "clear" so a FRESH empty widget replaces the old one (this is what clears the displayed file name -- base shiny has no `updateFileInput`; the input id MUST be `session$ns()`-namespaced because `renderUI` content is not auto-namespaced inside a module); **(2)** blank companion text inputs with `updateTextAreaInput(session, ..., value = "")`; **(3)** hold a server-side "cleared" guard -- `clearedFilePath`/`clearedText` `reactiveVal`s set on clear, and skip a read when the current value is `identical()` to the cleared one (a newly chosen file gets a new temp `datapath`, and any edited text differs, so genuine new input still loads; the one accepted edge is re-entering the EXACT cleared string, which the guard blocks). Implementing issue #1 (Pedigree Browser "Clear Focal Animals" left a loaded CSV that silently re-read on the next Update and kept showing the file name; the list-clearing checkbox itself had worked since 2020). The guard (moves 2/3) is what makes the behavior UNIT-testable via `shiny::testServer` -- assert through the module's returned reactive that a cleared file/text is not re-read and that NEW input still loads (load a tempfile CSV, Update, clear+Update, then a 3rd Update with the SAME `focalAnimalFile`/`focalAnimalIds` still set = the browser-not-reset state -> stays empty); a UI-structure test (`as.character(modPedigreeUI("id"))` no longer contains `type="file"` but DOES contain the `uiOutput` container id) covers move 1 RED-first. **What `testServer` canNOT verify is exactly the client-side half** -- the file name actually disappearing from the widget and the `updateTextAreaInput` round-trip -- **so that half needs an E2E (shinytest2 + chromote) assertion.** **Running this project's opt-in E2E suite locally for real-browser Phase-3E evidence needs BOTH env vars:** `NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` (the `create_test_app()` opt-in gate, helper-shinytest2.R) AND `NOT_CRAN=true` (else `skip_on_cran()` skips EVERY e2e test -- a plain `Rscript ... testthat::test_file(...)` run silently skipped 9/9 until `NOT_CRAN` was set; this is the same `NOT_CRAN` condition the `[regression-read]` glossary names, just as critical for the e2e suite). chromote + Chrome ARE installed on this machine, so the browser tests DO run when both vars are set (verified: 9 pedigree e2e tests, 13 assertions, all pass; the new test reads the file-name display via `app$get_js` on the `.input-group input[type=text]` value and the textarea via `app$get_value(input = "pedigree-focalAnimalIds")`, confirming the raw `tags$textarea(id = ns("focalAnimalIds"))` binds as a normal shiny text input). E2E added to a behavior ALREADY implemented is a VERIFICATION test (passes on first run), not strict RED-first -- be explicit about that; the RED-first coverage of the reset MECHANISM is the testServer UI-structure test (move 1). REFACTOR was correctly SKIPPED (owner-gated) -- `lintr` clean on the changed R + test files, no duplication. Implement session: code + tests + close-out on branch `issue-1-clear-focal-animals`, NOT published (FM #18/#25; publish PR uses "Closes #1" + a NEWS *Changes* bullet, Learning 157a). (S193, implement issue #1 -- clear focal animals)

#### Learning 181 -- In a PLANNING session whose design rests on non-trivial statistics or empirical claims, an adversarial-critique workflow phase that ACTUALLY runs checks against bundled data is high-ROI: it catches plausible-but-wrong claims BEFORE they enter the plan and misdirect the implementer. Planning issue #2 (evidence-based GVA gene-drop iteration-count advice). A 7-agent investigate/design/critique workflow (`wf_fdb7c410-95f`, ultracode) produced a first design pass with two confident-but-wrong claims that the adversarial critic -- prompted to REFUTE and to probe `qcPed` empirically -- overturned: **(1)** the "homozygous-rare animals inflate the per-animal Monte Carlo SE by up to sqrt(2), so multiply needed N by (1+rho)" claim is FALSE at the default `guThresh = 1` -- a rare allele has population frequency <= 1 (a single copy in the whole population), so a homozygote can never carry one; per-column rare counts are effectively {0,1} and the Bernoulli SE `sqrt(p(1-p)/(2K))` is adequate (slightly conservative), so guidance that would have ~doubled iteration counts was unfounded; **(2)** the rank-stability machinery was motivated by rank churn that does not measurably exist on the only bundled real pedigree -- `qcPed` is degenerate-bimodal (~56% of probands at gu=0 incl. the issue #76 de-inflated set, ~43% near gu=50, ~2/280 in the mid-range; Spearman = 1.0 at every N >= 125), so a TDD test of "recommend a small N" on qcPed passes TAUTOLOGICALLY -- a purpose-built dense-mid-range fixture must be built in RED FIRST or the test proves nothing. The critic also corrected a code mis-description (the GVA displayed rank is the bucketed `orderReport`/`rankSubjects` pipeline -- `gu > 10` cutoff, `-trunc(gu)`, `z <= 0.25` -- NOT `rank(indivMeanKin - gu)`), which moved the convergence diagnostic from generic Spearman to boundary-density counts. **General rule:** when a plan's load-bearing claims are quantitative, add a verification pass that EXECUTES against real bundled data, not one that only reasons; and re-verify the most load-bearing CODE citations firsthand before authoring (FM #11/#20, Learning 6) -- here `calcGU`/`calcA`/`reportGV`/`orderReport`/`modGeneticValue` were read directly, confirming `gu = rowSums(rare)/(2*iterations)` (`calcGU.R:97`) and the de-inflation at `reportGV.R:210-211`. **Caveat that must survive into implementation:** the critic's empirical `qcPed` numbers are themselves subagent-sourced and are NOT to be trusted as settled -- they are re-established in the implementation session's RED (the dense fixture + a golden-master `gu` before/after the `calcA` refactor are the verification step). **Design corollary (owner-steer handling):** when an owner steer is literally infeasible -- here "report convergence DURING the calculation," blocked because rare-allele classification needs the whole-population frequency table per iteration column (`alleleFreq.R:30`/`calcA.R:33-36`) while `geneDrop` accumulates all-iterations-per-animal not all-animals-per-iteration -- deliver the realization that PRESERVES THE INTENT (compute the convergence curve from the single completed run via nested column-prefixes `V1..Vk`, since the columns are i.i.d. replicates) rather than implementing the letter or rejecting it. (S195, plan issue #2)

#### Learning 182 -- The ratification gate is where subagent-sourced empirical claims must become FIRSTHAND, and the firsthand check must be against the authoritative function (ground truth), not just a re-derivation by the same method. Ratifying §8 of the issue #2 plan. Learning 181 had explicitly flagged that the plan's `qcPed` numbers were subagent-sourced and "NOT to be trusted as settled" -- S196 re-ran them firsthand and found 181's OWN degeneracy description was wrong, proving the flag was load-bearing: (1) the `qcPed` Undetermined count is **124/280, not 156** (156 is the *rankable* count -- the two were swapped); (2) `qcPed` is NOT "bimodal 56%/43%/2-mid-range" -- firsthand `{gu>0}` == `{both-unknown founders}` == `{the issue #76 Undetermined set}` are the **identical 124 animals** (`identical()` TRUE), every rankable animal is at `gu=0`, and **after the #76 de-inflation all 280 are at `gu=0`**; (3) `pedWithGenotype`/`rhesusPedigree` are the same, and `examplePedigree` is the ONLY bundled pedigree with rankable `gu` signal (294/2322) but its gu-bearing animals are structurally fixed founder alleles (gu=100/50), so **selection order is identical at N=5 and N=2000** (top-20 overlap 1.0, max rank change 0). Consequence: **NO bundled pedigree can validate a `gu`-based ranking/convergence test** -- a dense-mid-range fixture built in Slice 2 RED is mandatory, not optional (strengthens Dragon #2). The error mattered because the plan's D3 justification ("`qcPed` converges by N~250-500") was VACUOUS (no signal to converge); the grounded D3 evidence had to come from `examplePedigree` instead (order stable `<<1000`; per-animal SE `~1/sqrt(N)`: 0.79pp@1000 -> 0.56pp@2000 -> ~0.35pp@5000), which still supports aligning the default to 1000 but on honest footing. **Method that caught it:** replicate `reportGV`'s deterministic body once, gene-drop ONE `Nmax` run, recompute `gu` on nested column-prefixes (the i.i.d.-columns trick -- verified: prefix `gu` == `calcGU` to 0 abs diff), and **cross-check the headline counts against `reportGV(qcPed)` itself** -- the ground-truth call is what exposed "all 280 at `gu=0`", which a hand-replication alone (sharing the same conceptual error) might have reproduced. **Claims that DID hold firsthand** (so the critique was not all wrong): SE `~1/sqrt(N)`; `SE_exact/SE_approx` ~0.82-0.85 at `guThresh>=2`, ~1.0 at `guThresh=1` (Correction 1); the column-prefix trick; and `byID` is a no-op at `guThresh=1` (`TRUE`/`FALSE` identical). **General rule:** "an adversarial critic ran checks" (Learning 181) is necessary but NOT sufficient -- before owner decisions are recorded against quantitative claims, the ratifying session re-establishes them personally and validates against the real function, because a wrong number that survives the critique will otherwise be ratified into the plan and mislead the implementer. (S196, ratify §8 of the issue #2 plan)

#### Learning 183 -- To unit-test that a Monte Carlo standard error obeys the `1/sqrt(K)` (more-iterations -> smaller-SE) law WITHOUT RNG flakiness, exploit that the SE is a deterministic function of a FIXED allele table: duplicate every gene-drop iteration column of a bundled `AlleleTable` (e.g. `ped1Alleles`) so `K -> 2K` with identical per-column values, and the sample variance-of-the-mean SE then scales by the EXACT closed factor `sqrt((K-1)/(2K-1))` -- a strict-equality assertion, no seed, no tolerance band. Implementing issue #2 Slice 1 (`calcGUSE()`: per-animal genome-uniqueness SE `100 * sqrt(var(rare[i,]/2)/K)`). Pair it with an **exact golden test that recomputes the SE straight from `calcA()`** (`100*sqrt(apply(rare/2,1,stats::var)/K)`) rather than re-running the helper, so the test proves the formula independently of the implementation; both are deterministic because they read a fixed table, not a fresh gene drop. **Three concrete gotchas this slice surfaced:** (1) **`calcGU`/`calcGUSE` rely on `as.data.frame(<named numeric>)` taking the COLUMN NAME from the local variable name and the rownames from the vector names** -- so the local must literally be named `guSE` to yield a `guSE` column; mirror `calcGU` exactly when adding a parallel helper. (2) **`test_reportGV.R` pins the EXACT report column list via `expect_named` (two of them)** -- adding any `reportGV` output column is therefore a RED edit to those lists (they fail until GREEN adds the column), in addition to the new feature tests; grep `expect_named` before assuming an additive column is invisible to existing tests. (3) **Column carry-through is free but must be confirmed:** `orderReport()`/`rankSubjects()` subset rows and `rbind`/add-columns by NAME, so a new column threaded into the `cbind` at `reportGV.R:213-215` flows into `$report` automatically -- verified, not assumed. **Scope discipline (FM #18/#25):** `calcGUSE()` recomputes the rare matrix (a second `calcA` per `reportGV`); the obvious dedup (factor `rare` out of `calcA` so `gu` and `guSE` share one build) is deliberately Slice 2's job (plan Finding 7, where the column-prefix convergence tool needs it) and was NOT pulled forward -- a real but bounded, planned inefficiency, flagged in code and handoff rather than silently "improved." The same column-duplication + golden-from-`calcA` technique will test Slice 2's `gvaConvergence()`. (S197, implement Slice 1 of issue #2 -- guSE column)

#### Learning 184 -- GitHub's issue auto-close parser IGNORES English negation: a PR body or commit message that contains the literal token sequence `close[s]/closed | fix[es]/fixed | resolve[s]/resolved` immediately followed by `#N` will close issue N on merge to the default branch EVEN inside a negated clause like "does not close #N". Publishing Slice 1 of issue #2 (PR #83). The S197 handoff's explicit "do NOT use 'Closes #2'" warning WAS followed -- the PR used "Part of #2" in its lead -- but the body ALSO contained the sentence **"This is Slice 1 of 3 and does not close #2"**, and the substring "close #2" auto-closed the issue on merge (`gh pr merge 83 --merge` -> #2 went CLOSED). The intent was the exact opposite (Slice 1 of 3; Slice 3 closes #2). **Rule:** for an issue you intend to keep OPEN, the PR body AND every commit message that lands on the default branch must contain NO `<closing-keyword> #N` substring at all -- not even negated ("does not close #N", "won't fix #N", "not resolving #N" all still fire). Refer to the issue only with a non-keyword form: "Part of #N", "Addresses part of #N", "Slice 1 of N, #N", or "#N (stays open)". To state that it does NOT close, restructure so a closing keyword never abuts the number, e.g. "#2 remains open; Slice 3 will close it." (The same trap applies to the three S197/S196/S195 commit *messages* on the branch -- they used "Slice 1 of issue #2", which is safe because "issue" sits between any keyword and the number; "of issue #2" does not match.) **Detection (standing post-merge assertion):** immediately after `gh pr merge`, re-query the issue state -- `gh issue view N --json state` -- whenever the issue is meant to SURVIVE the merge; this session caught it within seconds because the merge step re-queried #2. Note `closingIssuesReferences` is NOT a field in this repo's `gh` version, so you cannot pre-check the GraphQL closing-links before merge -- query the issue's `state` directly, before and after. **Recovery (fast, non-destructive):** `gh issue reopen N`, then `gh issue comment N` documenting that the auto-close was an unintended side effect of the PR wording and why the issue stays open -- so the issue history is not misleading. **RECURRENCE (same session, the sharpest part of this lesson):** the trap fired a SECOND time because the S198 close-out *commit message* QUOTED the offending keyword-plus-number token while DOCUMENTING the trap, and pushing that commit to the default branch (`master`) re-fired the parser -- #2 auto-closed again, reopened again. So the rule is not "watch the PR description" -- it is "the literal `<closing-keyword> #N` token must not appear in ANY text GitHub parses for the default branch: the PR body OR any commit message landing on it, INCLUDING a message that merely quotes or explains the trap." When you must write about it, break the token (`close #<N>`, "the close-keyword + number", name the issue in a separate clause) and re-query `gh issue view N --json state` after EVERY push that could carry such a message, not just after the PR merge. File CONTENTS are safe -- GitHub parses commit messages and PR descriptions, not the diff -- so the same words are fine inside `SESSION_NOTES.md`/`CHANGELOG.md`/this learning; only commit messages and the PR body are live. (S198, publish Slice 1 of issue #2)

#### Learning 185 -- Building the "iteration count actually moves the selection order" fixture that Dragon #2 demands, plus three techniques that made Slice 2 (`gvaConvergence()`) a low-risk, purely-additive slice. Implementing issue #2 Slice 2. **(1) THE FIXTURE (the load-bearing dragon).** No bundled pedigree can exercise a `gu`-based ranking/convergence tool (Learning 182: every rankable bundled animal is at `gu=0` after the issue #76 de-inflation; `examplePedigree`'s gu-bearers are structurally-fixed founder alleles, order-stable at `N=5`). The MECHANISM that produces rankable MID-RANGE `gu`: genome uniqueness concentrates at founders (a founder's private gene-drop allele is "rare" -- freq 1 among probands -- in every iteration it is NOT inherited), and the #76 policy zeroes exactly those both-unknown founders, so signal vanishes. BREAK it by **EXCLUDING founders from the proband population** (`pop = descendants only`): then a founder's private allele among probands is carried only by descendants, and a descendant who is intermittently the SOLE proband carrier of a rare founder allele gets intermediate `gu` AND -- having known parents -- is not zeroed by #76. This is realistic (a colony "population" is living focal animals; founders are historical/dead). Tuning levers: more offspring per founder-pair -> lower `gu` (roughly `0.5^k`); and the order CONVERGES at a finite `N` only under MODERATE crowding -- too crowded (many animals at IDENTICAL true `gu` = permanent ties) never converges (Kendall tau-b plateaus below the 0.95 threshold forever), too separated (distinct `k`-levels) converges trivially at the grid floor (top-`k` stable at `N=5`). The winning fixture is a half-sib WEB (14 founder sires x overlapping wrapping windows of 5 founder dams over 15 dams, one offspring per pair, founders excluded -> 70 probands): the window overlap gives probands distinct-but-close true `gu` straddling the `gu=10` cutoff, so at low `N` the gene-drop estimate randomly crosses the cutoff (a highGu<->lowMk bucket flip is a large rank jump) and settles as `N` grows. Found by a 7-strategy parallel parameter-search workflow + a judge stage, then **RE-VALIDATED FIRSTHAND** (seed 11: unstable at `N=25`, top-20 overlap 0.75; converges by `N~800`; monotone curve; `recommendedIter` varies 200-800 across seeds 11/22/33). **(2) THE COLUMN-PREFIX TRICK NEEDS NO `calcA`/`reportGV` REFACTOR** (corrects the plan's Finding 7 framing). `calcA` ALREADY returns the per-column rare matrix and classifies each column's rareness from that column ALONE (`apply(alleles, 2L, countRare)`), so `rowSums(calcA(full)[, 1:k]) / (2k) * 100 == calcGU(prefix)$gu` EXACTLY (verified golden across `k` and `byID`/`threshold`). So `gvaConvergence` calls `calcA` ONCE on the `nMax` gene drop and aggregates nested prefixes -- Finding 7's "factor `rare` out of `calcA`" is satisfied by calling `calcA` once INSIDE the new function, not by changing `calcA`'s signature. The deterministic ranking scaffold (`kinship` -> `meanKinship` -> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` -> `scale`/zScores, `classifyParentage`, demographics, the #76 predicate) is reproduced by ORCHESTRATING the same building blocks `reportGV` calls + reusing `orderReport` -- `reportGV`/`calcA`/`orderReport`/`geneDrop` themselves UNCHANGED (SAFEGUARDS: do not refactor the central function for a feature). The adversarial review verified `buildOrder`'s order is IDENTICAL to a `reportGV`-style ranking on the same `gu`, so the measured "selection order" is the REAL one. Net: a purely ADDITIVE slice (new `R/` file + new test file + export + `man`), zero regression risk. **(3) RED TESTS FOR A SEED-VARYING DIAGNOSTIC ASSERT ROBUST INEQUALITIES, NOT THE EXACT VALUE.** `recommendedIter` varies 200-800 by seed, so pinning `recN == 800` would be cross-R-version-flaky (violates the TDD "deterministic" rule). The load-bearing assertion is DISCRIMINATION (the anti-tautology guarantee): fixture `recommendedIter` is FINITE and `>` qcPed's grid floor, the fixture's smallest-`N` order is unstable (`topOverlap < oMin`), and qcPed is `overlap == 1` AND `tau == 1` at every `N` (no rankable `gu` signal -> deterministic mean-kinship order). Pair with determinism (same seed -> byte-identical curve) and the recommendation-logic test (smallest `N` meeting BOTH criteria; every earlier `N` fails one). **(4) A POST-GREEN ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (ultracode) EARNS ITS KEEP ON THE UNSPECIFIED INPUT SPACE.** A fresh-agent skeptic, told to RUN R and find bugs, confirmed the primary path correct (half-split math, row alignment, `buildOrder == reportGV` order, `nRankable`/`nUndetermined`, determinism, `k>nRankable` clamp, empty-grid guard) but caught one real latent defect the strict-TDD suite missed: an UPPER-bound-only `grid` filter let a user-supplied `N=0` through -> `colsB = (0+1L):(2L*0) = 1:0 = c(1, 0)` (R's `:` REVERSAL, which is NOT an empty range) plus a `2*0` divide-by-zero -> a silent `NaN` "garbage row" in the curve. The `(N+1):(2N)` reversal at `N=0` is the classic R `:` trap; guard with a positive-integer lower bound (`grid[grid >= 1L & 2L * grid <= nMax]`). Fixed via a gated hardening MICRO-CYCLE (one RED test for the guard -> one-line GREEN -> re-verify), which is the protocol-clean way to fold a verification-found defect into an in-flight slice. General rule: a TDD suite proves the SPECIFIED behavior; an adversarial review of the UNSPECIFIED input space (0 / negative / empty / oversize params) is complementary and cheap, and finding bugs in YOUR OWN deliverable during verification is correct, not scope creep. (S199, implement Slice 2 of issue #2 -- gvaConvergence)

#### Learning 186 -- The Learning-184 GitHub auto-close trap is fully PREVENTABLE with a one-line proactive grep guard run BEFORE `gh pr create` (and before any commit message that lands on the default branch), turning Learning 184 from a reactive "catch-and-reopen after merge" control into a "it never fires" preventive one. Publishing Slice 2 of issue #2 (PR #84). S198 auto-closed #2 TWICE (a negated clause in the PR body, then a close-out commit message that quoted the token); S200 published the very NEXT slice with ZERO auto-close incidents by **writing the PR body to a scratch file and scanning it first**: `grep -niE '(clos|fix|resolv)[a-z]*[[:space:]]+#[0-9]' pr_body.md` -- expect NO match before creating the PR; the same scan applies to any commit message destined for `master`. Concrete practices that made this a clean publish: (1) **author the PR body in a file, not an inline `--body` string**, so it is greppable and reviewable before it goes outward (and pass it with `--body-file`); (2) **keep BOTH controls** -- the preventive grep AND the standing immediate post-merge `gh issue view N --json state` re-query (the backstop confirmed #2 stayed OPEN; do not drop it just because the grep passed); (3) **to say a PR does NOT close an issue, restructure so a closing keyword never abuts a number** -- S200's body referenced the issue only as "Part of #2" and described Slice 3 as "the remaining slice", never "closes #2"; even `#76` appeared only after the non-keyword word "issue". Also reaffirmed for a clean publish session (a faithful repeat of S198's recipe, minus the trap): a publish/docs session is **SOLO** (a serial irreversible git sequence -- a workflow adds risk, not coverage; held under ultracode), TDD code-phases are **N/A** (the code was written + tested under TDD in the prior implementation session), the build-equivalent for publish is **the CI `R CMD check` matrix** (×5 OS/version) PLUS a **firsthand on-`master`-after-reset smoke** that the merged symbol is exported AND runs (not just "CI was green"), and the close-out is **pushed to origin/master FF** ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]). (S200, publish Slice 2 of issue #2 -- gvaConvergence)

#### Learning 187 -- A "fix EVERY stale X" deliverable's completeness check must grep ALL tracked file extensions (use `git grep` with NO `--include` filter), because even a RATIFIED plan's own evidence inventory can have a blind spot -- and an adversarial completeness workflow catches multi-surface inconsistencies a single author misses. Implementing issue #2 Slice 3 (align the gene-drop default `5000 -> 1000L` + reconcile every stale "5000" doc + the `gvaConvergence()` vignette). The plan's mandated "84-hit 2D inventory" (the evidence grep that planning sessions are required to run) had **omitted `*.qmd`**, so a stale `| guIter | 5000L |` default claim in the tracked pkgdown article `vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd:193` was NOT in the inventory -- and my OWN first-pass grep repeated the omission (I used an explicit `--include=*.R,*.Rmd,*.Rd,*.html,*.md,*.yml` list). It was caught only by the DONE-criterion completeness grep run over `R/ man/ inst/ vignettes/` with no extension filter, then re-confirmed by a 3-agent adversarial completeness audit. **Four load-bearing techniques:**
**(1) Completeness grep = `git grep` over ALL tracked files, no `--include`.** For "reconcile every stale X," an `--include` allow-list is a foot-gun: the one extension you forget (`.qmd` in `articles/`, `.yml`) is exactly where a stale claim hides. `git grep` (tracked-only) also auto-excludes untracked local build artifacts (`vignettes/*.html`/`*.md` renders are NOT tracked here -- they showed stale "5000" but are regenerated, not shipped). Then CLASSIFY every hit into {must-change / factual-leave / out-of-scope}; a hit is not automatically a fix.
**(2) The classification needs an OWNER scope gate, because "stale 5000" has a boundary.** Pre-RED, split the 5000 sites into: the reportGV/geneDrop DEFAULT (change to 1000); factual DATA PROVENANCE (`lacy1989PedAlleles` genuinely HAS 5000 columns; editing it would FALSIFY the data description -- leave); and deferred-`fg` machinery (`calcFG`/`calcFEFG`/`assignAlleles`; `fg` SE is issue #82 -- leave, incl. `assignAlleles`'s pre-existing "Default is 5000" doc bug where `n` has NO default at all). Posed this as an `AskUserQuestion` ("how wide should the net go?"); owner chose "Core + reconcile guidance." A factual-provenance number is NOT a stale default and must not be swept up.
**(3) A default-VALUE change is cleanly strict-TDD-able and low-blast-radius.** RED = `expect_identical(eval(formals(reportGV)$guIter), 1000L)` (the contract) PLUS a behavioral EFFECT test (a default-`n` `geneDrop()` run yields exactly 1000 `^V[0-9]+$` columns) -- both deterministic, both fail at 5000 for the right reason. GREEN = the two-char value change + the coupled "Default is 5000" roxygen + `document()` (man regen is REQUIRED or `R CMD check` flags a usage/signature mismatch -- it is part of GREEN, not optional). Blast radius is bounded by grepping FIRST that every caller passes the value explicitly: here every `reportGV`/`geneDrop` test call passes an explicit `guIter`/`n`, the only internal callers (`reportGV.R:142`, `gvaConvergence.R:158`) pass `n` explicitly, and the shipped example data is STORED at its build-time count (not regenerated by a default change) -- so the change broke zero value-pinned tests. Mock-signature stubs that mirror the real signature (`local_mocked_bindings(reportGV = function(..., guIter = 5000L, ...))`) are doc-sync, not behavior -- update for consistency, but they assert nothing.
**(4) An adversarial completeness workflow earns its keep on a multi-SURFACE doc reconciliation.** A 3-agent audit (exhaustive iteration-grep / doc-surface consistency / new-vignette correctness) caught what the single-author pass missed: the `.qmd` default site; a RANGE contradiction ("A range of 2 to 100,000 simulations" in `gvAndBgDesc.html` + `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` vs the modular UI's actual 100-10,000 at `modGeneticValue.R:38` -- a different inconsistency AXIS than the default, but squarely within the DONE criterion "tell one consistent story," and code-grounded to fix); and a drift-prone vignette claim (prose tied to a MARGINAL build-time result -- `recommendedIter=800` only because `rankAgreement` there was 0.952, barely over the 0.95 threshold). **The vignette robustness rule:** a worked-example vignette whose numbers come from a seeded RNG must keep its PROSE qualitative (let the build-time table show the specifics) AND give the illustrated phenomenon a safety MARGIN -- here, widen the grid (nMax=3000, N up to 1500) so convergence is SUSTAINED across the top cells, so a future RNG shift that nudges one cell below threshold cannot flip `converged` to FALSE and contradict the text. Finding these in your OWN in-flight deliverable during verification is correct, not scope creep (cf. Learning 185(4)). (S201, implement Slice 3 of issue #2 -- default 5000->1000 + doc reconciliation + vignette)

#### Learning 188 -- When publishing a change whose new value ALREADY appears in a RELEASED NEWS section but at a NARROWER scope, do NOT edit the released line -- add a NEW dev-version bullet, and let the owner decide whether to document it at all. Publishing issue #2 Slice 3, which changed the `reportGV()`/`geneDrop()` FUNCTION default `5000 -> 1000`. The only existing "1000" mention in NEWS sat at `NEWS.Rmd:275` INSIDE the released `2.0.0 (20260618)` section, and it documented the Shiny **GVA tab** default (UI parity), NOT the function signature -- a narrower scope. Two rules. **(1) Released NEWS sections are immutable history** -- you never rewrite a shipped release's notes to retro-document a later change; the function-default change is unreleased work, so it gets a NEW bullet under "# nprcgenekeepr (development version)" -> Changes (append-don't-rewrite, Learning 171). **(2) The UI-tab claim and the function-signature claim are genuinely distinct user-facing facts** -- a caller of `reportGV(ped)` got 5000 iterations before this slice and 1000 after, so the new bullet is NOT redundant with line 275; the released line stays untouched. Whether to add the bullet at all is the OWNER's call (user-facing release-notes content), posed as a single `AskUserQuestion` (Changes-bullet-only / +vignette-doc-bullet / none); owner chose Changes-bullet-only. Everything else was a clean repeat of the S200 publish recipe: the Learning-186 grep guard confirmed `Closes #2` was the SOLE closing reference (other refs `#82`/`#84` carried no keyword), the GraphQL `closingIssuesReferences` query independently confirmed the PR would close ONLY #2, the post-merge `gh issue view 2` re-query confirmed it CLOSED, watch + fresh re-query (157b), owner-gated merge, ancestor-gated `reset --hard` + verified-merged-before-delete (146), close-out pushed FF. **General rule:** before editing any NEWS line that already mentions your value, check WHICH version section it lives in and WHAT scope it claims -- a released-section / narrower-scope match means a new dev bullet, not an edit. (S202, publish Slice 3 of issue #2 -- the merge that CLOSED #2)

#### Learning 189 -- When PLANNING a "report the sampling SE of a NONLINEAR statistic" task, the math derivation is the load-bearing risk, and the highest-ROI grounding is an independent derivation panel + an adversarial reconciler that actually RUNS the language (finite-difference gradient check against the authoritative function + an empirical multi-seed validation) -- it both settles the math AND surfaces adjacent defects a desk derivation never would. Planning issue #82 (`fg` = founder genome equivalents sampling SE; `FG = 1/sum(p^2/r)`, `p` deterministic, only `r` = gene-drop retention mean stochastic). The S203 workflow (`wf_8672ccdd-2bf`, 8 agents: inventory + `calcGUSE` wiring trace + tests/fixtures + current-surfacing + a 3-WAY INDEPENDENT derivation panel + a reconciler) produced, with high confidence: the gradient `dFG/dr_f = FG^2*p_f^2/r_f^2` (the reconciler verified it by finite differences vs `calcFG` on `lacy1989Ped`, agreeing to 5 decimals) and the recommended **influence/score-form** delta estimator `fgSE = sd(crossprod(g, R))/sqrt(K)` (algebraically the full-covariance sandwich but `O(K*F)`, never forms the F x F matrix, folds in the required within-iteration founder covariance), empirically validated (agreement ratio 1.020, coverage 0.953, 1/sqrt(K) scaling 1.94). **Five findings the math work surfaced that a single-author desk pass would likely have missed:** (1) a **latent silent-collapse BUG** in `calcFG`/`calcFEFG` -- a contributing founder with `r_f=0,p_f>0` makes `p^2/0 = +Inf`, and `na.rm=TRUE` strips only `NaN` NOT `Inf`, so `sum(...)=Inf` and `FG` silently returns 0; materially likelier at the new K=1000 default than the old 5000 (so a planning session can DISCOVER a correctness bug in code adjacent to its feature -- record it as a ratification decision, do not drive-by fix it); (2) a naive **per-iteration `FG_k` is degenerate** (a single lost founder allele per column -> `Inf` -> `FG_k=0`, collapsing essentially every iteration) -- NEVER batch single-column FGs; the linearization works because it divides by the K-mean `r-bar_f`, bounded away from 0; (3) the within-iteration founder covariance is **MATERIAL** (~46% on a deep 20-founder pedigree vs only ~3% on the 3-founder `lacy1989`) -- so the diagonal/independence approximation is not defensible AND `lacy1989` is too small to validate it; the validation slice MUST use a real deep pedigree (the same "no bundled fixture can validate this" lesson as Learning 182/185, now for `fg`); (4) `FG` is a colony-level **SCALAR**, so its SE is ONE number -- the `guSE` wiring mirror DROPS several integration points (no `orderReport` ride-through, no per-animal report column, no issue-#76 zeroing, no `max()` reduction) -- mirror a precedent in SPIRIT, not blindly point-for-point; (5) the degeneracy path (the `1/r_f^2` pole) has **no fast deterministic fixture** in the package -> the plan must CRAFT a tiny allele table directly (the `calcGUSE`/`ped1Alleles` pattern), not rely on a slow seeded `geneDrop` of `examplePedigree`. **Concrete reusable gotcha:** `git grep -niE '\bfg\b'` returns ZERO hits because POSIX ERE treats `\b` as a backspace, not a word boundary -- for any 2-letter token use `git grep -niwE 'fg'` (the `-w` word flag); a planning inventory that trusts the bare `\b` result would be empty and wrong (compounds Learning 187's "grep ALL extensions" with "use the right boundary flag"). **Process discipline applied:** orient -> owner picks "#82" -> light read-only grounding -> a SEPARATE pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` (planning vs implement-now; owner chose planning) -> 1B stub BEFORE technical work -> the plan IS the deliverable, decomposed as VERTICAL slices (compute -> validate -> surface, with validation as an explicit owner GATE before exposure, which is exactly the issue's "validate before exposing" ask) each with completion criteria + verification commands + a STOP, an evidence-based inventory with file:line (all surfacing line numbers re-verified FIRSTHAND before commit, FM #11/#20), a "here be dragons" list, and an owner-ratification checklist (D1-D6) -- then CLOSE OUT; do not start Slice 1 (FM #18, planning-to-implementation bleed). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the ARCHITECTURE/planning workstream + the ratified `issue2-...-plan.md` format), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (the #2 plan D6 was the spec -- planned the delta), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the scope gate + ASCII-only plan math), Learning 182 (validate against the authoritative function + no-bundled-fixture-can-validate), Learning 183 (the column-doubling K->2K deterministic shrink test reused for `fgSE`). **General rule:** for a planning session whose core is a derivation, do not just describe the approach -- spend agents to derive it three ways, reconcile adversarially, and EXECUTE the verification (run the math), because a wrong derivation ratified into the plan misleads every implementer after you, and the act of running it is what finds the adjacent bugs and the fixture gaps. (S203, plan issue #82 -- `fg` sampling SE)

#### Learning 190 -- A ratification session is not a rubber stamp: adversarially re-verify the recommendations against source BEFORE posing them, then fold each chosen option back into the plan -- and reconcile any text-vs-pseudocode contradiction the choice exposes. Ratifying the issue #82 plan (`docs/planning/issue82-fg-se-plan.md`, D1-D6 + slice plan). The plan was already strong (S203 did a 3-way derivation panel + an adversarial reconciler that ran R), so the temptation is to just forward its six recommendations to the owner and collect picks. Instead: (1) **re-grounded the load-bearing facts FIRSTHAND** (FM #11/#20) -- read `calcFG.R:61`/`calcFEFG.R:52` and CONFIRMED the silent-collapse bug is real (`p^2/0=Inf`; `na.rm=TRUE` strips `NaN` not `Inf` -> `S=Inf` -> `FG=1/Inf=0`), read `calcRetention.R:43` and confirmed the id-sorted-`r` vs position-`/` misalignment, read `calcGUSE.R` as the precedent; (2) ran a focused **3-agent adversarial re-verification** (one pair of decisions each, agentType `Explore`, structured verdicts) instructed to TRY to find a flaw or a tradeoff the owner is not being shown -- all six verdicts came back "sound," but two genuinely useful catches surfaced. **The key catch:** D4's recommendation text said HARD-FAIL (return `NA`+warning when `any(p>0 & r==0)`) but the Section 2.5 recipe PSEUDOCODE (`keep <- rhat > 0`) silently implemented SOFT-SUCCESS (drop the unretained founder, report `FG`/SE on the rest) -- a material behavior difference (false-confidence protection vs information recovery, and the reported FG refers to a DIFFERENT founder set) that the plan never surfaced as a decision. So I posed it as the explicit owner choice; owner chose hard-fail; I then **reconciled the recipe** (inserted an explicit pre-`keep` guard `if (any(p>0 & rhat==0)) { warning(...); return(NA_real_) }`) so the pseudocode the Slice 1 executor follows now MATCHES the ratified intent and Section 2.6 case 3 -- otherwise the executor would have coded soft-success from the recipe while believing they implemented the ratified hard-fail. The second catch: D6 "reconcile the parallel surfaces" was scope-ambiguous (does it include the longer-form `gvAndBgDesc.html` + the GVA vignette?) -- posed as a 3-way scope choice; owner chose broadest. **Recording the deliverable:** the ratification IS the deliverable, so it gets written INTO the plan, not just stated in chat -- a new "### 5.1 Ratified outcomes (Session 204)" block with each decision's chosen option, the Section 9 checklist boxes checked with the choices, the Status line flipped to RATIFIED, and the affected slice text (the recipe + the Slice 3 doc list) edited to the ratified behavior -- so the plan is now self-consistent and the executor reads choices, not open questions. **Question design:** 4 `AskUserQuestion`s covered all 6 decisions + the slice-count confirmation by bundling the pure-accept items (D1+D5+slices) into one and reserving slots for the genuine forks (D2/D4 coupled, D3 format, D6 scope); recommended-option-first, plain ASCII (`+/-` not the unicode sign, `->` not arrows), plain language ([[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[observation-vs-decision]] -- decisions are the owner's, I present + verify, owner picks). Close-out pushed FF ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]). NOT an implementation session (FM #18) -- Slice 1 is separate; Phase-3E runtime smoke is N/A (markdown plan, no runtime change). **General rule:** when the deliverable is "ratify a plan," the value you add over forwarding the recommendations is (a) re-verifying them against source so the owner ratifies sound defaults, and (b) catching where the owner's chosen option now contradicts something ELSE in the plan (a recipe, a downstream slice, a scope list) and editing those to match -- a ratified plan that still contains the pre-ratification contradiction will mislead the executor exactly as a wrong derivation would. (S204, ratify the issue #82 plan D1-D6)

#### Learning 191 -- When a code slice adds a name-aligned correctness guard ALONGSIDE an existing positional computation, an adversarial review of the IMPLEMENTATION (not just the math) must check the two for CONSISTENCY -- the mismatch is where a latent, already-shipped bug hides; and any "correct" reference value you computed during PLANNING must be checked against what the SHIPPED function actually returns, because a divergence is a bug, not a fixture footnote. Implementing Slice 1 of issue #82 (`R/calcFGSE.R` = the `fg` sampling SE; plus the ratified D2 zero-retention guard folded into `calcFG`/`calcFEFG` via a shared `@noRd` `checkFgDegeneracy()`). Strict TDD held cleanly (RED 11 assertions failing for the right reason -> GREEN -> REFACTOR extract-the-guard; every transition `AskUserQuestion`-gated; check 0/0/0, suite 0/0, spell 0, golden master `lacy1989` FG=2.180096626 unchanged). The math core was correct and well-tested (an INDEPENDENT sandwich-form oracle `sqrt(g' (cov(t(R))/K) g)` agreeing with the implementation's `O(K*F)` influence form `sd(crossprod(g,R))/sqrt(K)`; the oracle caught injected position/scaling bugs when probed). **The payoff was the ultracode adversarial review of the IMPLEMENTATION** (a general-purpose agent told to TRY to break the code, not re-derive the math): it confirmed `calcFGSE` correct, then found the real defect -- my new guard is name-aligned but `calcFG`/`calcFEFG`'s point-estimate formula `sum((fc$p^2)/r)` is still POSITIONAL, and `p` (getFounders/row order) and `r` (id-sorted via `tapply`/`rowsum`) are in DIFFERENT orders, so FG is silently WRONG on any unsorted-founder pedigree (Dragon D-3, but in the SHIPPED point estimate, not just the new code). Probed firsthand: `qcPed` ships **39.92** vs the correct **52.79**, and the wrong value is baked into `pedWithGenotypeReport`/`qcPedGvReport` (`fg=39.916464`); `examplePedigree`/`rhesusPedigree`/`pedWithGenotype` founders are all unsorted (affected), only `lacy1989`/`smallPed` are sorted (why golden master held and the suite -- which pins `fg` only as a column NAME, not a value, on the unsorted fixtures -- never caught it). **The meta-catch:** the issue #82 PLAN itself recorded "qcPed FG~52.8" (the reconciler computed it via an ALIGNED recipe) while the shipped `calcFG` returns 39.92 -- the plan's own correct value, sitting next to the shipped wrong value, was the unread smoking gun; nobody had run shipped-`calcFG`-vs-aligned-recipe side by side. **Scope discipline:** this is a pre-existing, MATERIAL bug whose fix needs regenerating shipped data objects (the bundled GV reports) + updating pins -- a blast radius outside Slice 1's ratified scope (the D2 guard was narrowly the `p>0 & r==0` zero-retention path). So I did NOT fold it in (FM #18/#25): posed the owner an `AskUserQuestion` (file-new-issue-and-finish vs expand-now), owner chose file-new-issue; filed **#86** (with the qcPed evidence and the dependency that it MUST land before #82 Slice 3 surfaces `FG +/- SE`, else a wrong FG pairs with a correct SE), fixed only the one doc-level item the review flagged (a misleading test comment), and closed out Slice 1. Slice 2's validation gate (a real deep pedigree) would independently re-surface the `calcFG`-vs-`calcFGSE` inconsistency before any exposure. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream + the `calcGUSE`/issue-#2 Slice-1 wiring precedent -- mirrored in SPIRIT: FG is a scalar so no per-animal column; note `calcGUSE` was itself never added to `_pkgdown.yml`, a pre-existing gap left as-is), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (the ratified plan Section 5.1/6 was the spec -- implemented the delta), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the phase gates + the scope-decision question), Learning 183 (the column-doubling `K->2K` deterministic shrink test `sqrt((K-1)/(2K-1))`, reused for `fgSE`), Learning 189 (the silent-collapse bug it predicted -- now guarded; AND its `git grep -niwE` 2-letter-token gotcha). **General rule:** an adversarial review earns its keep most when it checks not "is the new thing correct" (the tests already do that) but "is the new thing CONSISTENT with the old thing it sits beside" -- a correct new estimator bolted next to a quietly-wrong old estimator is a worse user experience than either alone, and the inconsistency is exactly what a test suite that pins the old value (or only its column name) will not see. (S205, implement issue #82 Slice 1)

#### Learning 192 -- When a bug fix entails regenerating bundled/frozen GOLDEN DATA, the load-bearing pre-implementation work is the data-blast-radius + reproducibility audit, and the regenerated golden value must come from running the ACTUAL documented production recipe firsthand -- not a planning estimate or a sub-agent's manual replica. Fixing issue #86 (`calcFG`/`calcFEFG` divided `p` in getFounders/row order by `r` id-sorted, BY POSITION -> wrong FG on unsorted-founder pedigrees; the one-line fix is `r <- r[names(fc$p)]`, the same alignment `calcFGSE`/`checkFgDegeneracy` already use). The CODE fix was trivial (two one-liners; golden master `lacy1989` FG=2.180096626 byte-unchanged; FE has no retention term, unaffected); the real work was everything around the bundled DATA. **(1) Blast-radius first (ultracode 4-agent workflow + firsthand spot-checks):** enumerate every bundled object embedding the wrong value (exactly 2 -- `qcPedGvReport`, `pedWithGenotypeReport`, both `$fg=39.9164644`), every test/doc that pins it (NONE in source -- only process-doc narrative; 0 tests break -- `test_reportGV.R` already pins the current full structure on FRESH output), and whether the frozen objects are STALE (they were -- predated `guSE`/`nMaleFounders`/`parentage`, so a documented-recipe regen refreshes structure too -- a scope fork I posed to the owner, who chose full regen). **(2) The near-miss that became the lesson:** the investigation agent computed the corrected `fg` via a MANUAL replica of `reportGV` and got 52.7546854; I baked that into the RED value-pin. But running the ACTUAL documented recipe (`set_seed(10); reportGV(qcPed, guIter=10000)`) under the fixed code gave **52.7641277** -- the agent's replica had not perfectly reproduced reportGV's RNG prefix (and `fg` also varies with `guIter`: ~52.778 at K=200, converging to 52.764 at K=10000; the issue's "52.79" was an illustrative K=1000 seed-1 figure, not the report's K=10000 value). I caught it at GREEN (the regen produced a different number than my pin) and corrected the pin to ground truth. **General rule:** a frozen golden value belongs to whatever the documented production recipe ACTUALLY emits, run firsthand; never pin a planning estimate or a sub-agent reconstruction. Structure the test so the **bug-discriminating** assertion (`expect_gt(fg, 50)` -- fails on the buggy 39.92, passes on any correct value) is SEPARATE from the **exact-value documentation pin** (`expect_equal(fg, 52.7641277, tol=1e-6)`); the discriminator proves the fix even if the pin needs a number you only learn at GREEN. **(3) Verify the recipe's seeding, don't assume:** the recipe doc said `set_seed(10)` -- a real package wrapper (`R/set_seed.R`, `sample.kind="Rounding"` on R>=3.6), NOT base `set.seed(10)` (`Rejection`); I almost "fixed a typo." Verified firsthand they yield the identical draw in this pipeline before trusting that my base-`set.seed` regen matched the doc. **(4) The fix closed a SECOND degeneracy path:** on the unsorted fixture the positional pairing landed a `p>0` contributor against a phantom `r=0` -> FG silently 0 with NO warning -- which the name-aligned #82 guard does NOT catch (it sees the truly-unretained founder has `p=0`); aligning `r` closes it. Strict TDD held (RED 6 assertions failing for the right reason -> GREEN; no REFACTOR -- minimal one-liners; scope-decision + PRE-RED->RED + RED->GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY #86 (no #82 Slice 2/3, no drive-by cleanups -- FM #18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with #82). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the documented `reportGV` data recipe in `R/data.R`), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (S205's handoff + the issue #86 body were the spec -- did the delta, not a re-investigation), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the gates + the two scope questions); Learning 191 (the bug this fixes -- S205's review predicted it), Learning 189 (the silent-collapse family). (S206, fix issue #86)

#### Learning 193 -- A "validate a Monte Carlo SE before exposing it" gate (issue #82 Slice 2) decomposes cleanly into strict-TDD-able PURE harness scorers (tested on tiny synthetic inputs with hand-computed answers) + a ONE-TIME recorded study (a build-ignored `data-raw/` runner) + a STATIC report; the load-bearing design choice is picking the validation pedigree by MEASURING which one actually exercises the failure mode, not by description -- and the harness's SE form must be tied to the SHIPPED estimator by an equality test so the study validates what users actually get. Implementing Slice 2 of issue #82 (the "validate before expose" gate for `calcFGSE()`, the `fg` sampling SE). The gate PASSED on both pedigrees (all seven checks), clearing Slice 3 to surface `FG +/- SE`. **(1) The shape that makes a slow stochastic validation strict-TDD-able.** Split the work into three layers: PURE SCORERS (`fgSEAgreementRatio = mean(SE)/sd(FG)`, `fgSECoverage`, `fgSEScalingRatio`, `fgSEFullFromMatrix`/`fgSEDiagFromMatrix`, `fgSEBootstrapFromMatrix`, `fgSEVerdict`) -- each pure/deterministic and unit-tested on crafted inputs whose answer is hand-computed (e.g. two orthogonal-centered retention rows give `cov=0` so full SE == diagonal SE == `sqrt(1/6)`); an ORCHESTRATOR (`fgSEValidate`) tested ONLY for shape + determinism on `lacy1989` at a tiny budget (it runs gene drops, so the full bands are NOT asserted in the suite -- that would be slow and flaky); and the SLOW B>=300-seed study, which runs ONCE via a `data-raw/` runner (build-ignored, never in CI) and whose numbers are embedded STATICALLY in a pkgdown `vignettes/articles/*.qmd` report (renders without re-running). The harness functions live in `tests/testthat/helper-fgSEValidation.R` so the suite unit-tests them AND the runner sources them (one definition, DRY). Net: zero new `R/` source, zero API/golden-master change -- a purely additive validation-infra slice. The verdict LOGIC is tested by feeding `fgSEVerdict` synthetic in-band/out-of-band summaries (PASS vs FAIL per check), so the ratified bands are exercised without the slow run. **(2) MEASURE which pedigree exercises the failure mode -- do not trust the plan's menu.** The plan offered "examplePedigree OR qcPed" as the real deep pedigree. A 3-line firsthand probe settled it decisively: per-seed cost (lacy 0.03s / qcPed 0.12s / examplePed-trimmed 0.40s -> B=300 at K=1000+4K is ~11 min, feasible in-session) AND -- the load-bearing signature -- the off-diagonal/bottleneck profile: `examplePedigree` full/diag SE = 0.692 with min `r` = 0.013 (5 founders r<0.10) vs `qcPed` full/diag = 1.013 with min `r` = 0.50 (none low). qcPed is WELL-RETAINED with negligible founder covariance, so it CANNOT validate the very thing the gate exists for (Dragon D-5: validating on a well-retained pedigree falsely blesses the diagonal/independence approximation). So the real-ped choice is settled by evidence, and I did NOT pose it as an owner question -- the data dictates `examplePedigree` (the deep bottlenecked one) + `lacy1989` (the fast deterministic anchor). **The headline result confirms it:** on `examplePedigree` the diagonal approximation OVERESTIMATES the SE by ~45% (full/diag 0.692; `0.0945/0.0654`) -- exactly Learning 189's ~46% prediction -- vs 0.1% on lacy; lacy alone would have shipped the diagonal shortcut and overstated colony uncertainty by nearly half. The full influence form is mandatory, and a real deep pedigree is mandatory to prove it. **(3) Tie the harness's SE to the SHIPPED estimator with an equality test.** `fgSEFullFromMatrix(R, p)` must equal `calcFGSE(ped, alleles)` to machine precision (a RED assertion on `lacy1989`), so the study measures what the package actually reports, not a re-implementation that could silently diverge -- the same "validate against the authoritative function" discipline as Learning 182/191. **(4) Two FP/tooling gotchas this slice surfaced.** (a) A coverage-boundary test literal `fgSECoverage(11.96, 1, ref=10, z=1.96)` FAILS because `11.96 - 10` is `1.9600000000000009` in floating point, just outside `1.96` -- the inclusive-`<=` implementation is correct, the TEST picked a non-representable boundary; fix the test to exactly-representable values (`11.5`, `z=1.5`) that still discriminate `<=` from `<` (a test-construction bug corrected during GREEN, intent and implementation unchanged -- cf. S206's golden-value-at-GREEN). Separate the bug-discriminating assertion from the exact pin, as always. (b) In a Quarto `.qmd`, a backtick span beginning with `r ` (e.g. a backtick-r-space-`0.75` span) is parsed as INLINE R CODE and aborts the render -- put a bare `r` in its own code span; only `r` triggers this (a `K = 1000` span is safe). And edit `inst/WORDLIST` with `sort(x, method = "radix")` (C-locale/ASCII byte order, matching the file) NOT bare `sort()` (locale-aware collation) -- the latter re-orders the whole file into a 187-line diff instead of +3. **(5) Process:** the ~11-min study was run in the BACKGROUND (harness-tracked -> re-invokes on completion, or sooner on error), so the session never blocked; the runner + its `.rds` + the article are all `.Rbuildignore`'d, so the slow study never touches `R CMD check`. Strict TDD held (RED 8 tests / 45 assertions failing for the right reason -> GREEN; no REFACTOR -- the harness is cohesive; PRE-RED scope + PRE-RED->RED + RED->GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY Slice 2 (no Slice 3 surfacing -- FM #18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with the whole #82 branch). Verify: full suite 0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, lint 0, article renders; Phase-3E runtime smoke N/A (no runtime/UI change -- `calcFGSE` is surfaced in Slice 3). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the ratified plan Section 6 Slice 2 was the spec), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (implemented the planned delta, did not re-derive), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the scope + phase gates), Learning 189 (the ~46% off-diagonal materiality it predicted -- now MEASURED at ~45% on the real ped; the "no bundled fixture / use a real deep pedigree" mandate), Learning 191 (validate-against-the-shipped-estimator), Learning 183 (the deterministic-SE testing mindset). **General rule:** for a "validate before expose" gate, the validation is only as good as the pedigree it runs on -- measure the candidate's failure-mode signature (here: off-diagonal covariance + thin-retention founders) and reject a fixture that looks adequate but structurally cannot exercise the thing under test; and make the harness measure the shipped function by construction, or the green report proves the wrong thing. (S207, implement issue #82 Slice 2 -- fgSE validation gate)

#### Learning 194 -- When surfacing an additive companion value (`fgSE` beside `fg`) across N display surfaces, the load-bearing decisions are (a) guard EVERY surface on present-AND-finite so old/bundled objects and degenerate (NA) results degrade to the bare value, and (b) RESIST extracting a shared formatter when the surfaces' BARE-value formats already differ -- the dedup would force a behavior change. Plus: an additive return element breaks exact-name-pin structure tests (update them, they are the contract, not discriminators); and a UI "runtime smoke" without a browser is faithfully done by driving the real render code paths (`testServer` `output$...`, the pure HTML/text functions) on a REAL `reportGV()` result. Implementing Slice 3 of issue #82 -- surface `FG +/- SE` in `reportGV()` + the GV-tab summary, Summary-Statistics founder table, text `summary()`, and `makeFounderStatsTable` HTML, with the broad D6 guidance reconciliation. The `guSE` wiring (issue #2 Slice 3) was the near-exact precedent and made this low-risk: thread `fgSE <- calcFGSE(ped, alleles)` from the SAME gene drop next to `fg = feFg$FG` (a colony SCALAR -- no per-animal `$report`/`$gu` column, plan F2), then display inline. **(1) The recurring shape: guard on present-AND-finite, degrade to bare.** Each of the four display sites is `if (!is.null(fgSE) && is.finite(fgSE)) sprintf("%.2f +/- %.2f", fg, fgSE) else <bare FG>`. The `&&` short-circuits NULL (older objects / the re-export of a NULL `fr$fgSE`) before `is.finite` (which is `logical(0)` on NULL and would error in `if`); `is.finite` also catches the ratified hard-fail `NA` (degenerate FG) -> show bare FG, never `+/- NA`. The bundled `pedWithGenotypeReport`/`qcPedGvReport` (left UNCHANGED per owner -- additive, not regenerated) are exactly the absent-`fgSE` path, so the backward-compat tests run against real shipped objects. **(2) Do NOT extract a shared `fgWithSE()` helper here -- the bare formats diverge.** The `+/-` (with-SE) form is identical everywhere, but the BARE form is NOT: gvSummary/modSummaryStats use `sprintf("%.2f", fg)`, `summary()` uses `as.character(round(fg, 2L))` (e.g. `52.7`, no trailing zero), and `makeFounderStatsTable` uses `as.character(round(fg,2))` AND a prior `is.na(fg) -> "N/A"` branch. A single sprintf-bare helper would change `summary()`/`makeFounderStatsTable` bare output from `22.3` to `22.30` (a behavior change, and it breaks the `grepl(">22.3<")` backward-compat pin). So the ~3-line guard is duplicated 4x ON PURPOSE; the cohesion is in the precedent, not a helper (cf. S207's same call). Without a helper, the `+/-` format-string trips `nonportable_path_linter` (the `/` looks like a path) at each site -- suppress with the project's `# nolint: nonportable_path_linter.` (inline where the line stays <=80; `# nolint start/end:` block on its own lines where indentation pushes the inline comment over 80, matching `getProductionStatus.R`). **(3) An additive return element breaks exact-name-pin tests -- update them as the contract.** Adding `fgSE` to `reportGV()`'s return made two `expect_named(gvReport, c("report",...,"fg","maleFounders",...))` blocks in `test_reportGV.R` fail; insert `"fgSE"` after `"fg"` to match the new (intended) structure. This is NOT weakening a discriminator -- it is recording the additive contract change (and `expect_named` is order-sensitive, so position matters). The integration test asserts PROPERTIES (`fgSE` present/numeric/length-1/finite/>0 on `qcPed`; ABSENT from `$report`/`$gu`) not an exact value, because reportGV's INTERNAL gene drop is not externally reconstructible (an external `set.seed(1); geneDrop(...)` gives DIFFERENT alleles -- reportGV consumes RNG before its gene drop -- so an external `calcFGSE` exact-match is impossible; `fg` finite+healthy on qcPed already implies `fgSE` finite+>0 since retention varies). **(4) Headless UI runtime smoke = drive the real render paths on real data.** Phase-3E "launch the app" is faithfully satisfied without a browser by running the ACTUAL render code: `testServer(modGeneticValueServer, ...)` with a real pedigree -> `output$gvSummary` contains `+/-`; `testServer(modSummaryStatsServer, founderStats=reactive(list(...fgSE=...)))` -> `output$summaryStats` founder cell contains `+/-`; and the pure `summary()`/`makeFounderStatsTable()` on a real `reportGV(qcPed)` result print `52.79 +/- 0.04`. That exercises the same code an app launch would, deterministically. **(5) D6 "broadest" doc scope, verified by reading not by the plan's list.** Of the plan's named doc surfaces, two (`gvAndBgDesc.html`, `_genetic_value_analysis.Rmd`) turned out to contain NO founder-genome-equivalents description at all (grep-confirmed) -- so "reconcile every FG surface" meant editing the five that DO describe FG and recording that two have nothing to reconcile, not forcing an edit into them. A guidance-content RED test (`genetic_value.html` must mention "founder genome") makes the in-app doc note a tested deliverable, mirroring the issue-#2 gu-guidance test. **(6) Tooling:** `devtools::document()` (run because `@return`/`@param` changed) also re-wrapped `man/calcGUSE.Rd` (pure whitespace -- the known local-roxygen artifact; `git checkout` it) and CORRECTED two stale data-doc `@format` lines (`pedWithGenotypeReport.Rd`/`qcPedGvReport.Rd`: `GVnprcmanag`->`nprcgenekeeprGV`, length `8`->`10`) left stale by S206's data regen -- accurate auto-output, kept (reverting just re-dirties on the next `document()`). Quarto render of an article leaves `*.html` + `*_files/` + an auto `.gitignore` -- delete them (build artifacts, not deliverables). Strict TDD held (RED across 5 files, every new test failing for the right reason -- `fgSE` length 0 / no `+/-` / `genetic_value.html` lacks "founder genome"; GREEN; no REFACTOR; PRE-RED scope + PRE-RED->RED + RED->GREEN all `AskUserQuestion`-gated). Scope discipline: did ONLY Slice 3 (no NEWS bullet -- folds into the publish session, Learning 157a; no bundled-data regen -- owner pick; FM #18/#25). On branch `issue-82-fgse`, committed locally, not pushed (publishes with the whole #82 branch). Verify: full suite 0/0, `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, lintr clean on touched lines, GVA article renders, Phase-3E PASSED. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT workstream; the `guSE`/issue-#2 Slice-3 wiring precedent; the ratified plan Section 6 Slice 3 + D3/D6 were the spec), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (implemented the planned delta), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the scope + phase gates; ASCII `+/-`), Learning 191 (the `calcFG` order bug #86, now fixed, so a correct FG pairs with this SE), Learning 193 (Slice 2 calibrated the SE this surfaces). **General rule:** surfacing an additive companion value is mostly the `guSE`-style wiring, but the two judgment calls are (a) guard present-AND-finite at every site so absent/NA degrade to the bare value, and (b) DON'T DRY the format when the bare paths already differ -- the duplication preserves behavior that a shared formatter would silently change. (S208, implement issue #82 Slice 3 -- surface FG +/- SE)

#### Learning 195 -- Publishing a multi-slice branch where the OWNER merges: re-run the FULL gate on the branch TIP (not a stale slice's gate), put a closing keyword in the PR BODY for EVERY bundled issue and VERIFY GitHub linked them, and advise a merge commit (not squash) so the slice commits survive. Publishing the whole `issue-82-fgse` branch (Slice 1 `calcFGSE`+guard / #86 founder name-align / Slice 2 validation gate / Slice 3 surface `FG +/- SE`) as **PR #87 -> master**; owner chose "open the PR, you merge it" + "merge commit, keep S205-S208" at the publish gate. **(1) The merge gate is the TIP, not the last slice.** Each slice ran its own `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)`=0/0/0; but this session adds the deferred `NEWS.md` bullets (Learning 157a), which changes the tree, so the AUTHORITATIVE pre-push gate is re-running the full set on the FINAL commit -- `check` 0/0/0 + clean-regression suite read (`test_dir`, `sum(failed)`/`sum(error)`, true offenders excl. `test-app-`/`test-e2e-` = 0) + `spell_check_package(".")`=0. Don't treat S208's green as the merge gate. (Ran both in the background while the owner answered the publish-method `AskUserQuestion` -- gate green by the time they replied.)
