NEWS.md is NOT covered by the package's `spelling.R` test by default (it checks Rd/vignettes/DESCRIPTION), so keep NEWS wording plain and rely on the explicit `spell_check_package` run; here `FG` was already in `inst/WORDLIST` and the run came back 0. **(2) Closing keywords -- the recurring auto-close trap, closed and VERIFIED.** Every prior slice commit named "issue #82"/"#86" with NO closing keyword on purpose (whole-branch publish, FM #18), so both issues were still OPEN at publish time. Put one keyword PER issue in the PR BODY (`Closes #82` and `Closes #86` on their own lines -- a single `Closes #82 #86` only links the first). Then VERIFY GitHub actually parsed them: `gh pr view --json closingIssuesReferences` is NOT a field in the installed gh (it errors with the available-fields list), so confirm via GraphQL -- `gh api graphql -f query='{repository(owner,name){pullRequest(number:87){closingIssuesReferences(first:10){nodes{number title}}}}}'` returned BOTH #82 and #86. A keyword GitHub doesn't link leaves the issue open after merge; verifying is cheap insurance against the trap the gotchas have warned about for many sessions. **(3) Owner-merges => the PR is the deliverable, the merge is not.** With "open PR, you merge it," the session DELIVERABLE is a review-ready PR (build evidence + closing keywords + a "use Create a merge commit, not squash" note in the body so the four slice commits and their issue refs survive); #82/#86 stay OPEN until the owner clicks merge, and master stays at `19e4b3d2` == origin/master (no local-ahead drift -- the close-out docs ride the PR branch to origin, satisfying [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] without touching master). **(4) Keep the publish commits clean.** Commit only `NEWS.md` in the publish commit; the SESSION_NOTES 1B stub stays in the working tree and is replaced by the full handoff in the separate close-out commit (CHANGELOG + this learning + handoff), which is pushed to update the PR. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (whole-branch publish model FM #18; the S202 PR-publish precedent; build-equivalent = `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] (posed the merge-method + history-style choices as one `AskUserQuestion`, ASCII labels), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **General rule:** a publish session's job is to make the merge SAFE and ONE-CLICK for the owner -- re-gate the tip, wire and verify the closing keywords for every bundled issue, and tell the owner exactly which merge button preserves history. (S209, publish issue #82 branch via PR #87)

#### Learning 196 -- Before overwriting bundled/golden data, VERIFY the documented recipe reproduces the COMMITTED values in-memory FIRST; a "purely additive" regeneration can mask a pre-existing NON-reproducible save, and a coarse aggregate can hide a difference a continuous statistic reveals. Regenerating the two bundled GV reports (`qcPedGvReport`/`pedWithGenotypeReport`) so they carry `fgSE` (added to `reportGV()` in S208). I had told the owner the regen was "purely additive" -- `fg` would reproduce the committed `52.7641277` and only `fgSE` would be added. **It was not, and the verify-before-save guard caught it.** **(1) Compute-and-verify-against-the-committed-value BEFORE `save()`.** The regen script computed both reports in memory and compared `fg`/`fe` to the committed values before writing; `fg` came back `52.7546854`, NOT the committed `52.7641277` (a real 0.0094 gap, not FP noise -- `fe` matched to 8e-9). The guard REFUSED to save and surfaced the discrepancy instead of silently overwriting a golden value. Never `save()` frozen data first and check later; gate the save on reproducing the committed numbers. **(2) An isolated worktree AT THE SAVING COMMIT decisively assigns the cause.** To rule out "a later change (S208's additive `fgSE` line) shifted `fg`," I ran the clean recipe at S206's own code state: `git worktree add --detach <scratch> 83d8640d`, then `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=FALSE R_LIBS_USER=<main renv lib> Rscript ...` to borrow the main project's library (a fresh worktree's renv lib is empty -> `pkgload` missing). It ALSO produced `52.7546854`. So the committed `52.7641277` was never reproducible from its own recipe -- a contaminated RNG-state save (the gene drop ran from a different RNG position than `set_seed(10)` immediately-before), not code drift. The worktree-at-the-saving-commit is THE diagnostic for "does the committed data match the recipe at the time it was saved." (Irony: `52.7546854` is the value S206's sub-agent originally computed before S206 "corrected" it to the non-reproducible number. Learning 192's "run the documented recipe firsthand" was right, but the firsthand run was itself contaminated -- the missing step was verifying the SAVED OBJECT reproduces, which this learning adds.) **(3) A coarse aggregate can hide a gene-drop difference a continuous statistic reveals -- diff old-vs-new to bound the blast radius.** Loading the OLD object from git (`git show HEAD:data/x.RData > tmp; load(tmp, envir=e)`) and comparing to the new showed `$report$gu`/`$rank`/`$value`, the `$gu` vector, and `$kinship` byte-IDENTICAL; ONLY `fg` changed (+`fgSE`). `gu` is reported as INTEGER percents -- too coarse to register the small clean-vs-contaminated gene-drop difference -- while `fg` (continuous) does. This old-vs-new diff is exactly what answered the owner's "are vignettes or `@examples` affected?" decisively: NO -- nothing any vignette/example reads from these objects changed, and no vignette/example reads `fg` from them anyway (the GVA article computes `fg`/`fgSE` LIVE on `examplePedigree`, not the bundled reports). **(4) `tools::resaveRdaFiles(\"data/\")` is a footgun -- scope it.** On a directory it recompresses EVERY `.RData` (here all 19), dirtying 17 unrelated files; pass the explicit file paths, and `git checkout -- data/` to undo the directory-wide churn before re-running scoped. **(5) A moved value is a PREMISE change -- re-surface it even mid-GREEN.** Because I had told the owner the regen was additive (`fg` unchanged), discovering `fg` actually moves to `52.7546854` (display `52.75`, breaking the #86 pin and the `52.76` display patterns) is a scope/premise change, not a detail. I stopped GREEN, reported, and re-gated via `AskUserQuestion` (proceed to the reproducible value / investigate the contamination / abort) rather than quietly editing a #86-established golden value. Then strict TDD: returned to RED to correct the value pins (`52.7641277 -> 52.7546854`, `52.76 -> 52.75`), confirmed they fail for the right reason (data still old), then GREEN saved. #86's name-alignment fix is INTACT -- only the precise saved number is corrected to its reproducible form. Verify: `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0, full suite 0/0, `spell_check_package` 0, only the two `.RData` modified; filed issue #88 for the incidental stale GVA `_freeze` (frozen output omits `fgSE`, predates S208). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT; build-equivalent `check(vignettes=FALSE)` 0/0/0), [[observation-vs-decision]] (the owner's "regenerate" was given on an additive premise I had supplied and then had to correct -- re-surfaced the changed premise as a decision, did not assume), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], Learning 192 (run the documented recipe firsthand -- EXTENDED here: also verify the saved object reproduces, since the firsthand run can itself be contaminated), Learning 194 (the additive-companion `fgSE` wiring this completes for the bundled objects). **General rule:** regenerating frozen data is not "additive" until proven -- verify the recipe reproduces the committed values in-memory before saving, attribute any mismatch with a worktree at the saving commit, diff old-vs-new to bound the blast radius (and to answer "what downstream is affected"), and re-surface a moved value to the owner as a decision rather than silently correcting it. (S210, regenerate bundled GV reports to carry fgSE + correct non-reproducible fg)

#### Learning 197 -- A "stale cache / re-render the docs" issue may have ZERO published impact: verify the LIVE artifact and the CI build model BEFORE treating a local cache as a publication defect. Working issue #88 ("the GVA article's Quarto `_freeze` is stale -> the published article omits `fgSE`"). The issue premise was FALSE for the published site, and three checks proved it: (1) **curl the live page** -- `https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/articles/genetic-value-analysis.html` already rendered `fe 109.67 / fg 47.62 / fgSE 0.29` (all three values); (2) **the CI build model** -- the freeze lives under gitignored `.quarto/` (`.gitignore:44`) and `pkgdown.yaml` does a clean `checkout@v4` with NO freeze-restore/cache step, so CI never sees a committed freeze and re-executes EVERY article fresh; (3) **the CI history** -- the pkgdown run on the S210 merge succeeded AFTER `fgSE` shipped (run 28264621260). **Root cause of the stale LOCAL freeze, and why it never reached publication:** the article loads `library(nprcgenekeepr)` (the INSTALLED package), and the local renv library held a stale 2.0.0 that predated `fgSE` (`renv::status` out-of-sync; `calcFGSE` not exported, `reportGV` body has no `fgSE`) -> `gv$fgSE` was NULL at local freeze time; CI instead installs the current source via `local::.` (setup-r-dependencies) so its render carries `fgSE`. The stale freeze was therefore a purely local, gitignored artifact with no repo or published impact. **The correct \"refresh\":** regenerating the freeze IN PLACE locally would REPRODUCE the stale output (the installed package still lacks `fgSE`), so the right move is to CLEAR the gitignored cache (commits nothing) and let CI / a post-`install()` build regenerate it correctly -- not to run a heavy install+render or, worse, re-bake the stale value. **Sibling audit:** all 6 articles publish (HTTP 200); GVA was the only one with a live `fgSE`-dependent value (correct); `fg-se-validation` uses static recorded numbers (`data-raw/fgSEValidation-results.rds`) so it is unaffected by definition. **When to apply:** any \"stale cache / re-render\" or \"the published docs are wrong\" issue -- FIRST determine whether the cache is committed and whether CI consumes it; if CI rebuilds fresh from source, the PUBLISHED artifact is the source of truth -- verify it directly (curl the live page, check the deploy run) before assuming a defect or spending a session \"fixing\" a non-defect. Surface a wrong premise to the owner as a disposition decision (close-as-verified vs act) rather than mechanically executing the issue's literal ask. Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (posed the premise-is-wrong finding + disposition via `AskUserQuestion`, did not silently close or blindly re-render), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (verified the real published state instead of re-running a \"fix\"), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]]. Extends Learning 196 (S210 filed #88 from the local freeze JSON without checking the live site -- the inference outran the evidence). **This was NOT a TDD code-phase session** (verification + docs + issue close; phase N/A). (S211, verify+document+close issue #88)

#### Learning 198 -- For a recurring inventory issue (one repeatedly re-audited, like #37), the non-redundant deliverable is the DELTA at the current HEAD, not a fresh full audit -- and the cheapest reliable way to find the delta is to diff the export/symbol surface between the prior-audit HEAD and now, then re-run the documented reachability recompute and RECONCILE the count by SET, not by total. Auditing #37 ("exported functions not used by the Shiny app"), already audited S65/S78/S97 (S97 declared the actionable surface fully drained). Per [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], started by reading the most recent audit doc (`docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md`) + the issue's own documented method, then scoped the delta inline: `git diff a5507a35..HEAD -- NAMESPACE` -> exactly 10 added `export()` lines, 0 removed; and a recompute of the issue's reachability method (`codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE)` transitive closure seeded at `runModularApp`/`runGeneKeepR`/`appUI`/`appServer`, sourcing all 220 `R/*.R` into a throwaway env -- renv not materialized in-checkout, so `pkgload::load_all` fails and static sourcing is the correct equivalent). **(1) The headline insight: the composition drifts even when the count holds.** The unused count stayed 39 across the FOURTH re-verification (S65/S78/S97/S212; exports grew 155->176), but THIS time 4 exports moved -- `getPedigree`/`getPedDirectRelatives` graduated keep-as-public-API -> USED (wired in *for free* by an unrelated file-pedigree-source refactor, the first #37 export to graduate without a dedicated wire-in), and `gvaConvergence` (new vignette helper) + `makeGrpNum` (the #29 rename alias, moved used->unused BY DESIGN) entered. `39 - 2 + 2 = 39`. So "the count is unchanged" is NOT evidence "nothing moved"; reconcile the SETS (`setdiff` both directions) and explain every mover, or a real status change hides behind a stable total. The S97 method note flagged this risk; this session is the proof. **(2) Scope the work-list inline first, THEN fan out adversarial verification over JUST the delta.** Rather than re-verify all 176 exports, the NAMESPACE diff + recompute yielded a 13-export delta (10 new + 2 graduated + 1 alias); a 26-agent evidence-then-refute workflow (Explore agents: stage 1 trace the firsthand call path for "used" or gather keep-evidence for "unused"; stage 2 independently TRY to refute) covered exactly those 13. A deterministic static-reachability "used" result is STILL worth one adversarial refute pass: 12/13 agreed, and the one refutation corrected a `loadSpeciesOverrides` downstream-consumer mis-attribution (right status USED -- it is called directly at `appServer.R:74` -- wrong claim about which module consumes the result). The recompute classifies; the workflow supplies citable call-path evidence + catches mis-attribution. **(3) Distinguish an intended status change from a regression.** `makeGrpNum` moved used->unused, which looks like a regression but is the deliberate #29 rename (app now calls `makeGroupNum`; `makeGrpNum` is a genuine `.Deprecated("makeGroupNum")` wrapper at `R/makeGroupNum.R:32`, still exported as the soft-deprecated alias -- keep-as-public-API, future-retire only after its deprecation cycle, NOT a stale export and NOT a defect). Confirm such a mover firsthand (read the definition + grep the live callers) before labeling it. **(4) Read-only audit -> issue close/update is OWNER judgment, not an auto-close** (S97 precedent): wrote the dated `docs/audits/...` report, recommended close-or-refresh-body, performed 0 issue-state changes. Disposition unchanged: 0 wire-in / 39 keep-as-public-API / 0 retire; `safeExecute` (0 callers ever) + `makeGrpNum` (post-deprecation-cycle) the only conditional future-retire candidates. Carried as applied: [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (did the delta, not a re-run), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the issue's own documented reachability method is the spec; AUDIT_WORKSTREAM report structure), [[observation-vs-decision]] (recommended, did not close the issue), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] (the deliverable-pick `AskUserQuestion`), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends the S97 #37 re-verification (same method, +1 method-confirmation: composition drifts under a stable count). **This was NOT a TDD code-phase session** (read-only audit; phase N/A each response). (S212, delta re-verification audit of issue #37)

#### Learning 199 -- For an EMPTY-BODIED feature issue, the planning deliverable is a design doc whose spec is reconstructed from the title + owner Phase-1 decisions (ASKED, not invented), written to the project's ACTUAL doc home, and ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED before commit -- the verification pass over the freshly-drafted plan catches design flaws that firsthand drafting misses. Owner picked "#13" ("Assign kinship coefficients into the kinship coefficient matrix based on outside information", filed 2020-11-20, **title only, empty body**). **(1) Empty body -> ask, do not fabricate a spec.** Posed TWO owner decisions via `AskUserQuestion` -- deliverable shape (design doc / plan / thin slice) and core semantics (pair-level override / full-matrix replace / both) -- and the title + answers ARE the spec. Process-history scan ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]) found no prior #13 work beyond the S62/S95 backlog audits' "genuinely open, large" tag. **(2) Consult project source of truth for the doc HOME, not just the content.** The base methodology's `DESIGN_WORKSTREAM.md` is UI/layout-specific (star component, column balance, mobile views) -- the WRONG fit for a backend algorithm/feature; the project's real convention is `docs/planning/issueN-<slug>-plan.md` (issue9/issue73 house style: status banner, firsthand evidence-based inventory, `Dn` decisions with options+recommendation flagged for ratification, vertical slices with RED/GREEN/DONE/Verify/Session-boundary/Dragons, consolidated dragons, ratification checklist). Drafting and ratification are SEPARATE sessions (FM #18) -- this session drafts; a follow-up ratifies before Slice-1 RED. ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]] applies to WHERE a deliverable lives, not only how it reads.) **(3) Find the single chokepoint + reuse the in-repo pattern.** The kinship matrix from `kinship()` (`R/kinship.R:69`, custom Therneau/Lange, id-named base matrix) is the one object every genetic consumer reads, so a pair-level override is mechanically "write `kmat[id1,id2]` + the symmetric twin"; the design is a leaf `applyKinshipOverrides` + validator threaded into `reportGV` as a `NULL`-default param -- the EXACT pattern `reportGV` already carries (`breedingTable`/`gestationTable` from #73), with `kinship()` left untouched (6 callers; the #9 blast-radius boundary). Three vertical slices (script core -> Shiny upload -> fallback paths + close), not horizontal (FM #25). **(4) THE META-LEARNING: adversarially verify the PLAN's design, not only its file:line inventory.** A 3-agent workflow (citation accuracy / consumer-inventory completeness / design red-team) over the DRAFT confirmed the core symmetric-REPLACE mechanism sound but caught FOUR real issues firsthand drafting missed, all folded in PRE-commit: (a) applying before `meanKinship` (`R/reportGV.R:124`) STACKS the override with the issue-#9 `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` `+sexMean/2` correction for the feature's OWN primary use case (one-unknown-parent animals) -- I had framed the placement as a pure benefit (D11, the #1 dragon, now a genetics-methodology decision for the owner); (b) `filterKinMatrix(probands, ...)` narrows `kmat` to the population subset, so a strict `stop()`-on-unknown-id leaf called directly by `reportGV` ABORTS the run for the canonical sire-dam case (sire/dam are usually ancestors, not probands) -> soft warn-drop must live in `reportGV`, not only the file loader (D5); (c) a `[0,1]` kinship range is too loose -- off-diagonal kinship is `<= 0.5` for non-inbred pairs (Cauchy-Schwarz) and the common `r`(relatedness)-vs-`f`(kinship) data-entry confusion (r = 2f) passes `[0,1]` and silently corrupts the matrix (D6); (d) `convertRelationships`' `relation` label is pedigree-CEPH-derived, not value-derived, so a value override yields self-contradictory rows (`kinship=0.5` next to `relation="No Relation"`) -- narrow the Slice-3 claim. Re-verified the workflow's NEW citations firsthand before writing them (FM #11). **General rule:** for a feature whose design must reason about a heavily-documented ADJACENT feature (here #9's mean-kinship correction, documented at length in the very session notes I'd read), that interaction is exactly where firsthand drafting blind-spots hide -- a red-team pass over the draft is high-ROI and the plan is not "done" until it is verified, even when every inventory citation is correct. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] (the deliverable+semantics `AskUserQuestion`), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends the issue9/issue73 planning-doc pattern. **This was a planning/design session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S213, design doc for issue #13 kinship overrides from outside information)

#### Learning 200 -- RATIFYING a high-ceremony design plan: batch the low-stakes recommendations via `AskUserQuestion`, but GROUND the `/grill-me` on the #1 load-bearing (genetics/algorithm) decision in a FIRSTHAND, EXECUTED analysis FIRST -- the analysis both makes the owner's call defensible AND can correct a load-bearing mechanics claim the draft's own red-team got wrong. This is the "ratification is a SEPARATE session" half of the issue9/issue73 pattern (Learning 199 named the drafting half). Owner picked "ratify the #13 design draft," then chose "batch recs, grill D11" at the process gate. **(1) Batch the low-stakes decisions, recommendation-first.** D1, D2, D4-D9 + slice order (10 items across 3 `AskUserQuestion` calls, each recommended option listed first and labeled "(Recommended)") -- the owner accepted every one as written (0 overrides). A ratification pass over a well-structured draft (each `Dn` already carries options + a recommendation + a §7 checklist, the issue9/issue73 house style) is fast precisely because the drafting session did that work; the batch just flips `[ ]` -> `[x]` with the resolved outcome. **(2) For the #1 dragon, verify firsthand and QUANTIFY before grilling -- do not grill on the draft's prose.** D11 (override interaction with the issue-#9 `+sexMean/2` unknown-parent mean-kinship correction) is a genetics-methodology call; a 4-agent workflow (`wf_a3c184ee-92b`: correction mechanics + issue-9 S177 ratification rationale + an EXECUTED numeric model on real `qcPed` + a structured-output synthesis) ran BEFORE the grill. It quantified what the draft only asserted ("stacks"): the stacking term IS `sexMean/2` ~= 1 SD of the colony distribution, ~8.4x the override's own effect, flipping a worked headline animal GV rank #6 -> #179 -- magnitude that makes "document-and-accept" indefensible and forces the fix. A `/grill-me` grounded in an executed model lets the owner (geneticist) make a defensible call, exactly as issue-9's own D2 was settled (S177). **(3) THE META-LEARNING: a ratification analysis can CORRECT the draft it ratifies -- even a claim the draft's red-team produced.** S213's D11 text (and its red-team) claimed the override "propagates into OTHER animals' corrections via their `sexMean` (uncorrected `original` snapshot)." FIRSTHAND-FALSE: the spurious `+sexMean/2` is written only to `corrected` (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:175`) while `sexMean` reads the `original` snapshot (`:174`), so the double-count CANNOT cascade; only the override's legitimate raw `Delta/N` (~0.017 SD) reaches peers. That correction de-risked the sub-decision (keep overridden animals as cohort peers -- nothing to fear). A red-teamed draft is not a verified draft; the ratification session re-verifies the load-bearing mechanics firsthand (FM #11/#20), and the plan records the correction explicitly rather than silently. **(4) Grill the decision TREE, and offer the premise-REVERSING option.** Walked D11 one question at a time, each with a recommendation: top-level fix-vs-accept; then the supersede-vs-independent crux that determines whether blanket-suppression (A) is even RIGHT (a single off-diagonal override fixes one cell, while `+sexMean/2` corrects the whole-row scalar) -- offered "keep the prior / accept stacking" (which REVERSES Q1) so the owner consciously rejects it rather than only picking among ways to implement an assumption; then the cohort sub-decision; then the v1 scope edges (both-unknown promotion, shared-sib-pair coupling -> document as limitations, FM #25). Owner ratified blanket-A + keep-cohort-peers + document-edges + track-targeted-option-C-as-follow-up. **(5) Record every answer back INTO the doc (DRAFT -> RATIFIED).** Status banner + a ratification-record paragraph; per-`Dn` `-> RATIFIED (S214)` tags; D11 fully rewritten (corrected mechanics + magnitude + grilled decision + the 4-part regression-test spec); Slice-1 scope/RED sharpened from "the ratified alternative" to the SPECIFIC blanket-A behavior so the executor has no interpretation gap; R11 + the §7 checklist flipped. **General rule:** a ratification session is not a rubber stamp -- batch the easy calls, but for any load-bearing genetics/algorithm decision verify the draft's mechanics firsthand + quantify the magnitude + offer the premise-reversing option BEFORE the owner decides, then write the resolved spec back into the plan precisely enough that Slice-1 RED needs no further interpretation. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the ratification HOME is the plan's own §3 + §7, not a new doc), [[observation-vs-decision]] (posed every decision; assumed none), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (grounded the grill in issue-9's S177 ratification record), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 199 (the S213 draft this ratifies). **This was a ratification/design session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S214, ratifying the issue #13 kinship-overrides design plan; D11 settled via `/grill-me`)

#### Learning 201 -- IMPLEMENTING a ratified high-ceremony plan slice under strict TDD: an "interpretation-ready" plan makes RED authoring fast, but still VALIDATE every test fixture firsthand on the real data BEFORE freezing the RED tests, and let an INDEPENDENT recomputation (not the implementation) be the assertion backbone. Owner picked "implement #13 Slice 1." The S214-ratified plan's §4 Slice 1 (scope/RED/GREEN/DONE/Verify/Dragons all reflecting the resolved D1-D11) was genuinely interpretation-ready, so RED was quick to draft -- but the high-ROI work was de-risking the fixtures and tests against `qcPed` FIRST. **(1) Validate the fixture on the real data before freezing the RED test.** The D11 4-part regression needs an animal that is BOTH a one-unknown-parent override target AND a valid cohort peer of another one-unknown animal. All 43 `qcPed` one-unknown animals are *sire*-missing, so every #9 cohort is male-only -- the "overridden animal is still a peer" part is only exercisable if the override target is MALE. An executed scratchpad exploration found `X=0K7VJN` (male, one-unknown) sitting in `Z=K0ACWS`'s male cohort, and confirmed all four parts reproduce (X's `+sexMean/2` suppressed -> `corrected == original`; Z still corrected; X in Z's cohort; suppress-vs-no-suppress differ ONLY at X), plus the legit raw colMeans flow to peers (Z rises 1.79e-05, the spurious term does NOT cascade). A RED fixture not checked against the data can assert an impossible condition (a female X could never be a male-cohort peer) and pass for the wrong reason. **(2) The independent base-R recomputation is the backbone.** Mirroring the existing issue-#9 Slice-2 test, the new `reportGV` test asserts the WHOLE `indivMeanKin` vector equals a base-R model that applies the override to `kmat`, recomputes `colMeans`, then runs the blanket-A correction -- so the test rests on the ALGORITHM, not on the implementation, and no-cascade falls out as "differ-only-at-X." **(3) Verify a test's control is sound before freezing it.** Confirmed baseline `reportGV(qcPed, guIter=100)` emits ZERO warnings, so the warn-drop test's bare `expect_warning()` cannot pass spuriously on some unrelated warning. **(4) When a ratified plan is internally inconsistent, the precise decision wins -- and say so.** The §4 RED bullet loosely wrote the validator "rejects > 0.5," but the precise D6 text and the §7 checklist say the standalone validator WARNS (the matrix-aware leaf rejects via the exact `sqrt(diag_ii*diag_jj)` bound). Implemented per D6/§7 (warn) and surfaced the reconciliation at the PRE-RED gate rather than silently choosing. **(5) Reuse the in-repo pattern; each function self-validates.** `checkKinshipOverrides` mirrors `checkGenotypeFile` (stop on structural/domain errors, warn on the soft range); `applyKinshipOverrides` is the strict leaf that also re-validates its own input -- a deliberate robust-public-API choice (cheap on a tiny frame), so `reportGV` validates once for the warn-drop and the leaf validates again; offered as REFACTOR, owner chose skip (a noted optional tidy, not a defect). `reportGV` threads only the SURVIVING overridden id-set into `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`, whose new `overriddenIds` guard `next`s at the TOP of the one-unknown loop (leaves the value at its override-influenced `colMeans`, never flags it, never removes it from others' cohorts). `kinship()` stayed untouched (D1). **(6) Close-out mechanics:** `NEWS.md` is GENERATED from `NEWS.Rmd` (edit the `.Rmd` source + `rmarkdown::render`, never hand-edit `NEWS.md`); make a `spell_check_package` flag on a common English word ("validator") go away by REWORDING the prose, not by padding the shared `inst/WORDLIST` with a non-domain word. Phase-3E genuinely N/A (Slice 1 is script-level; the Shiny smoke is Slice 2's gate) -- stated, not skipped. **General rule:** even when the plan removes all design ambiguity, the executor's firsthand value-add is (a) proving each RED fixture is REALIZABLE on the actual data, (b) anchoring assertions to an implementation-independent model, and (c) reconciling any residual plan inconsistency toward the precise ratified decision, out loud. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] (surfaced the #13-closed state + the D6 wording rather than acting silently), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 199/200 (the draft + ratification this implements). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S215, issue #13 Slice 1 -- outside-information kinship overrides in `reportGV`)

#### Learning 202 -- WIRING a script-level feature into the Shiny app (the "app delivery" slice) under strict TDD: the new risks are (a) a soft/optional input ABORTING its mandatory consumer, (b) extending a function's signature SILENTLY breaking the test doubles that mock it, and (c) doing an honest Phase-3E for Shiny wiring in a HEADLESS environment. Owner picked "merge PR #89; reopen #13 and continue the work" -> the single development deliverable was Slice 2 (upload an outside-kinship CSV in the Genetic Value tab; D7). **(0) Do the owner-directed outward-facing admin carefully, not blindly.** "Merge PR #89" is explicit authorization, but before merging I confirmed the full R-CMD-check matrix was green and SURFACED that the red `lint` check is the long-standing whole-codebase informational job (red on master through S211-S214; `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` not `BLOCKED` -> non-required), so the merge matched house convention and wasn't a blind merge-over-red. Report-faithfully ([[observation-vs-decision]]): name the red check and why it's non-blocking; don't silently merge or silently refuse. **(1) A nested reactive that uses `req()` ABORTS its caller.** The GV run (`gvResults <- eventReactive(input$runAnalysis, ...)`) must run whether or not an override file was uploaded. A `kinshipOverrideData` reactive written with `req(input$kinshipOverrideFile)` would, when called from inside `gvResults()`, throw the shiny silent-error up through the parent and ABORT the whole analysis whenever no file is present -- breaking the backward-compat (D10) path. Fix: guard an OPTIONAL input feeding a MANDATORY pipeline with an explicit `if (is.null(input$x)) return(NULL)` and let the consumer treat `NULL` as \"no override,\" reserving `req()` for inputs whose absence really should halt. **(2) Extending a real function's signature breaks test DOUBLES that hardcode the old one -- and only the FULL regression read catches it.** Slice 1 added `kinshipOverrides` to the real `reportGV`, but two issue-#73 tests in `test_modGeneticValue.R` mock `reportGV` via `local_mocked_bindings` with a HAND-COPIED signature; the moment Slice 2's module passed the new arg, those mocks errored \"unused argument.\" The two new test files passed in isolation -- the breakage showed up ONLY in the full clean regression read (`test_dir`, exclude `test-app-`/`test-e2e-`). General rule: when you change the call to a widely-mocked function, run the FULL suite, not just the file you added, and update every test double's signature to mirror the real one (the mock is a contract that must track the real signature). **(3) Headless Phase-3E for a Shiny-wiring change (FM #24 is REQUIRED for app slices).** \"Build-clean + unit tests pass\" is not enough for runtime wiring, but a headless agent can't click a browser file-picker. The defensible substitute: (i) launch the REAL app (`runModularApp(port=..., launch.browser=FALSE)` in the background), poll the port, fetch the page, and `grep` the served HTML for the new namespaced input id (`geneticValue-kinshipOverrideFile`) -- this proves the module change doesn't break app construction AND the new UI element is wired into the live app, not just into the module function in isolation; (ii) lean on a `testServer` integration test that drives the REAL module server with the actual `fileInput` data shape Shiny produces (a data.frame with `name`/`size`/`type`/`datapath` columns, `datapath` pointing at a real temp CSV) -> `reportGV` -> assert the ranking moves; (iii) state the residual gap (a true browser click-through needs a GUI/Chrome driver) EXPLICITLY -- do not let \"app starts\" masquerade as \"feature works end-to-end.\" Kill the background app afterward (`lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill`). **(4) Pick a deterministic fixture for an inherently-noisy module.** `reportGV` runs a random gene-drop, but mean kinship (`indivMeanKin <- meanKinship(kmat)`) is read straight off the (deterministic) kinship matrix. Overriding a BOTH-unknown founder pair (`F1,F2` -> 0.4) raises each one's mean kinship deterministically AND sidesteps the issue-#9 one-unknown correction entirely, so `expect_gt(ovrKin, baseKin)` is stable run-to-run with no seed. **(5) Reuse the app's reader/validator split and the soft-wrapper-at-the-consumer pattern.** `readKinshipOverrides` mirrors `getGenotypes` (Excel via `readExcelPOSIXToCharacter`, else `read.table`); the module's soft `tryCatch(withCallingHandlers(checkKinshipOverrides(read...), warning=notify+muffle), error=notify+NULL)` keeps the app non-fatal (D5) while letting a `>0.5` warning surface yet still apply (D6). The soft wrapper lives in the module (it calls `showNotification`, so it needs session context and can't be a pure helper -- noted at the REFACTOR gate, owner skipped). Stayed strictly in Slice 2: the breeding-group/summary-stats fallback recompute paths (`modBreedingGroups.R:173`, `modSummaryStats.R:357`) are Slice 3 (FM #18/#25); surfaced the R9 intermediate-state caveat (\"run the GV tab first\") in NEWS rather than bundling the fallback patch. **General rule:** an \"app delivery\" slice is where script-level correctness meets reactive plumbing -- budget the firsthand work for the three wiring-specific failure modes (optional-input-aborts-consumer, signature-change-breaks-mocks, headless-Phase-3E) rather than re-deriving the already-tested core. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 199/200/201 (the draft, ratification, and Slice-1 core this delivers to the app). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S216, issue #13 Slice 2 -- GV-tab kinship-override upload)

#### Learning 203 -- WIRING a feature into a DOWNSTREAM consumer's FALLBACK path (when the upstream delivery is a Shiny upload) is a plumbing-mapping problem first: read the actual reactive return lists and each consumer's prefer-vs-fallback branch FIRSTHAND, apply overrides only where the value is missing, and assert the DETERMINISTIC input of a stochastic consumer rather than its noisy output. Owner picked "1" (Slice 3, the FINAL slice, closes #13). **(0) Two owner scope-decisions BEFORE declaring RED, surfaced not assumed (a separate `AskUserQuestion`, then the phase gate).** R13 (relation label-vs-value divergence) = "narrow + document": the override moves the kinship VALUE everywhere (matrix, relationship-table `kinship` column, CSV export) but the relation LABEL stays pedigree-derived, with NO change to `convertRelationships` (which has other callers -- narrow avoids a shared-function blast radius). Branching = STACK Slice 3 on the unmerged slice2 branch (PR #90 open) rather than block the session on a merge. **(1) Verify whether the upstream module EXPOSES the input -- it usually does NOT.** Slice 2's `kinshipOverrideData` reactive was INTERNAL to `modGeneticValueServer`; its return list (`geneticValues`/`topAnimals`/`nAnalyzed`/`kinshipMatrix`/`founderStats`/`maleFounders`/`femaleFounders`) did not include it. Slice 3 had to ADD `kinshipOverrides = kinshipOverrideData` to the return list, then thread `gvResults$kinshipOverrides` through `appServer.R` to both consumer modules (mirroring `founderStats = gvResults$founderStats`). The plan's "source from the same input as Slice 2" line hid this expose+plumb step; reading the actual return list firsthand surfaced it. **(2) Distinguish consumers that get the value TRANSITIVELY from those that ALWAYS recompute -- they need different reasoning.** Breeding-groups `getKinshipMatrix` PREFERS `geneticValues()$kinship` (already overridden via Slice 1/2) and recomputes `kinship()` only as a fallback -> apply on the fallback branch only. Summary-stats `getKinshipMatrix` prefers a passed `kinshipMatrix()` but `appServer` passes `kinshipMatrix = NULL`, so it ALWAYS hits the fallback recompute -> it never saw overrides at all before Slice 3 (NOT "transitive then fallback" as the S216 gotcha framed it -- a finer firsthand read corrected this). Apply on the fallback branch in both; do NOT re-apply on the preferred branch (the GV-output matrix already carries them; REPLACE is idempotent but double-application is confusing). **(3) D11 does NOT apply to Slice 3.** The issue-#9 `+sexMean/2` correction lives only in `reportGV`'s mean-kinship SCALAR; the modules consume the raw kinship MATRIX (groupAddAssign threshold / `convertRelationships` / CSV export), so Slice 3 is pure matrix-cell replacement -- `applyKinshipOverrides` only, no `overriddenIds` threading. **(4) For a STOCHASTIC consumer, assert the DETERMINISTIC input, not the noisy output.** Breeding-group formation (`groupAddAssign`) is RNG-driven; rather than a brittle "these two are not co-housed" end-to-end assertion, the RED tests call `getKinshipMatrix` directly (testServer exposes the server's internal functions/reactives by name) and assert the matrix that FEEDS `groupAddAssign` carries the override (symmetric write) -- deterministic, isolated, and that matrix is the sole kinship input to formation. Same for summary-stats: assert `getKinshipMatrix()` and `relationshipData()`'s `kinship` column, not a rendered plot. **(5) Demonstrate R13 "narrow" with a pedigree-RELATED pair.** `convertRelationships` derives `relation` from pedigree CEPH structure and consults the kinship value only as a last tiebreak (`:87`, `kinship > 0 -> "Other"` for an otherwise-unrelated pair). So for an UNRELATED pair an override flips "No Relation"->"Other" via the EXISTING tiebreak (not new behavior); but for a pedigree-RELATED pair (F1 is O1's sire -> "Parent-Offspring") the override moves the value to 0.1 while the label stays "Parent-Offspring" -- the clean proof that the label is structural, not value-derived. **(6) DRY the soft warn-drop into ONE helper, but stop short of refactoring the working upstream.** The intersect + warn-drop + `applyKinshipOverrides` soft wrapper (D5 -- never abort on an id absent from the matrix) now lives in a single `@noRd` helper `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix` shared by both modules; `reportGV` keeps its own inline copy because it ALSO needs the surviving `overriddenIds` for the #9/D11 threading (the helper returns only the matrix), so a clean-DRY there would change the helper's contract and touch the working #9 path -- offered as REFACTOR, owner skipped. **(7) Close-out mechanics:** NEWS from `NEWS.Rmd` + render (retired the slice-2 "run the GV tab first" caveat); `spell_check_package` flagged the NOUN "recomputation" while the VERB "recomputes" (in roxygen) PASSED -> reword to the verb, do not pad `WORDLIST`; Phase-3E for a Shiny-wiring change = launch the REAL app, CONNECT a session (which runs `appServer` and instantiates the modules with the new arg, so a wiring error would surface) + grep the served page for the override `fileInput`, and state the browser-click gap explicitly. **General rule:** wiring a feature into a downstream consumer's fallback is a plumbing-mapping problem first -- read the actual reactive return lists + each consumer's prefer-vs-fallback branch firsthand, apply only where the value is missing, and assert the deterministic input of any stochastic consumer. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 199/200/201/202 (the draft, ratification, Slice-1 core, and Slice-2 app delivery this completes). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S217, issue #13 Slice 3 -- breeding-group + summary-stats fallback kinship-override paths; closes #13)

#### Learning 204 -- COMPLETING a stacked-PR merge arc is careful outward-facing admin, and a closing keyword ANYWHERE in a PR body fires on merge. Owner picked "merge PR #90," then (after the #90 merge accidentally closed #13) "Reopen, then merge PR #91." The deliverable was purely outward-facing (two PR merges + issue-state admin); no `R/`/test change -- TDD code-phases N/A, like the S214 docs-only session. **(1) Verify each PR's FULL R-CMD-check matrix is GREEN before merging -- `mergeable: MERGEABLE` is necessary but not sufficient.** `gh pr view <n> --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus` + `gh pr checks <n>`: `UNSTABLE` (not `BLOCKED`) means mergeable despite non-passing NON-REQUIRED checks -- here the only reds are the long-standing whole-codebase `lint` informational job (red on master since before S211) and `codecov/patch` (a coverage-delta check). Confirm macOS + Windows + all Ubuntu (devel/oldrel-1/release) jobs PASS, then merge -- the house "careful-admin" standard (Learning 202 §0): name the red check and why it's non-blocking; never blind-merge-over-red nor blind-refuse. **(2) For a STACK, merge the base PR with a MERGE COMMIT (not squash) and KEEP the base branch.** `gh pr merge <base> --merge` (matching the repo's PR #89 precedent `0438c2d5`) preserves the base PR's commit SHAs, so GitHub recognizes them as ancestors of `master` and the dependent PR's diff NARROWS to just its own commits; a squash would orphan those SHAs and force a rebase. Keeping the base branch protects the still-open dependent PR. **(3) Merging the base RE-TRIGGERS the dependent PR's CI** -- moving its base (master advanced) reruns its workflow on the new merge commit; two matrix jobs went `pending`. RE-VERIFY the dependent's matrix went GREEN on the new base before merging it; wait for `pending` to clear rather than merge over a pending correctness signal (UNSTABLE/non-required would have allowed an immediate merge, but careful-admin waits). A bounded background poll (`while gh pr checks <n> | grep -qi pending; do sleep 60; done`) re-invokes on completion without blocking. **(4) THE GOTCHA: a closing keyword (`closes`/`fixes`/`resolves` `#N`) ANYWHERE in a PR body -- even inside a descriptive sentence like "Slice 3 closes #13" -- is parsed by GitHub and FIRES on merge to the DEFAULT branch.** PR #90 ("Relates to #13 ... Slice 3 closes #13") auto-closed #13 the moment it merged, even though Slice 3 was not yet on master -- so the tracking issue read "done" prematurely. Closing keywords fire only on MERGE (that's why #13 stayed open while #90 sat open). **AUDIT a PR body for stray closing keywords before merging**; for a non-final PR that mentions the issue, phrase it as "Slice 3 WILL close #13" / "Relates to #13" with NO `closes #13` substring. **(5) To restore an accidentally-closed tracking issue whose final slice isn't merged yet: reopen it, then merge the final PR -- and reopen BEFORE the final merge** so the final PR's `closes #N` fires on an OPEN issue and produces the proper "closed via PR #N" linkage (firing `closes #N` against an already-closed issue is a no-op with no link). **(6) Issue lifecycle is the OWNER's call -- surface a premature close, don't silently reopen.** Posed reopen-and-pause / reopen-then-merge-final / leave-closed as an `AskUserQuestion` ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23) rather than assuming. **(7) Close-out for an admin session:** Phase-3E N/A (no `R/`/runtime code authored -- merged already-tested, CI-green code; the merged code's runtime is covered by the upstream sessions' Phase-3E + the dependent PR's green post-merge matrix, a fuller check than a local `devtools::check`); build-equivalent not run (markdown-only changes don't trigger it, per the S214 docs-only precedent); sync local master via `fetch`+`reset` not `pull`; push close-out docs to origin/master ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]). **The preventable ding:** a pre-merge audit of #90's body for closing keywords would have avoided the close->reopen->close churn on #13 -- prevention beats cure. **General rule:** an outward-facing stacked-PR merge is careful admin -- green-matrix-before-merge each PR, merge-commit + keep-branch for the base, re-verify the re-triggered dependent CI, audit PR bodies for stray closing keywords, reopen-before-final-merge to restore an accidentally-closed issue, and surface issue-state decisions to the owner. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 202 §0 (the careful-admin merge practice) and completes the issue #13 arc begun in Learning 199-203. **This was an admin/merge session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S218, completed the issue #13 merge arc -- merged Slices 2+3 to master; #13 CLOSED via PR #91)

#### Learning 205 -- DOCUMENTING a shipped feature's behavior + LIMITS in the in-app UI is a real, test-backable deliverable: assert the doc TEXT in the rendered UI and the static guidance assets, put it where the input is SUPPLIED, and mind that `helpText()` renders each argument as a separate text node. Owner asked "are item-3's [gvaConvergence / relationship-label / unknown-parent] implications documented in the app?", then "Documentation should appear at a minimum in the UI where the kinship coefficient(s) are supplied." The deliverable was DOCUMENTING the three issue-#13 deferred follow-ups (NOT implementing them) across three in-app surfaces. **(0) An "is X documented in-app?" audit is a FALSE-NEGATIVE risk -- enumerate ALL in-app surfaces and grep each broadly before concluding "absent."** The app's user-facing doc surfaces are inline `helpText()`, the per-tab `includeHTML()` guidance assets (`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html`), and the About/Documentation tab (`appUI.R`). The item-3 implications were recorded only DEVELOPER-facing (roxygen `@param` + code comments in `modSummaryStats.R`) and PACKAGE-level (NEWS, planning doc) -- ZERO in-app coverage; the override-upload helpText covered FILE FORMAT only. A function can be MENTIONED in guidance without its caveat: `gvaConvergence` already appears in `genetic_value.html`/`gvAndBgDesc.html`, but neither said it ignores overrides. **(1) Put the documentation where the input is SUPPLIED (the owner's "at a minimum" floor).** The first-class surface is the `helpText` at the override `fileInput` in `modGeneticValueUI`, not just the tab-level guidance HTML -- a user who never opens the guidance panel still sees the caveats at the upload control. Placement (supply-point only vs + guidance panels) and content depth were ratified via `AskUserQuestion` ([[observation-vs-decision]]): user-facing COPY that ships in the package is the owner's editorial call; offered a "review exact wording first" hold at the GREEN gate. **(2) THE RENDERING GOTCHA: `helpText(a, b, c)` renders each argument as a SEPARATE, newline-separated text node, NOT a space-joined string.** So an asserted multi-word phrase split across arguments ("...the relationship" / "label...") renders with a newline between the words and `grepl(fixed = TRUE)` MISSES it. Keep each asserted phrase inside ONE string argument -- or build it with a single `paste(...)` (whose space-join happens BEFORE it becomes one text node) so source lines stay <=80 while the rendered phrase is contiguous. The HTML assets have the same constraint: keep an asserted phrase on one line (no mid-phrase line break). **(3) For RED discriminators, the rendered UI INCLUDES the included HTML -- pick phrases absent from the WHOLE rendered output.** `as.character(modGeneticValueUI("gv"))` embeds `genetic_value.html`, which already contains "gvaConvergence" -- so asserting "gvaConvergence" in the UI test would FALSE-PASS at RED. Chose helpText-EXCLUSIVE phrases ("change the kinship value", "convergence check ignores", "relationship label") verified absent from the current 9.3KB rendered UI before freezing; 12/14 assertions failed (the 2 passing = the `nzchar(path)` file-exists guards), 0 errors. **(4) Phase-3E for a UI-TEXT change is still REQUIRED (FM #24) -- and read the app's ACTUAL port from its startup log.** Launched the real `runModularApp()`, fetched the served page, grepped for all six new caveat strings (all PRESENT; 0 startup errors). `runModularApp` set its OWN port (6013) and ignored my `options(shiny.port = 6099L)`, so the first curl hit a refused port -- parse "Listening on 127.0.0.1:PORT" from the log rather than assuming the port you requested. **(5) Documenting a known limitation is a legitimate small deliverable.** The three item-3 follow-ups (gvaConvergence-ignores-overrides; relationship label-vs-value divergence; the unknown-parent #9 edge cases) remain UNIMPLEMENTED, but the gap that users couldn't SEE the limits is now closed -- a cheap, high-value slice distinct from the deeper implementation work, which stays on the backlog. **(6) SESSION-BOUNDARY discipline: a "session" is a LOGICAL unit (orient -> one deliverable -> close out), not a chat window -- but don't claim/gate an implementation while still in Phase-0/1 discussion.** I slid from orientation + the doc-coverage investigation straight into writing the 1B stub and posing the RED gate; the owner flagged it ("shouldn't this be a new session"). Reverted the premature stub, clarified that the investigation was the front-half (orient/scope) of ONE deliverable with no committed artifact to close out separately, and re-claimed cleanly once the owner chose to continue in-conversation. The investigation feeding the scope is fine; auto-promoting it into a claimed deliverable before the owner sets the deliverable is the miss. **General rule:** documenting a feature's limits in-app is test-backable -- audit every in-app surface to avoid a false "undocumented" negative, write the docs where the input is supplied first, assert the text in the rendered UI + the static assets (each asserted phrase in one text node / one line), pick RED discriminators absent from the WHOLE rendered output, and smoke the real app on its actual port. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 199-204 (the issue #13 design/implementation/merge arc whose deferred limits this documents). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT (documentation) session (RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S219, documenting issue #13 item-3 kinship-override behavior + limits in the in-app UI)

#### Learning 206 -- WIRING an existing feature into a SIBLING consumer that shares a pipeline STAGE: read the sibling's FULL handling firsthand (a shared correction step makes "apply to the matrix only" a SILENT-DIVERGENCE trap, not a style choice); recognize when implementing X FALSIFIES a prior session's docs + the doc-tests pinning them; for a diagnostic whose output is INSENSITIVE to the change on the easy fixture, prototype the perturbation on the HARD fixture before freezing RED; and lint your edits (package loaded) before the owner has to ask. Owner picked "Item-3 IMPLEMENTATION follow-ups" -> (slice `AskUserQuestion`) `gvaConvergence` override support, the most self-contained of the three deferred item-3 follow-ups S219 documented. **(1) "item-3 follow-ups" is THREE deliverables -- surface the slice pick (1-and-done, FM #18/#25).** A category is not a deliverable; posed the slice choice (`gvaConvergence` / R13 relationship-label / D11) as a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` with the self-contained one recommended, then claimed only that slice. **(2) A sibling consumer that shares a pipeline STAGE needs the sibling's FULL treatment, not just the obvious half.** `gvaConvergence` mirrors `reportGV`'s ranking pipeline AND calls the same `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`; `reportGV` both (a) writes overrides into `kmat` AND (b) threads `overriddenIds` into the #9 correction (skips `+sexMean/2` for an overridden one-unknown animal -- D11 supersession). Applying only (a) -- the literal "reuse `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix`" ask -- would leave a one-unknown overridden animal RANKED differently than `reportGV` ranks it, defeating the diagnostic's whole purpose (it exists to characterize `reportGV`'s selection order). Reading the sibling's block firsthand turned "matrix-only vs match-reportGV" into a pre-RED owner decision (FM #13 literal-minimum avoided); owner chose Match reportGV -- a CORRECTNESS call, not a style one. The `overriddenIds` machinery already existed (this is NOT the deferred D11 option-C/both-unknown/sib-pair work). **(3) Implementing a follow-up can FALSIFY a prior session's docs AND the doc-tests pinning them -- fold the correction in.** S219 had documented (in `modGeneticValue` helpText + `genetic_value.html` + a NEWS bullet) that "gvaConvergence ignores overrides / ranks on pedigree kinship," and `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` asserted those exact phrases ("convergence check ignores", "does not use"). Honoring overrides made all of them FALSE. Surfaced "update the S219 docs now vs defer" as a pre-RED scope decision (owner: update now), then corrected the three doc surfaces + the two doc-test assertions as part of GREEN -- shipping a feature with contradicting docs/tests is incoherent. General: grep for prior in-app/NEWS statements about the behavior you are changing; the doc-test that locked the old statement is part of YOUR change surface. **(4) For a diagnostic whose OUTPUT is insensitive to the change on the easy fixture, prototype the perturbation on the HARD fixture BEFORE freezing RED.** `gvaConvergence`'s convergence metrics are gene-drop-churn-driven; on a no-gu pedigree (qcPed) both half-splits agree (overlap=1, rankAgreement=1) REGARDLESS of mean kinship, so an override is output-INVISIBLE there. A scratchpad copy of the function body (with the planned override block) run on the gu-bearing convergence fixture found a perturbation (5 offspring pairs @ 0.49) that GUARANTEED a curve change -- so the differential RED test (`expect_false(isTRUE(all.equal(base, over)))`) fails-then-passes for the RIGHT reason rather than risking a GREEN no-op. Reading-only prototyping of the EXISTING system to design a robust failing test is legitimate RED work. **(5) Honestly scope an assertion the function's public surface cannot express.** The #9-suppression NUMERIC (does an overridden one-unknown animal skip `+sexMean/2`?) is not observable through `gvaConvergence`'s return (it exposes convergence metrics, no per-animal mean kinship; no-gu pedigrees are output-invariant). Rather than build a brittle bespoke gu+one-unknown fixture (against the test file's stated anti-tautology philosophy), test 5 exercises the PATH (runs clean + override honored via the "N kinship override(s) applied" message on a real one-unknown qcPed animal, `X=0K7VJN`) and the numeric is covered by construction-parity with `reportGV` + `test_reportGV.R`'s D10/D5/D11 tests (the identical `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship(overriddenIds=)` call). State the residual; do not fake a direct assertion. **(6) Lint changed R files BEFORE the owner has to ask -- with the package LOADED.** Owner correction mid-session ("correct your coding/comment standards to avoid creating new lints"): I had introduced two `line_length_linter(80)` violations (the new `@param`; a former-last-arg line pushed to 81 by my added trailing comma). The CI `lint` is long-standing red, but my EDITS must be lint-clean. Establish the real signal by linting with `pkgload::load_all()` first: `object_usage_linter` "no visible global function" warnings drop to 0 once the namespace is present, and `object_name_linter` camelCase warnings appear only when linting a file OUTSIDE the project dir (the project `.lintr` allows `snake_case`/`CamelCase`/`camelCase`; `tests`/`vignettes`/`inst` excluded) -- so line-length/style are the real signal. New memory [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]. **(7) Close-out mechanics:** `spell_check_package` flagged the singular "proband" (WORDLIST has only the plural "probands") -> reworded to "analysis set" (matching `reportGV`'s own `@param` for the identical concept), did NOT pad WORDLIST; `man/` regenerated via `document()` (no NAMESPACE change -- already exported, helper `@noRd`); NEWS from `NEWS.Rmd` + render (removed the now-false clause from the S219 bullet, added the item-3 bullet); Phase-3E REQUIRED because the deliverable edits in-app UI text even though the core function is script-level (launched the real app, grepped the served page for the corrected text, read the actual port 6013 from the log). **General rule:** wiring a feature into a sibling that shares a pipeline stage is a "match the sibling's FULL handling" problem; implementing a follow-up can falsify prior docs + their doc-tests (fold the fix in); prototype the perturbation on the fixture where the change is observable before freezing RED; honestly scope assertions the public surface can't express; lint your edits (package loaded) before being asked. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]; extends Learning 199-205 (the issue #13 arc -- the first of the three item-3 follow-ups S219 documented as limits). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR offered, owner skipped).** (S220, issue #13 item-3 -- gvaConvergence honors kinship overrides)

#### Learning 207 -- Completing the push->PR->CI->merge arc for an ALREADY-FINISHED slice as careful outward-facing admin: the new nuggets are (a) confirming the lone CI red is PRE-EXISTING whole-package noise via the check-run ANNOTATIONS API when `gh run view --log` comes back empty, and (b) a git-state trap when syncing local master with an uncommitted 1B stub still in the tree. Owner picked "1" -- the full admin arc S220 suggested: push the branch, open a PR "Relates to #13" with NO closing keyword, watch CI to green, merge. **(0) Verify-then-merge, do not merge-on-`MERGEABLE`.** `MERGEABLE` + the owner's explicit "merge" IS authorization, but the careful-admin bar (Learning 204 / [[observation-vs-decision]]) is: confirm the FULL R-CMD-check matrix (macOS + Windows + Ubuntu devel/oldrel-1/release) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov checks are all green, and SURFACE the one red (`lint`) with evidence it is non-blocking, BEFORE merging -- not merge the moment GitHub says mergeable. **(1) When `gh run view <run_id> --log` / `--log-failed` returns EMPTY for a failed job, get the per-file findings from the check-run ANNOTATIONS API.** `gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/check-runs/<JOB_ID>/annotations` returned the 12 lint annotations after `--log` came back 0 bytes (a known gh quirk for some failed jobs). They were entirely PRE-EXISTING whole-package noise -- `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` (implicit-assignment / line-length / commented-code) plus a Node.js-20 workflow-deprecation warning -- with NONE in this PR's changed files (`gvaConvergence.R`, `modGeneticValue.R`), corroborating S220's "lint 0 on the changed files (package loaded)." That annotation evidence is what turns "lint is red" into "lint is the documented non-blocking red AND this PR added nothing to it" -- the concrete form of "surface the non-blocking red." **(2) Syncing local master with an uncommitted 1B stub present is a git-state trap.** `git checkout master` ABORTS when the working tree has uncommitted changes (the Phase-1B stub I had added to `SESSION_NOTES.md`), so a chained `git reset --hard origin/master` reset the CURRENT (feature) branch to the merge commit instead of fast-forwarding master -- discarding the stub and moving the WRONG pointer. Low harm here (the 1B stub is always superseded by the full handoff; `reset --hard` leaves untracked files like the audit HTML untouched), but the lesson: after the merge, get the tree clean FIRST (the stub is disposable -> let it go, or commit it), THEN `git switch master` + `git merge --ff-only origin/master`, and VERIFY `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and local-master == origin/master before trusting the sync. Do not chain `checkout` then `reset --hard` blind to the abort message. **(3) PR-body hygiene for a CLOSED tracking issue.** #13 is CLOSED; the body says "Relates to #13," and I grep-scanned the RENDERED body for any `clos(e|es|ed)/fix(es|ed)/resolv(e|es|ed)` adjacent to a `#` (none) before merge AND re-verified #13 stayed CLOSED after -- a stray closing keyword fires on merge to the default branch even inside prose (Learning 204 / S218 close->reopen->close churn). **General rule:** an admin/merge session's deliverable is a VERIFIED integration -- full matrix green + the lone red PROVEN pre-existing (via the annotations API if `--log` is empty) + a closing-keyword-free body for a closed issue + a clean, correctly-ordered local sync. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]; extends Learning 204 (the careful-admin merge practice) and closes out the S220 gvaConvergence slice (Learning 206). **This was an admin/merge session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S221, issue #13 item-3 -- merged PR #93)

#### Learning 208 -- Branch-hygiene deletion of MERGED feature branches as careful outward-facing admin: verify each branch firsthand, know that `git branch -d` checks the branch's UPSTREAM (not only HEAD), and enumerate the set from `git branch --merged` rather than trusting the handoff prose. Owner picked "All 5 issue-13 branches" (one `AskUserQuestion`). **(1) Verify-before-delete is the SAFEGUARDS "never delete without verifying it's committed" rule applied to branches.** Before deleting anything, confirmed all 5 issue-13 branches were merged into BOTH `master` and `origin/master` (`git branch --merged master` + `git branch -r --merged origin/master`) -- deleting a branch whose commits all live in master loses nothing (recoverable via the merge commits / reflog), which is exactly what makes the deletion safe. **(2) `git branch -d` compares against the branch's UPSTREAM, not only HEAD.** Four branches deleted cleanly with the safe `-d`; the fifth, `issue13-item3-gvaconv-overrides`, REFUSED -- "not yet merged to refs/remotes/origin/... even though it is merged to HEAD" -- because S221's `reset --hard` had moved its LOCAL tip to the merge commit `daa7728b` (the "ahead 1" quirk, Learning 207), so it is ahead of its remote feature tip while still fully in master. Resolution: prove containment explicitly with `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master` (exit 0) THEN `git branch -D` -- a VERIFIED force-delete, not a blind one. Do NOT reach for `-D` on the first refusal; confirm the ancestor relationship first. **(3) Enumerate the deletion set from git, not the handoff prose.** The S221 handoff named 4 branches but `git branch --merged master` showed 5 issue-13 branches (`issue13-slice1-kinship-overrides` was merged via PR #89 but never re-listed in the prose) -- the complete-job set came from the merge query, and slice1's omission was SURFACED to the owner rather than silently followed or silently expanded (FM #13 literal-minimum / [[observation-vs-decision]]). **(4) Remote deletion is outward-facing -> confirm the exact set first.** `git push origin --delete` modifies origin, so the precise set (5 vs 4; local + remote; whether to also sweep the unrelated stale merged branches `module`/`rlabkey-version-floor`, left alone as out-of-scope) was a single pre-execution `AskUserQuestion`; `dev` is a mainline branch and was never a candidate. **General rule:** branch hygiene = verify-merged-firsthand (BOTH refs) -> enumerate from `git branch --merged` -> confirm the outward-facing set with the owner -> safe `-d` first, and only `-D` after `merge-base --is-ancestor` proves containment. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]; extends Learning 207 (the "ahead 1" quirk this deletion had to handle). **This was an admin/branch-hygiene session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S222, issue #13 branch hygiene -- deleted 5 merged branches local+remote)

#### Learning 209 -- Adding a DISPLAY-LAYER FLAG for a documented divergence as the narrow alternative to a wider core change (issue #13 item-3 R13): when a prior session shipped the "narrow + document" fork of a reconcile/flag decision, the remaining work is the OTHER fork (flag), and the owner's blast-radius call (display-layer vs touching the shared core function) is a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`. Owner picked "Item-3 IMPLEMENTATION follow-ups -> R13" -> recon mapped the relationship display (it is NOT an on-screen table; the per-pair output reaches the user only via the "Export All Relationships" CSV + the returned `relationships` reactive) and the divergence (`convertRelationships`'s `relation` LABEL is pedigree-derived at `:51-91`; only the kinship VALUE is overridden, so a pair can self-contradict). **(1) "R13 reconcile/flag" is two FORKS and two SURFACES -- surface them, do not assume.** Fork A flag-vs-relabel: a single kinship coefficient does NOT identify a unique relationship (0.25 = parent-offspring OR full-sib; 0.125 = half-sib OR grandparent OR avuncular), so relabel-from-value is genetically UNSOUND -- flag (mark the pair, infer nothing) is the correct call; surfaced as the owner's genetics decision, not picked silently. Fork B blast radius: the display-layer choice (annotate in `modSummaryStats` AFTER `convertRelationships`, using the override frame the module already receives) leaves the shared core function untouched (S217's deliberate boundary; `convertRelationships` has other callers + feeds the class table), the narrowest single TDD slice. **(2) D10 byte-identical = add the flag column ONLY when overrides are supplied.** `flagOverriddenRelationships(rel, NULL/zero-row)` returns the input unchanged (`expect_identical`), so the no-override CSV/reactive stays exactly as before; the column appears only when an override is in play. **(3) Test the REAL app path, not the convenient one.** The module applies overrides only in the `kinshipMatrix = NULL` fallback recompute (`:374-376` -- the path appServer actually uses); the pre-existing relationship tests pass `kinshipMatrix = reactive(...)`, which RETURNS the provided matrix and BYPASSES overrides -- so an override integration test MUST pass `kinshipMatrix = NULL` + `kinshipOverrides = reactive(frame)` or it silently never exercises the feature. **(4) Match pairs UNORDERED.** `convertRelationships` emits canonical pairs; flag with a sorted-pair key (`pmin`/`pmax`, sep="\r") so an override given as (B,A) still flags the (A,B) row; an override pair absent from the table flags nothing and does not error (D5-consistent). **(5) Fold the doc forward (S219 lesson).** The in-app guidance already explained the divergence; ADD one sentence that overridden pairs are now flagged, pinned by a NEW doc-test asserting a distinct word ("flagged") -- chosen because "override"/"overridden" were already present -- WITHOUT disturbing the existing asserted phrases ("override", "relationship label", "pedigree-derived"). **(6) `@noRd` internal helper** -> no NAMESPACE/man change for the helper itself (only the edited module's `.Rd` regenerates), testable directly under `load_all`. **(7) Pre-existing lints are not yours to fix in a tight slice** -- the 3 `line_length_linter(80)` hits in `modSummaryStats.R` (an `@param` line I preserved verbatim + two `getKinshipMatrix` comment lines) pre-date this session; my added lines are all <=80 (zero NEW lints, [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]); left the pre-existing ones alone (scope discipline). RED was clean-for-the-right-reason: 6 helper ERRORS (function absent) + the with-override flag FAILURE + the doc-sentence FAILURE; the no-override D10 guard + `convertRelationships`/`makeRelationsClasses` stayed green (proves display-layer-only). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 205 (documenting item-3 limits in-app) and Learning 206 (the gvaConvergence item-3 slice) -- this implements the SECOND of the three deferred item-3 follow-ups (R13), leaving D11 (targeted option C / both-unknown / shared-sib-pair). **Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.**

#### Learning 210 -- The careful-admin push->PR->CI->merge arc is now ROUTINE (3rd run: S218/S221/S224) -- follow Learning 207, with one refinement to the local-master sync. Owner picked "outward-facing admin" -> integrate the finished S223 R13 slice (PR #94, merge commit `33b0b09f`; #13 stayed CLOSED). **(1) Verify-then-merge held again exactly as 207 documents:** the full R-CMD-check matrix (macOS release; Windows release; Ubuntu devel/oldrel-1/release) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov checks all green; the lone `lint` red PROVEN pre-existing via `gh api repos/.../check-runs/<job>/annotations` -- the same noise set (12 findings: 10 in `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` -- implicit-assignment / line-length / commented-code / nonportable-path; 2 `.github` -- a Node.js-20 workflow-deprecation warning + the exit-code-31 summary), with NONE in this PR's changed files (`flagOverriddenRelationships.R`, `modSummaryStats.R`). The annotations API remains the reliable path (`gh run view --log` still returns empty for the failed lint job). Do not merge-on-`MERGEABLE`; surface the lone red with evidence first. **(2) Refinement to the 207 sync trap -- STASH the stub, do not discard it.** 207's trap: an uncommitted 1B stub makes `git checkout master` abort, so a chained `reset --hard` resets the WRONG (feature) branch; 207's fix was "clean the tree FIRST -> the stub is disposable, let it go." Cleaner and marker-preserving: `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` (stash ONLY the stub, leaving the standing untracked audit HTML in place) -> `git fetch` -> `git switch master` -> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -> `git stash pop`. The pop applies cleanly because after the fast-forward, master's `SESSION_NOTES.md` == the merged feature version == the stash's base -- so the trap is avoided AND the ghost-session marker survives the sync (no need to throw it away). Verify `HEAD == origin/master` (== the merge commit) and the merged code is present on master (e.g. `R/flagOverriddenRelationships.R` exists; the wiring grep hits) after. **(3) PR-body hygiene for a CLOSED issue unchanged:** "Relates to #13"; grep BOTH the commit messages and the rendered body for the GitHub closing-keyword pattern (`clos|fix|resolv` adjacent to a `#N`); confirm #13 CLOSED before AND after merge. **(4) Branch deletion stays a SEPARATE step (S221->S222 split):** merge does not delete the branch -- left `issue13-item3-r13-flag-overridden` for a future hygiene pass rather than bundling (1-and-done). **General rule:** when the arc is routine the value is in NOT regressing -- run the same verify-then-merge + annotations-API proof + closing-keyword-free body, and use the stash-the-stub sync to keep the marker; do not invent novelty where the documented arc already applies. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]; extends Learning 207 (the admin/merge arc) and integrates Learning 209 (the R13 slice it merges). **This was an admin/merge session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S224, issue #13 item-3 -- merged PR #94)

#### Learning 211 -- "Implement the follow-up" can resolve to "there is no implementable slice -- track it instead": recon a deferred-work thread BEFORE declaring RED, and when every remaining sub-item is blocked on a design/genetics decision, FILE A TRACKING ISSUE as the deliverable. Owner picked "Branch hygiene; Item-3 follow-up". The carried thread framed the remaining issue-#13 item-3 work as "D11 IMPLEMENTATION follow-up (1 of 3 left)", which reads like a ready TDD slice. **It was not.** A 5-agent read-only recon workflow (`wf_c1143706-b45`) + the plan doc showed the D11 CORE (blanket supersession, option A) was ALREADY shipped + 4-part-tested (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:164-171` guard; `test_reportGV.R:539-610`), and the three things called "follow-up" are deferred v1 LIMITATIONS, each blocked: **option C** (targeted suppression) needs a NEW override-schema column because the missing-parent side CANNOT be derived (the parent is unrecorded -> no pedigree edges; a kinship value is path-agnostic) -> plan-mode; **both-unknown->one-unknown promotion** and **shared-parent sib-pair coupling** are genetics-methodology calls (#9 defers both-unknown; the `for (i in which(oneU))` loop at `:162` is per-animal independent) -> `/grill-me`-style ratification. **The right move:** do NOT default to writing RED tests against unratified behavior (that would invent a spec -- FM #13/#19 in spirit); surface "no ready slice -> here are the options" via a pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` ([[observation-vs-decision]]), and when the owner picks tracking, file ONE well-structured issue (#95, `enhancement`) that captures each follow-up's blocker + blast-radius file:line inventory + disposition, so the expensive recon is not lost (the follow-ups had been prose-only in `plan.md:153,212,230` / CHANGELOG S214 with NO issue number -- [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] confirmed no existing issue and that #76 was a different, already-done deferral). Outward-facing hygiene: "Follow-up from #13 (D11)" with NO closing keyword; confirm #13 AND #9 CLOSED before and after. **Branch-hygiene half (Learning 208 reused):** the merged branch's tip `68db38c9` was the ORIGINAL feature tip (NOT moved to the merge commit -- S224 fast-forwarded master rather than `reset --hard`-ing the feature branch as S221 did to gvaconv), so it was a clean ancestor of master AND origin/master and safe `-d` deleted it -- no `-D` force needed (the "ahead 1" quirk did NOT bite this time; verify which case you are in, do not assume `-D`). **Workflow caveat:** 2 of the 5 recon agents hit the StructuredOutput retry cap (5) and returned nothing -- I backfilled by hand (a `gh issue list --state all` delta scan + a direct read of `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:140-191`), no lost coverage; a structured-output schema with several REQUIRED free-text fields raises the retry-cap risk, so keep recon schemas lean (fewer required fields / allow short answers) or expect to backfill. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 208 (branch hygiene) and the issue-#13 item-3 arc (Learning 205/206/209/210). **This was an admin/disposition session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S225, issue #13 item-3 -> filed #95)

#### Learning 212 -- For a SCHEMA-EXTENSION / ARCHITECTURE plan, run the adversarial verification BEFORE the plan is "done", and re-verify a recon subagent's DIRECTIONAL/SEMANTIC claims firsthand even when its line numbers are right. Owner picked "plan option C design for issue #95"; deliverable = `docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md` (the 4th-override-column + targeted-suppression design that #95/D11 deferred). **(1) The two-workflow shape worked well:** a 9-agent read-only INVENTORY workflow (`wf_4012d83a-551`) captured firsthand current `file:line` across the ~12 affected files (correcting the parent plan's drifted citations -- the `+sexMean/2` add is `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:190`, not the plan's stale `:175`), then a 3-agent VERIFICATION workflow (`wf_38c97263-a2e`: citation accuracy / design red-team / consumer completeness) -- mirroring the parent plan's S213 verification, which is THIS project's bar for a planning deliverable. The verification earned its keep: it caught a **HIGH-severity backward-compat contradiction** (the draft's C1 absent-column default said "keep the prior", but D10 requires absent-column => blanket-A => SUPPRESS -- opposite outcomes for any pre-existing override on a one-unknown animal; the fix is TWO opposite defaults -- whole-file-absent => blanket-A, per-row-blank => known-side) and a **factually WRONG directional claim**. **(2) The meta-lesson (the wrong claim's root cause):** my FIRST (inventory) workflow's agent asserted "gvaConvergence computes parentage BEFORE its correction call (`:169`)" -- the LINE NUMBER `:169` was correct but the BEFORE/AFTER direction was WRONG (`:169` is AFTER the correction at `:157-163`, same as reportGV's `:258`). I propagated it into the draft's load-bearing "asymmetry" framing.
