The verification's citation agent flagged it and I CONFIRMED firsthand. **Rule: a subagent's directional / ordering / "already-has-X" / depends-on claims are NOT verified just because its `file:line` checks out -- re-read them firsthand (code wins; FM #11/#20 applied to subagent output).** Bonus correction from the same finding: the side classification needs `isU(ped$sire/dam)` (the missing SIDE), NOT `classifyParentage` (which returns only the parentage CLASS) -- so there was no hoist needed at all. **(3) Red-teaming improves the DESIGN, not just catches errors:** the draft proposed "add a NULL-default `overrideMissingSideIds` param"; the red-team showed that creates a NULL-vs-`character(0L)` opposite-semantics trap on the main path. The better design that emerged -- "caller computes the suppress set, redefine `overriddenIds`'s contract, function stays dumb/unchanged" -- has no new param, no ambiguity, no signature change. Let the adversary push you to a simpler design. **(4) Completeness grep + a completeness agent found doc surfaces the file-scoped readers AND the issue's own inventory missed:** `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/genetic_value.html:50-61` (a parallel user-facing limitation copy) and `tests/testthat/test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R:21-78` (a doc-consistency test that asserts the exact "current limitation" phrasing and BREAKS when the text is rewritten). A schema/UI change's blast radius includes phrase-pinning doc-consistency tests, which symbol-greps do not surface. **(5) The plan is a DRAFT; do NOT ratify the owner's calls in it (FM #19).** Option C's two prerequisites -- C1 (the irreversible 4th-column schema encoding) and C2 (the residual genetics-modeling rule: full-drop-on-any-missing-side vs partial-residual) -- are owner/geneticist gates settled via `/grill-me` against a firsthand numeric check, exactly as D11 was; the plan RECOMMENDS for each but marks the §7 checklist UNRATIFIED. Be honest in the recommendation: rule (i) is a JUDGMENT not a slam-dunk (it trades the case-(b) over-credit for a smaller SCOPED missing-side over-credit). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 211 (which filed #95) and the issue-13 plan's D11/S213-verification precedent. **This was a planning/architecture session -- TDD code-phases N/A (the plan is the deliverable; implementation is a SEPARATE later session, gated on Phase-0 ratification).** (S226, issue #95 option C)

#### Learning 213 -- A Phase-0 genetics-ratification grill: do the firsthand numeric check FIRST and PRESERVE its evidence IN the plan, lead with the upstream domain judgment, and reframe a "targeted-suppression" rule as a strict SUBSET of the blanket it refines. Owner picked "ratify C1 + C2 via `/grill-me`" (Phase 0 for issue #95 option C, the gate S226's plan left UNRATIFIED). Deliverable = the ratified §7 checklist + a new §8 evidence record in `docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md`; **0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (every recommendation ratified as written). **(1) Numeric check BEFORE the grill (the D11/S214 bar), grounded firsthand on real `qcPed`:** replicated the `reportGV.R:118-180` mean-kinship path (`getGVPopulation` -> `kinship` -> `filterKinMatrix` -> `meanKinship` -> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`) and measured the `+ sexMean/2` prior = **median ~1.34 SD** of the mean-kinship spread, a single half-sib override's raw effect = **~10%** of that prior (prior/raw ~9.9x), rank swings 49-121 of 280. **These independently reproduced S214's recorded "~1 SD" / "8.4x" numbers (`issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md:151-152`) -- cross-validating a firsthand computation against the prior session's recorded numbers IS the adversarial verification for a load-bearing number** ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]: I did the C2 DELTA, not a re-run). **(2) PRESERVE the evidence in the plan, not scratchpad.** S214's D11 evidence lived only in a scratchpad workflow output and was LOST; I wrote the numbers + a re-runnable reproduction recipe into plan §8 so the next session need not re-derive them. A genetics decision's evidence belongs in the durable artifact, not the ephemeral workflow log. **(3) The decisive REFRAMING the evidence surfaced (verified logically, not numerically):** rule (i) suppresses the prior iff an override informs the focal's MISSING side; blanket-A suppresses for ANY override -> rule (i) suppresses a **strict subset** of blanket-A's suppressions, so it is a **Pareto improvement over today** (it newly fixes case b -- known-side overrides -- and leaves case a EXACTLY as today). The large ~1.2 SD case-(a) residual is therefore **pre-existing, not introduced by option C** -- which is WHY shipping the genetically-incomplete rule (i) for v1 is safe and rule (ii) is a deferrable follow-up. **Separate "is the narrow rule worth shipping" (yes -- it strictly improves) from "is it genetically complete" (no -- needs the harder model); a numeric residual that looks alarming in isolation can be a no-op delta vs the status quo.** **(4) Grill order = walk the dependency tree, lead with the upstream domain judgment that gates the whole feature.** Q1 was NOT a technical call but the geneticist's judgment "are real overrides mostly known-side, missing-side, or a mix?" -- because option C/rule (i) only delivers value if known-side (case b) overrides actually occur; "mix" confirmed the feature is worth building before grilling HOW. Then C2 (genetics) -> C1 (schema, depends on C2) -> C1 sub-decisions -> technical confirmations. **(5) Reserve `AskUserQuestion` for the genuinely-open calls; confirm invariant-forced sub-decisions in prose.** C1.1 (two opposite absent-column defaults) and C1.3 (optional column) are FORCED by the D10 byte-identical invariant -- I stated them as derived-and-settled (owner may object) rather than asking, and grilled only the genuinely-open C1.2 (both/diffuse encoding) and the genetics gates C1/C2. Batched the architecture/test confirmations (C3/C4/C5/C6 + slice order + D10) into ONE consolidated confirm-or-amend question since none is a genetics call and S226's verification already hardened them. Grounded the one test-design rec (C4) firsthand first (`test_reportGV.R:545-555`: X is sire-missing, so pinning the `(X,Y)=0.25` fixture as case (a) keeps the blanket-A regression additive). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 212 (which drafted the plan) / 211 (which filed #95) and the D11/S214 ratification precedent (Learning 200). **This was a planning / Phase-0 decision session -- TDD code-phases N/A (the deliverable is the ratified decisions; implementation is a SEPARATE later session).** (S227, issue #95 option C Phase 0)

#### Learning 214 -- A targeted-suppression slice where the genetically load-bearing function is UNCHANGED: the behavior change lives in the CALLER + a new classifier, so the discriminating RED is on the case the refinement FLIPS, not the function itself. Owner picked "Slice 1 of option C"; deliverable = the script-core of issue #95 option C per the RATIFIED plan (RED `5448a1a8` -> GREEN `4cd3e509`; REFACTOR offered, owner chose skip; all 3 gates via `AskUserQuestion`; **0 corrections / 0 overrides**). **(1) The load-bearing function did not change -- only its contract did.** `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`'s `:164` guard (`if (focalId %in% overriddenIds) next`) ALREADY suppresses whatever id-set it is passed, so option C needed NO code change there -- only a docstring redefinition of `overriddenIds` from "ids carrying an override" to "the set to suppress" (C3), and the CALLER (`reportGV`) computing a narrower set. Consequence for TDD: the function's own "tests" (strict subset suppresses only those; full set = blanket-A) PASS at HEAD -- they are stays-green CHARACTERIZATION, not RED. **The genuine RED lived in the new pieces: the new classifier (`classifyOverrideMissingSide`, 7 cases error -- absent), the validator's new structural check (a `missingSideFor` not naming id1/id2 -> stop, fails at HEAD), and the caller's flipped case.** When refining a blanket rule to a targeted one, look for where the NEW code is (caller + classifier), not the unchanged core. **(2) Design the discriminating RED on the case the refinement FLIPS ([discriminating-RED]).** Option C changes behavior ONLY for case b (known-side override now KEEPS the prior); case a (missing-side) is unchanged from blanket-A. So the case-b `reportGV` test (blank `missingSideFor` -> `expect_gt(imk[[X]], original[[X]])`) FAILS at HEAD (blanket-A drops the prior; equal) -- 1/280 animals differ, surgical -- while the case-a test PASSES at HEAD (still suppressed) and is only a regression guard. Testing only case a would have been a non-discriminating RED that passes on the buggy build. **(3) The independent oracle for an option-C refinement is the EXISTING blanket-A oracle called with the refined suppress set** -- `i13_correctOptionC <- i13_correctBlanketA(ped, original, probands, <suppress set>)`, where the suppress set is computed in the test itself (not via the package helper) for true independence ([identical-proof] spirit). No new oracle algorithm needed. **(4) Reconstruct RED/GREEN/close-out as separate commits even when implemented in one working-tree pass:** stage selectively -- tests -> RED commit, R/+man -> GREEN commit, NEWS/CHANGELOG/learnings/handoff -> close-out commit -- to keep the strict-TDD history (matches S223). **(5) Net-zero lint MUST use the in-place stash, never the /tmp-copy method ([stale-namespace]/[lint-net-zero]).** Linting `git show HEAD:R/x.R > /tmp/x.R` loses the project `.lintr` -> shows default object_name/indentation lints (HEAD-in-/tmp reportGV = 31 lints vs 4 in-place) -- NOT comparable. Correct: `git stash push -- <tracked R files>` -> lint in-place (so `.lintr` applies) -> `stash pop`; proven net-zero (HEAD 6/4/2 vs current 6/4/1; the new file 0; my docstring rewrite even REMOVED one 81-char line). Match the lint-clean idiom: `toString(x)` not `paste(x, collapse=", ")`, and reword code-like comments (`+sexMean/2`) that trip `commented_code_linter`. **(6) Phase-3E N/A for a script-core slice (stated, not skipped):** the change is to a pure analytical function's (`reportGV`) logic with NO Shiny wiring/dispatch/startup change (the in-app upload is Slice 2), so FM #24 (build-passes-ship-it, about runtime wiring) does not apply -- the `reportGV` behavior is fully exercised by the test suite. Carried as applied: [discriminating-RED], [regression-read], [lint-net-zero], [stale-namespace], [right-sized-orchestration]; [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 213/212 (the ratified plan) and Learning 209 (the R13 display slice -- the prior issue-13 override slice). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.** (S228, issue #95 option C Slice 1)

#### Learning 215 -- The careful-admin push->PR->CI->merge arc bites when you treat it as ROUTINE and skip the documented verification steps: two pre-warned regressions (a closing-keyword NEGATION that auto-closed the tracking issue, and a `reset --hard` that discarded the owner's external edit). 4th run of the arc (S218/S221/S224/S229); owner picked "1" then "merge it once devel passes". The merge itself was correct (verify-then-merge held: full matrix green, lone `lint` red surfaced as the documented non-blocking noise, merged with a merge commit only after green). But I read SESSION_NOTES and NOT Learning 207/210 before acting, and regressed on both procedures those learnings exist to prevent. **(1) A NEGATED closing keyword STILL fires -- "does not close #95" auto-closed #95 on merge.** GitHub's closing-keyword parser does pattern-matching, not sentiment: the substring `close #95` triggers regardless of a preceding "not". S228's gotcha said "NO closing keyword" and Learning 207(3)/210(3) prescribe the mitigation I skipped: BEFORE merge, grep BOTH the rendered PR body AND the commit messages for `clos(e|es|ed)|fix(es|ed)?|resolv(e|es|ed)` adjacent to a `#N`. **Run that grep every time, and keep ANY such word away from a `#N` even in prose/negations** -- if you must mention closing, write "stays open" / "remains open", never "does not close #N". **It fired a THIRD time the same session -- in my close-out COMMIT MESSAGE** ("a negated closing keyword auto-closed #95"): the substring `closed #95` re-closed the issue when `28f99872` was pushed to `master`. The most insidious surface is the close-out commit/PR that DESCRIBES the incident -- the natural phrasing ("auto-closed #N", "fixes #N") is itself the trigger. Two safe habits: (a) when writing a `#N` immediately after a keyword, put a noun between them -- "closed the issue #95" / "auto-closed issue #95" does NOT match (the keyword must be immediately followed by the ref); (b) FILE CONTENTS never trigger (only commit messages + PR/issue bodies parsed on push/merge to the default branch), so describe the incident freely in CHANGELOG/notes, but sanitize the COMMIT MESSAGE. The routine final-state verification (`gh issue view 95`) is what caught all three -- keep that check at the very end of every merge/push session. Recovery when it fires: `gh issue reopen <n>`; fix the body via `gh api -X PATCH repos/O/R/pulls/<n> -F body=@file` (the `gh pr edit` path 401s on the same Projects-classic deprecation as `gh issue view <n>` -- use REST / `--json`); add a clarifying comment; re-confirm OPEN. **(2) `git reset --hard` with an unexplained modified tracked file present destroys whatever it is -- and during a long CI wait that file is probably the OWNER's edit.** `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` was clean at my Phase-0 `git status` and showed `M` only after the ~20-min CI watch (the owner had edited it in RStudio, mtime 16:04). I chained `git reset --hard origin/master` to sync local master and blew it away (owner confirmed it was minor lint cleanup, to be redone -- harmless only by grace). This is SAFEGUARDS pre-flight ("uncommitted changes from elsewhere -- do not touch; report and ask") + FM #22, and Learning 210(2) already gives the safe sync: `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` (the 1B stub only) -> `git fetch` -> `git switch master` -> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -> `git stash pop`; the stash pop applies cleanly post-FF and the stub survives. **Rule: re-check `git status` IMMEDIATELY before any `reset --hard`; if a tracked file is modified that you did not change, STOP, surface it, and stash/ask -- never reset over it.** (Recoverable-source note: RStudio keeps editor-buffer backups in `.Rproj.user/<id>/sources/session-*/<HASH>-contents` with the path in the sibling `<HASH>` JSON -- a real recovery path if the buffer is gone, though here the owner waved it off.) **(3) Meta-lesson -- "routine" is exactly where regressions hide.** Learning 210 says "when the arc is routine the value is in NOT regressing." The handoff stated the rules but left the PROCEDURES in PROJECT_LEARNINGS; I treated the arc as known and skipped consulting them ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]] in the breach). On any arc with a Learning number, RE-READ that learning before executing, even (especially) the Nth time. **(4) Branch hygiene stays a SEPARATE step (Learning 208/210(4)) -- did NOT bundle deletion; left `issue95-optionC-slice1` (local+remote) for a future pass (1-and-done).** Carried as applied (mostly in the breach): [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]; extends Learning 207/210 (the admin/merge arc whose documented steps I should have followed) and 208 (branch hygiene as the deferred step); integrates Learning 214 (the S228 slice it merged). **This was an admin/merge session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S229, issue #95 option C Slice 1 -- merged PR #96)

#### Learning 216 -- When a function's behavior change is NOT observable through its public output, make the strict-TDD discriminating RED real by extracting a shared helper and testing THAT (which also makes the lockstep structural). Owner picked "a then b" = (a) branch hygiene + (b) option C Slice 2 (bring `gvaConvergence` into lockstep with `reportGV`'s option-C behavior + document `missingSideFor` in-app). Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session; RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR-skipped; all 3 gates + a pre-RED approach gate + a mid-GREEN review hold, all via `AskUserQuestion`; **0 corrections / 1 owner course-correction**. **(1) The testability wall (the load-bearing insight):** `gvaConvergence` returns NO per-animal mean kinship; its only mean-kinship-sensitive output is the convergence curve, which is gene-drop-uniqueness-driven and (the existing `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R:14-23` documents this) mean-kinship-order-INVISIBLE on qcPed. So the option-C suppression (a one-animal ~1 SD mean-kinship delta) cannot be robustly seen in gvaConvergence's output -- a behavioral RED through it would be fragile or impossible. **Resolution: extract the changed logic into a shared `@noRd` helper (`prepareKinshipOverrides()` = validate -> warn-drop -> apply -> compute the option-C suppress set), unit-test the HELPER (clean discriminating RED: 8 cases error at HEAD because the function is undefined), and route BOTH `reportGV` and `gvaConvergence` through it.** This simultaneously (a) gives a real failing-test-first, (b) makes the report/diagnostic lockstep STRUCTURAL (one source of truth -- they cannot drift), which IS the slice's goal, and (c) realizes the plan's C5 "do not widen the divergence" without doing the deferred full 3-way de-dup (the module fallback paths keep `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix`). The behavior-preserving `reportGV` re-route is guarded by its extensive Slice-1 suite (106 assertions stayed green). **When the public output can't show the change, the seam IS the test surface -- and a shared seam doubles as the anti-drift mechanism.** **(2) Surface the test-design fork as a pre-RED `AskUserQuestion` (it is an author's approach decision per the phase-gate format).** The three real options -- shared helper (chosen) / mirror + expose a new return field / mirror + an empirically-tuned gu-bearing fixture -- differ in scope, API surface, and fragility; the owner's call, not mine. **(3) Recognize when the "delivery" half is already wired.** Slice 2's app-upload path was ALREADY green at HEAD: `readKinshipOverrides()` uses `check.names = FALSE` and returns all columns, so a `missingSideFor` column rides through -> `checkKinshipOverrides` (validates it, Slice 1) -> `reportGV` (already option-C-aware). So the `testServer(modGeneticValueServer)` test is a regression GUARD (passes at HEAD), NOT a discriminating RED -- the only true behavioral gap was the second consumer (`gvaConvergence`). Check what the existing wiring already delivers before assuming a "delivery slice" needs delivery code. **(4) `spelling::update_wordlist()` is DESTRUCTIVE to a curated WORDLIST -- it reconciles the file to EXACTLY the currently-flagged set, ADDING new words but REMOVING every curated entry no longer flagged.** Here it added `focals`+`supersession` but silently PRUNED 31 curated function-name entries (rbindlist, runGeneKeepr, ...); the owner interrupted. Recovery: `git checkout inst/WORDLIST` then surgical inserts in sorted position (`focals` after `focalAnimals`; `supersession` after `subfields`) -> diff is exactly `+2`, 0 removals. **A "convenience" tool that syncs a curated file to current state will delete curation as a side effect -- prefer a surgical hand-edit; if you must use it, diff the result before committing** (cf. [[check-status-before-destructive-git]] -- same family: verify before letting a tool blow away curated content). Note `supersession` was pre-existing in NEWS at HEAD and not in WORDLIST -- so HEAD was not actually spelling-clean for it; adding both reached spell=0 (the project bar). **(5) Net-zero lint when lifting a block verbatim:** a faithful extraction carries the source block's lints into the new file (`any(!x)`, `paste(x, collapse=", ")`); clean them in the new home (`!all(x)`, `toString(x)`) so the new file is lint-0 AND the source loses them = net REDUCTION. Reword comments that trip `commented_code_linter` -- the trigger is a line that parses as R (a trailing `reportGV()`, or `#NN` creating a nested comment); plain prose without `()` / `#NN` is safe. **(6) Phase-3E runtime smoke for a Shiny-doc + analytical change, achievable headlessly:** (a) FUNCTIONAL smoke -- call the actual functions the app calls (`reportGV`/`gvaConvergence`) on real `qcPed` and confirm the behavior delta (known-side override KEEPS the `+sexMean/2`: meanKin 0.009198 > missing-side SUPPRESS 0.006027); (b) APP-BOOT smoke -- launch `runModularApp(port=, launch.browser=FALSE)` in the background, `curl` the root, and grep the SERVED HTML for the change marker (`missingSideFor` present -> the helptext + `includeHTML(genetic_value.html)` render live), HTTP 200, then kill the pid. This is a genuine FM #24 runtime check without a browser. Carried as applied: [discriminating-RED], [regression-read], [lint-net-zero], [right-sized-orchestration]; [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 214 (Slice 1 -- the caller-computes-the-suppress-set design this helper now centralizes) and 212/213 (the plan + its ratification). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.** (S230, issue #95 option C Slice 2)

#### Learning 217 -- For a PR body / issue comment you author fresh, make the broad `clos|fix|resolv` grep return EMPTY (strip every such token, even harmless hyphenated or non-adjacent ones) rather than reasoning about whether a keyword is adjacent to a `#N`. Owner picked the outward-facing admin step (push branch `issue95-optionC-slice2` + open PR #97 "Relates to #95"), scoped to **push + open PR** (merge deferred, owner-gated). **Admin/PR session -- TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections.** This refines Learning 215's grep into a bright-line rule for content you control. **(1) Two surfaces, two rules.** GitHub's closing-keyword parser fires only on `keyword #N` in (a) commit messages and (b) PR/issue BODIES that reach the default branch -- FILE contents never trigger. For PRE-EXISTING commit messages you cannot rewrite, apply the narrow test: is any `clos(e|es|ed)|fix(es|ed)?|resolv(e|es|ed)` IMMEDIATELY followed by a `#N`? Here the 3 branch commits carried ZERO `#N` refs (they write "(issue 95)" / "Relates to issue 95" -- the bare word, never "#95"), so the lone "close-out" substring in the S230 commit subject was provably safe. For the PR body you AUTHOR, do not make that adjacency judgment at all -- write it with zero `clos|fix|resolv` tokens so a plain `grep -iE 'clos|fix|resolv'` returns empty; I rephrased a harmless hyphenated "close-out" (quoting a commit subject) to "docs/handoff" purely to clear the broad grep. S229's THREE firings all had a keyword TOKEN in an authored surface (a negated body, then a close-out commit message); a bright-line "no token in any authored body" rule stops all three where "negation is safe" reasoning did not. **(2) Verify against the RENDERED artifact, not the local draft.** After `gh pr create --body-file`, re-fetch with `gh pr view <n> --json body --jq .body` and grep THAT; then confirm the issue with `gh api repos/O/R/issues/<n> --jq .state` (the `gh issue view <n>` / `gh pr edit` paths 401 on the Projects-classic deprecation -- use REST / `--json`). Opening a PR does NOT close an issue (only a merge with a body keyword does), but the cheap end-check (`#95` still OPEN) is exactly what caught S229's firings -- run it immediately after PR creation and again after any merge. **(3) Commit messages use the bare-word form too.** Wrote the S231 close-out commit as "(issue 95)" / "PR 97" (no `#`) with zero clos/fix/resolv, grepped before pushing -- so the close-out commit that lands on master when PR #97 merges cannot fire (S229's third firing was exactly a close-out commit message). **(4) 1-and-done held:** delivered push + open PR per the owner's explicit scope; did NOT merge (the more consequential, owner-gated step) -- surfaced CI-watch + merge as the next session's pick. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]] (re-checked `git status` before the push -- clean but for the 1B stub + standing untracked HTML), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] (pushed the close-out to origin/branch so local==origin/branch; it reaches master at merge); extends Learning 215 (the negation trap) and 207/210 (the careful-admin arc). **This was an admin/PR session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S231, issue #95 option C Slice 2 -- PR #97 opened)

#### Learning 218 -- The careful-admin push->PR->CI->merge arc runs CLEAN when you read the arc's learnings (207/210/215/217) BEFORE acting -- the 5th run (S218/S221/S224/S229, now S232) and the first clean one since S229's triple closing-keyword firing. Owner picked "go with next suggested" -> merge the finished S230 Slice 2 (PR #97 -> merge commit `e893c90d`; #95 stayed OPEN). The whole value of this session was NOT regressing (Learning 210), and the way to not regress was the [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] step S229 skipped: I read 207/210/215/217 first, then executed each documented control. **(1) Verify-then-merge held -- did NOT merge on `MERGEABLE`.** Waited for the full R-CMD-check matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu release + oldrel-1 + devel) + pkgdown + test-coverage + both codecov to go green; the devel leg finished last (22:58:53 success) and only then did I merge. The lone `lint` red was proven pre-existing whole-package noise via `gh api repos/.../check-runs/<JOB>/annotations` (12 findings: 10 in `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R`, 2 `.github` workflow warnings; NONE in this PR's changed files) -- the documented "surface the lone red with evidence" step (207(1)/210(1)). **(2) Closing-keyword proof can be AIRTIGHT, not just "probably safe".** `git log origin/master..branch --format='%H%n%s%n%b' | grep -nE '#[0-9]+'` returned EMPTY -- NO branch commit carried any `#N` ref at all (they write the bare-word "(issue 95)"), so the lone "close-out" token in the S230 subject is provably inert (a closing keyword fires only when IMMEDIATELY followed by `#N`). The rendered PR body grepped empty for `clos|fix|resolv`. And I authored the merge commit message keyword-free and ref-bare: `gh pr merge 97 --merge --subject "Merge pull request #97 from rmsharp/issue95-optionC-slice2" --body "option C Slice 2: ... prepareKinshipOverrides helper (issue 95)"` -- so the merge commit that lands on `master` cannot fire (S229's 3rd firing was exactly a close-out commit message). Post-merge `gh api repos/O/R/issues/95 --jq .state` -> open (the end-check that caught all of S229's firings). **Bright-line, three surfaces:** branch commits (narrow adjacency test, they're frozen) + PR body (authored empty) + the merge commit message you author at merge time (`--subject`/`--body`, keyword-free, ref-bare). **(3) Local sync = stash-the-stub (Learning 210(2)), preceded by a `git status` re-check (Learning 215).** Confirmed only the 1B stub + standing untracked HTML were dirty (no unexplained modified tracked file), then `git stash push -- SESSION_NOTES.md` -> `git fetch` -> `git switch master` -> `git merge --ff-only origin/master` -> `git stash pop` (clean pop -- post-FF master's SESSION_NOTES == the stash base). Verified `master == origin/master == e893c90d` and the Slice-2 code is present. The marker survived; no `reset --hard` near a possibly-owner-edited file. **(4) NEW env nugget: `status` is a READ-ONLY special variable in zsh** (the session shell, initialized from the user profile). A `run_in_background` poll script that did `status=$(gh api ... --jq .status)` died immediately with `(eval):3: read-only variable: status` (exit 1). Use neutral names (`st`, `cc`, `concl`) in ANY Bash snippet here; other zsh specials to avoid assigning include `path`, `cdpath`, `pipestatus`. The intended mechanism was sound: a `run_in_background` loop that polls a CI check-run and `break`s on `status == completed` exits and re-invokes the agent -- the right way to wait on an EXTERNAL CI leg the harness cannot otherwise notify on (here the devel leg finished before the relaunch was even needed, so a foreground re-check sufficed). **(5) 1-and-done held:** branch deletion stays the deferred SEPARATE hygiene step (Learning 208/210(4)) -- left `issue95-optionC-slice2` (local + remote) for the next session; did not bundle. **General rule:** on any arc with a Learning number, RE-READ that learning before executing the Nth time -- routine is where regressions hide, and the documented controls (annotations-API proof, both-surfaces + merge-commit closing-keyword discipline with the post-merge end-check, stash-the-stub sync) are exactly what turn a triple-firing arc into a clean one. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 207/210/215/217 (the careful-admin arc) and integrates Learning 216 (the S230 slice it merged). **This was an admin/merge session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S232, issue #95 option C Slice 2 -- merged PR #97)

#### Learning 219 -- The deferred branch-hygiene step (Learning 208/210(4)) is a clean ~5-command admin task when you verify-merged-firsthand against BOTH refs first; the `-D`-forcing "ahead 1" quirk does NOT bite a branch whose tip is a clean ancestor of master. Owner picked the deferred hygiene step from S232's SUGGESTED NEXT: delete the merged branch `issue95-optionC-slice2` (local + remote). **Admin/branch-hygiene session -- TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** Third run of the S229->S230(a) deferred-hygiene pattern (slice1 deleted in S230(a); slice2 now). **(1) Verify-merged-firsthand = four firsthand checks, not one.** Before any delete: (a) `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master` AND (b) `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> origin/master` -- BOTH must pass (a branch merged locally but not on origin, or vice-versa, is a partial state); (c) local branch tip == `origin/<branch>` tip (identical, not diverged); (d) `git log --oneline master..<branch>` empty (no commits on the branch absent from master). Here the tip `421f80f2` passed all four against `master == origin/master == cd20817e`. **(2) Safe `git branch -d` succeeded -- the documented `-D` quirk did NOT apply.** Learning 207/208 warn that a merged branch can show "ahead 1" (e.g. squash/rebase merges) so `git branch -d` refuses and you must confirm with `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master` then `-D`. That quirk bites only when the branch tip is NOT a literal ancestor of master; here the PR #97 merge was a true merge commit so the tip IS an ancestor, and the safe `-d` (which refuses unless merged) exited 0. **Rule: always try `-d` first; only reach for the merge-base-check-then-`-D` path if `-d` actually refuses.** **(3) The remote delete is outward-facing -> confirm the EXACT command via `AskUserQuestion` before running it, then VERIFY removal.** Did the local `-d` first (reversible -- the commit lives on master, recreatable via `git branch <name> <tip>`), then asked the owner to confirm `git push origin --delete issue95-optionC-slice2` before running it. After the push: `git ls-remote origin <branch>` returns EMPTY (= gone on GitHub) and `git push origin --delete` also prunes the local `origin/<branch>` remote-tracking ref (confirmed absent from `git branch -r`). GitHub did NOT auto-delete the branch on PR merge, which is why this step was needed at all. **(4) 1-and-done / scope held:** deleted ONLY the named merged branch; left the other stale branches (`dev`, `module`, `rlabkey-version-floor`) untouched (out of scope, owner did not ask). This closes the option C Slice 2 arc end-to-end: S230 RED/GREEN -> S231 push+PR -> S232 merge -> S233 hygiene. Carried as applied: [[check-status-before-destructive-git]] (re-checked `git status`/verify-merged before any delete), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (read the arc's learnings 208/210/215/218 first), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] (close-out pushed to origin/master so local==origin); extends Learning 208/210(4) (branch hygiene as the deferred separate step) and 207 (the `-D` quirk) and completes the arc of 216/217/218. **This was an admin/branch-hygiene session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S233, issue #95 option C Slice 2 -- merged branch deleted)

#### Learning 220 -- A firsthand numeric check is necessary but NOT sufficient to ground a genetics/statistics decision: you must also verify WHERE each quantity enters the computation (the data-flow), or you can ratify a directionally-wrong model with confident-looking numbers. Owner picked live-thread #2 -> a `/grill-me` on #95 follow-up **rule (ii)** (partial-residual / scaled add-back), bundled with C1.2. **Decision/design session -- TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** The grill **REVERSED ~5 sessions of shipped, owner-ratified work** (D11 + option C Slices 1-2): the ratified disposition is to **revert the override-triggered suppression of the `+sexMean/2` prior to keep-all and REMOVE the option-C machinery** (`missingSideFor` column, `classifyOverrideMissingSide()`, the suppress path). **(1) The reframing and how it was found.** Grounded the grill with a 4-agent read-only workflow (`wf_1a1f64ba-976`: numeric on `qcPed` + code/math + C1.2 scope + conservation-genetics methodology), then **verified its load-bearing claim firsthand BEFORE presenting it** -- the claim overturns ratified D11, so trusting the subagents would have been reckless: overrides are written into `kmat` (`prepareKinshipOverrides.R:49`) BEFORE `meanKinship` (`reportGV.R:148`) and BEFORE the correction (`reportGV.R:157`). So a missing-side override pins ONE of N(=280) relationships and that value is ALREADY in `original`; the `+sexMean/2` prior estimates the AGGREGATE of all N missing-side relationships, so observing one should shrink it by only ~1/N (~0.0048 SD), not drop the whole ~1.33 SD. Full-drop (rule i) -- and its parent D11 blanket supersession -- over-correct by ~N (≈280×), swinging an affected animal's GV rank a median 86/280 the WRONG way (override animals look more genetically valuable). **(2) Why S227's "firsthand-numeric-grounded `/grill-me`" still missed it.** S227 measured the wrong quantity -- the override's direct ΔMK / prior (≈10%, legitimately "new information") -- and never checked that the override value is ALREADY in `original` before the correction. A magnitude is not a data-flow proof. **Rule: when a correction TERM is a scalar aggregate over N relationships and an override pins one of them already-in-the-matrix, the term should shrink by ~1/N; ground the decision on where each quantity ENTERS the computation, not just its size.** **(3) A `/grill-me` can legitimately conclude "revert recently-shipped, ratified work"** -- a valid, high-value outcome, not a failure; the remedy for a directionally-wrong genetics model is the reversal, documented. **(4) Methodology corroborated:** no conservation-genetics literature supports a partial-residual estimator; PMx (Hauser 2024) replaces one cell and recomputes, never lumping the prior into a scalar -- which issue-#13 override-the-cell ALREADY does for observed pairs, so PMx is documented as considered-and-not-needed (D5). The `+sexMean/2` prior is itself a package-only addition, not in Vinson & Raboin 2015. **(5) Deliverable discipline (1-and-done):** wrote the decision record + Phase-3 revert plan WITH the mandatory evidence-based grep inventory, the behavior invariant (keep-all), per-phase completion criteria, and dragons into `issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md` §9 (+ banner + §8E "resolved" note) and a superseded-in-part pointer at D11 in `issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md`; **did NOT implement the revert** (separate strict-TDD session). Closing-keyword discipline held on the issue comment (authored so the broad `clos|fix|resolv` grep is EMPTY; posted via `gh api`; `#95` verified OPEN after). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] (presented the reframing + a recommendation per decision, let the owner ratify each of D1-D5 rather than deciding for them), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8 before re-running the numeric check -- did the delta, not a full re-run), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 216 (the option-C slice this reverses) and supersedes in part the S214 D11 / S227 option-C ratification. **This was a `/grill-me` DECISION/DESIGN session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S234, issue #95 rule (ii) -> revert option C to keep-all)

#### Learning 221 -- When IMPLEMENTING a revert of shipped machinery, ground it with a read-only workflow that re-verifies the plan's grep inventory firsthand against the CURRENT tree; a revert plan's line-ranges reliably under-specify the mechanical SHAPE (braced-block guards vs the one-liner shown, trailing-comma cleanups when you remove a last argument, `@noRd` docstrings with NO generated `.Rd` safety net, and params that fall unused after a deletion). Owner picked "S234's suggested next" = the strict-TDD revert of option C / D11 to keep-all (plan §9C). **Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session; RED `034de14b` -> GREEN `cae27e94` -> REFACTOR none; all gates via `AskUserQuestion`; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Verify the inventory firsthand even when the plan ships one.** S234's §9C carried a firsthand grep inventory; I still re-ran it via a 19-agent read-only workflow (`wf_9298eddb-3a4`) against current master. It confirmed completeness (empty `planRefsNotFound`; per-file `missingSideFor` counts matched exactly) AND surfaced what the plan's line-ranges glossed: the suppress guard is a braced 8-line block at `:168-175` (not the one-liner the plan TEXT showed; `:166-167` are KEEP code), removing a last argument needs a trailing-comma fix on the preceding line, the `prepareKinshipOverrides` `@noRd` docstring has no generated `.Rd` to catch staleness (hand-edit required), and deleting the `classifyOverrideMissingSide` call left `ped`/`candidateIds` unused. **A plan's grep inventory is necessary but a revert edits the SHAPE of code (commas, braces, docstrings) the inventory does not capture -- re-grounding firsthand is what catches it.** **(2) Surface the author's-call forks a revert leaves open as a pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`.** Two the plan deliberately left ("rejects/ignores", plus the unflagged unused params): drop-vs-keep the now-unused `ped`/`candidateIds` (chose DROP -- `@noRd` internal, completes the revert, costs +2 call-site edits) and ignore-vs-reject a stray `missingSideFor` column (chose IGNORE -- the true revert ADDS no code; an old 4-column file still validates). Both change the RED tests, so settle them BEFORE declaring RED ([[observation-vs-decision]]). **(3) The discriminating RED per kind of removal.** A behavior FLIP (suppress->keep-all): flip the oracle (`expect_equal(imk[[X]], original[[X]])` -> `expect_gt(...)`) and rename the test's independent-model helper to the new semantics (`i13_correctBlanketA` -> `i13_correctKeepAll`, dropping its suppress branch). A removed PARAM: a signature guard `expect_error(f(..., overriddenIds=...), "unused argument")` (fails now -- the param exists; passes after removal). A removed RETURN field: `expect_null(res$suppressIds)` + `expect_named(res, "kmat")`. A removed INPUT-validation path: `expect_error(check(frameWithStrayCol), NA)` (errors now via the domain check; passes after under ignore). RED confirmed each fails against the unreverted code with NO collateral parse error (reportGV 2, prepareKinshipOverrides 7, docs 4, correct* 1, check* 1). **(4) Commit to EXACT doc phrases in RED and honor them in GREEN.** `test_kinshipOverrideDocs` greps the rendered helpText/HTML; I pinned "missing one parent"/"kept"/"underestimate" in RED and wrote exactly those into the `modGeneticValue.R` helpText + `genetic_value.html` (and, for consistency, NEWS) in GREEN -- the doc-consistency test (OC-R13, in reverse) couples code+docs so they move together. **(5) Lockstep via the shared helper, not parallel edits.** `reportGV`/`gvaConvergence` both route through `prepareKinshipOverrides`; I reverted the helper once (2-arg, `list(kmat)`) and made the identical 2-line edit at each call site -- did NOT parallelize the GREEN source edits (interdependent: the helper signature change must match both call sites; read-before-edit each). **(6) Phase-3E for a Shiny-doc + analytical revert, headless:** (a) app-boot -- `shiny::shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` inherits `"shiny.appobj"`; (b) live-UI -- `as.character(appUI())` greps for the new keep-all copy AND the ABSENCE of `missingSideFor`; (c) behavior -- `reportGV` with an override on a one-unknown animal keeps its `+sexMean/2` (0.0092 > 0.0060) and applies the cell-write. NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd via `rmarkdown::render` (github_document) -- edit the `.Rmd`, render, then inspect the diff for pandoc reflow artifacts (an `&` + a trailing parenthetical wrapped oddly; reworded to "and" + a plain sentence to fix it). **(7) 1-and-done:** the deliverable is the implementation, verified locally on branch `issue95-revert-keepall`; push + PR "Relates to #95" is the outward-facing NEXT session (the S231->S233 arc). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (fixed the 3 prose lines I authored at 81 chars; left the files' pervasive long `test_that` titles as the established in-file style), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]] (close-out committed on the branch; reaches master at merge); implements Learning 220 (the S234 decision) and reverses Learning 214/216 (the option-C slices). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.** (S235, issue #95 -- revert option C to keep-all)

#### Learning 222 -- When two deferred follow-ups share one structural blocker, a single `/grill-me` settles both, and "don't build" is a definitive, high-value disposition -- but ground a "don't build" decision the same way you ground a "build" one (verify WHERE each quantity enters / whether the premise is even observable), not just that the effect is small. Owner picked "#95 follow-ups 2 / 3"; bundled both via a pre-grill scope `AskUserQuestion` (owner's call). **Decision/design session -- TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** Output = ratified D1-D4 in `issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md` §10 (+ a follow-up-resolution pointer at D11 in `issue13-kinship-overrides-plan.md`). Dispositions: **FU2 (both-unknown->one-unknown promotion) = won't-build (not derivable); FU3 (shared-unknown-parent sib coupling) = accept (keep the independent per-focal loop) + document in the maintainer docstring.** **(1) The path-agnostic blocker is the SHARED through-line -- which is why bundling worked.** Both follow-ups die on the same structural fact: a kinship override carries no side information and an unrecorded parent has no id. FU2 can't tell which of two missing parents an override informs; FU3 can't tell whether two one-unknown animals share the *same* unknown parent. One grounding pass on the path-agnostic schema + the pipeline ordering serves both -- when deferred follow-ups share a blocker, grill them together. **(2) Verify the PREMISE is observable, not just that the effect is small (Learning 220 applied to a non-build).** The decisive FU3 finding came from running the detection firsthand on `qcPed`: the 2 candidate sib-pairs (`KY0D3C`/`HN5YTI`; `JPKPJC`/`PUS6EL`) each share a known *dam* and both miss the *sire* -- so whether they share the *same* unknown sire is **unknowable** (two offspring of one dam can have different unknown sires). "Negligible" was the grounding agents' framing; "the premise is undetectable" is the stronger, load-bearing reason -- and it only surfaced by checking *what the data can identify*, not the magnitude. For FU2, likewise: confirmed firsthand that the override is already in the matrix before the prior (so promotion would over-credit) and that no schema column carries side (so promotion isn't derivable) -- a "don't build" grounded on data-flow + observability, not on "it's rare." **(3) A grounding workflow's SYNTHESIS step can degenerate to placeholder output -- a schema guarantees SHAPE, not CONTENT; keep the load-bearing data in the return value.** `wf_964f8ea1-4bb`: 6 read-only grounding agents (Explore/haiku) returned real structured findings, but the final synthesis agent (opus, 392s, 11 tool calls over a ~500k-token JSON input) satisfied the schema with junk (`summary:"test"`, `question:"q"`). The script returned only `synthesis`, so the real grounding was nearly lost -- recovered it from the agent transcripts (`subagents/workflows/<wf>/agent-*.jsonl` -> the last `StructuredOutput` tool_use `input`). **Lessons: (a) return the raw grounding array too, not just the synthesis; (b) a single synthesis agent over a huge JSON is a failure point -- synthesize yourself when you'll run the grill anyway; (c) transcript recovery is the reliable fallback when a workflow's return value is degraded.** **(4) Decision/implementation split held (FM #18).** D3 scopes the FU3 note to a one-file `@noRd` maintainer docstring as a SEPARATE small session; wrote the recommended exact docstring text + insertion point (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:102`, before `@param`) + verify steps (no `.Rd` to regenerate; hand-add any wordlist term, not `update_wordlist`) into §10C so the implementer has it. Did NOT implement. D4: #95 closes when that note lands (deliberate, keyword-safe), not this session. **(5) Closing-keyword discipline on the #95 comment** (authored so the broad `clos|fix|resolv` grep is EMPTY; posted via `gh api`; `#95` verified OPEN after). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (a recommendation per decision; owner ratified each of D1-D4), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (re-verified the load-bearing claims firsthand against current master rather than trusting the haiku grounding agents), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8/§9 + the prior-analysis grounding before re-deriving -- did the delta), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (recorded in §10C), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; extends Learning 220 (the over-correction reframing, here applied to FU2/FU3) and closes the #95 follow-up arc end-to-end (FU1/rule ii/C1.2 at S234; FU2/FU3 at S236). **This was a `/grill-me` DECISION/DESIGN session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S236, issue #95 -- follow-ups 2 & 3 dispositioned)

#### Learning 223 -- The implementation tail of a deliberate decision/implementation split (FM #18) is a clean ~mechanical session when the deciding session pre-writes the exact text + insertion point + verify steps. S236 split the FU3 disposition into a separate small session and wrote the recommended docstring text, the insertion point (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:102`, before `@param`), and the verify list into §10C; this session (S237) executed it in one pass with 0 corrections. **(1) A `@noRd` roxygen docstring is structurally un-pinnable AND un-spell-checked -- so TDD is genuinely N/A, not a dodge.** roxygen comments are stripped at build and are not part of the function object, so no `testthat` test can reach them (confirmed firsthand: `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` pins only the live-app `ui` and the `genetic_value.html` `txt`, never this file -- [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], not the handoff's word); and `spell_check_package` checks generated `man/*.Rd` + DESCRIPTION + vignettes, none of which a `@noRd` function produces, so the docstring text is never spell-checked either. The ONLY real build risk a docstring-only R-source edit carries is the R CMD check non-ASCII NOTE. **(2) Keep docstring prose ASCII and match the file's existing roxygen markup.** §10C's "recommended text" used markdown backticks + em-dashes; normalized to `\code{}` (this file's house style, cf. its `\code{sexMean}` spans), ASCII ` -- ` (protects the 0/0/0 non-ASCII check), and US "modeled" (cf. `:85`) -- all three are house-style normalizations of explicitly-recommended (not verbatim-mandated) text, surfaced in the report. Verified ASCII-clean + all lines <=80 + the 3 relevant test files green before committing. **(3) Deliberate, keyword-safe issue close as the session's LAST act.** Added the docstring, verified, committed, THEN set issue 95 to CLOSED via `gh api` with a keyword-safe comment (no `clos|fix|resolv` adjacent to any `#N`), and verified #9/#13 stay closed + 95 closed at the very end (Learning 215/217/218). This closes the #95 arc end-to-end: S234 (reframe + revert decision) -> S235 (revert impl, PR #98) -> S236 (FU2/FU3 grill) -> S237 (FU3 docstring + close). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (<=80, lint-clean changed file), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (no `update_wordlist`), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a documentation/admin session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S237, issue #95 -- FU3 docstring landed, #95 closed)

#### Learning 224 -- A whole-package `lintr` pass on this package is gated by one distinction: ARTIFACT vs REAL. `lint_package()` run locally reports `object_usage_linter` "no visible global function definition for <pkg-fn>" for intra-package calls because the package namespace is not resolved; those vanish under `pkgload::load_all()` (object_usage -> 0) and NEVER appear in CI, which installs the package via `local::.` -- so the ground truth for "what is red in CI" is the actual `lint.yaml` run log, not the raw local count. Owner picked the carried "systematic whole-package lint pass" (clear the standing `lint`-CI red); remaining open issues deferred until after CRAN resubmission. **REFACTOR-class cleanup -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A (no behavior change; the existing suite + the `lintr` gate are the safety net); 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Separate artifact from real TWO ways before touching anything.** `load_all()` then `lint_package()` dropped 74 -> 43 (all 31 object_usage gone); the `lint.yaml` CI log listed exactly those 43 and zero object_usage -- two independent confirmations ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]] + [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]). Left all object_usage untouched; fixed only the 43. **(2) The 43 are all behavior-preserving** -- line_length wraps, `paste(.,collapse=", ")`->`toString`, `any(is.na)`->`anyNA`, implicit `0`/`2`->`0L`/`2L`, `any(!x)`->`!all(x)` (equivalent for non-NA logical), `gsub("\r")`->`fixed=TRUE` (CR matches identically), `nonportable_path`->`file.path()` -- verified by targeted tests (calcFG/FGSE/apply/check) + full suite (3173/0/0, only baseline-noise warnings) + `devtools::check` 0/0/0. **(3) Reflowing EXPORTED-function roxygen regenerates `man/*.Rd`.** Six exported-fn files re-wrapped -> `devtools::document()` rewrote 6 `.Rd` (whitespace-only, identical words; rendered help unchanged) -- they MUST be committed in sync. `@noRd`/internal-fn reflows do NOT regenerate any `.Rd` (Learning 223). Anticipate the man-page commit when a reflow touches an exported fn. **(4) Splitting a long `sprintf` format string via `paste0` can SHORTEN a label literal enough to trip a linter the full string did not** -- `paste0("  bootstrap  boot/delta = %.4f ...", ...)` made `nonportable_path` fire on "boot/delta" (a label, not a path); the full original string never tripped it. Fix: bind the whole format string to a variable (`fmtB <- "..."`) instead of splitting. A script not run by any test/check needs an explicit `parse()` check after editing (no test catches a split-string typo). **(5) `data-raw/` dev-runner idioms whose linter "fix" would be wrong get justified `# nolint` ranges, owner-confirmed** -- `source()`-ing test helpers (correct for a runner) and `system.time(x <- ...)` (times AND captures; splitting double-runs an ~11-min study) each get a `# nolint start: <linter>.` / `# nolint end` block (an inline `# nolint` overflows the very 80-char limit it would satisfy). The mechanical lints in the same file (paths -> `file.path()`, `2L`, wraps, commented-code prose) are still fixed. **(6) Outward-facing arc (owner chose full via `AskUserQuestion`):** branch -> <=5-file batched commits (SAFEGUARDS blast-radius) with a re-lint after each -> PR #99 -> all 8 CI checks green incl. the `lint` job (4m7s) + the full R-CMD-check matrix -> merge `8deeecf` -> branch deleted (verify-merged-firsthand, Learning 219). PR body keyword-safe; no `#N` so nothing auto-closes. **(7) Edit one file's multiple lints bottom-up** ([[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], owner tip this session) so earlier edits do not renumber later targets. Carried as applied: [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class lint-cleanup session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S238, whole-package lint pass -> standing lint-CI red cleared, PR #99 / merge `8deeecf`)

#### Learning 225 -- Removing development-process references (GitHub issue numbers, "Slice N" vertical-slice jargon) from documentation is gated by one distinction: PUBLISHED vs INTERNAL. A roxygen `#'` block renders to a man page (and the pkgdown reference / `?fn`) ONLY when the function is exported or `@`-documented; a `@noRd` block is stripped at build -- maintainer-only, the SAME category as a plain `#` source comment. So the in-scope set for "clean the user-facing docs" is non-@noRd roxygen + vignettes/articles + NEWS, NOT the @noRd docstrings, NOT the source comments (extends S238 gotcha #2 / Learning 224(3): exported-roxygen reflow regenerates `man/*.Rd`; @noRd does not). Owner observed user-facing docs leaked "issue #82, Slice 1"-style tags and asked "can you defend their inclusion?"; treated as a QUESTION ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23) -- firsthand inventory + honest defend/concede, then `AskUserQuestion` ratified "Published surfaces only". **REFACTOR-class docs-only; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Classify @noRd vs rendered BEFORE editing.** `grep @noRd` per file: of 23 files with tags, 7 were @noRd (left untouched) and 16 rendered (cleaned + `devtools::document()` regen, then `man/` re-verified for zero issue/slice + zero drift). An independent audit confirmed zero over-reach (the 7 @noRd + 36 source comments + NEWS issue-numbers all unchanged) and zero leaks (man/, vignettes, README, _pkgdown.yml, DESCRIPTION, inst ui_guidance all clean). **Over-reach is the failure mode** -- "finishing the job" on the @noRd files would have deleted maintainer breadcrumbs the owner chose to keep. **(2) What is DEFENSIBLE vs not.** DEFENSIBLE: issue NUMBERS in NEWS (standard R changelog convention -- tidyverse/CRAN norm) and in source comments (rationale breadcrumbs). NOT DEFENSIBLE: any "Slice N" a user sees (internal tracer-bullet unit), bare "(issue #NN)" parentheticals in rendered help a reader cannot follow, and an article structured around a dev plan ("the issue #82 plan, Slice 2 of three"). **(3) Reword for MEANING, not blind-delete.** Keep the policy NAME, drop the ticket ("decline-to-credit policy, issue #76" -> "decline-to-credit policy"); when a deleted tag was an antecedent, reword it too (`data.R` "the remaining part of that issue" would dangle -> "a separate planned enhancement"); keep the methodology concept, drop the ticket framing (fg-se kept "compute -> validate -> surface" + "validate before expose", dropped "plan for issue #82 (Slice 2 of three)"); name a mechanism instead of a ticket (gvaConvergence "issue #76 de-inflation" -> "`gu = 0` de-inflation"). **(4) A markdown footnote LABEL is spell-checked.** `spell_check_package` tokenizes `[^label]`; a digit-bearing label (`issue28`) slips through but a pure-letter rename (`transloc`) gets flagged -- use a real dictionary word (`location`). NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd via `rmarkdown::render` (github_document) -- edit the .Rmd, render, inspect pandoc reflow (only the changed paragraphs re-wrap). **(5) Verify a meaning-preserving reword ADVERSARIALLY, not just "are the tags gone."** A 4-agent audit (`wf_b3fff352-1be`: semantic-roxygen / semantic-articles / scope-completeness / adversarial-correctness, each schema'd) confirmed no leaks/over-reach/broken \code{}/\link{}/footnotes + all 16 `.Rd` parse via `tools::parse_Rd()` with zero re-document drift, AND caught one self-grep-invisible miss: the fg-se "ratified in the plan" -> "set for this study" softened the *pre-registration* nuance (bands fixed in advance -- a calibration-credibility claim); restored to "specified in advance for this validation study". A grep proves the tags are gone; only a semantic read proves the meaning survived. **(6) 1-and-done:** deliverable verified on branch `docs-strip-process-tags` (5 commits); push/PR is owner-gated. Surfaced (not done): `inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config:55` "(issue #73 Part 2)" in a user-copyable config template (source-comment KEEP pattern). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class docs-only session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S239, remove dev-process tags from published docs -> branch `docs-strip-process-tags`, owner-gated landing)

#### Learning 226 -- A documented `R CMD check --as-cran` gate result certifies a SNAPSHOT of the tree, not the package in perpetuity; it goes stale as the tree evolves, and a plan that records "tree unchanged since the gate" must be RE-VERIFIED, not trusted. The CRAN submission plan (`docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md` / phase5-runbook) recorded Phases 1-4 DONE with a local `--as-cran` = `0/0/2` gate (S134, 2026-06-18) and asserted "the code/data/metadata tree is unchanged since the S134 gate." That was true when written but had since gone stale: `git log --since="2026-06-18" --name-only -- 'R/*.R' 'man/*.Rd' NEWS* 'data/*' 'tests/**' 'vignettes/*'` showed **~74 commits** touching most of `R/`, all `man/`, NEWS, `data/*.RData`, ~50 test files, and the vignettes (the kinship-overrides feature #13/#95, the FG-SE validation work, the S238 lint pass, the S239 doc-tag strip). So the "just run Phase 5b" answer would have been wrong; the owner picked "run the local re-gate now" via `AskUserQuestion`. **VERIFICATION session -- TDD code-phases N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Re-gate BEFORE the owner runs expensive outward checks.** win-builder x3 + R-hub are ~2 owner-hours of async round-trips; discovering a regression there (or after submission) is far costlier than a ~4-min local re-gate. Build + check the TARBALL (not the source dir) from the package root so renv activates; run a TRUE `--as-cran` (no `_R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_` override). Result held: `Status: 2 NOTEs` = 0/0/2, both the documented false-positives (CRAN incoming-feasibility archived/new-submission; local HTML-tidy+V8 manual note absent on CRAN) -- read `00check.log` to CONFIRM the 2 notes' identity, do not trust the bare count. **(2) Timing is THIS package's archival cause and it grows with the suite.** Compared to the S134 baseline: tests 43s->**74s** (suite 1853->3173 assertions), vignette rebuild 16s->**24s**, examples ~stable 19->22s. All still `OK` with no single example near 5s, but on ~2-3x-slower CRAN/win-builder hardware tests could be ~150-220s -- so re-read the `--timings` table every re-gate, and Phase 5b on slow hardware is the only step that truly RETIRES the timing risk. Do not add slow un-`skip_on_cran`'d tests / heavy examples without watching that table. **(3) `--as-cran` SKIPS the module/E2E tests** via `skip_on_cran` -- which is correct (CRAN won't run them either); do NOT set `NOT_CRAN=true` for the gate (that is for local module/E2E runs only). The routine build-equivalent prior sessions cite (`devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)`=0/0/0) is FASTER but does NOT build vignettes or run `--as-cran` -- for a CRAN claim use the full tarball `--as-cran --timings`. **(4) `README.md` SHIPS in the tarball** (`.Rbuildignore` ignores only `README.Rmd` + `README.*.png`), so its badges render on the CRAN landing page and are part of CRAN prep. **(5) Badge-audit method (reusable):** fetch each badge SVG and read its `aria-label`/`<title>` (cranlogs / r-pkg.org / GitHub embed the rendered value THERE, not always in `<text>`), and test the LINK target's resolution separately -- a HTTP 200 on the image does NOT mean the badge is correct. Found 3 real defects in `README.Rmd:12-22`: (a) `:18` CRAN grand-total-downloads points to the wrong package (`grand-total/kableExtra`, renders "8.7M") instead of `grand-total/nprcgenekeepr` (~"13K") -- copy-paste; (b) `:19` malformed markdown, a stray leading `[` (`[[![R-CMD-check]`) renders a literal `[`; (c) `:20` bogus DOI -- `doi-10.32614` / `doi.org/10.32614` is only the CRAN *prefix* and resolves to a generic "DOI Name Values" page, whereas the real `10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr` resolves now (->CRAN page) and is the canonical fix. The defects were SURFACED (owner question, treated as a QUESTION per [[observation-vs-decision]]) and kept as a SEPARATE deliverable -- not bundled into the re-gate (one-deliverable / SAFEGUARDS mode-switch discipline). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a VERIFICATION session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S240, re-establish the local CRAN `--as-cran` gate on current master -> GREEN 0/0/2)

#### Learning 227 -- Executing an audit's surfaced-but-not-fixed findings (S240's 3 README badge defects) is a clean ~mechanical session, but two non-obvious mechanics gate it: a shields.io static-badge fix that puts a `/` in the MESSAGE field must URL-encode it as `%2F` (the LINK target uses the plain value), and because `README.md` SHIPS in the tarball (Learning 226(4)) a badge-URL change RE-STALES the S240 `--as-cran` gate and must re-clear it. Owner picked "fix the 3 README badge defects"; the DOI target was the one genuine fork, posed as an `AskUserQuestion` ([[observation-vs-decision]]) among CRAN-package-DOI / cited-paper-DOI / drop-the-badge -- owner chose the canonical CRAN package DOI. **Docs-only REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) The fixes (source `README.Rmd`, NEVER the generated `README.md`):** `:18` `grand-total/kableExtra` -> `grand-total/nprcgenekeepr`; `:19` drop the stray leading `[`; `:20` `doi-10.32614` / `doi.org/10.32614` -> badge `doi-10.32614%2FCRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr` + link `doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr`. **(2) shields.io `%2F` is load-bearing.** A raw `/` in `https://img.shields.io/badge/<label>-<message>-<color>` breaks the path; encode it `%2F` and the badge renders the decoded DOI -- verified firsthand by fetching the SVG and reading its `aria-label`/`<title>` ("doi: 10.32614/CRAN.package.nprcgenekeepr"), the FIX-side of Learning 226(5)'s audit method. The LINK uses the plain DOI and resolved HTTP 200 -> the CRAN package page (`curl -L` follows doi.org's redirect). The grand-total badge now reads "CRAN downloads 13K" (nprcgenekeepr), not kableExtra's "8.7M". **(3) Re-clear the gate, because README.md ships.** Per Learning 226(1) a documented gate certifies a SNAPSHOT; a README badge change IS a package change, so re-ran `R CMD build .` + `R CMD check --as-cran --timings <tarball>` from the package root -> `Status: 2 NOTEs` = 0/0/2 (the two documented false-positives), and `grep -niE 'URL|doi|cranlogs|shields|kableExtra' 00check.log` was EMPTY -- the only check-relevant signal for a URL edit is the URL check, and it flagged nothing. (A doi.org DOI link is exempt from CRAN's redirect-NOTE; the 3 new URLs each return 200, so no new NOTE was structurally possible -- the full gate is the authoritative confirmation of that reasoning, and honors S240 gotcha #1 that any package change re-stales the gate.) **(4) `build_readme()` re-stamps the version-date line.** `r nprcgenekeepr::getVersion()` renders "Version 2.0.0 (<build date>)", so regenerating today moved `(2026-06-19)` -> `(2026-06-29)` -- an EXPECTED side-effect, not a defect; the YAML front-matter `date: "2025-07-25"` is the AUTHOR's field and was left untouched (FM #22, do not edit unrequested). The `README.md` diff is exactly the 3 badges + that one date stamp -- confirm the diff is ONLY the intended change. Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]; executes the defects SURFACED by Learning 226(5). **This was a docs-only REFACTOR-class session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S241, fix the 3 README badge defects -> README.Rmd/.md, gate re-confirmed GREEN 0/0/2)

#### Learning 228 -- A curated owner-facing RUNBOOK certifies a SNAPSHOT of the environment + repo state the same way a `--as-cran` gate certifies a code snapshot (Learning 226), and it goes stale the same way -- before an owner executes an outward, expensive, async runbook, RE-VERIFY its load-bearing premises firsthand rather than trusting its own "verified (Session NNN)" lines. The CRAN Phase 5b runbook (`docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md`, S135/S136) rested on three premises that had ALL flipped by S242: (a) `devtools`/`rhub`/`gitcreds` "absent" -> now installed (`requireNamespace` all TRUE; the owner also installed command-line `hunspell` mid-session); (b) the 2.0.0 code lives on an `add-methodology` working branch -> that branch is merged + deleted and the code is on `master`; (c) `origin/master` "still at 1.1.0.9000, must open a new PR to master" -> `master==origin/master`@2.0.0, in sync (`git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...master` = `0 0`, empty `git diff --name-only origin/master..master`, `origin/HEAD`->master). Owner picked the docs-only refresh via `AskUserQuestion` after I SURFACED (not assumed) both the stale state AND that the core Phase 5b steps (win-builder x3, R-hub, `submit_cran()`) are owner-run/outward ([[observation-vs-decision]]). **Docs-only REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Whether a doc edit re-stales the `--as-cran` gate is decided by `.Rbuildignore`.** `cran-comments.md` (`^cran-comments\.md$`) and `docs/` (`^docs$`) are build-ignored, so they do NOT ship in the tarball and editing them leaves the S241 gate (0/0/2) intact -- NO re-gate needed (the cheap firsthand check is `grep .Rbuildignore`). Contrast Learning 227: `README.md` SHIPS, so a badge edit DID re-stale it. **(2) CRAN's "possibly misspelled words in DESCRIPTION" comes from GNU `aspell` on the Title+Description fields -- NOT from `inst/WORDLIST` and NOT from `spelling::spell_check_package` (hunspell+WORDLIST).** To reproduce locally you need a command-line speller for `utils::aspell()` to shell to; macOS Homebrew `hunspell` installs WITHOUT dictionaries (the `en_US` "Can't open affix or dictionary files" error) -- point it at the R `hunspell` package's bundled dict: `Sys.setenv(DICPATH=system.file("dict",package="hunspell"))`. The faithful incoming-feasibility call is `utils::aspell(<DESCRIPTION>, filter="dcf", program="hunspell")`; `filter="dcf"` scans only Title/Description and EXCLUDES the `<...>` reference URL, so URL fragments (pmc/ncbi/nih/nlm/https) are NOT flagged -- matching CRAN. This is faster + more faithful than wrapping a full `R CMD check` (which would also need `DICPATH` exported and isn't what CRAN runs anyway). **(3) hunspell's dictionary != GNU aspell's, so the local list is a PROXY; the authoritative list is win-builder's.** The proxy flagged exactly `EHR, kinships, LabKey, Macaca, mulatta, Raboin` (all correctly-spelled names/acronyms/Latin-binomial/valid-plural; the owner confirmed `Raboin` + `EHR` are "always detected"). Reconciled `cran-comments.md` NOTE 1 to that set: dropped `"studbooks"` (in the Description but NOT flagged by the proxy -> asserting it was flagged is unverified) and added the prominent flagged species name `"Macaca mulatta"`; kept the "for example" hedge and added a §4.2 note telling the owner to re-confirm the final list against the win-builder log. **(4) When the environment cannot run the literal task as phrased, SURFACE it and do the faithful reproduction -- do not silently substitute or give up.** "Reconcile against the local `--as-cran` log" was not literally executable (no command-line speller -> the local check emits no word list); I surfaced that, the owner installed `hunspell`, and the `utils::aspell` dcf call (the same machinery the check uses) produced the faithful list. **(5) An independent adversarial audit earns its cost on owner-facing prep:** a fresh agent (blind to my reasoning) re-reproduced the spell set, the branch sync, the tooling presence, and `rhub.yaml` existence against the live repo = PASS on all, no misdirecting defects; it caught 2 minor polish items (an imprecise "~74 commits" narrative figure -> "dozens"; a Quick-sequence "no push" vs §3 "push anyway" tension -> clarified §3's push as optional housekeeping). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a docs-only REFACTOR-class session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S242, refresh the CRAN Phase 5b runbook + reconcile cran-comments NOTE 1 -> both `.Rbuildignore`d, gate untouched)

#### Learning 229 -- A copyright/license notice written as roxygen (`#'`) instead of a plain comment (`##`) LEAKS into the rendered man page, and the leak is STRUCTURAL not cosmetic: at the FIRST `#'` line of an exported block it becomes the `\title{}`, and elsewhere the copyright + blank line hijack `\description{}` and demote the real first paragraph into a spurious `\details{}` block. (CRAN-facing -- man pages render on the package page + PDF manual.) Oriented for CRAN Phase 5b; the owner observed "copyright notices have not been updated" -- treated as a QUESTION ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23): firsthand audit, then `AskUserQuestion` ratified "Full normalization". **REFACTOR-class docs/metadata; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Two header patterns, one leaks.** Pattern A (187 files, CORRECT): copyright/part-of are `##` lines SANDWICHED inside the roxygen block (between `#'` lines) -- roxygen2 silently ignores non-`#'` comment lines, so they never reach the `.Rd` (proof: no Pattern-A file appeared among the 22 leaking `man/*.Rd`). Pattern B (25 files, the LEAK): copyright/part-of are `#'` -- mid-block they land in the body / spurious `\details{}`; at line 1 they become the `\title{}` (`getSpeciesGestation`, `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, both exported). **(2) The fix is a uniform line-level transform, not a structural move.** `^#' Copyright(c)` -> `## Copyright(c)` AND `^#' This file is part of nprcgenekeepr` -> `##` (convert BOTH -- converting only the copyright line would promote "This file is part of" to the new title), plus `Copyright(c) [0-9][0-9-]* R. Mark Sharp` -> `2017-2026` (the one regex also fixes the malformed bare `2026`). This turns Pattern B into Pattern A in place -- no relocation needed. DRY-RUN the perl (no `-i`) on each representative shape FIRST: line-1-leak (title), mid-block-leak, Pattern-A-year-only, malformed-year. For the 16 header-less files, PREPEND `## Copyright / ## This file is part of / <blank>` (all 16 opened with a `#'` title, so the `##`+blank sits safely above the block). **(3) Verify a 400+-file sweep by COUNTS + DIFF-CLEANLINESS, never eyeballing.** After applying: `git grep "^#' Copyright"` and `"^#' This file is part of"` must be 0; the year distribution must collapse to a single `2017-2026` bucket; `git diff | grep '^[+-]' | grep -vE 'Copyright|This file is part of|^[+-][[:space:]]*$'` must be EMPTY (proves zero code lines changed). Then `devtools::document()` and confirm `man/` changed ONLY ~22 files, `git grep Copyright -- man/` is 0, the 2 titles are real, NAMESPACE is unchanged, and `R CMD check` says "code/documentation mismatches ... OK". **The decisive sync proof: re-run `document()` a second time -> `git status --short man/` is EMPTY (zero drift = every committed `.Rd` is a faithful regen, not a hand-edit)** -- this is exactly what an independent blind adversarial agent used to confirm PASS A-I. **(4) `R/`+`man/` ship, so re-gate even for comment-only.** Both are in the tarball -> the `--as-cran` gate re-stales (Learning 226/227); re-ran build + `--as-cran --timings` = `Status: 2 NOTEs` = 0/0/2 (the two documented false-positives unchanged) -- a comment/title fix introduces no new note, but the gate is the authoritative confirmation. **(5) "Full normalization" = whole-package; commit the CRAN-relevant core separately.** The ratified option counted `R/` (197 stale years / 25 leaks / 16 bare) but full normalization also covers `tests/` (195) + `data-raw/` (3); committed `R/`+`man/` (`0c77f4d1`) separately from `tests/`+`data-raw/` (`d7556d5e`) so the CRAN-relevant core is cleanly revertable, and FLAG the full 413-source-file scope to the owner (the option text was R/-scoped -- extending to tests/ is correct for "full" but should be surfaced). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class docs/metadata session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S243, normalize copyright headers + fix roxygen man-page leak -> branch `normalize-copyright-headers`, owner-gated landing)

#### Learning 230 -- A doc-style AUDIT grounded in "authoritative" mechanical grep counts can ship a WRONG count into its own analysis -- so a separate ADVERSARIAL verification stage that RE-DERIVES the load-bearing numbers firsthand is what keeps the bad ground truth out of the deliverable, not the grounding itself. Owner picked issue #102 (roxygen2 documentation harmonization -- analysis + recommendation across all 226 `R/` files); the issue scopes it as analysis ONLY (implementation a separate follow-on). **AUDIT-class deliverable -- TDD code-phases N/A (the output is an assessment doc, no production logic); 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** Workstream `AUDIT_WORKSTREAM.md`; deliverable `docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md` (`.Rbuildignore`d `^docs$` -> does NOT ship -> gate untouched, no re-gate -- contrast Learning 229 where `R/`+`man/` ship). **(1) My OWN mechanical count was the defect, and the adversarial stage caught it.** A file-level loop claimed "166/167 exported functions have `@examples`; only `withinIntegerRange.R` lacks one" and I fed that into the workflow's ground-truth COUNTS; the independent verify agent re-swept firsthand and found `withinIntegerRange.R` HAS examples and **22** exported files lack them (true coverage **145/167**). The tell was arithmetic the verifier flagged: "146 `@examples` files vs 167 `@export` cannot leave only 1 missing." SANITY-CHECK the counts you pass as ground truth (cross-foot the totals) -- a wrong input silently became a fabricated "strength" in synthesis until an independent re-derivation killed it. **(2) Deep-read agents assert false PREMISES; pass authoritative ground truth in and re-confirm rendered defects against the actual `.Rd`.** Several inventory agents claimed "roxygen markdown is not enabled package-wide" -- FALSE (`DESCRIPTION:81` `Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)`); because I'd passed the correct global into the synthesis prompt the recommendation stayed right, but the false claim leaked into raw highlights. For the headline rendered-doc defects I read the generated `.Rd` myself (`man/calcGU.Rd` shows ONLY `\{Genome Uniqueness Functions\}\{\}` because `calcGU.R:42`'s explicit `@description` overrides ~34 lines of implicit text; `man/convertSexCodes.Rd:26-30` shows literal `\item\{F\} \{...\}` braces) -- the agent's word is a lead, the rendered output is the proof. **(3) The substantive finding: roxygen consistency here is a two-era CONVERGENCE, not a green-field design.** Legacy files embed the copyright between title and body and order `@return` before `@param`; the newest files (`getSpeciesGestation.R`, `applyKinshipOverrides.R`, `hasInvalidIdChar.R`) already follow the cleaner convention -- so the recommendation is "standardize the legacy majority onto the emerging modern style" across 8 dimensions (markup direction = raw-Rd since it dominates 1270-to-48; `@param`-before-`@return`; copyright ABOVE the block; `@noRd`-only internals; `@importFrom` over `@import` -- owner-confirmed CRAN preference). SEPARATE genuine rendered-doc DEFECTS (8 classes: D1 `calcGU` description override, D2 duplicate `_PACKAGE`, D3 escaped-brace lists, D4 `@param` for a nonexistent `candidates` arg, D5 wrong-function `@return` text, D6 title typos, D7 dead imports, D8 bare-blank-line block breaks) from style preferences and flag them "fix regardless of harmonization." **(4) Calibrate severity HONESTLY against rendered impact, not volume.** The copyright-PLACEMENT inconsistency is the single highest-VOLUME issue but `##` lines render fine, so it is moderate ("high-volume / low-impact"), NOT high -- and the genuinely rendered-impacting bare-blank-line breaks were broken out as their own defect (the verifier's calibration note). Avoid both severity inflation (volume != severity) and deflation (the buried 22-file examples gap). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was an AUDIT-class analysis session -- TDD code-phases N/A.** (S244, roxygen2 documentation harmonization analysis -> `docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md`, implementation deferred to a follow-on)

#### Learning 231 -- When IMPLEMENTING an audit's "fix all instances of defect class X", the audit's cited LOCATION list is a SAMPLE, not an inventory -- grep the WHOLE tree for the defect PATTERN and fix every hit, or you ship a half-fix that leaves the same defect visible in the very pages you "fixed". Owner picked issue #103 Stage 1 (fix rendered-doc defects D1-D8 from the S244 audit §4). **REFACTOR-class doc-correctness -- no behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the D2 approach).
