Branch `issue103-stage1-defects`, landing owner-gated (S243/PR-101 precedent for shipping `R/`+`man/` edits). **(1) The D3 enumeration undercounted.** The audit listed 6 files for the escaped-brace `\item\{X\} \{Y\}` pseudo-list defect; `grep -rn '\\item *\\{\|\\item\\{' R/` (run AFTER fixing the cited ones, to find the remainder) surfaced 3 more sites the audit never named -- `calcGU.R`'s AlleleTable list (the file was cited for D1 only), the **wholly-uncited** `getRequiredCols.R`, and **four MORE `\itemize` lists inside `qcStudbook.R`** beyond the single column-list the audit pointed at (sex/status/ancestry codes + a column-rename list). 8 files carried D3, not 6. The completion proof is a full-tree rescan returning ZERO `\item\{`/`\{--` in BOTH `R/` and `man/` -- do that rescan, do not trust the citation count. (The audit even hedged "...also ..." and "re-confirm line numbers" -- read enumerations as illustrative.) **(2) A duplicate `_PACKAGE` resolves cleanly by DELETING the legacy doc file, and roxygen auto-heals the alias.** roxygen MERGES two `_PACKAGE` blocks into one `<pkg>-package.Rd` (its header said `Please edit ... R/nprcgenekeeper.R, R/nprcgenekeepr-package.R`), so the legacy `R/nprcgenekeeper.R` was injecting the WRONG `\title{}` ("Genetic Management Functions"), a "Primary Data Structure" preamble, and escaped-brace `\seealso` garbage, plus spawning a thin `man/nprcgenekeepr.Rd` alias page. Deleting it (the modern usethis `nprcgenekeepr-package.R` is the keeper) regenerated the package page with the CRAN-standard auto title (`nprcgenekeepr: <DESCRIPTION Title>`), the clean DESCRIPTION-derived description, clean "Useful links" `\seealso`, AND **both** `\alias{nprcgenekeepr}` + `\alias{nprcgenekeepr-package}` -- the `\alias{nprcgenekeepr}` migrated to the package page automatically once the legacy `@name nprcgenekeepr` block was gone, so `?nprcgenekeepr` still resolves. VERIFY by reading the regenerated `.Rd` aliases + the gate (deleting a doc file is a judgment call with content-loss -> gate it: I posed delete-vs-preserve-overview via `AskUserQuestion`). **(3) A title-typo fix is a TWO-WAY WORDLIST edit.** `inst/WORDLIST` held the OLD typos (`obfucateDate`, `obfucateId`, `portential`) -- a prior session had added them so the typo'd `\title{}` lines passed `spell_check_package`. Fixing the typo ORPHANS those entries AND newly-flags the corrected compound token: `obfuscateDate`/`obfuscateId` (camelCase function names) are not dictionary words, so the corrected titles now trip the speller. Hand-edit WORDLIST in BOTH directions -- replace the two function-name typos with their corrected spelling (they sorted right before the already-present `obfuscatePed`), DELETE `portential` (its fix "potential" is a real word needing no entry) -- never `update_wordlist` ([[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]]). Re-run `spell_check_package` to confirm CLEAN. **(4) D7 "dead import" removal is provably zero-impact when the host function is commented out.** `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`'s `@importFrom gplots`/`@import RColorBrewer`/`@importFrom grDevices` produced NOTHING in NAMESPACE today -- the block is `@noRd` and the function below it is fully `##`-commented, so there is NO code object for roxygen to attach the import directives to, and roxygen silently drops the orphan block. Confirmed BEFORE removing: `grep gplots\|RColorBrewer` in DESCRIPTION = absent, in NAMESPACE = absent. So removal cannot create a "Namespace in Imports field not imported from" NOTE nor drop a live import. (The general rule: an `@import*` tag only reaches NAMESPACE if its block attaches to a real exported/`@noRd` OBJECT.) **(5) For doc-only REFACTOR, the regenerated `.Rd` is the proof, the gate is the certifier.** I read each fixed `man/*.Rd` firsthand (calcGU description restored; convertSexCodes/qcStudbook/getSiteInfo/getRequiredCols lists real; makeCEPH's D8 truncation healed; checkParentAge's previously-MISSING `\item` for `damAge` now present) -- the rendered output, not the source diff, is what confirms the fix. Then the `--as-cran` gate (R/+man/ ship -> re-gate, Learning 226/227) = `Status: 2 NOTEs` 0/0/2 with `Rd files OK / Rd cross-references OK / code-documentation mismatches OK / Rd \usage OK` (the doc-check sub-lines are the relevant signal for doc edits; the 2 NOTEs are the documented false-positives). Also caught + fixed the ONE new lint I introduced (a D6 title fix pushed `obfuscateDate.R:1` to 81 chars -> reworded "plus and minus" to "plus/minus"; the 2 remaining >80 lines are pre-existing `# nolint`-suppressed) ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class doc-correctness session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S245, fix roxygen doc defects D1-D8 -> branch `issue103-stage1-defects`, gate GREEN 0/0/2, spell CLEAN, owner-gated landing)

#### Learning 232 -- "`--as-cran` gate GREEN" is NOT "CI green": the local gate does NOT run `lintr::lint_package()`, and a line-number-keyed `.lintr` exclusion silently DESYNCS when an edit above it shifts lines -- so opening a PR for CI is what catches the regression a local gate structurally cannot. Owner picked option 1 ("open a PR for CI validation") to land the S245 Stage-1 doc work (issue #103, D1-D8). **Landing/process action + a 1-line `.lintr` fix -- no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the lint-fix approach). PR #104, base `master`, keyword-safe body ("Part of #103" -- does not auto-close the multi-stage tracker). **(1) The gate and the lint workflow are DIFFERENT checks.** S245 verified its work with `R CMD check --as-cran` (GREEN 0/0/2) and called the branch "DONE + VERIFIED", but `--as-cran` does NOT run lintr; the project's lint is a SEPARATE CI workflow (`.github/workflows/lint.yaml`, `lintr::lint_package()` with `LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true`). So a Stage-1-introduced lint regression rode silently into the branch and only surfaced on the PR's first CI run -- exactly what the owner's "open a PR for CI validation" choice was designed to catch. After ANY `R/` edit, either run `lint_package()` locally too or rely on the PR's CI; do not equate gate-GREEN with CI-green. **(2) Root cause: a hardcoded `.lintr` line-range exclusion desynced.** `.lintr` had `exclusions: list(..., "R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L, ...)` -- a line range calibrated to that file's dead, fully-`##`-commented function body (the file has ZERO live code; the body started at line 12 on `master`). S245's D7 deleted 3 dead `@importFrom`/`@import` lines ABOVE the body, shifting it up 3 lines, so the flagged `my_palette <- ...` line moved from 14 to 11 -- OUTSIDE the `12L:55L` window -> `commented_code_linter` fired. No `# nolint` was removed; pure line-number drift. **General rule: any config that pins behavior to absolute line numbers (`.lintr` ranges, coverage excludes, `# nolint` at a remembered line) is brittle to edits above it -- when you add/remove lines, re-align or de-couple the pinned ranges in the SAME commit.** **(3) Diagnose a CI lint regression by proving NEW-vs-PRE-EXISTING first, then the exact diff.** `gh run list --workflow lint.yaml` showed every recent `master` lint run = `success` and the prior `normalize-copyright-headers` PR = `success`, so THIS PR was the first failure => the regression is the branch's, not pre-existing (this is the decisive cheap check -- do it before theorizing). Then `git diff master..branch -- <file>` + `grep nolint <file>` pinned the mechanism (3 lines removed above a line-keyed exclusion; no nolint involved). **(4) Prefer WHOLE-FILE exclusion over a brittle line range for 100%-dead-code files.** Owner chose (via `AskUserQuestion`, [[observation-vs-decision]]) to change `"R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L` -> `"R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R"` (unnamed whole-file entry, like `inst/application`/`tests`/`vignettes`) -- removes the line-number coupling so a future shift cannot break it again; justified because `grep -nvE '^\s*#'` proved the file has zero live code and it has zero references in `R/`/`man/`/`NAMESPACE`/live tests. Verified firsthand BEFORE pushing: `lintr::lint_package(".")` = 0 lints. **(5) When the owner asks to "open a PR for CI validation," own the RESULT, not just the act.** Opening the PR is one `gh pr create`; the deliverable's VALUE is the validation outcome. Monitored CI to completion (background `gh pr checks` poll), and when it went red, diagnosed + fixed + re-ran to GREEN rather than reporting "PR opened" and stopping (FM #13 literal-minimum). Final: 10/10 checks PASS, `gh pr view --json` = state OPEN / mergeable MERGEABLE / mergeStateStatus CLEAN -- the full 5-platform R-CMD-check matrix + lint + pkgdown + coverage is a stronger certification than the local gate alone; the merge/landing stays the owner's decision (do NOT merge unilaterally). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process + lint-config-fix session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S246, open PR #104 for CI validation + fix the CI-surfaced lint regression -> branch `issue103-stage1-defects`, CI GREEN 10/10, PR mergeable, owner-gated merge)

#### Learning 233 -- Merging the feature branch you are CURRENTLY ON: stash the Phase-1B stub first, because the mandatory `SESSION_NOTES.md` stub is an uncommitted edit that BLOCKS the post-merge checkout to `master`. Owner picked "merge PR #104" to land Stage 1 of issue #103 (D1-D8 + the `.lintr` fix) onto `master`. **Landing/process action on already-CI-green doc work -- no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) The 1B-stub + merge-the-current-branch collision.** Phase 1B mandates writing a "Session N claimed" stub to `SESSION_NOTES.md` BEFORE technical work; when the session's deliverable IS merging the branch you are on, that stub becomes an uncommitted edit to a file that DIFFERS between the branch (it carries the S245/S246 sections) and `master` (it does not), so `gh pr merge --delete-branch`'s automatic `git checkout master` would refuse with "Your local changes to SESSION_NOTES.md would be overwritten by checkout." Fix: `git stash push SESSION_NOTES.md` before the merge, then `git stash pop` after -- the post-merge `master` SESSION_NOTES.md == the branch's S246 version (the merge brought it across), so the stash (recorded against that same S246 base) re-applies with ZERO conflict. (General rule: before merging the branch you're standing on, the working tree must be clean w.r.t. any file that differs between branch and target -- stash it.) **(2) A `--merge` PR merge of an already-CI-green branch needs NO local re-gate.** The merge commit `9b512b1e` has two parents (`170324d5` master + `3beaf2bc` branch tip) but `git diff 9b512b1e 3beaf2bc` is EMPTY -- master never moved after the branch forked, so the merged tree is byte-identical to the tip CI validated 10/10 (full 5-platform R-CMD-check matrix + lint + pkgdown + coverage). The standing "`R/`+`man/` ship -> re-gate" rule (Learning 226/227) is SATISFIED by that CI run; re-running the local `--as-cran` gate would only re-certify an identical tree. Re-gate is for NEW edits, not for landing already-validated commits -- prove identity with `git diff <merge> <tip>` = empty. **(3) `--delete-branch` leaves a stale remote-tracking ref; confirm + prune, don't trust `git branch -r`.** `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch` deleted the remote branch (verified authoritatively: `gh api repos/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/branches/issue103-stage1-defects` = 404 "Branch not found") AND the local branch, but `git branch -r` still listed `origin/issue103-stage1-defects` until `git fetch --prune` removed the stale ref (it also pruned the long-dead `normalize-copyright-headers` from PR #101). The API 404 is the proof the remote branch is gone; the lingering local ref is just unpruned. **(4) Match the repo's merge-strategy precedent unless told otherwise.** The owner said only "merge PR #104"; the established way to land `R/`+`man/` work here is a merge commit (`a4de6a84 Merge pull request #101`), so `gh pr merge --merge` (not `--squash`) preserved the well-formed S245/S246 commits and their messages -- offered squash as an alternative, owner did not object. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S247, merge PR #104 -> Stage 1 of #103 landed on `master` `9b512b1e`, branch deleted local+remote, local==origin)

#### Learning 234 -- A "relocate comments" REFACTOR sweep across hundreds of files is proven safe by two DETERMINISTIC whole-corpus checks, never by eyeballing the diff. Stage 2 of issue #103 (copyright-placement, audit Finding 8): an R-scripted transform moved the `## Copyright(c) ...` / `## This file is part of nprcgenekeepr` pair from inside each roxygen block to file-top, above the first `#'` line with one blank separator (the `getSpeciesGestation.R` exemplar) -- 203 of 225 files moved, 22 already-correct skipped. **REFACTOR-class; no behavior change, no rendered-doc change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate: full-sweep vs sample-first). **(1) The two checks that make per-file review unnecessary.** (a) ZERO `man/` drift: after `devtools::document()`, `git status --porcelain man/` is empty -- decisive because roxygen2 IGNORES every `##` (and plain `#`) comment, so relocating/removing them CANNOT reach any `.Rd`; the only edit roxygen could notice is the blank-`#'`-seam collapse, and merging two blank doc lines is a no-op for paragraph breaks. (b) ZERO code-line change: a code-only projection diff per changed file (`diff <(git show HEAD:f | grep -vE '^[[:space:]]*#|^[[:space:]]*$') <(grep -vE ... f)`; empty for all 204) proves no R logic moved. Together (a)+(b) cover the ENTIRE change surface, so a 200+-file sweep needs no manual diff review. **(2) The diff LOOKS bigger than the change.** Title `#'` lines appear as paired `-`/`+` with IDENTICAL text because git reframes the relocated-block hunk as delete-old + add-new; that is cosmetic reordering, not change -- do not mistake it for risk, and do not write a naive "unexpected line" grep (it fires on every moved-but-identical title); the right code-safety check is the comment-stripped projection in (1b). **(3) Classify-then-characterize BEFORE writing the transform.** Two read-only R passes (classify each file ABOVE/INTERLEAVED by comparing the copyright-line index to the first-`#'` index; then characterize edge cases) surfaced every special case up front -- 2 files carrying the pair TWICE (two single-function blocks each -> de-duplicate to ONE pair at top), 9 `mod*.R` with a `# section banner` at line 1 (prepend-at-very-top puts the pair above the banner uniformly), and 1 already-above-but-separator-missing file -- so the transform became a SINGLE deterministic function (remove all pairs, collapse the resulting double-`#'` seam, prepend pair+blank) instead of a pile of ad-hoc fixes. **(4) Blast-radius gate.** A 203-file mechanical sweep is high blast radius -> gated the start via `AskUserQuestion` (full-sweep vs sample-first), committed clean first, branch-based with landing owner-gated (Stage 1 precedent). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class doc-structure session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S248, copyright-placement sweep -> branch `issue103-stage2-copyright-placement`, code commit `af08b5bf`, UNPUSHED, landing owner-gated)

#### Learning 235 -- A line-shifting REFACTOR sweep clears CI lint on the FIRST PR run when (and only when) the predecessor ran `lint_package()` AFTER the sweep -- closing the Learning-232 gap that bit Stage 1. Owner picked "1" (Land Stage 2) and, via `AskUserQuestion`, the landing METHOD "open a PR for CI" (option (a) of the S248 landing gate) to land Stage 2 of issue #103 (the copyright-placement sweep). **Landing/process action on already-locally-verified doc-structure work -- no R-logic change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the landing method -- PR vs direct-merge vs review-first). PR #105, base `master`, keyword-safe body ("Part of #103. Stage 2 of 8" -- does not auto-close the multi-stage tracker). **(1) First-pass green BECAUSE the line-shift condition was already lint-checked.** The sweep moved the `##` copyright pair to file-top, shifting line numbers in all 204 files -- EXACTLY the condition that desynced Stage 1's line-keyed `.lintr` exclusion (Learning 232: the `12L:55L` range vs a 3-line shift). But S248 ran `lintr::lint_package()` = 0 AFTER the sweep and recorded it, so any desync was already surfaced/excluded; CI `lint` came back PASS (3m44s) on the FIRST run -- no diagnose-and-fix cycle, in contrast to S245->S246's red-then-green. The local `lint_package()` is a FAITHFUL proxy for CI lint (both run `lintr::lint_package()`), so once it reads 0 the PR's lint check is predictably green; the PR round-trip's residual value is the 5-platform / R-devel matrix coverage the local gate cannot give, NOT lint disagreement. **Rule: after ANY line-shifting `R/` edit, run `lint_package()` BEFORE opening the PR -- that is what makes the first CI run green and keeps the Learning-232 regression from riding in silently.** **(2) The close-out-docs commit re-triggers the FULL CI matrix.** The `lint`/`pkgdown`/`R-CMD-check`/`test-coverage` workflows trigger on `pull_request:` with NO `paths-ignore`, so committing the markdown close-out docs (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES) to the PR branch re-runs the entire ~18-min matrix even though zero `R/`/`man/`/`NAMESPACE` changed. It stays green (markdown cannot break R CMD check or lint -- `lintr` lints only `R/`, and those top-level files already exist in the green tree), and the payoff is that the merge-tip the owner lands is itself CI-validated (the S246->S247 precedent: S246's close-out commit WAS the validated tip S247 merged). EXPECT the second run and a transient `mergeStateStatus UNSTABLE` before it resolves -- do not mistake it for a problem. **(3) Own the RESULT (FM #13), gate the METHOD (not just the act).** The owner's "Land Stage 2" left the landing METHOD open, so I posed it via `AskUserQuestion` (PR / direct-merge / review-first) rather than assuming the Stage-1 precedent, then monitored CI to completion via a background `gh pr checks` poll and confirmed 10/10 + `mergeStateStatus CLEAN` before declaring done -- did NOT merge unilaterally (the merge stays the owner's call, matching the S246->S247 split). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S249, open PR #105 for CI validation -> Stage 2 of #103, CI GREEN 10/10, PR mergeable/CLEAN, owner-gated merge)

#### Learning 236 -- A roxygen TAG-REORDER sweep is proven safe by ZERO `man/` drift -- with ONE exception roxygen does NOT canonicalize: `@author`/`@references` keep their SOURCE order, so they must never be swapped. Owner: "merge PR #105; work on #103 this session" -> (1) merged PR #105 (Stage 2 landed on `master`, merge commit `12c7c353`), (2) issue #103 **Stage 3 -- block-order normalization (audit Finding 2)**: a deterministic R transform reordered roxygen tags to canonical order across 194 files / 206 blocks. **REFACTOR-class doc-structure -- no behavior/rendered-doc change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the `@examples`/`@export` order). **(1) roxygen2 canonicalizes rendered `.Rd` section order REGARDLESS of source tag order -- EXCEPT `@author` and `@references`, whose source order IS preserved into `\author{}`/`\references{}`.** So reordering `@return`/`@param`/`@examples`/`@importFrom`/`@export` produces byte-identical `.Rd` (the man/-zero-drift proof), but swapping `@author` vs `@references` (or interleaving their content) DOES change `.Rd`. First pass swapped them per a naive canonical key (references<author) and `man/kinship.Rd` was the lone drifter -- content moved between `\references{}` and `\author{}`. Fix: give `@author` and `@references` the SAME sort key so a stable sort PRESERVES source order (only kinship.R has both). General rule for any tag-reorder: tags that map to free-text `.Rd` sections roxygen does not reorder (author/references) must keep source order; tags roxygen canonicalizes (param/return/value/arguments/seealso/examples/usage/details) can be freely reordered and proven safe by zero drift. **(2) Plain-`#` comments INSIDE a roxygen block split a naive `#'`-contiguous parser, but roxygen2 SPANS them.** 8 files carry interruptions: `# nolint start/end` wrapping a long line (kinship.R) and `## ## rmsutilityr <fn>` source-attribution notes (checkRequiredCols/colChange/qcStudbook/...). roxygen treats the doc block as continuing across these (kinship's `.Rd` had all the post-nolint content), so it merges tags across the gap. Safe handling: reorder each `#'`-fragment INDEPENDENTLY and leave the plain-`#` line fixed in place -- this is correct when the interruption sits at a clean tag boundary (every fragment's max canonical key <= the next fragment's min key, so no tag needs to cross it) AND when no `.Rd`-bearing tag's CONTINUATION spans the gap. kinship.R violated the latter (a `# nolint` wrapped a `@references` continuation line), which is why only the author/references-same-key fix (1) -- not the fragment-independence -- made it drift-free. **(3) The deterministic safety suite for a tag-reorder sweep (no eyeballing 194 diffs):** (a) zero `man/` drift after `devtools::document()` -- the rendered-surface proof; (b) code+plain-comment projection diff = 0 (strip `#'` lines, compare HEAD vs working) -- proves no code/comment line moved; (c) per-block assertion `sort(region_before) == sort(region_after)` IN the transform -- proves only a PERMUTATION, no line content altered; (d) `lint_package()` = 0 (line shifts must not desync a `.lintr` line-keyed exclusion -- Learning 232/235); (e) re-characterize -> 0 residual deviations (goal achieved); (f) `--as-cran` 0/0/2 with Rd files/cross-refs/usage OK. **man/-drift CANNOT cover `@noRd` files (they generate no `.Rd`)** -- that surface is covered by (b)+(c) [provably a content-preserving permutation into canonical order, and `@noRd` renders nothing so there is no output to break] and, here, an independent 9-agent adversarial review of all 45 changed `@noRd` diffs (0 defects). **(4) A self-CONTRADICTORY spec is an owner gate, not a guess.** The audit's Finding 2 template said `@examples` LAST (after `@export`) in two places, but its "secondary disorder" bullet AND its named reference-exemplar files (`getSpeciesGestation.R`) put `@examples` BEFORE `@export` -- a genuine 3-vs-3 contradiction over a corpus-wide convention. Both choices are purely source-cosmetic (zero `.Rd`/NAMESPACE impact). Posed it via `AskUserQuestion` ([[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] when the source of truth is contradictory) -> owner chose `@examples` LAST. **(5) GitHub network-outage workaround: REST survives when GraphQL + git-over-HTTPS do not.** During a TLS-handshake-timeout outage, `gh pr merge`/`gh pr view --json` (GraphQL) and `git fetch`/`git push` (HTTPS) timed out, but `gh api` (REST) worked with retries -- merged via `gh api -X PUT repos/.../pulls/105/merge -f merge_method=merge`, confirmed `merged:true`, then DEFERRED the local `master` FF-sync + remote-branch delete + Stage 3 push to close-out and completed them when the network recovered (a merged PR on the remote is authoritative; the local sync is just bookkeeping). Stage 3 work itself is 100% local (edit R, `document()`, gate, lint), so the outage did not block the deliverable. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process + REFACTOR-class doc-structure session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S250, merge PR #105 + issue #103 Stage 3 block-order normalization -> branch `issue103-stage3-block-order`, commit `fb9a92ab`, gate 0/0/2, man/ zero-drift, owner-gated landing)

#### Learning 237 -- A backtick->`\code{}` markup sweep is rendered-NEUTRAL when `markdown = TRUE` (roxygen already expands `` `x` `` to `\code{x}` in the `.Rd`), so the success test is ZERO `man/` drift -- EXCEPT where the +5-char expansion pushes a source line past 80 chars, forcing a reflow whose `.Rd` drift is whitespace-only. Owner pick = "stage 4" -> issue #103 **Stage 4 (audit Finding 4)**: 45 backtick spans -> `\code{}` across 30 R/ files. **REFACTOR-class doc-markup -- no R logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; rendered docs unchanged; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`). **(1) Rendered-neutral, NOT a rendered change.** `DESCRIPTION:81` sets `Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)` with NO `@noMd` overrides, so roxygen already converts source `` `x` `` -> `\code{x}` in the generated `.Rd`. Changing the SOURCE backtick to `\code{}` therefore yields a byte-identical `.Rd` (26 of 30 files) -- the same zero-`man/`-drift proof as a tag-reorder. (My opening phase declaration wrongly said it would "change rendered output"; reading `DESCRIPTION:81` corrected it -- read the markdown setting BEFORE declaring the phase.) **(2) The audit's count ("48 backticks / 37 files, small/safe") undercounts ~2x and omits the EXCLUSION traps.** Reality: 105 backticks on `#'` lines, of which only 90 (= 45 spans) are genuine `\code{}` targets; the other 15 must NOT be converted -- backtick-quoted NON-SYNTACTIC R names INSIDE `@examples` (`` `si re` ``, `` relClasses$`Relationship Class` ``, `` `n` `` in `## ` example comments), the markdown INLINE-R `` `r lifecycle::badge('experimental')` `` (converting breaks the badge), and broken `` `word' `` typographic quotes (`` `and'``/`` `or'`` -> a separate rendered defect). Classify each backtick by its line-number-vs-its-block's-`@examples` boundary, and beware MULTI-BLOCK files (`set_seed.R` has 3 blocks -> the "first `@examples` line" heuristic misleads; cross-check export status + the actual owning block). **(3) Conversion adds 5 chars/token -> some lines cross the 80-col lint limit -> reflow, but reflow moves `.Rd` line breaks.** The tension: NOT reflowing => `lint_package()` fails ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]); reflowing => `man/` drift. Resolution: reflow with MINIMAL non-cascading word-moves -- often move the FIRST word of the overflowing line UP to the prior line (which has slack), avoiding the down-cascade through a packed paragraph -- then PROVE the `.Rd` drift is whitespace-only via a whitespace-normalized HEAD-vs-working comparison (Rd fills paragraphs, so intra-paragraph line breaks render identically). 4 of 30 files needed this. NB: do not break a line right after a `/` joining two `\code{}` (e.g. `\code{"M"}/\code{"F"}`) -- the newline renders as an inserted space; break at a real word boundary instead. **(4) The deterministic safety suite for a markup-token sweep:** (a) backtick count drops by exactly 2x the target-span count and the RESIDUAL count == the exclusions (byte-verified list); (b) **word-sequence projection** -- canonicalize BOTH `` `x` `` and `\code{x}` to a bare token + collapse whitespace, then HEAD == working for every changed file (proves only backtick->`\code` + whitespace moved; no word dropped/added/reordered; covers `@noRd` files too, which generate no `.Rd`); (c) `man/` drift = byte-identical OR whitespace-normalized-identical; (d) `lint_package()` = 0; (e) `spell_check_package` CLEAN; (f) `--as-cran` 0/0/2 with Rd line widths / code-documentation mismatches / Rd \usage OK. **(5) Run the `--as-cran` gate from the PROJECT ROOT (renv active), not a scratchpad cwd.** `R CMD build` from outside the project makes the copied package bootstrap `renv` against an empty cache and fail with "dependencies ... are not available"; from the root the project `.Rprofile` activates the renv library (deps present), and `.Rbuildignore` excludes `renv`/`renv.lock`/`.Rprofile` from the tarball so the vignette-install step does not re-bootstrap. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class doc-markup session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S251, issue #103 Stage 4 markup unification -> branch `issue103-stage4-markup`, code commit `002b77c9`, gate 0/0/2, 45 conversions / 30 files / 6 exclusions, `get_and_or_list` defect deferred)

#### Learning 238 -- A DIRECT-MERGE landing of an already-`--as-cran`-certified branch needs NO local re-gate and gets NO CI, so the local cert of the EXACT merged commits is the SOLE certification -- confirm the merge brought the right content with a CHEAP deterministic sanity check on the landed `master` tree, not a gate re-run. Owner: "land stage 4" -> via `AskUserQuestion` the landing METHOD "direct-merge" (the Stage-3 precedent `72f5391e`) to land issue #103 **Stage 4** (markup unification) onto `master`. **Landing/process -- no R-logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate: the landing method). **(1) Direct-merge has NO CI safety net (unlike the PR path of Learning 235), so there is no 5-platform re-validation** -- the only certification is S251's local `--as-cran` 0/0/2 (+ `lint_package()` = 0 + zero `man/` drift) on the EXACT commits being merged. Prove identity instead of re-running the ~15-min gate: `master` never moved since the branch forked, so `git diff 87abc0fc..ff0ca8fd` is exactly S251's two commits (`002b77c9` + `3f8c1865`) -- Learning 233 (2)'s "a merge of an already-validated branch needs no re-gate" applies to a direct-merge too (the identity argument is the same; only the certifier differs -- local gate here vs CI there). **(2) BUT direct-merge loses CI's independent re-check, so substitute a CHEAP deterministic sanity check on the LANDED tree matched to the change** -- for this markup sweep: backtick chars on `#'` lines == 15 (the 6 intended exclusions, == S251's 105->15), `\code{Pedigree}` == 25, `\code{kValue}` == 10, and the deferred `get_and_or_list.R:10` defect still PRESENT (correctly NOT folded in). Confirms the merge brought the CONVERTED content (not a stale/pre-conversion tree) in seconds. This is the direct-merge analogue of CI's role: not a re-gate, a content-identity spot-check. **(3) The stash-stub -> checkout -> merge `--no-ff` -> push -> pop -> `branch -d` sequence (Learning 233 (1)) reapplies the 1B stub cleanly** because the post-merge `master` SESSION_NOTES base == the branch base the stash recorded against. Stash ONLY the tracked-modified files (`SESSION_NOTES.md` + `.DS_Store`) so the untracked `PED_GV_AUDIT*.html` standing keep is undisturbed; `--no-ff` matches the Stage-3 merge-commit precedent. **(4) A LOCAL-ONLY branch (never pushed) needs only `git branch -d` after the merge** -- no remote ref, so no `gh api` 404 check / `git fetch --prune` (that cleanup in Learning 233 (3) was specific to a PR-landed branch deleted via `--delete-branch`). And the predecessor's close-out docs ride the merge onto `master`, so this session's close-out simply appends to them on `master` and pushes -- no branch round-trip ([[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S252, direct-merge Stage 4 of #103 -> landed on `master` merge commit `ff0ca8fd`, branch deleted local-only, local==origin)

#### Learning 239 -- Converting a whole-package `@import pkg` to explicit `@importFrom` is provably safe when the proof is a NAMESPACE diff of EXACTLY the `import(pkg)` line removed -- and you get there by a BARE-name census BEFORE editing, not by trusting the audit's count. Owner pick = "stage 5"; via `AskUserQuestion` scope = futile.logger only (defer shiny/Matrix), landing METHOD = PR-for-CI. **REFACTOR-class import-declaration refactoring -- no new behavior; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates: scope + landing). **(1) An `@import`/`@importFrom` change is NAMESPACE-only -> the proof surface is the NAMESPACE diff + the check/test runtime, NOT `man/` drift.** Import tags never appear in `.Rd`, so `man/` drift is ZERO and irrelevant (unlike the Stage 1-4 rendered-doc proofs); the success test is that `document()` produces EXACTLY the predicted NAMESPACE delta. PREDICT the delta from a usage census first, then confirm -- here "one line removed: `import(futile.logger)`" held byte-for-byte. **(2) A whole-pkg `import(pkg)` survives in NAMESPACE as long as ANY file keeps `@import pkg`; to drop it, ALL `@import pkg` tags must go.** Convert each to `@importFrom pkg <fns-it-uses>`; a file that uses NO function of pkg (a DEAD `@import` -- here `getPedDirectRelatives.R`) is REMOVED, not converted (nothing to import). Do not leave one "for safety" -- it defeats the stage. **(3) The census: find every BARE (unqualified) call to pkg's functions; `pkg::fn`-qualified calls need no import and are unaffected.** Grep code lines (exclude `#'`) for the package's function patterns. Every bare `futile.logger` call was `flog.debug` (6 files), already imported via other files' `@importFrom` (appServer/modInput); all other refs were `futile.logger::`-qualified -- so removing `import(futile.logger)` could only orphan a bare non-flog.debug call, of which there were NONE. That census IS the proof the delta is exactly the `import()` line. **(4) Runtime-verify a NAMESPACE stage with the `--as-cran` import checks + an EXPLICIT smoke against the INSTALLED namespace, NOT `load_all()`.** `checking dependencies in R code ... OK` + `checking R code for possible problems ... OK` (catches "no visible global function for flog.debug") + examples/`--run-donttest`/tests OK run against the freshly-installed pkg with imports ENFORCED -- the authoritative runtime check. `pkgload::load_all()` makes ALL functions visible regardless of imports -> it would HIDE a missing-import bug; for the explicit smoke, prepend the `.Rcheck` dir to `.libPaths()` and `library()` the installed build (confirmed whole-pkg futile.logger gone; bare `flog.debug` fired; `getPedigree()` -> 280 rows). The handoff's "verify method dispatch" warning targets S4 pkgs (Matrix, deferred) -- `flog.debug` is a plain function, no dispatch surface. **(5) The audit's enumeration was STALE -- re-grep the live tree.** The S244 audit's "8 easy = 7 futile.logger + 1 RColorBrewer" was wrong: RColorBrewer's dead `@import` was already removed by an earlier D7 cleanup (no `@import`, not in DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE; only `grDevices::colorRampPalette` survives in a commented-out body), so the real scope was 7 futile.logger, one dead. `grep -rn "@import " R/*.R` for the ACTUAL whole-pkg tags before scoping (Learning 231: cited locations are a sample, not an inventory). **(6) Op gotcha: a shell var set as `VAR=... ; Rscript -e '...Sys.getenv("VAR")...'` is EMPTY in the child unless `export`ed** -- the first runtime smoke silently loaded the OLD renv namespace (tell: "loaded from: .../renv/library"), proving nothing about the new tree; hardcode the path or export, and ASSERT the load path (`grepl("s253gate", system.file(...))`) before trusting a smoke. **(7) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate): CI GREEN 10/10 first run** (lint 3m54s + 5-platform R-CMD-check incl. devel 18m28s + pkgdown + coverage + codecov), PR #106 MERGEABLE/CLEAN -- multi-platform confirmation that the import removal resolves identically everywhere (R namespace mechanics are platform-independent, so CI's value is breadth-of-confidence, not a different failure mode). Did NOT merge unilaterally (owner's call, S246->S247 / S249 split). The close-out-docs commit re-triggers the full matrix (Learning 235 (2), no `paths-ignore`) -- accepted as the cost of an ACCURATE handoff (the CI result must be known before the close-out commit); the real fix (a `paths-ignore` workflow config) is a separate gated decision, not folded in. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class import-declaration session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S253, issue #103 Stage 5 import conversion -> branch `issue103-stage5-imports`, code commit `9811a945`, NAMESPACE delta = `import(futile.logger)` removed, gate 0/0/2, full NOT_CRAN 0/0, runtime smoke OK, PR #106 CI 10/10 green)

#### Learning 240 -- Dropping a redundant `@keywords internal` from an `@noRd` block is the CLEANEST possible REFACTOR: the tag is INERT on an `@noRd` block (roxygen generates no `.Rd` for it, and `@keywords` never touches NAMESPACE), so the proof is zero `man/` AND zero `NAMESPACE` drift -- and NO runtime smoke is needed (unlike a true `@import` NAMESPACE stage, Learning 239). Owner-directed two-item session: (1) merge PR #106 (Stage 5 landing), (2) issue #103 **Stage 6 -- internal-marker cleanup (audit Finding 5)**. **(1) landing/process; (2) REFACTOR-class doc-metadata; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the Stage-6 landing method -> direct-merge). **(1) `@keywords` on an `@noRd` block reaches NO output.** `@noRd` suppresses `.Rd` generation, so `@keywords` (which only ever populates a `\keyword{}` field in a generated `.Rd`) is dead metadata there, and `@keywords` never affects NAMESPACE -- therefore removing it from `@noRd` helpers is provably zero-`man/`-drift AND zero-`NAMESPACE`-change. Predicted it from that semantics, then PROVED it: `devtools::document()` left `man/` and `NAMESPACE` untouched and `git diff <pre-stage6>..<merge> -- NAMESPACE man/` is empty. Even cleaner than the Stage-5 import change (which DID move NAMESPACE) -- no runtime smoke is warranted because nothing rendered or dispatched changed. **(2) Policy: `@noRd` for unexported helpers; reserve `@keywords internal` for the package-level doc only.** Removed 5 redundant tags -- 4 in `modPotentialParents.R` (`flattenPotentialParents`/`firstPedigreeSpecies`/`pedigreeGestationDefault`/`prefillGuardAllows`) + 1 in `readFocalAnimalIds.R`, each paired with `@noRd` -- and KEPT `nprcgenekeepr-package.R:4` (the package doc, which IS documented, so its `@keywords internal` is meaningful). **(3) The audit's Finding-5 enumeration was STALE (Learning 231 again).** It listed 4 files incl. `nprcgenekeeper.R` (the duplicate legacy `_PACKAGE` doc, Finding D2) which NO LONGER EXISTS (resolved earlier -> 3 files now), and implied ~1 occurrence in `modPotentialParents.R` which had since grown to 4 internal helpers -> real scope = 5 removals / 2 files, not the implied ~2. `grep -rn "@keywords internal" R/` before scoping -- the audit is a sample, not an inventory. **(4) A clean PR merge waits for CI 10/10 GREEN + `mergeStateStatus CLEAN`, not `UNSTABLE`.** At orientation PR #106's re-run (triggered by S253's close-out-docs commit, Learning 235 (2)) was IN_PROGRESS -> watched it to 10/10 via a background `gh pr checks --watch` rather than merging into UNSTABLE (which would waste the PR-for-CI cert S253 chose); then `gh pr merge 106 --merge --delete-branch` -> merge commit, local FF-sync, stub-stash pop. Did NOT merge before owner direction or unilaterally (FM #13 / S246->S247 split). **(5) Direct-merge cert = local `--as-cran` + landed-tree sanity check (Learning 238 reused).** No CI on a direct-merge, so the freshly-run local gate (0/0/2) on the exact committed tree IS the cert; confirmed the merge brought the right content with a cheap deterministic check on `master` (the `@keywords internal` census = only the package doc; the merge stat = only the 2 R/ files, 5 deletions; the `NAMESPACE`/`man/` diff empty). **(6) Two owner-directed deliverables in one session is owner-set scope, not FM #2 scope creep.** The owner said "merge pr #106" then "then stage 6"; handled as two sequential items with full verification each and ONE combined close-out -- the "1 and done" rule guards against AGENT self-direction, which did not happen here. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a landing/process + REFACTOR-class doc-metadata session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S254, merge PR #106 [Stage 5, merge commit `fc0b2b72`] + issue #103 Stage 6 internal-marker cleanup -> code commit `b3a3fbe0`, direct-merged via `318ed491`, 5 `@keywords internal` removed / 2 files, gate 0/0/2, zero `man/`+NAMESPACE drift)

#### Learning 241 -- The troff/LaTeX single-quote idiom `` `word' `` (open-backtick, close-apostrophe) is a rendered-doc DEFECT under roxygen `markdown = TRUE`, and the fix is `\sQuote{}` -- verified by RENDERING the `.Rd`, not by reading the source diff. Owner pick = "get_and_or_list" -> fix the carried-open defect in `R/get_and_or_list.R:10` (`@param conjunction`). **REFACTOR-class documentation fix -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: fix approach `\sQuote{}`; landing method direct-merge). **(1) Why `` `word' `` breaks under markdown mode.** With `DESCRIPTION` `markdown = TRUE`, every backtick is an inline-code delimiter, so `` `and' or `or' with `and' `` pairs the 1st+2nd backticks into a code span (`\verb{and' or }`), leaves `or'` literal, and leaves the trailing single `` `and' `` as a stray backtick -> the garbled `.Rd` `This is usually \verb{and' or }or' with `and'`. The author intended typographic single quotes (the troff `` `x' `` idiom), which markdown does not honor. **(2) The fix is `\sQuote{}` (or `\dQuote{}`), the canonical Rd macro for directional single/double quotes -- and it SURVIVES markdown mode.** CommonMark only backslash-escapes ASCII punctuation, so `\sQuote{and}` passes through unchanged (backslash+letter stays literal; braces are not markdown-special) and roxygen emits it verbatim into the `.Rd`, where Rd renders it as 'and' in HTML and PDF. Confirmed empirically: after `document()`, `man/get_and_or_list.Rd` reads `\sQuote{and} or \sQuote{or} with \sQuote{and}` and `Rd2txt` renders 'and' or 'or' with 'and'. **(3) This defect class is INVISIBLE in a source diff and even in a naive `.Rd` scan -- you must RENDER to verify.** The source looks like intentional quoting; the `.Rd` `\verb{...}` looks like a valid macro. Only rendering (`tools::Rd2txt`/`Rd2HTML`) exposes the garble. So the proof surface for a rendered-doc fix is the RENDERED output, plus `man/` drift limited to the one file and an empty NAMESPACE diff. **(4) A doc-only fix has no RED/GREEN but IS rendered-drift -- unlike the Stage 1-6 zero-drift work.** No behavior/logic/NAMESPACE change (function body, return, examples, exports all untouched; no dedicated test needed -- the fn is exercised indirectly by 6 R files), so TDD RED/GREEN is N/A and it is REFACTOR-class; but unlike the Stage 4 markup sweep (rendered-neutral) or Stage 6 (`@noRd`-inert), this INTENTIONALLY changes the rendered `.Rd`, so the success test is "rendered output now correct", not "zero drift". Keep line length <=80 by wrapping the roxygen prose across two `#'` lines (Rd fills paragraphs -> intra-paragraph breaks render identically), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]. **(5) grep confirmed NO sibling `` `word'`` defects** -- `grep -rn "\`[a-zA-Z]*'" R/` matched only this one line (Learning 231: verify the live tree, do not assume the class is isolated). **(6) Landing = direct-merge (owner gate), cert = local `--as-cran` 0/0/2 + landed-tree sanity check (Learning 238).** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation fix -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S255, fix `get_and_or_list` rendered-doc defect -> branch `fix-get-and-or-list-squote`, code commit `b0d8e3e8`, direct-merged via `a23e4eb9`, `\sQuote{}` x3 / 1 R file + `.Rd`, gate 0/0/2, rendered 'and'/'or'/'and')

#### Learning 242 -- Adding runnable roxygen `@examples` is REFACTOR-class doc work, but UNLIKE the Stage 1-6 zero-drift stages it BOTH changes rendered `man/` AND changes what `R CMD check` EXECUTES (the examples now RUN) -- so the proof surface is "`checking examples ... OK` under `--as-cran`", not "zero drift", and each drafted example should be RUN (via `load_all`) BEFORE placement to avoid a failed-gate round-trip. Owner pick = "Stage 7 -- examples policy" (issue #103 audit Finding 1 / §7). **REFACTOR-class; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (3 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: scope [complete Stage 7], policy-doc home [new `docs/` file], landing [PR-for-CI]). **(1) The guard ladder -- one rung, lowest honest:** bare-runnable (cheap/deterministic/side-effect-free, inputs from `data/`/`inst/`/inline literals, file writes to `tempdir()`) = DEFAULT and preferred (it is continuously checked); `\donttest` = safe-but-genuinely-SLOW only (it runs under `--run-donttest`, so NOT for network dependence); `\dontrun` = genuinely un-runnable (live LabKey/EHR/network, launches the app, or needs an unshipped file). **`if (interactive())` is RETIRED as an example guard -> use `\dontrun` for app launchers.** **(2) "One guard ladder" requires fixing EXISTING mis-rung guards, not just adding new examples** -- a completeness critic caught that adding-examples-only would leave the ladder half-harmonized: 5 `\dontrun` blocks actually ran clean (inline/`pedGood` inputs, message-only side effects) -> demoted to bare-runnable (`makeFounderStatsTable`, `makeGeneticSummaryTable`, `logModuleEvent`, `safeExecute`, `runQcStudbook`); 4 LabKey `\donttest` need a live EHR -> promoted to `\dontrun` (`getDemographics`, `getLkDirectAncestors`, `getLkDirectRelatives`, `setLabKeyDefaults`). **(3) The audit's exemption list was WRONG for 2 of 32 (Learning 231 again -- an audit is a sample; read the BODY, not the name):** `shouldShowChangedColsTab` (returns a logical) and `processQcStudbookResult` (returns a list of data.frames) were labeled "tab-UI" but contain NO Shiny code -> they are directly callable and DO need examples (add-set = 9, not 7). The census otherwise confirmed the audit with ZERO drift (180 exported, 148 with `@examples`, 32 without). Correctly-exempt: 18 `mod*UI/Server`, `appUI`/`appServer`, the genuine `tabPanel()` builders `getChangedColsTab`/`getErrorTab`, and the deprecated alias `makeGrpNum`. **(4) A file-writing example stays bare-runnable by targeting `tempdir()`** (`saveDataframesAsFiles`, matching its own test). **(5) A new `docs/` policy file needs a `.gitignore` whitelist:** `.gitignore` has `docs/*` + per-subdir `!docs/<dir>/` whitelists, so a new `docs/conventions/ROXYGEN_EXAMPLES_POLICY.md` is IGNORED until you add `!docs/conventions/` + `!docs/conventions/**`; `docs/` is `.Rbuildignore`d, so a dev-process policy there ships nowhere ([[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]]). **(6) A read-only discovery Workflow** (parallel census of exported-without-examples + guard inventory + example-data inventory, then per-utility draft + adversarial actually-run verify, then a critic over the classifications; 18 agents) gathered evidence BEFORE the owner scope gate -- it confirmed the audit and surfaced the 2 mislabels + the mis-rung guards. **(7) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate):** adding/un-guarding examples makes them EXECUTE on every CI platform, and CI is the ONLY cross-platform verification (the local `--as-cran` gate is macOS-only) -- so PR-for-CI is worth more here than for a rendered-neutral stage. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S256, issue #103 Stage 7 examples policy -> branch `issue103-stage7-examples`, code commit `d6e6b8dc`, PR #107; +`@examples` on 9 fns, 2 `if(interactive())`->`\dontrun`, 5 `\dontrun`->runnable, 4 `\donttest`->`\dontrun`, policy doc, gate 0/0/2 `examples ... OK`)

#### Learning 243 -- A roadmap "stage" that bundles TWO judgment-heavy findings is multi-session work -- split it by Finding at an owner scope-gate, and re-scan MULTI-EXPORT files before trusting a first-title-per-file census. Owner pick = "Stage 8" (the LAST #103 stage), but Stage 8 = Finding 3 (title voice, ~60-70 rewrites) **AND** Finding 6 (`@inheritParams`/`@family` de-dup, 66-file `ped` drift); the audit itself said "consider splitting across sessions." **REFACTOR-class, rendered-drift; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (3 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: slice = Finding 3; Shiny = exempt; approve-table = approve-all; landing = PR-for-CI). **(1) Present the scope reality, then let the owner pick the slice.** A cheap deterministic census (grep titles + classify voice; grep `@param ped/ids/kmat/threshold` distinct descriptions) sized both findings BEFORE any workflow -- Finding 3 = ~100 exported non-conforming titles, Finding 6 = `@param ped` in 66 files with ~20 distinct descriptions -- proving one session can't hold both. The scope gate is the AUTHOR's call to POSE, the owner's to DECIDE (do the heavy discovery Workflow AFTER the gate, on the chosen slice only -- S256's over-heavy-workflow weakness). **(2) A first-`#'`-title-per-file census UNDERCOUNTS titles in multi-export files.** Each `mod*` file exports BOTH a UI and a Server function, each with its OWN title (`X Module - UI Function` at the top, `X Module - Server Function` ~100 lines down); `autoIdFormat.R` exports Get+Set. The census caught only the first -> re-scan every file with `>=2 @export` before finalizing the work-list (here it revealed 9 hidden mod Server titles, tied to the Shiny decision). **(3) Title voice is rendered-drift but NAMESPACE-neutral -> proof = every title matches its approved target (0 mismatch) + empty NAMESPACE diff + `--as-cran` Rd/examples OK.** Titles populate `\title{}` only; no `@export`/import change. PREDICTED then PROVED the empty NAMESPACE diff. The success test is NOT zero-drift (99 `.Rd` intentionally change) -- it is "titles harmonized + gate green". **(4) A guarded bulk-edit script + full re-census is the safe way to apply ~100 deterministic title edits; reserve hand-edits for special cases.** The script replaces the title paragraph (first run of non-blank non-`@tag` `#'` lines) with one imperative line, GUARDED by "the file's current first title line is a prefix of the recorded old_title" and "paragraph <=4 lines" (skip+report otherwise) -- 96/100 applied, 0 skips; then re-extract every title and assert == target (0 mismatches). The 4 explicit-`@description` reverts were hand-edited. **(5) Reverting a malformed braced `@description{...}` to implicit fixes a latent D1-class defect AND reorganizes -- render to confirm.** roxygen's multi-paragraph rule: 1st para = title, 2nd = description, LATER paras -> `\details`. So dropping `@description{`/`}` on `findGeneration`/`groupAddAssign` moved their secondary paragraphs into `\details` (all text preserved -- `Rd2txt` confirmed no literal braces, `grep` confirmed "maximal independent set"/"Subsequent trips" still present). Two inline `@description ` reverts are trivial prefix-drops. **(6) A duplicate SOURCE file needs both blocks synced so the ONE generated `.Rd` is deterministic.** `getSimSires.R` DEFINES and `@export`s `getPotentialSires` (a near-identical duplicate of `getPotentialSires.R`); only `man/getPotentialSires.Rd` exists and NAMESPACE exports it once. Editing one block's title could let collation order pick either -> set BOTH to the SAME new title ("List potential sires"); the duplicate *file* is a separate CODE defect surfaced for a future dedup session (out of Finding-3 scope). **(7) Shiny `mod*`/app titles exempted (owner), parallel to the Stage-7 `@examples` exemption** -- descriptive `X Module - UI/Server Function` is a recognized module convention; converting all 20 (11 UI + 9 hidden Server) to `Build the X module UI` is churn for arguable benefit, and would introduce the informal "GeneKeepR" name in `appUI`/`appServer`. **(8) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate):** large `man/` churn (99 pages) benefits from cross-platform Rd checks (line-width/cross-reference can vary by platform); the local `--as-cran` gate is macOS-only. Do NOT merge unilaterally (FM #13 / owner's call). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S257, issue #103 Stage 8a title voice -> branch `issue103-stage8a-title-voice`, code commit `8e76e49a`; 100 exported titles normalized + 4 `@description` reverts, 99 `.Rd`, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2 `examples ... OK`, PR #108 for CI)

#### Learning 244 -- De-dup `@param` drift with `@inheritParams` by DELETING ONLY THE TARGET `@param` LINE -- that makes donor choice collision-proof; keep formals that share a NAME but not a MEANING on SEPARATE donors; and an `@family` tag on a `@noRd` function is inert. Owner scope-gate (via `AskUserQuestion`) = slice "Safe formals + @family" of issue #103 **Stage 8b -- Finding 6** (`@inheritParams`/`@family` de-dup), deferring the big `@param ped` drift (66 files, ~46 genuine-variant meanings) to its own session. **REFACTOR-class doc work; no R-logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates: scope; landing = PR-for-CI). **(1) `@inheritParams donor` inherits ONLY the callee formals that are (a) in the callee's signature, (b) NOT documented locally, AND (c) documented on the donor -- so if the edit removes ONLY the one `@param <formal>` line, the ONE now-undocumented shared formal is the ONLY thing inherited. Donor choice therefore carries ZERO collision risk regardless of the donor's other formals** (e.g. `@inheritParams getParents` on `getOffspring(pedSourceDf, ids)` inherits only `ids`, never `pedSourceDf`; `@inheritParams calcGU` on `calcA` inherits only `threshold`, leaving `alleles`/`byID` locally documented). This kills the audit's "pick a minimal-signature donor" worry -- any function that documents the formal well is a safe donor. **(2) A formal shared by TWO meanings needs TWO donors -- never one.** `@param threshold` names an *integer allele-rarity max-copies count* in `calc*` (donor `calcGU`) AND a *numeric minimum-kinship level* in group formation (donor `filterThreshold`); merging them into one donor would silently mis-document one cluster. Census by DISTINCT description text (not just occurrence count) to catch this: 6 occ / 4 distinct split cleanly 3-and-3 across the two meanings. A single function (`getAnimalsWithHighKinship`) legitimately carries TWO `@inheritParams` tags (kmat from `meanKinship`, threshold from `filterThreshold`) -- roxygen allows it. **(3) De-dup only where meaning is truly generic; keep genuine variants bespoke.** `@param ids` = 24 occ / 15 distinct -> only 12 were the plain "character vector of animal IDs" (converted); the other 12 carry real callee-specific meaning ("or NULL to restrict the analysis", "to be flagged as part of the group/population", "original IDs", "to test") and were LEFT bespoke. `kmat` similarly: 5 generic -> `meanKinship`; 3 "dense, symmetric, id-named" / "proband" variants kept. The de-dup target is *drift*, not *count*. **(4) An `@family` tag on a `@noRd` function is INERT -- exclude it.** roxygen generates no `.Rd` for `@noRd`, so the family "See also" renders nowhere and the function never appears in siblings' family lists. `calcFounderContributions` (an internal `@noRd` helper) was dropped from the `genetic value analysis` family; only the 8 EXPORTED `calc*` got the tag. **(5) A multi-export file needs the `@family` tag on EVERY exported block, anchored on the function definition.** Each `mod*` file exports a UI *and* a Server function (18 blocks / 9 files), each its own `#'` block; the guarded apply script found each block by walking up from the `name <- function` line to its own `@export` and inserting `@family` there -- tagging only the first would drop half the family. The 4 `@noRd` helpers in `modPotentialParents.R` correctly got no tag (no `@export` to anchor on). **(6) `document()` IS the `@inheritParams` correctness gate.** Inheritance resolves at `document()` time; an unresolved/misspelled donor warns loudly. A clean `document()` + EMPTY NAMESPACE diff + `--as-cran` `code/documentation mismatches ... OK` together prove the de-dup introduced no doc/signature mismatch. **(7) Adversarial per-callee verification BEFORE editing pays off on subtle-semantics doc work.** A 24-agent read-only Workflow (one agent per proposed `@inheritParams`, reading the callee BODY + donor to judge donor-accuracy AND meaning-loss; one per family for membership + export structure) returned 20/20 CONFIRM and surfaced the `@noRd`-family-inert point, the ancestors-vs-relatives naming nuance, and two out-of-scope defects -- cheaper than a failed-gate round-trip or a shipped wrong description. **(8) Pass Workflow candidate data INLINE, not via `args`** -- a nested `args` object arrived `undefined` (`args.inherit.map` threw); embedding the plan as `const` in the script was robust. **(9) Surfaced but DEFERRED (scope discipline, FM #8):** `getPedDirectRelatives.R`'s description body + `@return` still say "Gets direct ancestors from labkey `study` schema" (wrong for a source-agnostic pedigree fn; title already fixed in 8a) -- a description-accuracy defect noted, NOT fixed mid-de-dup; likewise `reportGV` as a family anchor and the `@param ped` half left for their own sessions. **(10) Landing = PR-for-CI (owner gate):** 42 `man/` pages + the package's FIRST `@family` tags benefit from cross-platform Rd/cross-reference checks; local `--as-cran` is macOS-only. Do NOT merge unilaterally (FM #13). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S259, issue #103 Stage 8b de-dup -> branch `issue103-stage8b-dedup`, code commit `bafaa1e8`; 20 `@inheritParams` conversions / 19 files + 25 `@family` tags, 38 `R/` + 42 `.Rd`, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE `examples ... OK`, PR for CI)

#### Learning 245 -- De-dup of `@param ped` (Finding 6's big half) is CLUSTER-aware, not single-donor: the 46 distinct descriptions split by MEANING into (a) pure-phrasing-drift clusters -- generic "pedigree information" and "...IDs listed in `candidates`" -- and (b) requirement-bearing clusters -- required-fields (id/sire/dam), complete-pedigree (req. fields + "no partial parentage" / "stops with an error"), demographic (birth/death/departure/exit), and genuine one-offs (nullable, "not checked", cross-ref). Only the (a) clusters collapse safely to ONE donor each (`reportGV` for generic, `getPotentialSires` for candidates); the (b) descriptions carry real per-function information that a blind `@inheritParams` would ERASE, so they stay bespoke (owner scope-gate picked (a) only this session; (b) deferred). **REFACTOR-class doc de-dup -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (1 owner scope-gate). **(1) Adversarial per-callee verify catches MIS-clustered files that the census text alone would wave through.** `filterPairs.R` had copy-pasted the candidates text ("including the IDs listed in `candidates`") but has NO `candidates` formal and no candidate linkage in its body -- so pointing it at the candidates donor would assert something factually false. A 28-agent read-only workflow (reads each callee BODY + donor; judges donor-accuracy AND meaning-loss) returned 27 CONFIRM / 1 KEEP_BESPOKE, flagging exactly that file. Matching the census cluster is necessary but NOT sufficient; the body decides. **(2) The over-inheritance proof for a MULTI-param donor is the per-`.Rd` diff, not just the aggregate NAMESPACE diff.** `getPotentialSires` documents `ids` (via its own `@inheritParams getParents`), and 3 candidate callees have an `ids` formal not literally `@param`-documented -- a naive "undocumented formal the donor documents" check flags a collision. But those 3 already `@inheritParams getParents` (which supplies `ids` and wins by FIRST-MATCH order), so `ids` is safe. Proven empirically after `document()` by asserting every changed `.Rd` changed ONLY `\item{ped}` and that `\item{ids}` was UNCHANGED in those 3 files. This complements S259's "delete only the target `@param` line" rule (which prevents over-inherit for LOCALLY-documented params) by also covering the inherited-elsewhere case: keep the earlier `@inheritParams` first, and append the new one at the old `@param ped` position (which already sits after it). **(3) A source de-dup can render byte-identically -- 12 of 27 files here produced NO `.Rd` delta** because their old `@param ped` text already matched the donor exactly; the SOURCE still got cleaner (one `@inheritParams` vs a drifted `@param`). So the collapse proof is the SOURCE-level re-census (`@param ped` 67->40 occurrences / 46->33 distinct), not the count of changed man pages. **(4) `code/documentation mismatches ... OK` under `--as-cran` is the `@inheritParams` correctness gate** (an unresolved donor also warns at `document()` time); here it passed with examples/tests/Rd/vignettes all OK and the sole archived-maintainer NOTE.

#### Learning 246 -- A roxygen `@param` description CONTINUES across blank `#'` lines (they are paragraph breaks, not block terminators) until the NEXT `@tag` -- so a byte-identical census that stops at the first blank line SILENTLY MERGES a caveat-bearing description into its caveat-free base cluster, hiding exactly the requirement-bearing text that MUST stay bespoke. Owner scope-gate = "byte-identical clusters only" of issue #103 **Stage 8b-`ped` continued** (the requirement-bearing clusters S259/S260 deferred). **REFACTOR-class doc de-dup -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty, 0 `man/` changes); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates: scope; landing = direct-merge). **(1) The census bug that nearly corrupted two GVA functions.** A first-pass census extractor broke each `@param ped` block at the first blank `#'` line; that made `calcFEFG` (base + blank + "The pedigree must have no partial parentage ...; `calcFEFG` stops with an error otherwise") and `calcRetention` (base + blank + "It is assumed that the pedigree has no partial parentage") normalize IDENTICAL to the caveat-free base (`calcFE` / `calcFounderContributions`) and mis-cluster as "byte-identical" -- a blind `@inheritParams calcFE` would have DELETED both caveats (the exact corruption the handoff warned of). Fix the extractor to continue through blank `#'` lines, stopping only at the next `@tag`/non-roxygen line (corrected census: 40 occ / 33 distinct, was 31); and READ the actual block before editing as the backstop -- that read is what caught it here, before any edit. **(2) SHA1-equal clusters are the SAFEST possible collapse -- proof is ZERO `man/` delta.** Chose only the 3 SHA1-equal clusters (C3a sire+dam x4 donor `trimPedigree`; C5-base req-fields x2 donor `calcFE`; C4 id-only x2 donor `setPopulation`); after `document()`, git showed ONLY the 5 `R/` files changed with `man/` AND NAMESPACE UNCHANGED. Each rendering inheritor's `\item{ped}` renders byte-for-byte from the donor, so its `.Rd` is untouched -> the SOURCE re-census (`@param ped` 40->35) is the collapse proof, not any man-page diff (cf. Learning 245 (3)). **(3) `@inheritParams DONOR` works cleanly INTO an `@noRd` function (rendered-invisible, source-only de-dup) but an `@noRd` function is a POOR DONOR (no `.Rd` to inherit from) -- flip the direction.** C4's `resetGroup` is `@noRd` -> made the exported `setPopulation` the donor; C5-base's `calcFounderContributions` is `@noRd` -> inherits from the exported `calcFE`. `document()` clean, no orphan `.Rd`, no warning; 2 of the 5 collapses (calcFounderContributions, resetGroup) are therefore source-only (no rendered benefit, still valid drift-reduction). **(4) A donor that documents ONLY the shared formal is over-inheritance-proof by construction** (`calcFE` documents `ped` alone); for multi-param donors (`trimPedigree`: probands/ped/removeUninformative/addBackParents; `setPopulation`: ped/ids) the S259 "delete ONLY the target `@param` line" rule + locally-documented-wins keeps inheritance to `ped` alone -- PROVEN by the empty `man/` diff (no new `\item{}` appeared). **(5) A factual scope correction is NOT a re-gate.** The owner's decision was "byte-identical clusters only"; the census bug meant C5-base/C4 delivered 1 inheritor each (not the gate's stated 3+1) because calcFEFG/calcRetention were never byte-identical. Excluding them is REQUIRED by the owner's own criterion -- reported transparently and proceeded without a redundant `AskUserQuestion` (FM #7/#23; [[observation-vs-decision]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S262, issue #103 Stage 8b-`ped` continued -> direct-merge to `master`; 5 `@inheritParams` byte-identical collapses / 5 `R/`, 0 `man/` changes, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, lint 0, spell clean)

#### Learning 247 -- Prose similarity is NOT semantic similarity: a near-identical `@param` description can itself be a COPY-PASTE bug, so READ THE BODY before collapsing a handoff-suggested "near-identical pair." This generalizes Learning 246 (which fixed the census *extractor*) up one level to the *handoff's own cluster claims*: a predecessor's "collapsible pair" is a hypothesis to verify against the code, not a fact to execute. Owner scope-gate (via `AskUserQuestion`) = "id/sire/dam cluster (3)" of issue #103 **Stage 8b-`ped` continued** (the phrasing-variant + demographic clusters S262 deferred). **REFACTOR-class doc de-dup -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff empty); TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates: scope; landing = direct-merge). **(1) The C6 "demographic pair" was a mirage.** S262's handoff said `createPedTree` + `setExit` were "a near-identical pair (1-word Oxford-comma diff -> collapsible with trivial normalization)" -- their `@param ped` prose IS near-identical. But `createPedTree`'s BODY uses only `id`, `sire`, `dam` (its own line-21 comment: "This function uses only id, sire, and dam columns"; zero `birth`/`death`/`departure`/`exit` references), whereas `setExit` genuinely computes `exit` from `death`/`departure`. So `createPedTree`'s demographic `@param ped` is copy-paste drift FROM `setExit`, and collapsing the "pair" would have CEMENTED the wrong description onto `createPedTree`. Reading the body refuted the pair and turned it into a surfaced defect (fix `createPedTree` to id/sire/dam in its own session). Lesson: treat a predecessor's cluster claim exactly like a census cluster -- necessary signal, but the body decides (cf. Learning 245 (1)). **(2) A phrasing-variant collapse CHANGES the callee's rendered text to the donor's, so it DOES produce `man/` churn (unlike S262's byte-identical, 0-`man/`-delta collapses) -- and the collapse can double as a bug fix.** The 3 collapses (`convertRelationships`, `getFounders`, `removeAutoGenIds` -> `@inheritParams getDescendantPedigree`) each rewrote one `\item{ped}` to the donor's canonical text; `removeAutoGenIds`'s rewrite also corrected its "dame" -> "dam" typo. The over-inheritance proof is still the per-`.Rd` diff (ONLY `\item{ped}` changed; `\item{kmat}`/`\item{ids}` intact and in order) plus the source re-census (`@param ped` 35->32). **(3) A SECOND `@inheritParams` supplies an undocumented shared formal only if no EARLIER `@inheritParams` documents it (first-match wins).** `convertRelationships` already had `@inheritParams meanKinship` (for `kmat`); before adding `@inheritParams getDescendantPedigree` at the old `@param ped` position, verified `meanKinship` documents ONLY `kmat` -- so the new tag cleanly supplies `ped` and `getDescendantPedigree`'s other formal `probands` (not a `convertRelationships` arg) is ignored. Check the donor is EXPORTED (has an `.Rd` to inherit from) -- `getDescendantPedigree` is; `getPedigreeSource`/`unknown2NA` are `@noRd` and were correctly excluded/kept-bespoke. **(4) Right-size the verify + the landing to the churn.** A 3-collapse slice warranted a 3-agent read-only refuter Workflow (not the 24-28-agent fleets of S259/S260) -- one adversarial agent per proposed collapse, all 3 CONFIRM. And 3 pure-prose `.Rd` changes (NAMESPACE-neutral, `Rd cross-references ... OK` locally) warrant DIRECT-MERGE (S262 low-churn precedent), not PR-for-CI (which S259/S260 reserved for 42-52-`.Rd` churn / executing examples). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S263, issue #103 Stage 8b-`ped` continued -> direct-merge to `master`; 3 `@inheritParams getDescendantPedigree` phrasing-variant collapses / 3 `R/` + 3 `.Rd`, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 1 NOTE with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, lint 0, spell clean; C6 pair refuted, createPedTree + removeDuplicates surfaced)

#### Learning 248 -- A duplicate function-definition `.R` file (two `@export`ed roxygen blocks defining/documenting the SAME object) is INVISIBLE in NAMESPACE but shows up as a DOUBLED `.Rd`: roxygen2 silently MERGES the two same-topic blocks, so the man page cites BOTH source files on line 2 (`% Please edit documentation in R/getPotentialSires.R, R/getSimSires.R`) and renders `\usage`/`\value`/`\description`/`\examples` TWICE. Deleting the redundant file + `document()` collapses each to one copy with an EMPTY NAMESPACE diff -- roxygen dedupes the two `@export`s to a single `export()` line, so removing one file never moves NAMESPACE (predicted, then proven: sha `2560cecd...` identical before/after). Owner approach-gate + landing-gate via `AskUserQuestion` on issue #103's last NON-doc-only surfaced item. **REFACTOR-class dead-duplicate removal -- zero behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (2 owner gates: approach = delete + re-document; landing = direct-merge). **(1) The doubled `.Rd` is the tell of a duplicate function-definition file -- and a latent issue #109 doc defect.** The rendered help page for `getPotentialSires` showed its usage, value, description, and full example block twice (line 2 named `R/getPotentialSires.R, R/getSimSires.R`); the fix (delete the dup, re-document) reduced the `.Rd` to a single clean copy -- so grepping `man/*.Rd` line-2 comments for entries that cite two source files is a cheap detector for the whole class. **(2) Deletion-safety for a duplicate `.R` is a five-part FIRSTHAND chain, not a trust of the handoff's "likely delete".** (a) `diff` the two files -> the function BODY is byte-identical (only cosmetic roxygen whitespace + a `1L` vs `1` example differ), so source load order is immaterial to runtime; (b) the surviving file is the CANONICAL one (name matches the function, owns the `.Rd`, uses the correct integer `1L`); (c) every reference across `R/` + `tests/` + vignettes + `inst/_pkgdown.yml` is to the FUNCTION `getPotentialSires` (survives in `getPotentialSires.R`), NONE to the file -- a bare-string sweep for `getSimSires` hit only `SESSION_NOTES.md`; (d) NAMESPACE diff empty; (e) the file is committed/tracked (SAFEGUARDS "verify committed before rm") with no `source()`/`Collate` path reference. **(3) "Was this file ever a REAL distinct function that got clobbered?" needs `git log --all -S "<name> <- function"`, not just the working tree.** The file is named `getSimSires.R` -> a reader might fear it once held a real `getSimSires` (breeding-sim, cf. issue #10) that a bad copy-paste overwrote, which would argue for RESTORE not DELETE. `git log --all -S "getSimSires <- function"` returned EMPTY across all history -> the function NEVER existed; the filename was aspirational. Nothing to restore -> delete is unambiguously correct. **(4) A non-doc-only deletion reuses the Learning 239 verify triad: empty NAMESPACE diff + single-file `man/` diff + INSTALLED-namespace smoke (not `load_all`).** Confirmed `document()` touched ONLY `getPotentialSires.Rd` (no downstream `@inheritParams getPotentialSires` churn -- the ~10 callees' `.Rd` were untouched because the merged param text was unaffected), full test suite 0/0 (1334 contexts; `test_groupAddAssign` + `test_makeGroupMembers` are the getPotentialSires regression gate), `--as-cran` 0/0/2 with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, and -- against the `.Rcheck` INSTALLED namespace -- `getPotentialSires` still exported + callable end-to-end (returned a length-3 vector), `getSimSires` correctly absent. **(5) Right-size: a fully DETERMINISTIC evidence chain (diff + grep + git-log + build gate) does NOT need an adversarial Workflow.** The load-bearing claim ("safe to delete") is proven by byte-level diff + NAMESPACE identity + installed smoke, not a probabilistic finding needing independent refutation (cf. Learning 239's deterministic import proof); prior sessions right-sized the same way. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class dead-duplicate-removal session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S268, issue #103 -> direct-merge to `master`; deleted `R/getSimSires.R` [byte-identical duplicate of `getPotentialSires`], regenerated `getPotentialSires.Rd` collapsed 2x->1x, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2 with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, lint 0, spell clean, installed-namespace smoke OK)

#### Learning 249 -- A dormant, never-exercised parameter branch is NOT a safe no-op: RUN it before trusting either a "has no effect" characterization OR a predecessor's one-line suggested fix -- both are hypotheses, and strict TDD's RED step is what falsifies them. `getPedDirectRelatives(unrelatedParents = TRUE)` was flagged by S269 as "no effect (same as `FALSE`); GREEN = assign the discarded `addIdRecords()` return." Running it on a pedigree with a referenced-but-absent parent (`Z` named as a dam with no ego record) falsified BOTH: it CRASHES (`replacement has 1 row, data has 0`), and assigning the return errors at the SAME line -- because `addIdRecords()` retrieves rows FROM `fullPed` (`fullPed[fullPed$id %in% ids, ]`), and here `fullPed == ped` with the `unrelated` ids absent from `ped` BY CONSTRUCTION, so the retrieval is always 0 rows and `addToPed$sire <- NA` fails on a 0-row frame. First STRICT-TDD behavior fix of the long #103 doc stretch. **Behavior fix to an exported fn -- RED -> GREEN; REFACTOR none needed; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: open issue #114 first; approach = implement the documented placeholder behavior; PRE-RED->RED, RED->GREEN, GREEN->REFACTOR; NEWS = yes; landing = PR-for-CI). **(1) A predecessor's suggested GREEN for dead code is a HYPOTHESIS; RED is what falsifies it.** S269's "assign the return" was plausible from reading the branch statically, but the branch had never run. Writing the RED test FIRST surfaced the crash AND proved the naive fix wrong BEFORE any implementation -- the exact payoff of tests-first on a never-executed path. This lifts Learning 247 (a handoff's *cluster* claim is a hypothesis to verify against the body) from doc de-dup to behavior work: the handoff's *fix* is a hypothesis too. **(2) The right fix SYNTHESIZES rows; do not reuse a helper built for a different shape.** `addIdRecords()` is designed for a TWO-TIER case (a `fullPed` strictly larger than `partialPed`, ids PRESENT in `fullPed`) -- its only real caller `addBackSecondParents.R:51` passes such ids and it is correct there. In `getPedDirectRelatives` there is ONE `ped` and the unrelated ids exist nowhere, so no retrieval helper can produce them; the branch must CONSTRUCT placeholder rows (`relatives[rep(NA_integer_, n), ]` for an all-NA row with the right columns, then `$id <- unrelated`, `$sire`/`$dam <- NA`, `rbind`, reset rownames). Leave the correct-for-its-own-caller helper untouched (scope discipline, FM #8). **(3) Running the actual function IS the proof -- no adversarial Workflow needed for a deterministic crash.** A ~20-line REPL probe under `pkgload::load_all` (the `FALSE` result, the `TRUE` result via `tryCatch`, the simulated "assign the return", and the documented TARGET output) established every load-bearing claim deterministically; a fan-out of skeptic agents adds nothing over executing the code (right-sizing, cf. Learning 248 (5)). **(4) A behavior change to an exported fn takes the FULL non-doc gate + Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke + NEWS + PR-for-CI** -- unlike the S262-S269 doc-only direct-merges. Verified: RED (2 `test_that` added; the placeholder test FAILED with the documented error, the no-unrelated-parents guard passed), GREEN (both pass), full suite 0 real failed / 0 real errors (1166 real contexts, `NOT_CRAN=true`), `lintr` 0, `spell_check_package` clean, NAMESPACE diff EMPTY (signature unchanged), `--as-cran` 0/0/2 with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK` + a re-grepped `testthat.R [66s/66s] OK`, and a `.Rcheck` installed-namespace smoke (imports enforced, NOT `load_all`) returning the `Z` placeholder row with NA parents. NEWS.Rmd dev-version bullet added + NEWS.md regenerated (`\#114` matches the file's existing escaped issue-ref style).
