Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a STRICT-TDD behavior-fix session -- RED -> GREEN executed; REFACTOR = none.** (S270, issue #114 -> branch `fix/issue-114-unrelated-parents`, fix commit `4bbfb32a`, PR #115 for CI; `R/getPedDirectRelatives.R` synthesize-placeholders + 2 tests + NEWS, NAMESPACE empty diff, gate 0/0/2, lint 0, spell clean, Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke OK)

#### Learning 250 -- `gh pr merge --rebase --delete-branch` run from the PR branch does the REMOTE merge FIRST (the PR merges and a "Fixes #NN" issue closes immediately), THEN tries to switch off the branch to delete it locally -- so ANY uncommitted working-tree file aborts that second step (`local changes to <file> would be overwritten by checkout`) and leaves BOTH the local and remote branch undeleted **with the merge already applied**. The merge is NOT rolled back by the failed delete. Owner-directed landing of PR #115 (the S270 issue #114 fix); merge method owner-gated (`AskUserQuestion`) = Rebase. **Landing/merge of already-CI-certified code -- no new `R/` change, no local re-gate (10/10 green CI + a proven byte-identical tree ARE the certification); TDD N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) Ensure a FULLY clean tree before invoking `gh pr merge --delete-branch`, not just the known standing-keep.** This session self-inflicted the abort: `.DS_Store` (the tracked standing keep) was stashed pre-emptively for the rebase-pull, but the S271 SESSION_NOTES **stub** written moments earlier on the same branch was NOT -- and that dirty file is what blocked the post-merge checkout. Stash/commit EVERYTHING (or run the merge from a clean state) first. **(2) If it aborts, VERIFY the merge before any git surgery, then finish cleanup by hand.** `gh pr view <N> --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit` + `gh issue view <NN> --json state` confirmed MERGED + CLOSED before I touched the tree ([[check-status-before-destructive-git]]) -- do not assume the whole command failed just because its last step did. Then: stash the dirty file, `git checkout master`, `git pull` (fast-forward to the merge commit), pop the stashes back (carefully, preserving any UNRELATED stash lower in the stack), and complete the `--delete-branch` intent manually. **(3) `delete_branch_on_merge=false` at repo level means NOTHING auto-deletes** (`gh api repos/OWNER/REPO --jq .delete_branch_on_merge`) -- the branch must be deleted explicitly: `git push origin --delete <branch>` (remote) + `git branch -D <branch>` (local). **(4) A REBASE-merge gives the landed commits NEW, non-ancestor SHAs**, so the local feature branch is not an ancestor of `master` and `git branch -d` (safe) REFUSES it; use `git branch -D` (force) -- but ONLY after proving the content is on master: `git diff <branch> <mergeCommit> --stat` must be EMPTY (identical trees; the rebase preserved content, only re-parented it). Right-sized verification: a merge of tree-identical, CI-certified code needs a Phase-3E runtime smoke on the merged tree (ran `test_getPedDirectRelatives.R`, all pass), NOT a redundant local `--as-cran`. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. **This was a landing/merge session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S271, issue #114 -> PR #115 rebase-merged to `master` @ `79125a0b` [fix `ce9edad0`], issue #114 CLOSED, branch deleted local + remote, Phase-3E smoke on merged master OK)

#### Learning 251 -- In a roxygen2 **markdown-mode** package (`Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)`), a roxygen line preceded by a blank `#'` line and indented 4+ spaces becomes a markdown **code block** -> the generated `.Rd` wraps it in `\preformatted{}`; any raw Rd markup the author *also* put on that line (`\code{...}`, `\link{...}`, `\strong{...}`) then renders **literally** (verbatim), because markup is not processed inside `\preformatted{}`. That is issue #109's error class (`R/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.R:11` -> `man/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.Rd`: the man page showed the text `\code{available[[i]] <- setdiff(...)}` instead of the code). **REFACTOR-class doc rendering fix -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (3 owner gates: audit scope = confirmed-class-only, fix form = drop the `\code{}` wrapper, landing = direct-merge). **(1) Fix = DROP the redundant `\code{}` wrapper** and leave the indented line as a plain code block (markdown-mode idiom); do NOT un-indent it -- that preserves the author's intent to *display* the snippet. **(2) Audit the class from the GENERATED `.Rd`, not the R/ source.** The authoritative detector brace-matches each `\preformatted{...}` block in `man/*.Rd` and flags any `\code`/`\link`/`\strong`/... inside -> exactly **1** hit across all 203 `.Rd`. A grep of R/ source for indented `\code{}` lines OVER-REPORTS badly (~40 hits) because `\item \code{}` list entries and wrapped `@param`/`@return` prose are ALSO indented but render correctly -- they are not blank-line-preceded code blocks. The rendered artifact is the arbiter: a real instance of the class appears inside `\preformatted{}` in the `.Rd`; for 39 of the 40 source hits it does not. **(3) `tools::checkRd()` run standalone reports a "Non-ASCII contents without declared encoding" note that is a FALSE POSITIVE when `DESCRIPTION` declares `Encoding: UTF-8`** -- `R CMD check --as-cran` reads the encoding and passes (clean per S270), so do not chase it. **(4) Right-sized:** a deterministic, artifact-proven finding (brace-matched detector + empty NAMESPACE diff + `--as-cran` Rd checks) needs no adversarial Workflow -- a Workflow is for probabilistic/semantic audits, not a mechanical class the generated file settles exactly (owner picked confirmed-class-only over the offered broad multi-agent audit). Sibling of Learning 248 (a reusable detector for a distinct roxygen documentation-error class). (S272: issue #109 markup-in-preformatted fix; direct-merge to `master`; `--as-cran` 0/0/2 GREEN)

#### Learning 252 -- **`R CMD check --as-cran`/`checkRd`/`document()` cannot catch factually-wrong-but-valid-Rd documentation; only a doc-vs-code READ can -- and the dominant source is copy-paste drift between SIBLING functions.** Issue #109's second clause ("audit the code for similar documentation errors") was answered by a broad multi-agent semantic audit of all 203 man pages that found **38 confirmed doc errors, every one of which passes the full build gate cleanly** (3 Critical / 22 Moderate / 13 Minor; 15 factual-mismatch, 12 wrong-return, 10 wrong-param, 1 misleading-render). Owner scope-gate (`AskUserQuestion`) = "Broad audit". **Analysis-only, REFACTOR-class; no `R/` modified; fixes deferred to an owner-gated follow-on; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) The deterministic layer is a red herring for #109 -- clear it FIRST, then read.** A pre-pass proved every mechanically-decidable class clean: all 8 S244 defects D1-D8 fixed; the #109 markup-in-`\preformatted{}` class = 0 (S272 holds); `\link` xrefs 100% resolve (206 `\alias` topics); doubled-`.Rd` = 0; empty `\code{}` = 0; data-doc `\item` columns == actual `names()`; and crucially **`devtools::document()` = zero warnings + zero man/ drift**, so the LITERAL reading of the issue title ("roxygen2 errors in functions within R") is already false -- the reported bug was a *rendering* error (fixed S272), and the real remaining surface is *semantic accuracy*, which no tool checks. Doing this pre-pass first (a) prevented re-reporting known-fixed items and (b) scoped the expensive agent fan-out to the one class that needs judgment. **(2) The root cause is systemic copy-paste, not 38 independent typos -- so fix the PATTERN.** Prose describes a *sibling* function or an *earlier* version of the code: a `getPotentialSires` `@param ped`/`@param minAge` donor bug that cascades to `calculateSexRatio`/`removePotentialSires` via `@inheritParams` (4 findings, ONE donor edit); `checkChangedColsLst`/`checkErrorLst` (logical-scalar returns documented as "NULL / the list"); `correctParentSex`/`removeDuplicates` (`reportErrors=TRUE` alternate return undocumented); `kinshipMatricesToKValues`/`kinshipMatrixToKValues` (identical wrong "diagonal = 1.0" -- this package's `kinship()` uses coancestry, self-kinship `(1+F)/2` = 0.5); `saveDataframesAsFiles`/`makeExamplePedigreeFile` (both document `fileType="xlsx"` but `stopifnot` accepts `"excel"`); `getPedMaxAge`/`getPyramidAgeDist` (both claim "live animals only" but neither filters); `makeCEPH` (documents the relationship cascade that lives in its caller `convertRelationships`). Several findings were confirmed by RUNNING the documented example (`addAnimalsWithNoRelative` returns a list not a df; `offspringCounts` has no `id` column; `geneDrop` column order reversed) -- the running function is the arbiter, extending Learning 251's "the generated `.Rd` is the arbiter". **(3) THIS is where an adversarial Workflow earns its keep -- unlike the deterministic Learnings 248/251.** A single detector settles a mechanical class exactly (248/251, rightly done solo); a "audit for *similar* errors" mandate spans an open-ended SEMANTIC space no detector covers, so it needs fan-out breadth + adversarial skepticism: 17 find-batches (~12 man pages each, doc read vs. source/body) -> per-finding verify (default-REFUTE, read the actual files) -> session-author firsthand re-verification of all 3 Critical + a cross-class Moderate/Minor sample + the 1 refuted (`getRequiredCols` -- correctly refuted: "(optional)" qualifies the *value* form "Date or NA", not column-omissibility; that the SAME pass confirmed the near-identical `\code{N}`-typo in `getPossibleCols` while refuting this shows the calibration held). 56 agents, 0 errors, 39 candidates -> 38 confirmed / 1 refuted (2.6% refute rate = well-scoped finders + same-bar verify, corroborated by the author sample holding in every case). **(4) Do-the-delta ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]): read the prior audit BEFORE fanning out.** `docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md` (S244) + the #103 work already covered *harmonization* (voice, `@param` de-dup, ordering) and its rendered defects D1-D8; #109's lens is *errors*, a DIFFERENT axis. The agents were handed the already-clean deterministic results + the KEEP-BESPOKE / harmonization exclusions so they reported only genuine reader-facing errors, not #103 style nits. **(5) Workflow tooling gotcha: the `args` value reaches the script as a JSON STRING, not a parsed array** -- slicing it silently yields character-chunks (a first run failed instantly with "batch.map is not a function", 878 "batches" from a 10527-char string). Parse defensively at the top: `const ROWS = typeof args === 'string' ? JSON.parse(args) : args`. [[workflow-args-arrive-as-json-string]]. **(6) FM #22 caught in the wild:** mid-session `git status` showed `.gitignore` + `inst/WORDLIST` modified -- NOT mine; a transcript grep (no agent touched them) + mtimes (21:09-21:11, before the workflow ran) proved they were the OWNER's manual edits (the two small S272-suggested items: build-artifact gitignore rules + the `errored` WORDLIST hand-add). Left untouched; committed only the audit deliverable by explicit path ([[check-status-before-destructive-git]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was an AUDIT-class analysis session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A; the 38 fixes are a separate owner-gated deliverable.** (S273, issue #109 broad audit -> `docs/audits/ISSUE_109_DOC_ERROR_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md`; 38 confirmed / 1 refuted; no `R/` change; deterministic pre-pass all-clean; multi-agent semantic sweep + adversarial verify + author firsthand re-verification)

#### Learning 253 -- **An `@inheritParams` donor fix corrects INHERITED params but NOT a callee's LOCAL duplicate of the same param -- so after a donor edit, grep for local copies the audit's "callees fix themselves" cascade analysis will miss.** Fixing the 38 S273-audited doc errors (owner scope-gate via one `AskUserQuestion`, 4 questions: all-in-one / doc-fix-direction / defer #9 to #110 / direct-merge). **REFACTOR-class, doc-only; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** 34 `R/` files → 38 `man/*.Rd` (the extra 4 = the donor cascade). **(1) The cascade discovery.** Fixing `getPotentialSires` `@param ped` (`candidates`→`ids`, #34) propagated via `@inheritParams` to SIX pages -- `calculateSexRatio` (#26), `removePotentialSires` (#32), AND three the audit never flagged (`groupAddAssign`, `fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio`, `makeGroupMembers`). But the audit's §4 "the `@inheritParams` callees fix themselves" was WRONG for one param: `removePotentialSires` carried a **LOCAL** `@param minAge` (lines 7-9) with the same stale "group formation / kinships ignored" text as #11; a local `@param` OVERRIDES the inherited one, so the donor fix could not reach it -- the audit enumerated the ped instance (#32) but missed the sibling minAge. Deleting the local duplicate so it inherits the corrected donor is the pattern fix the audit intended, and it is a **39th** fix beyond the 38 findings. **Lesson: a donor fix is only complete after a completeness grep proves no callee shadows the donor with a local copy.** **(2) Distinguish genuine pattern-instances from legitimate uses in that grep.** `grep 'minimum age to consider in group'` hit 9 files; only the 2 non-group-formation siblings (`getPotentialSires`, `removePotentialSires`) were wrong -- the other 7 ARE group-formation/kinship functions where the wording is accurate (the thorough audit correctly did not flag them). Same for `xlsx` (correct in file-*readers*, wrong only as a documented `fileType` *value*), "live animals" (correct in `calcGU`), `POSIXct`/`YYYYMMDD` (correct in real date functions). The grep finds candidates; the code decides which are bugs -- do not blanket-fix a matched string. **(3) The find→apply→verify Workflow shape for a batch of well-specified mechanical edits** (vs. Learning 252's open-ended semantic *audit*): (a) a parallel read-only fan-out (one agent per source file) returns byte-exact edit specs -- each anchor copied verbatim, verified against the function body; (b) a SINGLE controller (me) applies them with a strict exact-match script (`content.count(anchor) == 1` or ABORT that edit and report) -- keeps tree ownership (SAFEGUARDS) and makes a paraphrased anchor fail loudly instead of clobbering; agents do NOT edit in parallel (same-file races; worktree-per-file + merge is infeasible when `man/` regenerates once at the end); (c) a parallel adversarial re-verify (one skeptic per finding reads the REGENERATED `.Rd` against the code) → 37/37 CONFIRMED_FIXED, 0 problems, and it correctly left pre-existing #103-class typos (`kinshipMatricies`, `columms`, "length on") untouched. `--as-cran`'s `code/documentation mismatches … OK` is a necessary but WEAK check (it only catches `@param`-vs-signature and `\usage` drift, not prose-vs-behavior); the doc-vs-code re-read is what actually certifies a semantic fix. **(4) Clear a `spell_check_package()` flag by wrapping the identifier in `\code{}`, not by editing WORDLIST** -- `spelling` skips `\code{}`/`\item{name}` content (that is why the package's hundreds of `\code{camelCase}` identifiers never trip it), so `ignoreHerm` and `proc` (from `proc.time()`) cleared once wrapped, which is ALSO the correct doc style. This avoided touching `inst/WORDLIST`, which carried the owner's uncommitted `errored` addition (FM #22) -- a WORDLIST edit would have forced a bundle-or-ask decision; the `\code{}` fix sidestepped it entirely. **(5) FM #22 held:** committed only my files by explicit path; left the owner's `.gitignore` (build-artifact rules -- which also silently gitignored this session's tarball/`.Rcheck`) and `inst/WORDLIST` untouched. **Phase-3E N/A (stated):** doc-only, empty NAMESPACE diff, no runtime surface. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[workflow-args-arrive-as-json-string]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (S274, issue #109 fix 37/38 findings + 1 audit-missed sibling; direct-merge to `master`; `--as-cran` 0/0/1 GREEN; 37/37 adversarial verify)

#### Learning 254 -- **A finding deferred to another open issue is often NOT actually blocked by it -- before re-deferring, test whether the defer-reason blocks the *minimal correct* fix, not the *ideal final* one.** Finishing issue #109 by fixing its one remaining finding (**#9 `runModularApp`**, which S274 deferred to #110) and CLOSING the issue (owner scope-gate via one `AskUserQuestion` = "Fix #9 now + close #109", over close-only / fix-without-closing). **REFACTOR-class, doc-only; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** 1 `R/` line → 1 `man/*.Rd`. **(1) The defer-reason didn't block the minimal fix.** `runModularApp`'s `@details` said "Use `\code{\link{runGeneKeepR}}` to run the original monolithic version" -- false today (`runGeneKeepR()` is a `lifecycle::deprecate_soft` alias whose body just calls `runModularApp()`; its own doc says the monolithic app was retired). S274/the audit deferred it to #110 because a full *replacement wording* might depend on #110's rename decision (rename `runModularApp`→`runGeneKeepR`?). But the sentence is wrong REGARDLESS of #110, and stating the current fact ("`runGeneKeepR()` is a soft-deprecated alias for this function" -- the audit's own recommended fix, mirroring `runGeneKeepR`'s reciprocal `@seealso`) neither presumes the deprecation persists nor blocks a future rename (a rename rewrites this doc anyway, as part of #110). **Lesson: distinguish "the ideal final wording is blocked" from "any correct fix is blocked" -- the latter is what actually justifies deferring; a stale FALSEHOOD should be corrected to today's reality now, not left standing while an unrelated design question waits.** **(2) Run the `R CMD build`/`check --as-cran` gate from the PACKAGE ROOT, not the scratchpad -- this repo uses renv.** Backgrounding the gate from the session scratchpad failed instantly at the vignette-install step (`dependencies 'anytime','bslib','data.table',… are not available`): with no `.Rprofile` in cwd, renv never activated, so `.libPaths()` pointed at a bootstrap library with nothing installed. `.Rbuildignore` excludes `renv`/`renv.lock`/`.Rprofile` (so the built tarball stays clean), but the build/check subprocess still needs the deps, which only the repo-root `.Rprofile`→renv library (`~/Library/Caches/org.R-project.R/.../renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-…`) provides. Fix: `( cd <repo-root> && R CMD build . && R CMD check --as-cran nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz )`; the tarball + `.Rcheck` land in the repo root but are now gitignored (owner `6d445e0d`). Prior sessions ran it there implicitly; this makes the renv-cwd dependency explicit so no session repeats the failed scratchpad run. **(3) Close-out completeness for a multi-clause/batch issue:** #109 had three clauses (reported rendering bug S272 / "audit for similar errors" S273 / fix all 38 findings S274+S275); closing was correct ONLY after the LAST finding was fixed + gated -- verified all 38 addressed (37 S274 + #9 here + the 39th sibling S274) before closing. The *naming* question (#9's cousin) correctly lives on as a SEPARATE issue (#110); closing #109 does not swallow it. **Verify (firsthand):** `document()` touched only the 2 intended files; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; `lintr` 0 (new line 75 ≤80); `spell_check_package` clean (no WORDLIST churn); `--as-cran` GREEN 0/0/2 with `code/documentation mismatches … OK` + `Rd cross-references … OK` (the `\link{runGeneKeepR}` target still resolves) + `testthat.R [67s/67s] OK`. **Phase-3E N/A (stated):** function body unchanged, empty NAMESPACE diff, no runtime surface. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class doc-fix session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S275, issue #109 fix finding #9 `runModularApp` + CLOSE #109; direct-merge to `master`; `--as-cran` 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff; lint 0; spell clean)

#### Learning 255 -- **Reversing a `lifecycle` deprecation is a clean, backward-compatible SWAP when both names stay exported -- and STRICT-TDD-able if you make the RED tests genuinely flip.** Issue #110 questioned the 2.0.0 decision to deprecate the informative `runGeneKeepR()` in favor of the low-information `runModularApp()`. Owner design-gate (`AskUserQuestion`) = "Reverse, keep both names" (over reverse-and-REMOVE-runModularApp / keep-as-is): `runGeneKeepR()` becomes the canonical, undeprecated function holding the `shiny::shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` + `runApp` body; `runModularApp()` becomes a `deprecate_soft(when="2.0.0", what="runModularApp()", with="runGeneKeepR()")` alias forwarding to it. **BEHAVIOR/API change (deprecation direction reversed) -> STRICT TDD, each phase gated; direct-merge landing owner-gated; 0 corrections / 0 overrides.** **(1) The swap is backward-compatible and NAMESPACE-neutral.** Both functions stay `@export`ed, so the NAMESPACE diff is EMPTY and existing `runModularApp()` callers keep working (soft warning, no hard break); only the `@importFrom shiny shinyApp runApp` moved from the alias's roxygen to the canonical function's (package-level, so no NAMESPACE change). Un-deprecating a recently-soft-deprecated function is low-risk. **(2) RED-test design for FLIPPING a deprecation -- two traps.** (a) A naive "runGeneKeepR() is NOT deprecated" test using `expect_no_warning(..., class="lifecycle_warning_deprecated")` PASSES TRIVIALLY against the still-deprecated current code, because `deprecate_soft` is SILENT from a non-global/test caller -- so it is not a valid RED signal. Fix: wrap the call in `withr::local_options(lifecycle_verbosity = "warning")` to FORCE the soft-deprecation to warn, so the assertion actually fails RED against current code and passes only once the deprecate call is removed. (b) The strongest RED signal is the DELEGATION DIRECTION: mock the sibling (`local_mocked_bindings(runGeneKeepR = function(...) "GK")`) and assert `runModularApp()` returns the sentinel -- currently it returns the shiny sentinel (calls shiny directly), so it fails RED cleanly. (c) Mock BOTH `shiny::shinyApp`+`shiny::runApp` (`.package="shiny"`) AND the sibling delegate in EVERY test, so that whichever code path runs (pre- or post-change), no real Shiny server launches during the RED run -- a failing test must fail fast, not hang. `local_mocked_bindings(.package="shiny")` DOES intercept explicit `shiny::fn()` calls (it rebinds the namespace that `::` reads). **(3) A rename is only DONE when the docs that NAME the function are updated too (FM #13 literal-minimum).** Left as-is, README/vignettes/NEWS would tell users to call the now-deprecated name, contradicting the code. The shared `vignettes/manual_components/_running_shiny_application.Rmd` is a child chunk of BOTH `README.Rmd` and 2 vignettes (one edit, three surfaces); plus 9 `runModularApp()` mentions across 5 `vignettes/articles/*.qmd`, a `NEWS.Rmd` dev-version bullet (issue-ref `(#110)` -- the NEWS-issue-ref exception to [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]]), a `loadSiteConfig.R` doc `\link`, and CLAUDE.md. This also incidentally cleared the "for testing purposes" stale phrasing S275 flagged (runModularApp's roxygen was rewritten as a deprecation note). **(4) Two renv-env doc-render gotchas.** (a) `devtools::build_readme()` FAILS here -- it does a fresh clean `local_install` that aborts on missing deps; render directly instead: `pkgload::load_all("."); rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd", output_file="README.md")` (and same for `NEWS.Rmd`). (b) `rmarkdown::render` can leave a stray `README.html` byproduct in the working tree; if it is still there when `R CMD build .` snapshots the package, the tarball includes it and `--as-cran` raises a NON-STANDARD extra NOTE ("Non-standard file/directory found at top level: README.html"). Remove render byproducts BEFORE building (my first --as-cran run showed 3 NOTEs for exactly this; removing README.html and re-running returned the standing 0/0/2). **Verify (firsthand):** full suite 0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; `lintr` 0 on 3 changed R files; `spell_check_package` clean; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; **`--as-cran` (clean tree) GREEN 0/0/2** (standing benign pair) with `code/documentation mismatches … OK` + `Rd cross-references … OK` (both `\link` targets still resolve) + `testthat.R [68s/68s] OK`. **Phase-3E DONE (not N/A -- this was a behavior change):** the reversed contract runs against the built+installed package via testthat.R inside --as-cran, AND a direct `load_all` behavioral smoke confirmed runGeneKeepR() emits no deprecation while runModularApp() emits one whose message names runGeneKeepR(). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]. **This was a BEHAVIOR-change STRICT-TDD session (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated).** (S276, issue #110 reverse the deprecation -> `runGeneKeepR()` canonical / `runModularApp()` soft-deprecated alias; direct-merge to `master`; `--as-cran` 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff; issue #110 CLOSED)

#### Learning 256 -- **A doc-audit "candidate code-fix" can be a REAL behavior bug hiding behind a doc that was reconciled to the buggy code; decide which of doc/code is authoritative from the SURROUNDING intent, then fix under STRICT TDD.** Issue #109's audit finding #31 flagged `makeSimPed()`'s `@description` as a doc-vs-code mismatch; S274 (doc-only) "fixed" it by rewriting the DOC to match the code ("Existing known parents are overwritten"). This session (owner-picked from the S276 candidate list) asked whether the CODE was the defect. It was: `makeSimPed`'s loop assigned each listed id's sire/dam UNCONDITIONALLY (`sample(vec,1L)`, or `NA` for an empty vector), never checking whether the existing parent was already known -- so an animal with one KNOWN and one unknown parent had its known parent silently OVERWRITTEN (or ERASED to `NA`), biasing the simulated-kinship estimate. The surrounding intent overwhelmingly said "impute only UNKNOWN parents": the original 19-month doc, the still-present `@examples` comment ("any unknown sire/dam is replaced"), `createSimKinships`'s `@return` ("possible parents for animals with unknown parents"), and the builder `getPotentialParents` (title "for animals with unknown parents"; selects `is.na(sire) | is.na(dam)` -- an OR, so mixed known/unknown animals ARE listed, which is exactly what makes the overwrite bite in the real pipeline). Owner scope-gate (`AskUserQuestion`) = "Guard makeSimPed" (over fix-getPotentialParents / doc-only). **(1) ULTRACODE read-only investigation BEFORE touching code (TDD-safe at pre-RED).** A 9-agent read-only Workflow (5 investigate: intent/behavior/blast-radius/methodology/test-impact; 4 adversarial verify incl. a firsthand R reproduction) established it was a real hazard, that the primitive is DEFENSIBLE under the vignette's "singleton candidate for a known parent" convention, and that the root cause is the `getPotentialParents -> makeSimPed` boundary. The adversarial split (2 CONFIRMED_BUG / 1 REFUTED_NOT_BUG / 1 DEPENDS_ON_INTENT) CONVERGED once you see WHERE each verifier placed the defect; guarding makeSimPed fixes the wired hazard for free AND makes the primitive robust regardless of caller encoding. Blast radius is narrow -- the sim-kinship trio is NOT in the production GVA pipeline (that uses geneDrop + correctUnknownParentMeanKinship); makeSimPed is exported public API reached in-package only via examples/tests + the un-invoked `inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R`. **(2) RED for a "preserve" contract:** build a ped with a KNOWN parent and assert it is UNCHANGED after makeSimPed (fails against the overwrite code -- 3 such tests: multi-element-pool overwrite, empty-vector erase, both-known), PLUS one "still imputes an unknown parent" guard so GREEN cannot be "never assign anything." The existing lacy1989Ped tests are unaffected (A/B/E are NA/NA founders -> the guard collapses to prior behavior with the same RNG order). GREEN = guard to assign a parent ONLY when the existing value is `NA` (robust to an id absent from `ped`); NAMESPACE diff EMPTY. **(3) A behavior change to a SEEDED Monte-Carlo function re-baselines downstream characterization goldens -- run the FULL SUITE to find them ALL, don't trust a partial prediction.** The audit predicted 2 shifted goldens; the full suite found **4 files / 7 assertions** (createSimKinships, cumulateSimKinships, countKinshipValues, summarizeKinshipValues) -- all because `smallPed`'s `A` has a KNOWN sire `Q` now correctly preserved (was overwritten). VERIFY the shift is the CORRECT mechanism (confirmed A's sire == Q, and raw kinship(A,J)==0 across all sims because A's fixed founder sire is unrelated to J's drawn parents) BEFORE re-baselining, and RECOMPUTE each value from the corrected code (do NOT paste an agent's predicted number). **(4) The build-equivalent (`--as-cran`) is the safety net a behavior change needs -- a VIGNETTE can break even when every unit test passes (FM #24).** `simulatedKValues.Rmd` joined two independently-`sd>0`-filtered summary frames by ROW POSITION (a latent bug); the corrected kinship distribution made the two filtered sets differ in size (84 vs 94 rows) -> `data.frame(...)` "arguments imply differing number of rows" -> vignette re-build FAILS -> `R CMD build` FAILS. Fix: `merge(stats_short, stats_long, by = c("id_1","id_2"), suffixes = ...)` (align by key, robust to size) in BOTH comparison chunks. My initial "no vignette change needed" judgment was WRONG; only the vignette rebuild caught it. A behavior fix is NOT done until the build passes. **Verify (firsthand):** 4 new makeSimPed tests pass; full suite 0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; `lintr` 0 on the changed R file; `spell_check_package` clean; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; **`--as-cran` (clean tree) GREEN 0/0/2** (standing benign pair) with `code/documentation mismatches … OK` + `Rd cross-references … OK` + `checking examples [21s] OK` + `testthat.R [67s] OK` + vignettes rebuilt. **Phase-3E DONE (not N/A -- behavior change):** the guarded contract runs against the built+installed package via testthat.R inside --as-cran + the `@example` + a direct `load_all` smoke (A's known sire Q preserved while NA founders still imputed). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a BEHAVIOR-change STRICT-TDD session (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated), with an ultracode read-only investigation Workflow at pre-RED.** (S277, makeSimPed #31 -- guard to impute only unknown (NA) parents / preserve known parents; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff)

#### Learning 257 -- **A doc-audit "code-fix candidate" list can be entirely NOT-warranted -- when the audit's own recommendations section already classifies every remaining finding as pure-doc, a "code fix" is a discretionary widening, not a bug; confirm read-only at pre-RED and do NOT force one.** The owner asked to take on the "remaining #109-audit doc-fixed *code-fix* candidates" (5 findings where the audit offered a code-change alternative to S274's doc fix: A `saveDataframesAsFiles`/`makeExamplePedigreeFile` `"xlsx"` alias; B `geneDrop` column reorder; C `getPedMaxAge`/`getPyramidAgeDist` living-filter; D `assignAlleles` `n = 5000` default; E `is_valid_date_str` `format`). A pre-RED **ultracode read-only investigation** (10 agents: 5 investigate + 5 adversarial verify, 0 errors; TDD-safe -- no code/tests written) found **ALL 5 are `no-docfix-correct`**, adversarially confirmed with concrete reproductions: A -- `"excel"` is the internal token since the file's birth, `"xlsx"` never selected any branch (`git log -S` empty); B -- breaking, every in-repo consumer is name-based (zero functional benefit) and a reorder breaks a positional test (`as.numeric` on `"sire"/"dam"` -> NA) + diverges live output from the frozen V-first `.rda` example data; C -- self-defeating, living-only is ALREADY delivered downstream by `fillBins` (`is.na(exit)`) and `getPedMaxAge` can't even filter (its test input `pedOne` has no `exit`/`status` column); D -- `5000` is the exact pipeline default the owner deliberately abandoned in S201/#2 (`geneDrop` now `n = 1000L`); E -- `format` dead since 2019, exported + pkgdown-listed with zero in-repo callers, both sub-options (implement/drop) breaking. **This matched the audit's own §6 ("every remaining fix is a pure-doc REFACTOR-class edit"), which the S276/S277 handoffs pointed to but did not foreground -- so the "code-fix candidate" framing over-promised actionability.** **(1) Don't force a code change on a false premise (FM #23 / [[observation-vs-decision]]).** When all 5 came back not-warranted, I did NOT manufacture a fix; I reported the finding, surfaced the 2 genuine items the investigation turned up (below), and posed a scope gate. The pre-RED investigation's real payoff on a "nothing warranted" answer is that it REDIRECTS the session to the genuine defect it surfaces. **(2) The genuine defect: S274 fixed `geneDrop`'s `@return` to V-first (`V1..Vn, id, parent`) but left the CONSUMER docs id/parent-first.** This became the deliverable (owner scope-gate = "Fix AlleleTable doc mismatch"). **REFACTOR-class, doc-only; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** **(3) The owner-named 2-file scope was INCOMPLETE vs the true inventory -- grep the whole doc surface for the same object and fix ALL descriptions for ONE canonical order (FM #13 literal-minimum), but BOUND the scope by category.** The investigation named `calcGU`+`calcA`; an evidence-based grep found FOUR exported consumers asserting/leading id/parent-first (`calcGU` @itemize, `calcA` "The first 2 columns provide the animal ID", `calcFGSE`, `calcGUSE`) -- all reordered. Three categories decided the boundary: **asserts wrong order** (calcGU/calcA -- "the first N columns are ...") = MUST fix; **enumerates columns leading with id/parent** (calcFGSE/calcGUSE) = reorder for consistency; **references "AlleleTable" abstractly with no order stated** (calcFG/calcFEFG/calcRetention) = LEAVE (nothing to reconcile); **@noRd with a deeper error** (`getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes` -- its `@param`/`@return` mislabel the intermediate list `$alleles`/`$counter` as the final "data.frame id, parent, V1..Vn", wrong in TYPE and order) = FLAG, do NOT half-fix (fixing only the order still leaves it saying "data.frame" for a list); `R/data.R` already V-first (id/parent = cols 5001/5002) so no change. **(4) Verification for a doc-only reword that touches `\link{}` lines:** the key `--as-cran` checks are `code/documentation mismatches … OK` AND `Rd cross-references … OK` (proves the reworded `\link{calcFG}`/`\link{calcGU}` targets still resolve), plus NAMESPACE empty diff + `lintr` 0 + `spell_check` clean. GREEN 0/0/2 (standing benign pair). Phase-3E N/A (stated): function bodies unchanged. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class doc-fix session (TDD RED/GREEN N/A), preceded by an ultracode read-only investigation Workflow at pre-RED that DECLINED all 5 candidates and surfaced the real fix.** (S278, #109 follow-up: reconcile AlleleTable column-order docs [calcGU/calcA/calcFGSE/calcGUSE] to geneDrop's V-first output; 5 code-fix candidates investigated & declined; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; NAMESPACE empty diff)

#### Learning 258 -- **A large "finish the X" enhancement with genuine design ambiguity is a PLANNING deliverable, not a code deliverable -- read the abandoned code + reference material FIRST (it often already DECIDES the "open questions"), author a vertical-slice plan, and adversarially review it before committing.** Issue #112 ("finish the genetic diversity heatmap or dashboard") had commented-out dead code, a pointer to 1210 lines of meeting notes, and 5-column spec ambiguity. Owner scope-gate (`AskUserQuestion`, pre-RED) = "Design/plan doc first" (over build-the-renderer-now / build-the-assembler-now). **Planning-workstream: the plan IS the deliverable; NO code/tests written (FM #18/#19 planning-to-implementation bleed avoided); implementation deferred to the per-slice sessions the plan defines.** **(1) Reading the abandoned implementations collapsed most "open questions" (SESSION_RUNNER Learning 6 applied).** The spec listed 5 metrics with "undefined" cutoffs; but 3 of the 5 already had working, individually-tested `@noRd` provider functions (`getProportionLow`/`getIndianOriginStatus`/`getProductionStatus`) whose CODE already decided the thresholds -- e.g. the "which Production definition (20190810 vs 20190916)?" conflict is settled by `getProductionStatus` implementing the 20190810 thresholds; the "low value cutoff" is decided upstream (the helper counts value LABELS == "Low", not a numeric cut). The helpers were ORPHANED (only caller = a commented-out test) but TESTED -- a half-built feature, not greenfield (FM #21). Only 2 of 5 columns needed new work (Inbreeding = buildable from `kinship()`; Flags = no data source -> DEFER). **(2) Ultracode used correctly at pre-RED: TWO read-only Workflows (TDD-safe).** A 4-agent scope investigation (meeting-notes / GV-pipeline / app-wiring / dead-code-deps) built the evidence base; THEN a 3-agent adversarial plan review (accuracy / completeness / slice-structure lenses) caught 10 real, file:line-evidenced defects in my OWN draft -- incl. two majors I got wrong: (a) I wrote group state was "not available" when `modBreedingGroupsServer` ALREADY returns `groups`+`groupKinship` (`appServer.R:315` just discards the return -> capture is one line, and the Inbreeding column can reuse the existing per-group kinship matrices instead of recomputing `kinship()`); (b) I omitted a meeting-note line (the graphic's title / "Genetic Diversity Report" naming) that bears on the tab-name decision. Also caught a false "S2 and S3 may be reordered" (S3 hard-depends on S2's provider; the independent pair is S1/S2). **Adversarially reviewing your OWN plan before committing catches assumption-level errors the plan's reader would otherwise trust (FM #11 gaps-from-memory / Learning 6).** **(3) 2 of the 4 investigation agents returned placeholder junk (`{"summary":"test","findings":[{"claim":"a","evidence":"b"}]}`) -- a StructuredOutput failure mode; do NOT trust a workflow's aggregate blindly.** The other 2 agents happened to cover the ground; I recovered the missing load-bearing facts (helper existence, deps, `.Rbuildignore`) firsthand with a few greps rather than re-running the workflow. The 2nd (review) workflow, whose schema explicitly said "never emit placeholder findings," returned 0 junk -- an explicit anti-placeholder instruction in the schema helps. **(4) Vertical slices, not horizontal layers (FM #25):** the plan sequences S1 (ggplot2 `geom_tile` renderer fed a hand-built matrix = tracer bullet, visible output day 1) -> S2 (Inbreeding provider) -> S3 (per-group assembler ties data->render end-to-end) -> S4 (Shiny module + one-line group capture + Phase-3E), each passing "if I stop here, does something work?". **Verify:** plan proofread for internal consistency; all 10 review findings applied; NO code/tests/NAMESPACE touched (planning-only; build-equivalent = doc consistency); Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a PLANNING session -- the plan is the deliverable; TDD RED/GREEN/REFACTOR N/A this session (they govern the future slices); two ultracode read-only Workflows at pre-RED (scope investigation + adversarial plan review).** (S279, issue #112 genetic diversity dashboard design & implementation plan; `docs/planning/issue112-genetic-diversity-dashboard-plan.md`; direct-merge to `master`; no code)

#### Learning 259 -- **When executing a "delete dead code" step (even one the owner just ratified), grep the WHOLE tree -- including process/history docs (SESSION_NOTES, old `docs/planning/*`, audits) -- for the target's DECISION history BEFORE the `git rm`, not just its technical state; a file can be provably dead yet under a standing author "do not delete."** Owner picked issue #112 Slice S1 (the plan's tracer bullet): a new exported `makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap(stats)` ggplot2 `geom_tile` red/yellow/green heat map from a group x metric `colorIndex` matrix, plus the D2 dead-code deletion. **NEW EXPORT -> STRICT TDD (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated); 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (4 pre-RED ratifications + 3 phase-gates + 1 naming reconsideration + 1 delete-confirmation, all owner-answered). **(a) A "delete dead code" step needs a DECISION-history check, not just a state check.** The ratified D2 said delete the dead, fully-commented, build-ignored `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`; I `git rm`'d it and cleaned `.Rbuildignore`, THEN a broad `grep -rn <name> .` (run during verification) surfaced a prior explicit author **"do not delete"** decision (issue #30, S53-56 -- adversarial verification had once protected it from a tidy-up agent). The file's current technical state (dead, no `@import`, build-ignored) told me it was SAFE to delete; it did NOT tell me someone had DECIDED to keep it, and my ratification question hadn't mentioned it. Right move (SAFEGUARDS "surface the contradiction before deleting"): pause pre-commit, show the owner the prior decision + why its premises are now void -- the feared namespace fallout was disproven by Learning 231 (the file's dead `@import`s were already removed, so deletion changed NAMESPACE by **0 lines**, re-verified firsthand via a `document()` diff), and the "sole scaffolding for the unbuilt feature" reason evaporates the moment you are building the replacement -- and get an INFORMED re-confirmation. Never silently reverse an author decision even when your ratified task implies it. **The order fix:** for any delete/rename, `grep -rn <target> .` across the WHOLE tree BEFORE the `git rm`, so a standing decision gates the delete instead of surfacing after it ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] applied to deletion; S279's plan already prescribed the recoverability `git log` check, which is necessary but not sufficient). **(b) Clean ALL references to a deleted file; some hide.** Deleting the file left stale pointers in TWO configs -- `.Rbuildignore:21` (obvious) and `.lintr:17` (a whole-file exclusion) -- and a narrow "grep R/ tests/ NAMESPACE man/" missed the `.lintr` one; only the broad tree grep caught it. **(c) Confirm a linter is enforced before "fixing" its warnings.** GREEN drew 5 `implicit_integer_linter` lints; unlike the object_usage/object_name artifacts ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]), this one IS active -- `.lintr` enables it via `linters_with_tags(c("style","best_practices",...))` and the existing code is clean of it -- so they were real, and I fixed them (`c(1L,2L,3L)`, `angle=45L`, `hjust=0L`). Read `.lintr` to classify a lint real-vs-artifact rather than assuming. **(d) Pin ggplot-object test assertions to the INSTALLED version at pre-RED.** ggplot2 here is **4.0.3** (S7-backed). Before finalizing RED I confirmed firsthand that `expect_s3_class(p,"ggplot")`, `ggplot2::layer_data(p,1L)$fill` (returns the literal `"red"`, not hex), `p$data` factor levels, the `GeomTile` geom class, and `p$scales$get_scales("fill")` carrying class `ScaleDiscrete` all behave as asserted -- so the RED tests were provably satisfiable in GREEN, no wasted cycle. The strong, order-independent color check: single-value inputs (all-1s -> all `"red"`, etc.) prove the 1/2/3->red/yellow/green mapping without position math, plus per-color tile counts on a mixed fixture. **(e) For a plot-producing deliverable, the runtime smoke is RENDERING it.** Building the ggplot object and passing `layer_data` assertions is not proof it draws; `ggplot2::ggsave(tempfile(fileext=".png"), p)` producing a non-empty PNG is (FM #24 -- build/tests-pass != it works). D6 note: S1's input is a `colorIndex`-only matrix, so "show the value on each tile" is deferred until S3 supplies raw numbers -- color-only for the tracer bullet. **(f) Naming a renderer vs a feature:** when the owner asks whether a broader name (`Dashboard`/`Report`) fits better, separate the FUNCTION (returns one `ggplot` heat map -> literal `...Heatmap`) from the composite MODULE that will wrap it with data/controls/guidance (-> the `Dashboard`/`Report` name belongs there, on the future S4 `modGeneticDiversity`). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a NEW-EXPORT STRICT-TDD session -- RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each gated.** (S280, issue #112 Slice S1 -> exported `makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap`; deleted dead `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`+test; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite clean; lint_package 0; spell clean; runtime render smoke)

#### Learning 260 -- **For a stoplight-metric provider (a per-group function returning `colorIndex` ∈ {1,2,3}), the load-bearing pre-RED decision is what an EMPTY denominator returns -- decide it explicitly with the owner and choose NA-over-a-color, not the sibling's silent NA→"healthy" fallthrough.** Owner picked issue #112 Slice S2: a new internal `@noRd` `getKinshipWithMaleStatus(group, kmat, minFemaleAge=3L, minMaleAge=5L, threshold=0.015625)` -> `list(fraction, color, colorIndex)` reporting the fraction of breeding-age females (sex `"F"`, age ≥ 3) essentially unrelated (kinship ≤ 0.015625) to ≥ 1 breeding-age male (sex `"M"`, age ≥ 5), colored red <0.6 / yellow [0.6,0.9] / green >0.9. **NEW INTERNAL PROVIDER -> STRICT TDD (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated); 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (3 pre-RED ratifications + 3 phase-gates, all owner-answered). **(a) The empty-denominator branch is a policy decision, not a default -- surface it.** The plan (§8.2) flagged the sibling `getProductionStatus`'s NA→green trap (a 0-dam group reads as healthy green, `getProductionStatus.R:85,96`); the analogous trap here is a group with no females ≥ 3 (fraction 0/0). I did NOT copy the sibling; I posed it as an explicit pre-RED `[RATIFY]` with three options (NA/insufficient, red-for-attention, error) and the owner chose NA -- so the provider returns `fraction`/`color`/`colorIndex` all `NA`, and the S3 assembler/S4 renderer owns how to display the NA state (matches the S280 renderer gotcha #2: the current heatmap accepts only {1,2,3}, so an NA index is a deliberate future-render decision, not a silent pass-through). **Distinguish the truly-undefined case from a well-defined zero:** "no eligible MALES but females exist" is a real 0.0 → red (a genuine management problem, breeding-age females with no breeding-age mate), whereas "no eligible FEMALES" is 0/0 → NA; only the latter is ambiguous. **(b) Keep a data-provider PURE; the app-vs-non-app source choice belongs to the caller.** The plan's "reuse `groupKinship` in-app, fall back to `kinship()` outside" describes where `kmat` COMES FROM, not a branch inside the provider -- so the ratified signature takes a ready-made `kmat` (a peer to the 3 existing pure `@noRd` providers) and the S3 assembler picks the source. This also makes RED trivially deterministic: hand-build a named `kmat` (off-diagonal 0.25 = "related", diagonal 0.5, specific female-male pairs set to 0 = "unrelated") + a tiny id/sex/age frame, and you control every boundary exactly (3-of-5 = 0.6, 9-of-10 = 0.9, kinship == 0.015625 counts via `<=`). **(c) A `sex` column with THREE values needs an explicit test.** The pedigree's `sex` is `"F"`/`"M"`/`"U"` (confirmed firsthand on `examplePedigree`), so `females = sex=="F"` and `males = sex=="M"` each silently drop `"U"`; a dedicated test (a `"U"` member that WOULD flip the result if miscounted as a male) locks that in rather than trusting the filter. **(d) `.lintr` excludes `tests/`, so mirror the newest sibling test file's style, not the oldest.** The S1 test file omits the `library()` preamble the older `test_getProductionStatus.R` carries (both work under `load_all`), and only the `R/` file is linted -- there GREEN drew two ENFORCED lints (`paste(...collapse=", ")`→`toString()`; implicit-integer `0`→`0.0`), fixed in REFACTOR after confirming both are `.lintr`-active and the sibling code is clean of them ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]; Learning 259c: read `.lintr`, classify real-vs-artifact). **(e) Phase-3E for a caller-less `@noRd` provider is honest to state, not skip:** there is no app/runtime surface until its first caller (S3), so the runtime exercise IS the unit tests running against the built+installed package inside `--as-cran`'s `testthat.R` -- a full app launch is an S4 concern (FM #24 applies where there's a runtime surface; say so rather than claiming a smoke test that has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a NEW-INTERNAL-PROVIDER STRICT-TDD session -- RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each gated.** (S281, issue #112 Slice S2 -> internal `getKinshipWithMaleStatus`; no export/NAMESPACE change; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite clean; lint_package 0; spell clean)

#### Learning 261 -- **A "blocked on owner questions" slice is usually a read-only-investigate-THEN-ratify sequence, not a wait: some "open questions" are answerable from the code (a false blocker), and only the genuinely-clinical ones go to the owner.** Owner picked issue #112 Slice S3: a new exported, PURE `getGeneticDiversityStats(groups, ped, geneticValues, kmat, housing="shelter_pens", currentDate=Sys.Date())` that per group calls all 4 providers and returns the group x 4-metric `colorIndex` frame (`group`,`Value`,`Origin`,`Production`,`Inbreeding`) that `makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap()` (S1) renders. **NEW EXPORT -> STRICT TDD (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated); 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (a read-only pre-RED evidence Workflow + 3 owner decisions + 3 phase-gates, all owner-answered). **(a) Triage the plan's "open questions" into investigable vs decidable before asking the owner.** Plan §6 listed Q1-Q3 as blocking. Only Q1 (which Production threshold set is clinically canonical -- 20190810 vs the later 20190916) is a genuine owner call; Q2 (housing per group) and Q3 (where the "Low" value label is produced) are "does X exist / where is it produced" questions the CODE answers. A read-only pre-RED Workflow (4 investigate + 4 adversarial-verify, TDD-safe, 0 code/tests) resolved Q3 outright -- found the producer the S279 plan explicitly could not locate: `rankSubjects.R:37/39/41` emits `"Low Value"`/`"High Value"`/`"Undetermined"`, surfaced via `reportGV(ped)$report` -- established Q2 (no housing source anywhere in data model/group result/config -> caller-supplied `housing`), and armed Q1 (code already implements 20190810 on a rolling window). Three "blockers" collapsed to three framed decisions answered in one `AskUserQuestion`. **(b) The adversarial verify pass earns its cost by REFUTING the plan, not just confirming it.** The verifiers confirmed the load-bearing facts but also (i) refuted a plan assumption -- only `getKinshipWithMaleStatus` returns an NA `colorIndex`; `getProductionStatus` maps NA->green (`getProductionStatus.R:85,96`), so the NA-reconciliation is ONE provider not two -- and (ii) surfaced a param-name trap: `getIndianOriginStatus(origin=...)` must be fed `ped$ancestry` (the ancestry factor from `convertAncestry`, tokens CHINESE/HYBRID/...), NOT `ped$origin` (the import-facility name); its yellow band is unreachable from live data because `convertAncestry` never emits `BORDERLINE_HYBRID`. A finder-only sweep would have shipped the plan's `origin` into the signature and silently always-greened Origin. **(c) An assembler that fans out to N providers must reconcile each provider's UNDEFINED/NA output against the downstream renderer's strict domain -- that reconciliation is an owner decision.** S1 rejects NA (accepts only {1,2,3}); `getKinshipWithMaleStatus` returns NA for a female-less group. Owner ratified undefined->red (surface missing data, don't repeat the NA->green trap), applied uniformly in the assembler (an empty Value denominator after dropping `"Undetermined"` -> red too). The renderer's contract is load-bearing on the assembler's output, so the assembler -- not the renderer -- resolves NA for the first working version. **(d) Don't blindly reuse a helper the investigation suggests: `getCurrentAge()` is hardwired to `lubridate::today()`** (`getCurrentAge.R:16-18`, no reference-date arg), so it is non-deterministic AND inconsistent with `getProductionStatus`'s `currentDate` window. The assembler instead derives age from `birth` using its OWN `currentDate` param (same interval/duration formula) -- deterministic for tests and consistent with the production birth window. Read the helper before wiring it. **(e) Deterministic fan-out fixtures: build one all-green (3,3,3,3) and one all-red (1,1,1,1) group so every provider's contribution is unambiguous, with births far from the age thresholds so the derived-age rounding never matters.** `currentDate=2020-07-01` -> window 2018-2019; adults born 2008/2009 (age ~12, clearly >=3/>=5), offspring born 2018-2019 (age ~1-2, clearly <3/<5); a full named `kmat` with only the green group's female-male pairs set unrelated; the `gv` frame drives Value, `ped$ancestry` drives Origin, birth/exit dates drive Production. **(f) D7-style doc fix = describe what the code DOES, surgically.** `getProductionStatus`'s `@details` was a HYBRID (20190916 fixed-date prose + 20190810 thresholds); the fix rewrote ONLY the fixed-date prose to the rolling `currentYear-2..currentYear-1` window, left the already-correct 20190810 threshold list intact, corrected `@param minParentAge` 2->3, aligned `@return` to "at least 30 days". `@noRd` -> no man page -> the fix lives in source roxygen only (still correctness-valuable); no behavior change, empty behavioral diff. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a NEW-EXPORT STRICT-TDD session -- RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each gated; preceded by a read-only ultracode evidence Workflow at pre-RED.** (S282, issue #112 Slice S3 -> exported `getGeneticDiversityStats` + D7 `getProductionStatus` doc fix; NAMESPACE +1 export; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite clean; lint 0; spell clean)

#### Learning 262 -- **For a "wire an existing helper into the running app" slice (a new Shiny module + tab + shared-state capture), the load-bearing pre-RED work is mapping the REACTIVE CONTRACTS firsthand, and Phase-3E is a headless full-app `testServer` when the e2e suite skips.** Owner picked issue #112 Slice S4 (the last implementation slice): a new module `R/modGeneticDiversity.R` (`modGeneticDiversityUI`/`Server`, both `@export`ed) that assembles live breeding-group/genetic-value/kinship reactives into the S3 `getGeneticDiversityStats()` and renders the S1 heat map, a new "Genetic Diversity" tab, and capture of `modBreedingGroupsServer`'s previously-discarded return into `shared$breedingGroups`. **NEW MODULE + RUNTIME/REGISTRATION CHANGE -> STRICT TDD (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each `AskUserQuestion`-gated) + MANDATORY Phase-3E; 0 corrections / 0 overrides** (a read-only pre-RED evidence Workflow + 3 scope decisions + 3 phase-gates, all owner-answered). **(a) The wiring's correctness lives in the reactive contracts, so verify them firsthand -- a param-name or "which reactive" mistake silently produces a wrong or empty dashboard.** The load-bearing facts a read-only pre-RED Workflow (6 investigators + adversarial verify) CONFIRMED: `modBreedingGroupsServer` returns `groups` as a **list of character ID vectors** (exactly the assembler's `groups` shape); the FULL kinship matrix the assembler needs is `modGeneticValueServer`'s `kinshipMatrix` reactive (`fullResults()$kinship`, dimnames=ids), **NOT** the exposed per-group `groupKinship` (which is usually NULL because its UI checkbox defaults FALSE) **and NOT stored in `shared`** (so it must be passed as the reactive directly: `kinshipMatrix = gvResults$kinshipMatrix`), while the GV report frame (id+value) IS in `shared$geneticValues`; and `appServer.R:315` DISCARDS the breeding-group return (a bare call, no `<-`), so S4 captures it (`bgResults <- ...`) + an `observe` copying `bgResults$groups()` into a new `shared$breedingGroups` field (mirrors the existing `geneticValues` capture at `:281-284`; stays NULL until the user forms groups -> natural graceful degradation). **(b) When the shinytest2 e2e suite SKIPS in the sandbox (no chromote), a runtime/registration change is still Phase-3E-verifiable headlessly -- `shiny::testServer(shinyApp(appUI(), appServer), {...})` executes the ENTIRE `appServer` body at session init**, mounting every module (incl. the new one, the `bgResults` capture, and the `shared$breedingGroups` observe) and letting you read NAMESPACED module outputs (`output[["geneticDiversity-guidance"]]`) and shared fields. This is a real runtime exercise (FM #24), not a "could not verify" -- it caught nothing here only because the wiring was correct, but a top-level reference error or a mount-time crash would surface. Pair it with `appUI()`-renders-the-tab + `shinyApp(...)`-constructs checks. Don't claim a browser smoke you can't run; do run the `testServer`-on-the-whole-app substitute. **(c) A module consuming `req()`-gated upstream reactives must read them DEFENSIVELY so it degrades to guidance, not a blank tab.** Upstream reactives that are NULL/req-silent-error until an analysis runs or a button is pressed (`gvResults$kinshipMatrix` = `req(fullResults()); ...`; `bgResults$groups()` = an unfired `eventReactive`) will, if read raw, halt the whole reactive chain silently -- blanking the guidance output too. Wrapping each read in `tryCatch(r(), error=function(e) NULL)` converts "not ready" (NULL or silent error) to NULL, and the dependency is still registered (the read happens before the error), so the tab shows guidance now and re-renders when the upstream becomes ready. The testable public contract is the returned `list(stats, heatmap)` of reactives; **assert plots via the returned reactive + a `GeomTile` layer check, never by reading a `renderPlot` output** (`output$heatmap` forces a graphics device and is flaky in `testServer` -- and the house tests never do it). **(d) A module server that calls a DATE-RELATIVE helper needs a `currentDate = Sys.Date()` arg** so tests can pin the date and reuse the deterministic fixture -- the assembler's Production metric uses a birth window relative to "now", so a `Sys.Date()`-hardwired module would have non-deterministic tests; the app mounts it with the default, tests pass `2020-07-01`. **(e) A latent RED-test defect can be MASKED by the missing-function error and only surface at GREEN -- that's the loop working, fix the harness without changing assertions.** My RED server blocks referenced `result` before binding it via `session$getReturned()`; in RED the `testServer(modGeneticDiversityServer, ...)` call errored first (function not found), hiding it; at GREEN the call succeeded and execution reached the undefined `result`. Corrected by adding the bind line to each block (no assertion changed). **(f) Adding a `@family` member regenerates EVERY sibling's `.Rd` "Other ... modules" cross-reference -- ~18 modified `man/*.Rd` are expected, not noise; diff one to confirm it's only the cross-ref addition (not roxygen version drift) before committing.** NAMESPACE gained exactly the 2 expected exports (every module here exports BOTH UI and Server). **(g) Scope discipline held: the owner deferred the optional Home-page card** (the Home row is a full 3x4 Bootstrap grid; a 4th card needs a new row/rebalance; several existing tabs -- ORIP, Potential Parents, GV&BG -- have no Home card), keeping the S4 diff on the module + wiring (SAFEGUARDS mode-switch rule). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a NEW-MODULE + RUNTIME-WIRING STRICT-TDD session -- RED->GREEN->REFACTOR, each gated; preceded by a read-only ultracode evidence Workflow at pre-RED; Phase-3E via headless full-app `testServer`.** (S283, issue #112 Slice S4 -> module `modGeneticDiversity` + "Genetic Diversity" tab + `shared$breedingGroups` capture; NAMESPACE +2 exports; direct-merge to `master`; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite 1170 clean; lint 0; spell clean)

#### Learning 263 -- **Closing the LAST slice of a multi-slice feature: close the broad feature issue as complete (with a per-slice commit summary) + open a NEW focused issue scoping ONLY the deferred/blocked remainder -- don't leave the broad issue open indefinitely.** Owner picked (via `AskUserQuestion`) the disposition of issue #112 ("finish development of the genetic diversity heatmap or dashboard") now that all four buildable slices had shipped (S1 renderer, S2 provider, S3 assembler, S4 module+tab; dashboard live) and only the ratified-deferred S5 Flags (genotype/phenotype) column remained -- blocked because NO genotype/phenotype flagged-animal data source exists in the package (plan D5 / §6 Q4). **Issue hygiene, NOT code: no `R/` change, no TDD phase, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface).** **(a) Disposition pattern:** close the feature issue as *completed* with a summary comment that lists each shipped slice + its commit hash (so the closed issue is self-documenting), and open a new `enhancement` issue scoping ONLY the deferred remainder + its exact blocker + the "work when unblocked" steps -- this keeps the open-issue list accurate (the dashboard is DONE; only a blocked enhancement remains) and preserves the deferred work as a tracked item rather than orphaning it in a plan doc. Also record the now-shipped feature in `ROADMAP.md` "What's Built" (closing a feature issue as complete while the feature inventory omits it is an incomplete job, FM #13). **(b) `gh` CLI capability correction (refines the standing "gh issue view exits 1" note):** on this repo `gh issue create`, `gh issue comment`, and `gh issue close --reason completed` all work fine; ONLY `gh issue view` and `gh pr edit` hit the deprecated-projectCards exit-1 bug -- for those two, read/patch via `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/...`. Create the NEW issue FIRST to get its number, then reference it in the closing comment, then close. **(c) Scope discipline:** touched only the coupled process docs + ROADMAP; left a separate stale audit-tracking entry (ROADMAP line 33 / NEW-20, the dead `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R` deletion already done in S280) for a future session rather than fixing it under this closure (SAFEGUARDS mode-switch / "while I'm at it"). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was an ISSUE-HYGIENE close-out session -- no code, no TDD phase.** (S284, issue #112 closed complete -> #116 opened for deferred S5 Flags column; ROADMAP "What's Built" updated; no code/no runtime change)

#### Learning 264 -- **A coverage-backfill session begins with a MEASUREMENT-VALIDITY step, not test-writing: run `covr` under `NOT_CRAN=true` or the `skip_on_cran` module tests silently vanish and you chase phantom gaps.** Owner picked issue #111 ("enhance code coverage"), clarified the scope is ALL R code lacking quality coverage (the three named example lines are illustrative, and are now 97-98% covered anyway -- the issue is stale), then chose this session's slice via `AskUserQuestion`: the deterministic-helper tier. **Test-only backfill + one dead-code deletion; PRE-RED->RED gated; 16 helper files -> 100%; overall 91.23% -> 92.96%; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite 1437 clean.** **(a) The default `covr::package_coverage()` UNDERCOUNTS this package by ~8 points because 39 test files carry `testthat::skip_on_cran()` and covr does not set `NOT_CRAN`.** A first read said 83.54% with `modBreedingGroups` at 24%, `modGeneticValue` 30%, `modInput` 77%, `set_seed` 70%, `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix` 39%; re-running with `NOT_CRAN=true` gave a true 91.23% with those modules at 98.6/96.3/87.3% and BOTH of those two helpers already at **100%**. Writing tests off the first read would have (i) added redundant tests for already-covered lines and (ii) mis-prioritized the big Shiny modules as gaps when they are near-complete. **Always establish the true baseline (`NOT_CRAN=true`) AND dump the per-line uncovered set per file before writing anything** -- target the exact red lines, not the file percentage. **(b) Coverage backfill maps AWKWARDLY onto strict RED->GREEN: the code already works, so the new tests PASS on first run -- there is no failing RED and no production GREEN.** Frame it honestly at the gate: the "RED" is the coverage gap itself (each target line verified red in the covr baseline); write each test to the function's DOCUMENTED behavior; a pass gains the coverage, and a FAILURE surfaces a latent bug -> a separate gated fix (do NOT fix in the coverage slice). One production change did ride along -- deleting dead code -- which is not RED/GREEN either; treat it as a separate safe cleanup verified by the full suite + build. **(c) Verify each workflow-supplied backfill sketch against the SOURCE before trusting it -- the sketches are un-adversarially-verified and one was wrong in an instructive way.** A classification Workflow (8 deep + 2 batch agents, read-only, TDD-safe at pre-RED) produced accurate per-file sketches EXCEPT `fixColumnNames`: it asserted `first_name %in% newColNames`, but tracing (and an empirical `load_all` run) showed the "overreach cleanup" writes to `cols` which line 39 (`cols <- newCols`) immediately overwrites -- the restoration never reaches the returned `newColNames` (`c("First Name","Second Name")` -> `firstname, secondname`). That is a real latent BUG. Per scope discipline: characterized the ACTUAL (defective) behavior in the test (clearly commented) to gain the line coverage, and filed it as issue **#117** -- did not fix it. Empirically validate anything non-obvious (fixtures that must thread a full pipeline: `gvaConvergence` origin-branch / all-founder-NA-metrics, `getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes` NA-first-allele, the LabKey `getDemographics`-mock column order = `siteInfo$mapPedColumns`) with a throwaway `Rscript -e 'load_all()...'` run before committing the test. **(d) A 0%-coverage file is a DEAD-CODE signal, not just an untested one -- check for callers before writing a test for it.** `agePyramidPlot.R` (0%) was an unexported (`@noRd`), zero-caller duplicate of the live `getPyramidPlot()`; the right fix is deletion (+ its two stale `inst/_pkgdown.yml` reference-index lines, which pointed at a non-existent man page), not a characterization test. Grep `R/ tests/ NAMESPACE man/` for callers first (Learning 259); left the historical `inst/extdata/meeting_notes` references untouched (rendered historical record, not active docs -- FM #22). **(e) `.lintr` here excludes `tests/`** (and `inst/extdata`, `vignettes`, `inst/application`), so test-file line lengths are not lints; since no `R/` source was added or modified (only a deletion), lint is non-regressing by construction -- but confirm with `lintr::lint()` on the new files (returns 0 under the package config). Mocking toolkit that worked: `testthat::local_mocked_bindings` for package-internal calls (`qcStudbook`, `create_wkbk`, and `shiny::showNotification`/`getDefaultReactiveDomain` via `.package="shiny"`), `mockery::stub` for a named callee inside the function under test (`getDemographics`), `withr::local_options`/`local_envvar`/`local_tempfile` for isolation. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session -- PRE-RED->RED gated; preceded by a read-only ultracode classification Workflow; a genuine bug found -> characterized + filed #117, not fixed; Phase-3E N/A (no runtime behavior changed).** (S285, issue #111 slice 1 -> 16 helpers backfilled to 100% + deleted dead `agePyramidPlot.R`; overall coverage 91.23% -> 92.96%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/2 GREEN; full suite 1437 clean; lint non-regressing; spell clean; bug #117 filed)

#### Learning 265 -- **A module can read "31.7% covered" while its ENTIRE server body is untested -- UI-shape tests plus a `deparse()` structural grep count source lines without ever driving the server, so the file percentage lies about WHAT is covered.** Coverage-campaign slice 2 (owner chose it via `AskUserQuestion` over {modORIPReporting / appServer / small residuals}): backfill `modORIPReportingServer`. **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; 12 `testServer` tests; `R/modORIPReporting.R` 31.7% -> 100%; overall 92.96% -> 95.03% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 1449 clean.** **(a) Diagnose the SHAPE of the gap, not just the number, before writing tests.** Reading the file's percentage would have suggested "add a few tests"; reading the existing tests showed the real shape: `test_modORIPReporting.R` only checks UI tab-gating (`appUI` HTML `grepl`) + a `deparse(appServer)` structural grep, and `test_modSiteConfig.R` only asserts the module functions `exists()` / `is.function()` / have the right `formals()`. None drives the module through `shiny::testServer`, so all three renderers (`siteInfo`/`colonySummary`/`geneticDiversity`), both `downloadHandler`s, and the returned reactive ran ONLY under the opt-in browser e2e (`test-e2e-orip-module.R`, skipped without shinytest2 + chromote). The fix is one `testServer` suite that executes the whole server body headlessly. **(b) `testServer` output-access idioms for this codebase (validated firsthand):** read a `renderUI`/`renderTable` output via `as.character(output$x)` -- but this returns a **length-2 character vector** (`[1]` = the HTML, `[2]` = `"list()"` for the htmlDependency list), so assert with `any(grepl(pattern, as.character(output$x)))`, never a scalar `grepl`. Read a `downloadHandler` by assigning its output to a path -- `path <- output$downloadX; df <- utils::read.csv(path)` -- the content function runs and returns the written temp-file path (idiom lifted from `test_modGeneticValue.R`). Read the module's return via `session$getReturned()`. Pass every upstream as a `shiny::reactive(...)` in `args=list(...)`; a `req(x)`-guarded output only checks that the reactive object `x` is truthy (it is, being a function), then `x()` is evaluated inside. **(c) For 100% (zero uncovered lines) cover BOTH branches of every guard, not just the happy path:** `siteConfig` supplied (table) vs NULL (`getSiteInfo` fallback) vs a reactive that `stop()`s (`tryCatch`->NULL -> "not available"); pedigree/gv populated vs empty (`nrow==0`) vs NULL; the download report with all sections vs with NULL ped+gv (which also exercises the `getSiteInfo` else-branch and the skipped `if(!is.null(...))` bodies); demographics with data vs placeholder. **(d) Repeat S285's de-risking discipline:** before writing the test file, run a throwaway `Rscript -e 'load_all(); testServer(...)'` probe that prints each output + reads each download CSV, to confirm the fixtures produce the exact asserted strings (counts, `sprintf("%.4f")` means) and to discover idiom quirks (the length-2 `as.character` above) -- zero test-authoring surprises. **(e) Backfill on CORRECT code has no bug to file and Phase-3E is N/A** (unlike S285's `fixColumnNames`): the tests pass on first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/` source changes, no runtime behavior, so no smoke-test target (not a skipped check). **(f) `--as-cran` via `devtools::check(args="--as-cran")` here returned 0/0/0 (Status: OK), cleaner than the documented 0/0/2 -- the CRAN-incoming "archived/New submission" + HTML-Tidy NOTEs don't fire under `devtools::check`; both are GREEN, don't be alarmed by the discrepancy.** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 2 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S286, issue #111 slice 2 -> `modORIPReportingServer` 31.7% -> 100% via a 12-test `testServer` suite; overall 92.96% -> 95.03%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 1449 clean; lint non-regressing; spell clean)

#### Learning 266 -- **`appServer` (a top-level Shiny server function) reads 0% while the only existing test checks helpers + a `deparse()` grep + `appUI()` HTML greps -- the fix is a full-app `shiny::testServer(appServer, {...})`, but the wiring OBSERVERS read CHILD-module reactives, so covering their branches means STUBBING the child servers, not setting `shared`.** Coverage-campaign slice 3 (owner chose it via `AskUserQuestion` over {`appServer` 0% / residual-modules bundle / `modInput` only}): backfill the `appServer` body. **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; a 6-test `testServer` suite; `R/appServer.R` 0% -> 100%; overall 95.03% -> 97.91% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 1455 clean.** **(a) Same 0%-shape as the S265 module gap but at the app level:** `test_appServer_dynamicTabs.R` covers only the tab helpers (`shouldShowChangedColsTab`/`getErrorTab`/`getChangedColsTab`), a `deparse(appServer)` structural grep, and `appUI()` HTML greps -- nothing DRIVES `appServer`, so the whole server body (logger/config init, the 6 nav `observeEvent`s, every module mount, and the wiring observers) ran only under the skipped browser e2e. `shiny::testServer(appServer, {...})` (a bare server fn, no `shinyApp()` wrapper needed) executes the entire body at session init. **(b) Two-layer design.** Layer 1: a bare `testServer(appServer)` boot with the REAL modules + `session$setInputs(goto_input=1L, ...)` for all six buttons -> covers init + all mounts + the nav handlers (an integration smoke that the whole app wires up without error). Layer 2: the data-dependent observer branches -- QC notifications (`L139-170`), dynamic Error/Changed-Cols tab insert/remove (`L196-252`), and the pedigree/GV propagation into `shared` (`L265`/`L284`) -- all read the CHILD-module return reactives (`inputResults$cleanedStudbook/qcSummary/errorLst/pedigreeFileName/changedCols`, `pedigreeResults$pedigree`, `gvResults$geneticValues`), **NOT `shared`**, so you cannot reach them by setting `shared`; you must control the children. `testthat::with_mocked_bindings(.package="nprcgenekeepr", modInputServer=stub, modPedigreeServer=stub, modGeneticValueServer=stub, modBreedingGroupsServer=stub)` where each stub returns `list(<name> = shiny::reactive(ctl$<name>()), ...)` backed by `shiny::reactiveVal()`s created at mount time (in-domain) and stashed in a control env, then drive them inside the expr with `ctl$qc(list(errors=,warnings=,records=)); session$flushReact()`. **(c) `testServer`'s expr evaluates IN the server function's environment, so assert on `appServer`'s observable effects directly:** `shared$currentStudbook/currentPedigree/geneticValues/breedingGroups` (the wiring observers) and the `errorTabShown()`/`changedColsTabShown()` `reactiveVal`s (the tab observer) are all reachable by name.
