This makes it a clean UNIT test of the glue -- the child modules are covered by their own suites; `appServer`'s decision logic is the unit. **(d) Control the gate helpers to reach every branch:** cover both `targetTab` positions of the Changed-Cols tab via `checkErrorLst` -- a real `errorLst` from `qcStudbook(pedFemaleSireMaleDam, reportErrors=TRUE)` HAS QC errors (`checkErrorLst` TRUE -> Error tab shown -> Changed-Cols targets `"Error List"`), while a clean pedigree with a space-renamed sire column (`\`si re\``, `qcStudbook(..., reportChanges=TRUE)`) is non-NULL but error-free (`checkErrorLst` FALSE -> Changed-Cols targets `"Input"`); cover the QC-notification branches by mocking `showNotification` into a recorder env and driving `qcSummary` to records>0 / errors>0 / warnings>0; cover the ONPRC-only ORIP mount (`if (shouldShowOripTab(...))`) by mocking `shouldShowOripTab -> TRUE` + a `modORIPReportingServer` recorder (default env keeps it FALSE -> both branches). **(e) De-risk with a throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe BEFORE writing the file (S285/S286 discipline):** it proved `with_mocked_bindings(.package="nprcgenekeepr")` intercepts child servers AND imported functions (`showNotification`) AND package functions (`shouldShowOripTab`) called from inside `testServer`, that `reactiveVal` control drives the observers, and that the fixtures yield the intended `checkErrorLst` TRUE/FALSE -- zero authoring surprises. **(f) Two test-hygiene traps.** (i) Booting `appServer` calls the bare `getSiteInfo()` (logger init `L46`) with no config file -> a benign "configuration file is missing" warning per boot; muffle ONLY that specific message via a `withCallingHandlers(expr, warning=function(w) if (grepl("configuration file is missing", conditionMessage(w), fixed=TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning"))` helper wrapping each `testServer` call -- NOT a blanket `suppressWarnings`, so genuine warnings still surface (the sibling `modORIPReporting_server` suite emits 0 warnings only because it passes an explicit `siteConfig` and never hits the bare `getSiteInfo`). (ii) In the wiring test, injecting a minimal `data.frame(id="a")` pedigree makes the REAL non-read downstream modules (`modPyramid`/`modSummaryStats`/`modGeneticDiversity`/`modPotentialParents`) emit empty-data ggplot `min`/`max`/`geom_vline` warnings -> stub THOSE to a `noopServer <- function(id, ...) invisible(NULL)` too (their mount lines are already covered by the bare-boot test, so no coverage is lost). **(g) A test-file top-level single-letter global (`K`) collides by NAME with other test files** (`test_calcGUSE.R`, `test_reportGV.R`) -- harmless under testthat 3e per-file environment isolation (full suite + `--as-cran` both green with all three present), but rename to a distinct name (`ctl`) to remove the reviewer false-alarm and any fragility under a non-isolated run mode; a `K$`-only rename is safe with `replace_all` because `groupKinship`/`kinshipMatrix` contain no `K$`. **(h) Impact math:** a SINGLE 0% file of 354 lines dragged overall down ~3 points, so `appServer` 0% -> 100% lifted overall 95.03% -> 97.91% -- a large per-slice gain from one file. Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass on first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 3 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S287, issue #111 slice 3 -> `appServer` 0% -> 100% via a 6-test full-app `testServer` suite; overall 95.03% -> 97.91%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 1455 clean; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 267 -- **A residual Shiny-module coverage gap (`modInput` 87.89%) is a scattered set of error/guard handlers, output renderers, and download handlers -- dump the EXACT uncovered lines (`covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`) and target them, and watch two traps that make a "covered-looking" line stay red.** Coverage-campaign slice 4 (owner chose `modInput` via `AskUserQuestion` over the residual modules {`modInput`/`modPyramid`/`modSummaryStats`/`modPotentialParents`}). **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; a 9-test `testServer` suite; `R/modInput.R` 87.89% -> 100% (zero uncovered lines); overall 97.91% -> 98.76% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0.** **(a) Target red LINES, not the file %.** `covr::zero_coverage(cov)` filtered to the file gave the precise 62-line gap; each line mapped to one input state the three existing suites (`test_modInput*.R`) never supply. **(b) `testServer`'s expr runs IN the server env, so reach inner machinery by name -- two techniques cover most of a module.** (i) Call the module's INNER helpers directly for their guard/branch lines: `readDataFile(NULL, ...)` (NULL-guard), `readDataFile(list(name="p.xlsx", datapath=<fixture xlsx>), "fileTypeExcel", ",")` (the `readxl::read_excel` branch -- every prior test uploads `.csv`/`.txt`, so the real Excel read never ran), `readDataFile(list(datapath=<missing>), ...)` (the `tryCatch` read-error -> NULL + `showNotification`, wrap in `suppressWarnings` for the expected "cannot open file" warning). (ii) INJECT `storedResults(list(cleaned=, errors=, warnings=, changedCols=, hasChangedCols=, genotype=))` directly (it is a `reactiveVal` in scope), then `session$flushReact()` and read `output$qcSummaryUI` / `output$qcErrors` / the three `downloadHandler`s -- exercising every renderer branch in exact states (errors>0 vs ==0, warnings>0 -> the `panel-warning` class) WITHOUT driving a full QC run. **(c) A double-`tryCatch` swallows a naive mock -- mock the OUTER function, not the inner.** The observer's `qcResult` error handler (`modInput.R:474-491`, "QC Processing Error") is reached only if `runQcStudbook` THROWS; mocking `qcStudbook` to `stop()` does NOT reach it because `runQcStudbook` has its OWN internal `tryCatch` (`runQcStudbook.R:84-107`) that catches the stop and returns a valid `hasErrors` list. So mock `runQcStudbook` itself to `stop()` (and, to ALSO cover the raw-`errorLst` handler at `:464`, mock BOTH `qcStudbook=stop` + `runQcStudbook=stop` in one test); mock `qcStudbook={warning(...); getEmptyErrorLst()}` for the raw-`errorLst` warning handler at `:466` (its warning is absorbed internally by the real `runQcStudbook`, so nothing escapes the observer). All via `testthat::local_mocked_bindings(.package="nprcgenekeepr")`. **(d) `as.numeric("")` is SILENTLY `NA`; `as.numeric("NA")` warns.** To reach the `if (is.na(minAge)) minAge <- 2.0` fallback (`:453`) you must feed a value that coerces to `NA` WITHOUT a warning -- `""` works, but `"NA"`/`"abc"` warn and so are caught by the surrounding `tryCatch(warning=function(w) 2.0)` and never reach the `is.na` line. Probe `as.numeric()` condition behavior before assuming which literal is silent. **(e) The `futile.logger::flog.debug()` lazy-message trap (the reason two lines stayed red after the outputs rendered fine).** Branch code placed INSIDE a `flog.debug()` MESSAGE argument (`modInput.R:595/597`, an `if (nrow(errors) > 0L) paste0(...) else ""` inside the "Rendering qcErrors table" debug string) is NEVER evaluated at or below the default (INFO) threshold: `flog.debug` checks its threshold first and never forces the `msg` promise, so `output$qcErrors` renders correctly (class `json`, non-null) while the message -- and its branches -- never run. covr then shows the renderer body covered EXCEPT those in-message branch lines. Fix: raise the logger to DEBUG (`futile.logger::flog.threshold(DEBUG, name="nprcgenekeepr")`) before rendering -- exactly the path the app's "Debug on" checkbox exercises -- so both `nrow(errors)` branches build their strings; route the appender to a temp file (`flog.appender(appender.file(tempfile()), ...)`) to keep the console quiet and restore the console appender + INFO threshold with `withr::defer(..., envir = parent.frame())` in a small `local_debug_logging()` helper. **General rule: a lazy logger can hide branch coverage -- if uncovered lines sit inside a debug/trace message, raise the threshold, do not just call the output.** **(f) Probe-first discipline paid twice (S285/S286/S287):** a single throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe validated all 15 mechanisms (silent-NA literal, the three direct `readDataFile` calls, both mock shapes, the `storedResults` injection + output/download reads) BEFORE writing; a SECOND targeted probe then diagnosed the `flog.debug` trap (capturing the DEBUG log lines "First error: E" and "Rows: 0" proved which source lines the threshold change unlocks) BEFORE a second covr build -- so only one corrective edit and one confirming covr run were needed. Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass on first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 4 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S288, issue #111 slice 4 -> `modInput` 87.89% -> 100% via a 9-test `testServer` suite; overall 97.91% -> 98.76%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite clean; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 268 -- **A residual Shiny-module coverage gap can be a SINGLE untested code path, not a scattered set -- `modPyramid` (89.72%) was uncovered ONLY in its `downloadPlot` content function; the renderers were already green because `testServer` flush cycles evaluate them.** Coverage-campaign slice 5 (owner picked #111 and the `modPyramid` slice directly). **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; a 2-test `testServer` suite; `R/modPyramid.R` 89.72% -> 100% (zero uncovered lines); overall 98.76% -> 98.91% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0.** **(a) Not every module gap is scattered -- dump the exact uncovered lines FIRST and let the shape tell you the scope.** `covr::zero_coverage(cov)` filtered to the file showed the ONLY uncovered lines were the `downloadPlot` content function body (`modPyramid.R` L140, L142-151, the PNG-writing path). Everything else -- `output$pyramidPlotUI`/`pyramidPlot`/`pyramidStats` and the `observe` -- was ALREADY covered. **Why the renderers were already green (contrast Learning 265's modORIPReporting, whose whole server body was 31.7%):** the existing `test_modPyramid.R` never accesses those outputs, but its `session$setInputs(...)` / reactive-read calls trigger `testServer` flush cycles that evaluate the bound outputs anyway; a `downloadHandler`, by contrast, runs its content function ONLY when the download is invoked (`path <- output$downloadPlot`), which no existing test did -> that one path was the whole ~10-point gap. **Lesson: read the uncovered-line DUMP, do not assume "module at 89% == many scattered branches"; here it was one handler, and the whole slice was 2 tests.** **(b) The fixture must satisfy the RENDERING helper's column contract, which differs from the existing tests' fixture.** `getPyramidPlot()` (called inside both the plot renderer and the download content fn) requires `sex` and `age` columns; the existing `test_modPyramid.R` fixtures carry `id`/`sex`/`birth` (NO `age`) and get away with it only because they never render the plot. Driving the download therefore needs a fixture with a numeric `age` column (`data.frame(id, sex = rep(c("M","F"), n), age = <spread of years>)`), and the OTHER inputs (`ageBin`/`ageUnit`/`colorScheme`/`showCounts`/`ageLabelSize`) must ALSO be set, or `getPyramidPlot` errors: `if (ageUnit == "months" ...)` with `input$ageUnit == NULL` is `if (logical(0))` -> "argument is of length zero". **(c) Cover BOTH branches of the height guard, and assert the artifact is genuinely a PNG.** L140 `plotHeight <- if (!is.null(input$plotHeight)) input$plotHeight else 600L` -- one test sets `plotHeight = 900L` (non-null), one leaves it unset (`else 600L`). Read the download via `path <- output$downloadPlot`, then assert `file.exists(path)`, `file.size(path) > 0`, and the PNG file signature (`identical(readBin(path, "raw", 4L), as.raw(c(0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47)))`) -- a stronger check than size>0 that the content function actually wrote a valid image. Probe-validated warning-free before writing (S285-S288 discipline; note `plotrix::pyramid.plot` prints `par("mar")` "5 5" to stdout when the plot draws -- benign console noise, not a warning). Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 5 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S289, issue #111 slice 5 -> `modPyramid` 89.72% -> 100% via a 2-test `downloadPlot` `testServer` suite; overall 98.76% -> 98.91%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite clean; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 269 -- **A residual Shiny-module gap can be MIXED-shape (a few scattered guard branches + a large block of never-invoked download handlers) -- and the "download" tests that look like coverage are `deparse()` structural greps, so the handler BODIES were never run.** Coverage-campaign slice 6 (owner said "proceed with next candidate slice" -> `modSummaryStats`, the lowest residual at 94.34%). **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; an 8-test `testServer` suite; `R/modSummaryStats.R` 94.34% -> 100% (zero uncovered lines); overall 98.91% -> 99.41% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0.** **(a) Dump the exact uncovered lines first (`covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`) -- the 36-line gap fell in three groups:** `asDataFrame()`'s `data.table` branch (L350), the two boxplot NULL guards (`meanKinshipBoxPlotGG` L510, `guBoxPlotGG` L570), and ELEVEN download-handler content/filename bodies (L748-847). The bulk (30 of 36 lines) was download handlers -- and `modSummaryStats` HAS six "download" test files, but they are `deparse(modSummaryStatsServer)` structural greps (`test_modPlotDownload.R`) or UI HTML greps; `test_modSummaryStats_relationships.R` even asserts, wrongly, that "we can't directly test downloadHandler content in testServer". Only `downloadKinship` was ever driven (the parity suite). **Lesson: a module with many download-related test files can still have every handler BODY uncovered -- greps over `deparse()` count source text without executing it.** **(b) `path <- output$downloadX` runs BOTH the content fn AND the (multi-line) filename fn.** The probe's files came out named `first_order_relationships_<Sys.Date()>.csv` / `relationship_classes_<date>.csv` -- the correct generated names -- proving the multi-line `filename = function() {...}` bodies (L769, L788) executed too, so a single `output$downloadX` access covers content AND filename lines (single-line `filename = function() paste0(...)` lambdas don't even get a separate covr line-tick). **(c) covr measures LINE coverage, not branch -- pick the fixture that runs the most lines.** The z-score download handlers wrap `plot <- zscoreHistogramPlot(); if (!is.null(plot)) ggsave(...)`; supplying a gv WITH the real plural `zScores` column makes the plot non-NULL so all four lines (assign, `if`, `ggsave`) run -- 100% line coverage in one test, no need to also test the NULL-skip branch. **(d) The two boxplot NULL guards are reached by DROPPING the column, and the resulting broken sibling outputs error SILENTLY.** `meanKinshipBoxPlotGG`/`guBoxPlotGG` `return(NULL)` only when their column is absent; feed a gv missing `meanKinship` (resp. `genomeUniqueness`) and read the RETURNED reactive (`session$getReturned()$meanKinshipBoxPlot()`) -> NULL. The histogram outputs (`output$mkHist`) that need that same column DO error on the `testServer` flush (`pretty(range(NULL))` -> "invalid 'x'"), but `testServer` STORES an output's error rather than throwing it -- it only rethrows when you ACCESS that output -- so as long as the test never reads the broken output, it passes cleanly (probe-verified before writing). **(e) TEST-AUTHORING trap: `read.csv`'s `type.convert` coerces a single-letter `"F"` to logical `FALSE` (`"M"` stays character).** Asserting `all(females$sex == "F")` on a downloaded founder CSV failed (FALSE == "F") while the male assertion passed by luck -- read such CSVs with `colClasses = "character"` when a column holds `T`/`F`/`TRUE`/`FALSE`-like tokens. **(f) HARNESS trap: `testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")` does NOT load the package** -- run `pkgload::load_all(".")` FIRST (as the CLAUDE.md fast-single-file recipe does) or nearly every bare-name test errors, producing a false ~226-offender read that LOOKS like a catastrophic regression but is a missing `load_all`. A green `covr::package_coverage()` overall (its run IS a full-suite execution) corroborates real suite health when the standalone `test_dir` read looks wrong -- reconcile before panicking. Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 6 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S290, issue #111 slice 6 -> `modSummaryStats` 94.34% -> 100% via an 8-test `testServer` suite; overall 98.91% -> 99.41%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 270 -- **A residual Shiny-module MIXED gap can be as small as 9 lines and include eventReactive INTERNAL input-guard branches that the existing happy-path fixtures STRUCTURALLY cannot reach -- and one NULL-input state can cover two uncovered groups at once.** Coverage-campaign slice 7 (owner said "continue #111" -> `modPotentialParents`, the lowest residual module at 94.67%). **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; a 3-test `testServer` suite; `R/modPotentialParents.R` 94.67% -> 100% (zero uncovered lines); overall 99.41% -> 99.53% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0.** **(a) Dump the exact uncovered lines first (`covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`) -- the 9-line gap fell in three groups:** the `potentialParents` eventReactive's NULL-pedigree `return(NULL)` (L257) and NULL/NA gestation fallback `maxGest <- 210L` (L261); the `statusMessage` renderUI "No pedigree is loaded" warning branch (L283-287); and the `downloadParents` filename + content handlers (L321, L324). **(b) The existing suite could not reach the eventReactive guards because its server fixtures are all happy-path.** `test_modPotentialParents.R` exhaustively covers the four pure helpers and drives `tableData()`/`gestationDefault()`, but every server test passes a VALID pedigree and a NUMERIC gestation, so `if (is.null(ped)) return(NULL)` (L257) and `if (is.null(maxGest) || is.na(maxGest)) maxGest <- 210L` (L261) never execute -- a module can sit at 94.67% with its INTERNAL input-guard branches untested even though the happy path is thoroughly exercised. **Lesson: when a module's residual lines are input guards, the happy-path fixtures will never reach them; drive the NULL/NA inputs explicitly.** **(c) One NULL-pedigree state covers TWO uncovered groups at once.** Booting with `pedigree = shiny::reactive(NULL)` + `session$setInputs(findParents = 1)`, then READING `session$getReturned()$tableData()` forces the eventReactive -> `return(NULL)` (L257 -> flatten -> 0-row), and READING `as.character(output$statusMessage)` forces the renderUI's no-pedigree guard (L283-287) -- both branches guard the SAME NULL-pedigree state; assert with `any(grepl("No pedigree is loaded", as.character(output$statusMessage)))` (the length-2 renderUI `as.character` quirk from Learning 265/268 holds here too). **(d) The OTHER statusMessage branches + `resultsTable` were ALREADY green via `testServer` flush** (Learning 268's mechanism): the existing tests' `setInputs`/reactive-reads evaluate the bound `renderUI`/`renderDT` outputs in their reached states (found-candidates, no-fromCenter, 0-row, not-yet-clicked), so only the never-reached no-pedigree branch stayed red -- confirming again that a `downloadHandler`, which runs ONLY when invoked, is the classic residual (L321 filename + L324 content, via `path <- output$downloadParents`, which writes a real 50-row CSV with columns `id,nSires,nDams,sires,dams`). **(e) The NA-gestation fallback (L261) needs a VALID ped so control reaches past the NULL-ped guard:** `session$setInputs(maxGestationalPeriod = NA, findParents = 1)` with a rhesus `fromCenter=TRUE` ped -> `is.na(NA)` TRUE -> `maxGest <- 210L` -> `getPotentialParents` runs and still yields the full candidate set (assert `nrow(td) > 0` and `"BRI2MW" %in% td$id` -- proving the NA input did not break the computation, a stronger check than "no error"). The `observeEvent(pedigree(), ...)` prefill observer has `ignoreNULL=TRUE` (default), so a NULL pedigree never fires it -- booting with `reactive(NULL)` is safe (no prefill error). **(f) Probe-first discipline (S285-S290) paid again:** one throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe validated all three mechanisms (the NULL-ped double-coverage, the NA fallback, and `path <- output$downloadParents` returning a 50-row CSV) BEFORE writing -- zero authoring surprises. **(g) Tooling note: `covr::zero_coverage(cov)` returns columns `filename, functions, line, value`** -- select `line` (the FILE line number); the data.frame's printed ROW NAMES are global indices, NOT source lines (selecting `first_line`/`last_line` errors "undefined columns selected"). Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 7 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S291, issue #111 slice 7 -> `modPotentialParents` 94.67% -> 100% via a 3-test `testServer` suite; overall 99.41% -> 99.53%; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 271 -- **A heavily-tested Shiny module's residual gap can be ENTIRELY defensive shape-guards + one alternate condition-handler that the happy-path suites structurally cannot reach -- and a single mocked-return value can cover several cascading NULL guards at once.** Coverage-campaign slice 8 (owner said "continue #111" -> `modGeneticValue`, the last residual Shiny module at 96.26%). **Test-only; PRE-RED->RED gated; a 5-test `testServer` suite; `R/modGeneticValue.R` 96.26% -> 100% (zero uncovered lines); overall 99.53% -> 99.71% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0. This CLOSES the residual-Shiny-module tier of #111 -- all seven modules now 100%.** `modGeneticValue` already had TWO large suites (`test_modGeneticValue.R` ~60 tests + `test_modGeneticValue_kinshipOverrides.R`), yet sat at 96.26% because every uncovered line was a branch those happy-path fixtures cannot reach. **(a) Dump the exact uncovered lines first (`covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`) -- the 13-line gap fell in five groups:** the `kinshipOverrideData` `> 0.5` warning handler (`showNotification` + `invokeRestart("muffleWarning")`, L193-197); the `gvResults` empty-probands fallback (`probands <- ped$id; ped$population <- TRUE`, L244-245); the `return(report)` guard when the report lacks `indivMeanKin` (L286) and the three downstream NULL-report guards in `gvaView`/`geneticValues`/`topAnimals` (L319, L442, L454); the demote `else` when the report has no `value` column (L298); and the `gvSummary` `genomeUniquenessSE` fallback when `guSE` is absent (L366). **(b) One mocked-return value covers FOUR cascading NULL guards at once.** Mock `reportGV` to return `list(report = NULL, ...)`: `report <- gvReport$report` is NULL, `!"indivMeanKin" %in% names(NULL)` is TRUE, so `gvResults()` returns NULL via L286 -- and that NULL then cascades so reading `gvaView()` (L319), `geneticValues()` (L442), and `topAnimals()` (L454) each short-circuit to NULL. One state, four lines. The lesson: when several consumers guard the same upstream reactive, drive the upstream to the guarded value ONCE and read all the consumers. **(c) The WARNING handler is a DIFFERENT path from the already-covered ERROR handler.** The existing override suite tests a valid <= 0.5 override (no condition) and a malformed file (`checkKinshipOverrides` `stop()`s -> the module's `error` handler). The uncovered `warning` handler needs an override VALUE > 0.5: `checkKinshipOverrides` `warning()`s (not `stop()`s) on an off-diagonal > 0.5 (valid only for inbred pairs, D6); the module surfaces it as a `showNotification` and MUFFLES it (`invokeRestart("muffleWarning")`), keeping the validated override. Reading `kinshipOverrideData()` alone exercises the handler -- no `reportGV` run needed -- and asserting `expect_no_warning(...)` around the read characterizes the muffle. (Do NOT drive a real `reportGV` with a 0.6 override: `applyKinshipOverrides` enforces the positive-semi-definiteness bound and would reject it, aborting the run -- an unrelated failure. The warning fires in the reactive BEFORE reportGV, so a mock or a direct read is the clean vehicle.) **(d) A mocked `reportGV` lets you control the report SHAPE to reach shape-guards** -- `report = NULL` (missing-`indivMeanKin` path), a report WITHOUT `value` (demote `else` -> `rep(FALSE, nrow)`), a report WITH `genomeUniquenessSE` but NOT `guSE` (the `gvSummary` middle `else if`). Note `trimPedigree()`/`findGeneration()` run on the real ped BEFORE `reportGV`, so the fixture must still be a valid pedigree even when reportGV is mocked; a minimal founders+offspring ped suffices. **(e) The empty-probands fallback (L244-245) is reached most directly by giving the ped a `population` column that is all FALSE** -- that skips the population-INFERENCE block (L232-238, already covered) and leaves `probands <- ped$id[ped$population]` empty, firing the fallback. **(f) covr is LINE coverage: several sibling `else` branches were NOT in the uncovered set** (the `mkCol`/`guCol` "meanKinship"/"genomeUniqueness" fallbacks, the `fgDisplay` bare-FG else) -- existing tests already reach them via the `fakeGvReport` mocks and `getValidPedigreeSubset` variations. Trust the dump: do NOT pad tests for branches covr already reports green; target ONLY the lines in `zero_coverage()`. **(g) Probe-first discipline (S285-S291) paid again:** one throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe validated all five mechanisms (the muffled warning read, the population-all-FALSE fallback, the report=NULL 4-line cascade, the no-`value` demote, and the `genomeUniquenessSE` summary branch) BEFORE writing -- zero authoring surprises. **(h) Helper-name hygiene:** name new-file helpers distinctly (`mkCovPed`/`covFileInfo`/`covFakeReport`) so they cannot collide with the sibling files' `makeValidTestPed`/`fileInfo`/`fakeGvReport`. Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). **Campaign note: with all residual Shiny modules now 100%, the remaining #111 gaps are small single-file helpers (`checkKinshipOverrides` 96.43%, `loadSpeciesOverrides` 96.72%, `dataframe2string` 96.88%, `getPotentialParents` 96.88%, `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` 97.30%, `modPedigree` 97.72%, ...) -- a different slice shape (pure-function/branch backfill, not `testServer`).** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 8 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.** (S292, issue #111 slice 8 -> `modGeneticValue` 96.26% -> 100% via a 5-test `testServer` suite; overall 99.53% -> 99.71%; last residual Shiny module closed; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on new file; spell clean)

#### Learning 272 -- **Once the Shiny-module tier is closed, the remaining #111 residuals are TINY single-file helper gaps (often 1-2 lines) that batch safely into ONE session -- and the batch is the OWNER'S scope decision, made from a per-file exact-line triage, with a "probe-first, drop-if-it-resists" safeguard; one of the six lines turned out to be genuinely unreachable dead defensive code and was documented-and-left, not forced.** Coverage-campaign slice 9 (owner said "continue #111 with checkKinshipOverrides", then -- when I surfaced that the remaining gaps looked trivial -- asked whether more than one could be handled safely in one session). **Test-only; three `AskUserQuestion` gates (PRE-RED->RED for checkKinshipOverrides, then a batch-SCOPE decision, then a combined PRE-RED->RED for the other five); SIX plain-function files 96.43-98.36% -> 100%; overall 99.71% -> 99.83% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on all six changed files.** **(a) After the module tier, the slice SHAPE changes: these are called directly, so the vehicle is an ordinary `test_that` with a small crafted fixture, NOT `testServer` (exactly as S292's handoff predicted).** A single-file helper can sit at 96-98% because ONE line is unreached: `checkKinshipOverrides` L47 = the non-numeric-`kinship` `stop()` (every existing test passes numeric/NA/negative, never a character/factor column); `orderReport` L42 = the `getFounders(ped)` fallback when the report lacks a `parentage` column; `getPyramidPlot` L40 = the `ageUnit=="months"` age*12 branch; `loadSpeciesOverrides`/`mergeSpeciesOverrides` L139-140 = the append branch for a species NOT in the bundled table (every test overrides "RHESUS", already present -> only the else/match branch runs); `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` L146/L152 = TWO distinct guards (unnamed `indivMeanKin` -> early return; ped with no `exit` column -> `exit` defaulted to `as.Date(NA)`) needing two separate calls; `getPotentialParents` L151-152 = the empty-`potentialDams` fallback (a candidate dam with NO offspring is dropped by the proven-breeder `births_plus_minus_one` filter, emptying the set and forcing "accept all old-enough females"). **(b) REUSE the prior slice's saved covr object to pinpoint lines without a 3-4 min re-run.** `checkKinshipOverrides`'s sole uncovered line (L47) was read straight out of S292's `cov292_after.rds` via `covr::zero_coverage(cov)[filename ~ file]` -- the standing "measure with `NOT_CRAN=true`" discipline meant the artifact's 96.43% matched to the decimal, so no covr call was needed to SCOPE the gap. **(c) The batch question was the OWNER'S decision, not mine to assume (FM #23 / observation-vs-decision).** The honest answer was "yes for the genuinely trivial ones, but simplicity is NOT uniform -- triage first": I dumped EVERY remaining sub-100% file's exact uncovered lines from the fresh covr object and classified them (trivial plain-fn / borderline / Shiny-module-needs-own-slice) BEFORE recommending, then posed a scope `AskUserQuestion`. The efficiency case is real: the expensive part is the two ~3-4 min verifications (full covr + `--as-cran`), paid ONCE for the batch instead of N times; the per-file work (dump lines, write 1-3 tests, lint) is minutes. Batching complete VERTICAL slices (each file: test -> 100% -> verified) is NOT horizontal slicing (FM #25) and, when the owner scopes it upfront as the session's single deliverable, is NOT "1-and-done" erosion. **(d) Fan the independent single-file investigations out to parallel probe agents (a `Workflow`), each PROVING reachability with `covr::function_coverage(fn, code)` before any repo file is touched.** `function_coverage` instruments ONE function while running an arbitrary `code` block with the package loaded (handles intra-package deps, unlike single-file `file_coverage`); check that the TARGET line numbers are absent from `zero_coverage(cov)$line` -- other lines the fixture misses stay uncovered by IT alone but are covered by the existing suite, so the union is 100%. Six agents, ~163s wall-clock, throwaway probes in scratchpad only (no tracked file touched until the gate). **(e) The probe caught the one genuinely-UNREACHABLE line and the safeguard fired: document-and-leave, do NOT force it.** `dataframe2string` L49 (`rowNames <- as.character(seq_len(NROW(m)))` under `addRowNames` when `dimnames(object)[[1L]]` is NULL) is dead for the documented input type: `dimnames.data.frame(x) == list(row.names(x), names(x))`, so `dimnames[[1L]]` mirrors `row.names()` and is NULL only when `nRows==0`, already intercepted upstream at L36-37; a matrix can't reach it either (no rownames -> nRows==0 -> L37; with rownames -> the L47 `if` is FALSE). Reaching it needs a hand-crafted malformed object the function is not meant to accept. It is inherited defensive code from `print.data.frame`; I left production UNTOUCHED (a `# nocov` or a waiver is a separate production decision, out of scope for a test-only session) and documented it -- rather than writing a bad test against behavior the function does not support. **(f) Assertion values still need a real run to confirm.** `function_coverage` proves the line EXECUTES but does not check `expect_*`; the two numeric/behavioral assertions I was least sure of (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` foc = 0.05 + .40/2 = 0.25; `getPotentialParents` fallback `dams == "DAM"`) were confirmed only by running the test files (all six: 0 failed / 0 error / 0 warning). Mirror an existing sibling fixture when one matches (the foc/mp/dk shape came straight from the file's `clampPed` test). Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). **Campaign note: after S9 only THREE files remain sub-100% -- `dataframe2string` (unreachable L49; a waive decision) and the two Shiny modules `modPedigree` (97.72%) and `modBreedingGroups` (98.56%), which are back to the `testServer` slice shape (S289-292).** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 9 of #111) -- three-gated; test-only; no bug found (all six lines were correct code, one unreachable); Phase-3E N/A.** (S293, issue #111 slice 9 -> six single-file helpers to 100% [checkKinshipOverrides, correctUnknownParentMeanKinship, loadSpeciesOverrides, orderReport, getPyramidPlot, getPotentialParents]; dataframe2string L49 proven unreachable and left; overall 99.71% -> 99.83%; probe-workflow-assisted; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on all six changed files; spell clean)

#### Learning 273 -- **The final #111 residuals are the last two Shiny modules -- back to the `testServer` slice shape -- and a defensive line that is REACHABLE-out-of-contract (unlike a provably-unreachable one) gets a TEST, not a waive: the guarded degenerate input then warns downstream and the warning is suppressed at the test.** Coverage-campaign slice 10 (owner said "continue #111", then approved BATCHING both remaining modules in one session -- their gaps were trivially small on inspection, S293's stated condition for a batch). **Test-only; one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED->RED + scope gate, one landing gate; two `testServer` files (2 + 3 tests); `modPedigree.R` 97.72% -> 100% and `modBreedingGroups.R` 98.56% -> 100%; overall 99.83% -> 99.99% (`NOT_CRAN=true`); --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on both new files.** **(a) The two residual modules' gaps were the two classic `testServer`-residual shapes:** never-invoked `downloadHandler`s + an error handler the happy-path never trips (`modPedigree` L274-277 = the focal-file `read.csv` error branch, reachable only with a bad `focalAnimalFile$datapath`; L371/L374 = the export download filename + content, via `path <- output$exportPedigree`); and defensive guards the happy-path structurally cannot reach (`modBreedingGroups` L175 = the `getKinshipMatrix` branch returning a geneticValues-SUPPLIED kinship matrix -- every existing test and the whole `_kinshipOverrides.R` suite pass `geneticValues=NULL`, forcing the pedigree RECOMPUTE, so the supplied-matrix branch never ran; L505/510/515 = the NULL-`groupResults` guards in the returned `score`/`unassigned`/`groupKinship` reactives -- existing tests always `formGroups` first, so `groupResults()` is never read while still its initial `reactiveVal(NULL)`). **(b) One test covers THREE NULL-guards at once by reading the returned reactives BEFORE forming groups:** boot the module, do NOT click `formGroups`, then read `session$getReturned()$score()` / `$unassigned()` / `$groupKinship()` -> each hits its `if (is.null(res)) return(...)` default (0L / character(0) / NULL). The internal `reactiveVal` (`groupResults`) is directly readable in the `testServer` eval env, so L175 was PROVEN by asserting `groupResults()$kmat` is `identical()` to the supplied matrix -- the strong check that the supplied-matrix branch ran, not a recompute (per [testServer-mechanics]). **(c) The load-bearing NEW distinction: reachable-out-of-contract vs provably-unreachable defensive code.** `modBreedingGroups` L269 (`currentGroups <- currentGroups[seq_len(numGp)]`) is guarded by `if (length(currentGroups) > numGp)`; the author's own comment says `currentGroups` "can never exceed numGp" because it is built with `seq_len(numGp)` (seeding) or is length 1 (unseeded). So the guard fires ONLY when `numGp < 1`, i.e. `nGroups=0` -- below the UI `min=1`. Unlike `dataframe2string` L49 (S293: provably unreachable by ANY input, structurally, so document-and-waive), L269 IS reachable: the `covr::function_coverage` probe drove `nGroups=0` and hit L269 with no uncaught error. **By the project's own standard (test reachable defensive branches; waive only the truly-unreachable), a reachable line gets a test.** The owner confirmed test-not-waive at the gate. **(d) A degenerate out-of-contract input that reaches a guard often MISBEHAVES further downstream -- suppress the incidental warning, not the branch.** `nGroups=0` makes `groupAddAssign(numGp=0)` emit "number of items to replace is not a multiple of replacement length"; the L269 guard itself is clean (a `[integer(0)]` subset, no warning), so `suppressWarnings(session$setInputs(formGroups=1))` keeps the suite clean while still covering L269. Same pattern for `modPedigree`'s error handler: `read.csv` on a nonexistent path WARNS ("cannot open file") before it THROWS, and the module's `tryCatch(error=)` catches only the error -> wrap the triggering `setInputs` in `suppressWarnings` (verified after: full suite still 7 baseline warnings, 0 new). **(e) `covr::function_coverage(<moduleServerFn>, { <testServer scenarios> })` proves per-line reachability for MODULE code too, before the gate.** Instrument the module server fn, run all the intended `testServer` scenarios in the code block, then confirm every TARGET line is ABSENT from `zero_coverage(fc)$line` (other module lines the probe scenarios miss stay in the list but are covered by the existing suite, so the union is 100%). This caught nothing wrong here (all 11 target lines reachable) but is the same discipline that flagged `dataframe2string` L49 in S293. Backfill on CORRECT code -> tests pass first run (RED = the coverage gap), no `R/`/NAMESPACE change, Phase-3E N/A (no runtime surface; FM #24 has no target). **Campaign note: after S10 the ONLY file below 100% package-wide is `dataframe2string.R` (96.88%, the proven-unreachable L49 waive decision) -- so #111's line-coverage work is effectively complete pending that one owner `# nocov` call; the two Shiny modules and all single-file helpers are at 100%.** Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL coverage session (slice 10 of #111) -- PRE-RED->RED gated; test-only; no bug found (all lines correct; L269 reachable-and-tested, not waived); Phase-3E N/A.** (S294, issue #111 slice 10 -> `modPedigree` 97.72% -> 100% [2 tests] and `modBreedingGroups` 98.56% -> 100% [3 tests] via `testServer` suites; L269 reachable-out-of-contract so tested via nGroups=0, not waived; overall 99.83% -> 99.99%; probe via `function_coverage`; no NAMESPACE change; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0 on both new files; spell clean)

#### Learning 274 -- **A proven-unreachable defensive line has THREE dispositions, and the choice is the OWNER'S: leave it (accept sub-100%), WAIVE it (`# nocov`), or REMOVE it (delete the dead code). When removal is chosen, PROVE behavior-inert with `identical()` over an input battery BEFORE the edit -- do not remove on reasoning alone -- and treat it as a `[refactor-only]` change (no synthetic RED, gate PRE-RED->REFACTOR).** Immediate follow-up to S294: the S294 handoff framed `dataframe2string` L49 as leave-or-`# nocov`; on explanation the owner picked a THIRD option -- remove the dead branch outright (L47-50, `if (is.null(rowNames)) rowNames <- as.character(seq_len(NROW(m)))`). **REFACTOR-only; one PRE-RED->REFACTOR gate + one landing gate; `dataframe2string.R` 96.88% -> 100% and OVERALL PACKAGE 99.99% -> 100.00%; --as-cran 0/0/0; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0. This effectively completes #111 (100% line coverage package-wide).** **(a) The dead-code proof (why L49 is unreachable):** for a data frame `dimnames(object)[[1L]]` IS `row.names(object)`, which is never NULL for a frame with >=1 row (R materializes automatic row names); the 0-row case already returns at L36-37 before this branch; and a matrix either has 0 rows (`length(row.names(m))==0` -> L37) or has row names (`dimnames[[1L]]` non-NULL -> the `is.null` guard is FALSE). So no input the function can process reaches L49. **(b) Prove it EMPIRICALLY, not by argument ([verify-first]/[identical-proof]).** Define a guard-removed copy of the fn and compare `identical(current(x), removed(x))` over a battery: `pedOne`, a frame with DEFAULT row names, a frame with CUSTOM `row.names()`, 1-column, 1-row, 0-column, 0-row, a matrix WITH row names, a matrix WITHOUT row names, and `addRowNames=FALSE` -- all 10 identical here. This is the removal analogue of the waiver decision: a WAIVE (`# nocov`) hides the line; REMOVAL deletes it; both need the same unreachability proof, but removal ALSO needs the identical-output proof because it changes the source the function actually runs. Even in the impossible-to-reach case removal would not crash (`c("", NULL)` -> `""`, cbind-recycled) -- worth checking so removal is provably safe, not just coverage-cosmetic. **(c) `document()` as the `[document-zero-delta]` check can surface PRE-EXISTING, UNRELATED drift -- do not bundle it (FM #8 scope creep).** Running `devtools::document()` after the edit produced ZERO `man/` delta (the removal is roxygen-inert, correct) but silently rewrote `NAMESPACE`, dropping `importFrom(lubridate, day)` + `importFrom(lubridate, month)` -- two redundant lines no roxygen `@importFrom` declares (`getPyramidPlot.R` calls `lubridate::day`/`lubridate::month` via explicit `::`, which needs no import). That drift PRE-DATES this session (the committed NAMESPACE was already non-idempotent under `document()`), is unrelated to `dataframe2string`, and is harmless (package checks 0/0/0 with the extra lines). **REVERT the unrelated NAMESPACE change** (`git checkout NAMESPACE` -- safe per [[check-status-before-destructive-git]] because it was MY `document()` output, understood and explained, NOT a user edit) to keep the commit scoped to the dead-code removal, and REPORT the drift as a separate ~30-second cleanup for its own session rather than folding it in. **(d) Removing the single last dead line took the WHOLE package to 100%** (covr `NOT_CRAN=true`, `percent_coverage` == 100.0000, the sub-100 file list empty) -- the culmination of the S285-S294 #111 campaign. Backfill/refactor on CORRECT code: no new test, existing `test_dataframe2string.R` still 0/0/0; Phase-3E N/A (pure exported fn, only caller is the test file, no runtime wiring; FM #24 has no target). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-only dead-code-removal session (issue #111 tail) -- PRE-RED->REFACTOR gated; production source change (behavior-inert, proven); no bug found; Phase-3E N/A.** (S295, issue #111 -> removed proven-dead `dataframe2string.R` L47-50; `identical()`-proven over 10 inputs; `dataframe2string.R` 96.88% -> 100% and OVERALL PACKAGE -> 100.00%; unrelated NAMESPACE `lubridate` day/month drift found + reverted + flagged; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0; document() zero man/ delta)

#### Learning 275 -- **The first real BUG-FIX after the #111 coverage campaign: a coverage slice that CHARACTERIZED a known defect (with an issue number + a "when fixed, update these assertions" breadcrumb) makes the fix session's RED step turnkey -- flip the characterization tests to the corrected contract and add edge cases. Root-cause was a classic dead-write (restoration written to a local that the next line overwrites); the fix is a one-line retarget, but the value is in mapping blast radius (an idempotent downstream workaround MASKS the bug, so Phase-3E is satisfied by proving the integration output identical, not by a full app launch) and keeping scope surgical.** Issue #117: `fixColumnNames()`'s "clean up possible overreach" block (L31-36) restored the genotype headers `first_name`/`second_name` (collapsed to `firstname`/`secondname` by the L28 underscore strip) by writing to `cols`, which L39 (`cols <- newCols`) immediately overwrote; the function returns `newCols`, so the restore never reached `newColNames` and BOTH the space form (`"First Name"`) and the underscore form (`"first_name"`) came back collapsed. **Strict TDD; PRE-RED->RED (with surgical-scope decision) + RED->GREEN + landing gates; `R/fixColumnNames.R` 6-line in-body change; `tests/testthat/test_fixColumnNames.R` rewritten 2 -> 8 blocks/10 assertions; full suite 0/0/0; --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); lint 0; spell clean; document() zero man/ delta.** **(a) The predecessor coverage slice left a perfect breadcrumb.** S293's #111 backfill hit L31-36 by feeding the space form, but per scope discipline it CHARACTERIZED the defective output (asserting `newColNames == c("firstname","secondname","id")`) in clearly-commented "KNOWN DEFECT ... tracked as issue #117 ... when fixed these assertions must be updated" tests, and filed the issue with the root cause + the `newCols`-vs-`cols` fix hint. So this session's RED was not discovery -- it was converting those two characterization tests to the CORRECT contract and widening to edge cases. **Lesson: when a coverage slice reaches buggy code, do not silently pin the bug -- pin it, comment it as a known defect, file the issue with the fix location, and leave a "update these assertions when fixed" note; the fix session then inherits a turnkey RED.** **(b) The bug is the classic dead-write / write-to-discarded-local.** `newCols <- gsub("_","",cols)` computes the collapsed vector; the block wrote the RESTORE to `cols` (the pre-strip value) while `newCols` (the returned vector) kept the collapsed form; `cols <- newCols` then threw the restore away. Fix = retarget the block to `newCols` (condition `any(tolower(newCols)=="firstname")`, assignment `newCols[...] <- "first_name"`, and the `secondname` twin) so it lands on the returned vector -- exactly what the sibling `fixGenotypeCols()` already does on a ped's names. Minimal, no refactor (REFACTOR skipped: the block now mirrors `fixGenotypeCols`, and extracting a shared helper across the two functions would be cross-function scope creep -- FM #8; the redundant `tolower()` keeps parallel structure and is harmless). **(c) Map the blast radius before scoping -- an idempotent downstream workaround was MASKING the bug.** `fixColumnNames()`'s only production caller is `qcStudbook()` (`:179`), which LATER runs `fixGenotypeCols()` (`:288`) -- a `@noRd` "this is not a good fix ... a better solution is to avoid the problem" workaround that restores `firstname`->`first_name` on the ped's names. So the app's OBSERVABLE output was already correct (the workaround caught it); only DIRECT callers of `fixColumnNames` saw the collapsed names. This makes Phase-3E satisfiable WITHOUT a full app launch: the net `qcStudbook` behavior is provably unchanged (the three adjacent tests -- `test_qcStudbook`/`test_species_first_class`/`test_exampleData_columnNames` -- pass unchanged, and a live `qcStudbook()` run on a genotype-bearing pedigree returns `first_name`/`second_name`, no collapsed forms). **Kept scope SURGICAL (owner-gated at PRE-RED): fix `fixColumnNames` only; leave `fixGenotypeCols` as a harmless idempotent safety net for a separate future evaluation, rather than removing the now-redundant workaround in the same session (FM #8).** **(d) A bug-fix RED should include a regression INVARIANT guard that passes before AND after.** Alongside the 7 failing corrected-contract assertions, one test fed a no-genotype header (`ego_id`/`sire.id`/`dam_id`/`sex`/`birth_date`) and asserted the canonical mapping (`id`/`sire`/`dam`/`sex`/`birth`) AND the exact `underScoreRemoved` diagnostic (reusing `test_qcStudbook.R:123`'s known-correct string) -- it passed in RED and stayed green in GREEN, proving the fix is surgical (touches only the genotype path, not the normal one). This is the bug-fix analogue of the coverage-slice invariant guard. **(e) Do NOT over-engineer an incidental diagnostic.** `colChange()` builds the `underScoreRemoved` change-record string; the retarget cleaned up the previously-spurious record for the underscore form (now `character(0)` -- the strip is net-cancelled by the restore) as a byproduct, while the space form still records a defensible `"firstname ... to first_name ..."` note. The issue's complaint was the wrong `newColNames`, not the record's wording; asserting the underscore-form `character(0)` is a legitimate correctness check (the spurious record is gone), but chasing a "perfect" space-form record would have been scope creep for no user-visible gain -- left as-is. **(f) Verify like any package-code change even for a 6-line fix:** RED shows the exact failures (7/8 blocks, 9 assertions), GREEN flips them all green with the invariant still green, then full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) 0/0/0 with the 7 baseline warnings unchanged, lint 0 on both files, spell clean, `document()` zero `man/` delta (in-body change is roxygen-inert; the re-surfaced `lubridate` day/month NAMESPACE drift is the known pre-existing S295 non-idempotency -- reverted per [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], not this session's), `--as-cran` 0/0/0. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a genuine BUG-FIX session (issue #117) -- strict TDD RED->GREEN, no REFACTOR needed; production source change (behavior-changing for direct callers, net-inert through `qcStudbook`); the first non-coverage deliverable after the S285-S295 #111 campaign.** (S297, issue #117 -> retargeted the `fixColumnNames()` overreach-cleanup block from `cols` to `newCols` so `first_name`/`second_name` reach `newColNames`; `test_fixColumnNames.R` rewritten 2 -> 8 blocks; `fixGenotypeCols` redundancy left for a separate session; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0; spell clean; document() zero man/ delta)

#### Learning 276 -- **Closing a "separate finding, own session" spinoff is a turnkey next-session deliverable, and RESOLVING a standing gotcha means the gotcha must be REMOVED from future handoffs -- not carried forever. For a GENERATED file (NAMESPACE), the regeneration TOOL is the fix (do not hand-edit a "do not edit by hand" file); the safety mechanism is a GUARD that the tool's diff is EXACTLY the expected change with zero collateral delta, which is the generated-file analogue of the `identical()`-battery proof used for hand-edited source.** The ~30-second NAMESPACE cleanup that S294/S295 (Learning 274c) first surfaced and S297 (Learning 275) re-carried: `devtools::document()` was non-idempotent for `NAMESPACE` because two lines -- `importFrom(lubridate, day)` and `importFrom(lubridate, month)` -- had no roxygen `@importFrom lubridate` tag anywhere in `R/`, so each regeneration dropped them. **REFACTOR-only (generated-file regeneration, no behavior change); one PRE-RED->REFACTOR gate + one landing gate; NAMESPACE loses exactly 2 lines, zero `man/` delta; full suite 0/0/0 (`NOT_CRAN=true`); spell clean; --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); 0 corrections.** **(a) Prove redundancy + behavior-inertness by grep, not by an `identical()` battery -- because nothing EXECUTED changes.** Unlike S295's dead-code removal (which deleted a line the function actually ran, so it needed a 10-input `identical()` proof), removing an unused `importFrom` changes only the package's import environment, not any code path -- PROVIDED no code depends on it. Two greps settle it: (i) of the 13 `importFrom(lubridate, ...)` lines, only `day` and `month` lack a roxygen `@importFrom lubridate` tag (the other 11 all have one), so those two are the orphans `document()` will drop; (ii) `day`/`month` are used ONLY as fully-qualified `lubridate::month`/`lubridate::day` (`getPyramidPlot.R:99,101`) and there is NO bare `month()`/`day()` call in `R/` (`grep -rE '\b(month|day)\(' R/ | grep -v 'lubridate::'` = empty). A fully-qualified `::` call resolves at call time regardless of imports, so removing the import is inert. **(b) The regeneration GUARD replaces the identical-proof.** Run `devtools::document()` and require the diff be EXACTLY `-importFrom(lubridate,day)` + `-importFrom(lubridate,month)` with zero `man/` delta; if `document()` produces ANY other change, STOP and reassess (it would mean unrelated roxygen drift, or a hand-curated NAMESPACE block being clobbered). Here the diff was exactly the two deletions. Do NOT hand-edit `NAMESPACE` (its header says "Generated by roxygen2: do not edit by hand") -- the tool is the correct author; the owner was offered a surgical hand-edit alternative at the gate and chose `document()`. **(c) RESOLVING a standing gotcha is itself the deliverable's payoff -- and the gotcha must now be DROPPED from the handoff, not repeated.** A second `document()` run left `NAMESPACE` byte-identical (md5 unchanged), proving `document()` is now IDEMPOTENT for `NAMESPACE`. The "`document()` is non-idempotent for NAMESPACE -- revert the day/month drift" gotcha that S295->S297 carried in every handoff is now STALE and REMOVED from this session's forward gotchas (carrying a resolved gotcha forever is the mirror image of FM #17 protocol-erosion: noise that outlives its cause). **(d) Phase-3E for an import-only change is covered by `--as-cran`.** The check rebuilds vignettes and runs examples + the full test suite, exercising `getPyramidPlot` (the `lubridate::month`/`lubridate::day` caller); Status: OK with the imports removed IS the runtime proof that no path broke -- no separate app launch needed (same reasoning as Learning 275c: the deliverable's runtime surface is exercised by the check battery). **(e) A prior session's "flagged for its own session" spinoff is high-ROI turnkey work.** S295's Learning 274c carried the exact two lines, the why (no roxygen tag; explicit `::` usage), the harmlessness (checks 0/0/0 with them), and the fix recipe (`document()`); the CHANGELOG S295 entry even had a "Separate finding" bullet naming it. That handoff discipline made this session's scoping a near-zero-cost confirm-then-regenerate. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-only generated-file-cleanup session (the S295-flagged NAMESPACE spinoff) -- PRE-RED->REFACTOR gated; behavior-inert (grep-proven, no `identical()` battery needed); no bug found; resolves the `document()` NAMESPACE non-idempotency gotcha; Phase-3E covered by --as-cran.** (S298 -> `devtools::document()` dropped redundant `importFrom(lubridate, day)` + `importFrom(lubridate, month)`; grep-proven behavior-inert; diff = exactly 2 deletions + zero man/ delta; document() now idempotent for NAMESPACE; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; spell clean; landed as `aff4d262` on master, owner chose direct-commit)

#### Learning 277 -- **Removing an executed-but-INERT call is an `identical()`-battery REFACTOR (S295 family), NOT the grep-only case (S298) -- because something DOES run; but the "test" a bug-fix handoff asks for when it says "add a RED" is a passing CHARACTERIZATION/invariant guard, not a true failing RED (you cannot write a red-then-green test for a no-op removal). Prove the no-op EMPIRICALLY by identity-override, add the guard where the removal makes an upstream function the SOLE guarantor, and verify the proof is NON-VACUOUS.** S297 (Learning 275) fixed `fixColumnNames()` to restore `first_name`/`second_name` into the returned `newColNames`, then explicitly LEFT the downstream `fixGenotypeCols(sb)` workaround at `qcStudbook.R:288` as "a harmless idempotent safety net for a separate future evaluation" (FM #8). This session is that evaluation: with `fixColumnNames()` now normalizing every spelling to canonical `first_name`/`second_name` BEFORE line 288, `fixGenotypeCols` (which renames only a column literally named `firstname`/`secondname`) has nothing to do -- a provable no-op. **Strict TDD REFACTOR (behavior-inert removal of executed code); one PRE-RED->REFACTOR gate + one landing gate; `R/qcStudbook.R` loses one line; `tests/testthat/test_qcStudbook.R` gains one guard test (mkGenoPed helper + 5-spelling invariant); full suite 0/0/0 (`NOT_CRAN=true`, 3733 tests, 7 baseline warnings unchanged); lint 0; spell clean; document() zero man/NAMESPACE delta; --as-cran 0/0/0 (Status: OK); 0 corrections.** **(a) This is the S295 branch (`identical()` battery), NOT the S298 branch (grep-only) -- classify by "does anything EXECUTED change?".** S298 removed an unused `importFrom` (nothing runs -> two greps settle it). Here the removed line `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` ACTUALLY EXECUTES on every happy-path `qcStudbook` call; whether it's a no-op depends on the runtime state of `sb`'s names at line 288, which reasoning alone should not settle. So prove it like S295's dead-branch removal: an `identical()` battery BEFORE the edit. **(b) Simulate the removal by identity-override, which is EXACTLY equivalent.** `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` with `fixGenotypeCols <- function(ped) ped` is `sb <- sb`, i.e. the removed line. `assignInNamespace("fixGenotypeCols", function(ped) ped, ns="nprcgenekeepr")` then `identical(qcStudbook_with(x), qcStudbook_without(x))` over a battery is a complete proof for the enumerated inputs -- no need to hand-edit + reload a second copy of the source. Battery: 7 genotype-header spellings (underscore/space/period/collapsed/upper/mixed) + the shipped 31-row genotype fixture + `examplePedigree` + `pedGood`, each under `reportErrors=FALSE/TRUE` and `reportChanges=TRUE` -> all `identical()==TRUE`. **(c) PROVE the proof is non-vacuous, and TRACE that the removed code never fires.** A green `identical()` battery could be vacuous if none of the inputs exercised the code. Two guards: (i) a trace/counter on `fixGenotypeCols` showed it saw a bare `firstname`/`secondname` column **0 times** at line 288 across the whole battery (so it's genuinely inert, not accidentally-equal); (ii) with `fixGenotypeCols` forced to identity, a `firstname` input STILL emerges as `first_name` -- proving `fixColumnNames` (not the removed call) is doing the work. **(d) The bug-fix handoff's "add a RED" for a no-op removal means a passing invariant guard, not a failing RED.** A removal that changes NO observable behavior cannot have a red-then-green test (if a test's result flipped, the code was not redundant). The honest TDD phase is REFACTOR (gate PRE-RED->REFACTOR, S295/S298 precedent); the valuable "test" is a CHARACTERIZATION guard added as the refactor's safety net: assert `qcStudbook()` on a genotype-bearing input keeps `first_name`/`second_name` and never `firstname`/`secondname`. Add it FIRST, confirm it passes WITH the call present (locks the current invariant), THEN remove the call and confirm it still passes. **(e) Add the guard where the removal makes an upstream function the SOLE guarantor.** Before this session, genotype-column survival through `qcStudbook` was double-guarded (`fixColumnNames` + the `fixGenotypeCols` net); there was NO end-to-end `qcStudbook` test for it (only `test_fixColumnNames.R` at the unit level and `test_modInput_qcStudbook.R`'s SEPARATE-file merge path, which uses integer `first`/`second`, not `first_name`/`second_name`). Removing the net makes `fixColumnNames` the sole guarantor, so an integration guard at the `qcStudbook` level is genuinely NEW protection, not redundant coverage -- it fills a real gap the removal creates. **(f) Adversarial verification earns its cost on a correctness-sensitive removal.** Because a wrong removal would silently corrupt genotype handling (genome-uniqueness / GV reports depend on it), an `ultracode` refutation workflow (4 agents, each REFUTE-prompted with a distinct attack surface: exotic spellings; reintroduction paths through `addUIds`/`addParents`/`removeDuplicates` + the `reportErrors=TRUE`/missing-column branches; real shipped fixtures; duplicate/collision/single-genotype inputs; each RUNNING real R) returned 0/4 refutations -- concordant with the own-battery proof. Use adversarial fan-out for removals whose blast radius is a silent data-corruption path, not for cosmetic cleanups. **(g) Phase-3E for a call-removal is covered by `--as-cran` + the integration tests.** `--as-cran` rebuilds vignettes and runs examples + the full suite, exercising `qcStudbook` (its `@examples` runs `qcStudbook(examplePedigree)`) with the call gone; Status: OK IS the runtime proof no path broke -- plus the new guard test and `test_modInput_qcStudbook.R` cover the genotype paths. No separate app launch (same reasoning as 275c/276d). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-only redundant-call-removal session (the OTHER S297 spinoff) -- PRE-RED->REFACTOR gated; production source change (behavior-inert, `identical()`-proven + adversarially verified 0/4); no bug found; a durable end-to-end guard test added where the removal makes `fixColumnNames` the sole guarantor; Phase-3E covered by --as-cran.** (S299, issue #117 -> removed the redundant `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` at `qcStudbook.R:288`; identity-override `identical()`-proven over 10 inputs, trace fires 0x, 4/4 adversarial agents could not refute; added a 5-spelling genotype invariant guard to `test_qcStudbook.R`; `fixGenotypeCols` function + its test left for a separate session; --as-cran 0/0/0 GREEN; full suite 0/0/0; lint 0; spell clean; document() zero man/ delta; landed as `436dc749` on master, owner chose direct-commit)

#### Learning 278 -- **Retiring a self-admitted workaround is a MULTI-SESSION staged retirement (fix-at-source → remove-redundant-call → remove-orphaned-function), and each stage picks a DIFFERENT removal proof: the call removal (S299) was the S295 `identical()`-battery branch because the call still EXECUTED; the function removal (this session) is the S298 grep-branch because after S299 the function has ZERO in-package callers, so nothing executed changes. Classify by "does anything executed IN-PACKAGE change?" — call still runs → `identical()` battery; function already caller-less → grep suffices.** Issue #117's `fixGenotypeCols()` was a band-aid whose own `@noRd` note prescribed its own removal ("This is not a good fix. A better solution is to avoid the problem. Currently qcStudbook() blindly changes all of the column names by removing the underscores"): it repaired the genotype headers `first_name`/`second_name` that `fixColumnNames()`'s blanket underscore strip collapsed to the *un-documented* `firstname`/`secondname` (every describing file — `data.R`, `getPossibleCols.R`, `checkGenotypeFile.R`, `geneDrop.R`, `headerDisplayNames.R` — documents them canonically WITH underscores, so the collapse was a self-inflicted inconsistency). S297 (`fdcfc042`) fixed the source, S299 (`436dc749`) removed the redundant call, S300 removed the orphaned function -- three sessions, each FM #8-surgical. **(a) A `@noRd` function deletion is documentation-inert:** no man page exists, so `document()` produces zero `man/`/`NAMESPACE` delta and there is nothing to regenerate -- confirm by `ls man/ | grep <fn>` (empty) then `document()` + `git status --porcelain man/ NAMESPACE` (empty). This is the tell that it is the grep-branch, not the `identical()`-branch. **(b) A deletion's evidence inventory must sweep the WHOLE repo, not just `R/` + `tests/`:** the predecessor's "called only by its own test" claim was scoped to `R/`, but `git grep fixGenotypeCols` over `inst/*` surfaced TWO dangling `_pkgdown.yml` reference-index entries (a pkgdown `reference:` list can name a `@noRd` topic that has no man page to link -- latent cruft). The deletion is the natural time to clean them; the grep is what finds them. Run `git grep <sym> -- 'R/*' 'tests/*' 'man/*' 'NAMESPACE' 'inst/*' 'vignettes/*'` before ANY symbol deletion. **(c) Leave historically-accurate comments that reference the removed symbol** (`test_qcStudbook.R:340-342` explain WHY the S299 guard exists; "the fixGenotypeCols() safety net is gone" is *more* true after this session) -- do not scrub prose that documents the reason a test exists. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (REFACTOR-only dead-code deletion; PRE-RED→REFACTOR + landing gated; full suite 0/0; document() zero delta; spell clean; --as-cran 0/0/0 Status OK; landed as `374d08c5` on master, owner chose direct-commit)

#### Learning 279 -- **A triage/scoping session's single highest-value move is checking whether the reported "conflict/bug" is a KNOWN, DELIBERATELY DEFERRED design decision -- and the answer lives in `docs/planning/`, not in the code alone.** Issue #119 ("`minParentAge` seems to conflict with newer sex-specific minimum reproductive ages") read like a bug, but a `git grep` for the sibling symbols surfaced that the package already has TWO notions of "minimum age to be a parent": the legacy scalar `minParentAge` (sex/species-agnostic; default 2, but 3 in `getProductionStatus`) used by QC (`checkParentAge.R:94-95`), candidate-finding (`getPotentialParents.R:97`) and production status, versus a newer species+sex table (`speciesGestation$minMaleBreedingAge`/`minFemaleBreedingAge` via `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`) used by ONLY the #9 GVA unknown-parent correction. **The reframing came from ONE line in the planning doc:** `docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md` §8-D (line 229) explicitly fenced this off -- *"Do NOT retrofit `getPotentialParents`/`checkParentAge` ... Unifying breeding-age determination package-wide is a follow-up."* #119 IS that follow-up. Finding it changed the triage verdict from "defect to patch" to "planned unification to schedule," dropped the severity (no code defect), and handed the executor the load-bearing dragon for free (the `species` column is often ABSENT -- `qcPed`/`breederPed` carry none -- so any retrofit must default gracefully, never error; §8-D lines 227-228). **(a) A scalar-vs-table divergence is a design-UNIFICATION decision (owner's call on override precedence + unknown-species fallback + slice order), not a one-edit patch** -- so triage lays out options (unify-on-table / sex-scalars-only / document-only / won't-fix) and DEFERS the direction to a planning session rather than picking one; the owner did exactly that here. **(b) Blast radius decides delivery shape:** both QC decision sites sit under `qcStudbook` (the app's front door) and `getPotentialParents` feeds breeding-group formation -- high blast radius is WHY #9 deferred it and WHY any fix is vertical slices (one consumer per session, strict TDD), not a sweep. **(c) `gh` projectCards workaround extends ([[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]):** `gh issue view <N>` FAILS on this repo (deprecated `projectCards` GraphQL), but reading via `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/<N>` WORKS and posting via `gh issue comment <N> --body-file` WORKS (the createComment mutation doesn't touch projectCards) -- so an issue with no body is still fully triageable + annotatable from the CLI. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (the planning doc was the process history), [[observation-vs-decision]] (presented options, let the owner decide the direction), [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]]. (Triage/scoping only -- no code changed; PRE-RED throughout; deliverable = `docs/audits/ISSUE_119_MINPARENTAGE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md` + a condensed comment on #119; Phase-3E N/A -- no runtime behavior touched.)

#### Learning 280 -- **When the owner proposes a direction as a QUESTION ("can X be replaced by Y?"), a planning session's job is to answer the feasibility, then surface the ONE design fork the proposal glosses -- and let the owner choose with that fork explicit -- before writing a line of the plan.** Issue #119's owner opened the planning session with "can `minParentAge` be replaced by `minSireAge` and `minDamAge`? This will require ... sex-specific conditional coding everywhere." The literal proposal is sex-specific SCALARS (the triage's Option 2), but the codebase already had a species+sex TABLE (`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, tested, used by the #9 GVA correction) that encodes sex AND species -- so pure sex scalars would half-solve #119 and add a THIRD parallel notion of breeding age (the exact risk the triage flagged). The high-value move was NOT to just plan the literal ask, but to pose the fork via `AskUserQuestion`: (A) plain sex scalars vs. (B) sex scalars that DEFAULT to the species+sex table and act as optional overrides vs. (C) keep the `minParentAge` name. The owner chose B -- which is the triage's recommended Option 1 wearing the owner's preferred parameter names, resolving both halves with one source of truth. **(a) A "can we do Y?" from the owner is `observation-vs-decision`/`question-as-instruction` applied to a DIRECTION, not a file edit -- answer the feasibility (yes), give the implication (Y as written solves only the sex half and forks the model three ways), then WAIT on an `AskUserQuestion` rather than planning the literal ask.** [[observation-vs-decision]]. **(b) Ground the option text in FIRSTHAND reads of the decision sites, not the triage's summary** -- reading `checkParentAge`/`getPotentialParents`/`getProductionStatus`/`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge` before drafting options surfaced facts the triage compressed away: the floor must key on the PARENT's species (checkParentAge holds only the offspring row -> needs a sire/dam species merge mirroring `sireBirth`/`damBirth`); `getProductionStatus` counts FEMALES ONLY so only a dam floor applies there; `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge` already falls back to 2 for absent species, so species-less data (qcPed/breederPed) is automatically back-compat under model B. These became the plan's load-bearing dragons. [[consult-project-source-of-truth]]. **(c) A second, orthogonal ratification prevented a wrong default:** back-compat of the deprecated name is independent of the age model, so it was a SEPARATE question -- the owner's answer was a nuance neither pre-baked option captured ("deprecate in the R signatures but fully migrate the Shiny UI"), which only surfaced BECAUSE it was asked rather than assumed. **(d) Deprecation planning must trace the INTERNAL caller chain, not just the public entry:** `deprecate_warn` always warns, so if a slice migrated `checkParentAge` but left `qcStudbook` calling it via the alias, the package would trip its OWN deprecation warning on every QC run (and fail vignette rebuild) -- so the QC vertical (checkParentAge -> qcStudbook -> runQcStudbook) must move together in one slice. This is why the slices are cut by CONSUMER (vertical), each carrying its plumbing + tests + docs, not by layer. **(e) Preserve existing goldens EXACTLY; only ADD species-aware assertions:** model B degrades to floor 2 for species-less fixtures, so `checkParentAge(qcPed, minParentAge=6L)` must still return `nrow 6` (now via the alias, with a warning asserted) -- the plan's completion criteria pin this per slice, so the executor cannot mistake a golden shift for progress. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (the S301 triage + issue9 §8-D were the process history), [[observation-vs-decision]], [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (plan tells the executor to keep issue numbers / "Slice N" out of rendered help/vignettes), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Planning only -- no code changed; PRE-RED throughout; deliverable = `docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md`; 2 `AskUserQuestion` ratifications, 0 corrections; Phase-3E N/A -- no runtime behavior touched; implementation is separate one-slice-per-session work under strict TDD.)

#### Learning 281 -- **Deprecating a parameter on a front-door function (`qcStudbook`/`checkParentAge`/`runQcStudbook`) with `lifecycle::deprecate_warn` ripples across the WHOLE package -- the SAME slice must (a) rewire internal RUNTIME callers, (b) preserve emergent back-compat semantics, and (c) quiet the package's own test suite; none of that is optional plumbing.** Issue #119 Slice 1 (the QC vertical) under strict TDD, ONE slice. **(a) `deprecate_warn` warns whenever the deprecated arg is present, so an internal RUNTIME caller that still passes it can SILENTLY BREAK:** `modInput.R:456-467` wraps its `qcStudbook()` call in `tryCatch(..., warning = function(w) getEmptyErrorLst())`, so a self-inflicted deprecation warning would be caught and turned into an EMPTY errorLst -- the app's QC would silently produce nothing. Fix = a minimal Slice-1 rewire of modInput's two QC call sites to the new params (single UI field feeds both; the two-field UX stays Slice 4). The plan had scoped modInput entirely to Slice 4, but the swallowing forces the runtime caller to move WITH its function -- grep `tryCatch(.*warning` around any deprecated internal caller. **(b) Preserve emergent (undocumented) back-compat, not just the documented contract:** `minParentAge = NULL` historically DISABLED the check (`age < NULL` -> `logical(0)` -> 0 rows), and 22 `test_qcStudbook.R` tests plus potentially external scripts rely on it; the literal ratified "alias sets both params" would map NULL->NULL = table lookup = check ENABLED, which `stop()`s on fixtures like `pedOne` (3 flagged at floor 2). The shim special-cases `minParentAge = NULL` to `-Inf` floors (never-flag) to reproduce the skip EXACTLY -- the ratified principle "external scripts do not break" decided this without re-gating. **(c) A front-door deprecation floods the ENTIRE suite because that function is test SETUP everywhere (~20 files); do NOT migrate 24 files (scope creep into Slices 2-4's test files) NOR rely on `deprecate_warn`'s once-per-8h rate-limit (fragile, non-deterministic which warning surfaces).** The recognized `lifecycle` pattern is a 3-line `tests/testthat/setup.R` with `options(lifecycle_verbosity = "quiet")`: the package's own test run is deterministically clean, external users still get warned (they don't set the option), and every deprecation-asserting test forces `"warning"` locally (`lifecycle::expect_deprecated()` / `withr::local_options`, as `test_runModularApp_alias.R` already does) so those are unaffected -- and it auto-covers Slices 2-5's future deprecations.
