Owner ratified this over per-call `suppressWarnings`. **(d) Verify the deprecation `when=` version against DESCRIPTION FIRSTHAND -- the plan's value was stale:** the plan said `1.1.0.9000`; DESCRIPTION is `2.0.0` (bumped S131 `e24a53a2`), matching the existing `runModularApp` deprecation -- one read caught it. **(e) The shared resolver pays off:** `resolveBreedingAge()` (@noRd, wraps the tested `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`) is called by `checkParentAge` for BOTH the sire floor (`sex="M"`) and the dam floor (`sex="F"`), keying on the PARENT's species via an id->species map built BEFORE the merges reorder rows; species-less data degrades to floor 2 so every existing golden is numerically unchanged and only species-aware assertions are ADDED (dragon #4 held). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (the `nonportable_path_linter` fired on "and/or" -- a `/` false positive -- reworded to "and"), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED evidence -> RED (new `test_resolveBreedingAge.R` + species-aware/back-compat/deprecation tests + migrated goldens across 3 files) -> GREEN (min impl + modInput rewire + setup.R) -> concluded no-refactor per owner; 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 2929 pass/0 fail/0 error; `R CMD check --as-cran` 0 ERROR/0 WARNING/1 baseline NOTE (archived); vignettes rebuild OK; lint 0; Phase-3E runtime smoke of the rewired QC calls PASS.)

#### Learning 282 -- **When a slice replaces a flat scalar cutoff with a per-row species+sex floor, the faithful minimum implementation is often a single vectorized floor computed ONCE, not the plan's literal "move the cutoff into each branch" -- and reusing a slice-1 helper makes slice 2 an afternoon, not a rebuild.** Issue #119 Slice 2 (`getPotentialParents`) under strict TDD, ONE slice, 0 stakeholder corrections. **(a) "Move the flat cutoff into the sire and dam selections" reads as two edits, but because the Slice-1 `resolveBreedingAge()` is already vectorized and keys each row's floor on that row's OWN sex (M->sire floor, F->dam floor), the clean realization is ONE floor vector for the whole candidate pool -- `candFloor <- resolveBreedingAge(species-or-NA, ped$sex, minSireAge, minDamAge)` computed once after `ped` is final -- and a single `ba <- ped[birth <= focalBirth - dYear*candFloor]`.** The downstream `ba[sex=="M"]` / `ba[sex=="F"]` splits then pick up already-correctly-floored candidates; behaviorally identical to per-branch cutoffs but one changed line instead of restructured control flow, and the dam-fallback (`ba[sex=="F" & present]`) and gestation blocks (`births`, `births_plus_minus_one`, computed from full `ped`) need no touch. Data.table finds the length-`nrow(ped)` `candFloor` in the function env exactly as it already found the scalar `minParentAge`; alignment holds because `ped` is not mutated after `candFloor` is computed. **(b) The species key is SIMPLER here than in Slice 1 -- it keys on the candidate's OWN row, not a parent-of-offspring merge.** `checkParentAge` held offspring rows and had to merge sire/dam species (the floor keyed on the parent); `getPotentialParents`' candidates ARE the potential parents on their own rows, so `ped$species`/`ped$sex` are read directly (guarded by `if ("species" %in% names(ped))` -> `NA` -> fallback 2). Do not cargo-cult Slice 1's merge where the row already carries the key. **(c) Golden migration preserves numbers EXACTLY because an explicit override beats the table:** migrating the 13 existing `minParentAge=2` calls to `minSireAge=2, minDamAge=2` keeps flat-2 even for the species-bearing tests (194-206: `species="RHESUS"`), because `resolveBreedingAge` overrides win over the table floor -- so the back-compat golden comparisons stay valid without special-casing. Verify fixture arithmetic FIRSTHAND before writing RED (computed the floor-4/floor-2.5 cutoff dates so a 3-yr male and a 2.25-yr female land on the intended side WITH margin, and confirmed `removeAutoGenIds` keeps hand IDs) -- cheaper than debugging a wrong assertion after GREEN. **(d) A signature-changing slice whose tests share a golden object built at file-source produces a COARSE-but-valid RED:** the migrated setup call `getPotentialParents(..., minSireAge=2, minDamAge=2)` errors `unused arguments` on the old code, so the WHOLE file errors (0 pass / 1 error) rather than showing individual assertion failures. That still satisfies RED (the new behavior is provably absent); don't chase a per-test-failure RED by leaving the golden on the deprecated alias -- migrate it and accept the file-level error. **(e) The internal-caller rewire repeats from Slice 1 but this one was NOT warning-swallowing:** `modPotentialParentsServer`'s `getPotentialParents` call is not wrapped in `tryCatch(warning=)`, so passing the deprecated alias would surface a console warning, not a silent break -- yet still rewire it (`minSireAge = minParentAge, minDamAge = minParentAge`, module signature unchanged) for D2's "the Shiny UI trips no deprecation" and to keep the package clean; the two-field UX stays Slice 4. Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]], [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]] (13 scattered golden migrations, applied by unique-context match). (Strict TDD: PRE-RED evidence + fixture-arithmetic verification -> RED (13 golden migrations + species-aware sire/dam + per-sex overrides + deprecation-path tests) -> GREEN (getPotentialParents signature+shim+candFloor, modPotentialParents rewire, roxygen + `man/getPotentialParents.Rd`) -> concluded no-refactor per owner (the shim is now 4x but the owner chose at S303 NOT to extract it); 3 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 2939 pass/0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; `devtools::check(--as-cran)` 0 ERROR/0 WARNING/0 NOTE (vignettes rebuilt); lint 0; Phase-3E: behavior-preserving module rewire covered by 87 passing `test_modPotentialParents*` server tests, interactive app smoke deferred to Slice 4's real UX change.)

#### Learning 283 -- **Not every consumer in a unification campaign should adopt the unified default -- a domain-distinct threshold is preserved as an EXPLICIT concrete value, so its slice is a "rename-the-scalar + deprecation-shim" (default stays a number, NOT the campaign's `NULL`->table), and the shim must NOT cargo-cult the front-door's legacy branches it never had.** Issue #119 Slice 3 (`getProductionStatus` + `getGeneticDiversityStats` caller) under strict TDD, ONE slice, 0 stakeholder corrections. **(a) The RATIFY (R1: the 2-vs-3 default) is a DOMAIN decision, not a code decision -- pose it BEFORE declaring RED, separate from the phase gates.** `getProductionStatus` counts breeding-capacity females for a demographic ratio (Production = Births/Dams) at a dam floor of **3**, where the rest of #119 defaults to 2 / the species+sex table. Whether "breeding-capacity female" for that ratio is the same concept as the QC parent-age floor is a colony-management judgment the owner owns; a 2-option `AskUserQuestion` (preserve 3 vs. unify to the table, with the ratio-shifting consequence spelled out) settled it -- owner chose preserve. So Slices 1-2's pattern (new params default `NULL` -> table lookup) does NOT transfer: here the param default stays the concrete `3L` and the caller keeps passing `3L`; unifying would silently move every production golden. **(b) The deprecation shim is DELIBERATELY simpler than Learning 281's front-door shim -- do not copy the branches this consumer never had.** `getProductionStatus` never supported `minParentAge = NULL` as a "disable the check" idiom (unlike `checkParentAge`): `age >= NULL` -> `logical(0)` would have errored, so no back-compat relies on it -- therefore NO `-Inf`-disable branch (adding one would invent behavior). And because the new param's default is a concrete `3L` (not `NULL`), you CANNOT detect "was `minDamAge` explicitly passed" via `is.null()`; the realistic legacy caller passes only `minParentAge`, so the shim maps it unconditionally-when-non-null (`if (!is.null(minParentAge)) minDamAge <- minParentAge`) and the deprecated arg wins if both are somehow passed -- documented in a comment. Match the shim to THIS function's real history, not the template. **(c) A man-page-free, runtime-untouched slice is legitimate -- verify that, do not manufacture ceremony.** `getProductionStatus` is `@noRd` so `devtools::document()` yields NO man/NAMESPACE diff (confirmed via `git status` after document; the `lifecycle` imports already entered NAMESPACE in Slice 1), yet keep its roxygen `@param`/`@details`/`@return` accurate anyway. Its only caller `getGeneticDiversityStats` is `@export` but ONLY its internal body line changed (`minParentAge = 3L` -> `minDamAge = 3L`), so ITS man page is unchanged too. **Phase 3E:** the change is behavior-preserving (production goldens byte-identical) and its runtime path (`modGeneticDiversity` -> `getGeneticDiversityStats` -> `getProductionStatus`) is covered by passing `test_modGeneticDiversity` / `test_getGeneticDiversityStats` server tests -- so NO app launch is needed this slice (contrast Slice 4, which changes the UX and REQUIRES the interactive smoke). Distinguish "changes runtime behavior" (FM #24, launch) from "behavior-preserving internal edit under test" (suite suffices). **(d) Coarse file-level RED via a shared local var, again (cf. 282(d)):** renaming the test file's `minParentAge <- 3.0` local to `minDamAge <- 3.0` and migrating the 11 golden calls errors the whole file (`unused argument (minDamAge = minDamAge)`) on the old signature -- valid RED (new behavior provably absent); the deprecation-path test additionally REDs on the *absence* of a warning (`lifecycle::expect_deprecated` sees none because `minParentAge` is still a plain param). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (R1 posed, not assumed), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (plan §3 + S304 handoff), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (lint 0), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED evidence + R1 ratification -> RED (11 golden migrations + minDamAge override test + deprecation-path test) -> GREEN (getProductionStatus rename+shim+filter, getGeneticDiversityStats caller edit, roxygen; no man page) -> concluded no-refactor per owner (shim now 5x; owner re-affirmed NOT extracting); 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 2944 pass/0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; `devtools::check(--as-cran)` 0 ERROR/0 WARNING/0 NOTE; lint 0; document() no diff.)

#### Learning 284 -- **A UI-rename slice's test blast radius reaches BEYOND the R source + unit tests: it also hits the e2e/app-driver layer AND assertions that encode the old contract WITHOUT naming the renamed symbol -- so `grep <symbol>` is necessary but NOT sufficient; also grep the user-facing label, the namespaced input ids the e2e layer drives, and structural assertions (return-list length/names, help-text wording), then RUN the migrated tests to flush the rest. And a real Shiny-UX slice earns a REAL headless-app Phase 3E, not a testServer stand-in.** Issue #119 Slice 4 (the two-field sire/dam UX + `modPotentialParents`/`appServer` wiring) under strict TDD, ONE slice, 0 stakeholder corrections. **(a) `grep minParentAge` migrated the obvious references but MISSED three kinds of straggler that broke only when the tests ran:** (i) the entire `test-e2e-*` app-driver layer -- five files set `dataInput-minParentAge` and asserted the `"Minimum Parent Age"` pane label (the app-driver uses the namespaced INPUT ID and the rendered LABEL, neither of which is the bare symbol); (ii) `test_modInput.R` assertions with no `minParentAge` string at all -- a help-text `grepl("Parents must be at least as old")` and two `expect_equal(length(result), 9)` return-list-size checks (the count silently encodes the old single reactive); (iii) `stubInput` in `test_appServer_server.R`, a mock that mirrors `modInputServer`'s return contract. **Lesson: for a rename/UI migration, grep the SYMBOL, the LABEL, the namespaced INPUT ID, and the CONTRACT-SHAPE assertions (length/names) -- and treat a green single-file run as the real inventory: the three stragglers surfaced as `fail=6`/`error=4` only after `NOT_CRAN=true` test runs, not from grep.** **(b) A genuine Phase 3E for a Shiny UX change = install the working tree + drive the REAL app in headless Chrome via the opt-in `shinytest2` suite -- stronger than testServer and the only thing that catches label/wiring regressions.** Prior slices' "Phase 3E" was testServer coverage (legitimate when behavior-preserving); Slice 4 CHANGES the UX (FM #24), so: `R CMD build` + `R CMD INSTALL` the tarball, then run the migrated `test-e2e-*` files with `NPRC_RUN_E2E=true NOT_CRAN=true` (the suite is opt-in behind `create_test_app()`'s env gate AND `library(nprcgenekeepr)` -> the INSTALLED copy, so the working tree MUST be installed first). 44 live-browser assertions confirmed the field renders, `dataInput-minSireAge` accepts input without crashing, and the full appServer->modPotentialParents wiring holds the locked 50-candidate regression. Verified headless Chrome launches first (`chromote::ChromoteSession$new()`). **(c) The `if (is.function(x)) x() else x` reactive-or-scalar normalization lets ONE module arg accept either a wired reactive (from `appServer`) or a plain scalar/`NULL` (from unit tests) -- so `appServer` passes `inputResults$minSireAge` directly (no wrapper) AND the tests pass `minSireAge = 4` without wrapping every arg in `reactive()`.** shiny reactives ARE functions; a numeric/`NULL` is not -- a clean, well-defined discriminator. **(d) Distinguish a REAL check WARNING from a build-flag artifact before claiming clean.** `R CMD build --no-build-vignettes` leaves `inst/doc` empty, so `R CMD check --as-cran` emits two WARNINGs ("Files in vignettes but no files in inst/doc" / "Directory inst/doc does not exist") that are NOT defects -- `devtools::check(--as-cran)` (which BUILDS vignettes, as Slices 1-3 used) shows the true **0/0/0**. The archived-on-CRAN NOTE likewise appears only on a direct-tarball check. Run the build-equivalent the way the prior slices did before writing "check clean." **(e) `test_modInput*.R` are `skip_on_cran`:** a single-file `testthat::test_file()` without `NOT_CRAN=true` silently reports `0/0/0` (everything skipped, not passing) -- always set `NOT_CRAN=true` for these, or a broken test masquerades as a clean file. **R2 (owner-ratified):** blank fields -> `NULL` -> the species+sex table default is what actually resolves #119 in the app (the old field prefilled `2.0`, forcing a flat floor); wiring Potential Parents to the Input-tab floors gives QC and PP one source of truth. Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (R2's 3 sub-decisions posed, not assumed), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (plan §3 Slice 4 + S305 handoff), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (a comment tripped `commented_code_linter` -- `reactives -- wired` parses as `reactives - -wired`; reworded to plain prose, lint 0), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]], [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (issue refs live in source comments, not the rendered help text), [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]] (bottom-up deletions in the 1160-line test file), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED evidence + R2 ratification -> RED (2 new test files + migrated the retired single-field API across 6 unit files + stubInput + 5 e2e files; confirmed failing for the right reason) -> GREEN (`parseOptionalAge` helper, two UI fields, getData rewire, two exposed reactives, module signature + is.function normalization, appServer wiring, roxygen) -> concluded no-refactor per owner; 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 3632 pass/0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders; `devtools::check(--as-cran)` 0/0/0; lint 0 on 3 changed R files; `man/modInputServer.Rd` + `man/modPotentialParentsServer.Rd` regenerated; Phase 3E 44 live-browser assertions pass.)

#### Learning 285 -- **The final "docs slice" of a deprecation migration has a genuine test-first artifact (a source-scanning guard), the render is its build-equivalent, and the verification is asymmetric: `devtools::check` rebuilds `vignettes/*.Rmd` but NOT the pkgdown `vignettes/articles/*.qmd`.** Slice 5 of #119 (owner said "Slice 5") — migrate every user-facing `minParentAge=` reference to `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` so the package renders without tripping its own `deprecate_warn`. **Test-only RED, PRE-RED→RED + RED→GREEN gated, concluded no-refactor; 8 code calls + prose/tables across 6 vignette files migrated; full suite 3633 pass/0 fail/0 error/0 true offenders (+1 = the guard); `devtools::check(cran=TRUE)` 0/0/0 with a2interactive rebuilt and ZERO deprecation warnings in the build log; spelling clean; 0 stakeholder corrections.** **(a) A docs-only slice CAN satisfy strict TDD with a real automated RED artifact:** a static source-scanning `testthat` guard (`test_vignettes_no_deprecated_minParentAge.R`) that lists every `.Rmd`/`.qmd` under `vignettes/` and fails if any line matches the CALL regex `minParentAge[[:space:]]*=[^=]` — prose/backtick/underscore mentions (`` `minParentAge` ``, `_minParentAge_`, the `input_minParentAgeSequence.png` filename, a `{r label-minParentAgeSequence}` chunk label) all lack the `=` so they don't match; it failed on exactly the 8 code-call sites (RED), passes after migration (GREEN), and stays a durable regression guard while the deprecated alias lives. The render (via `devtools::check` for `.Rmd` + spelling) is the docs build-equivalent — no unit test replaces it, but the guard prevents re-introduction. **(b) Preserve each numeric floor VERBATIM — `minParentAge = 0.0` → `minSireAge = 0.0, minDamAge = 0.0`, NOT `NULL`:** in `a2interactive.Rmd` the `0.0` is a DELIBERATE low floor that still flags impossible negative-age (swapped-birthdate) parents, whereas `NULL` disables the age check entirely (`-Inf` floors) — mapping `0.0`→`NULL` would silently kill the error the vignette is demonstrating. **(c) Empirically re-verify data-dependent doc claims — don't assume flat→sex-specific maps 1:1:** the "setting minParentAge to 3.5 causes an error" footnote turned out to be triggered by DAMS (two ~3.3-yr dams in `ExamplePedigree.csv`; every sire older), so `minSireAge=3.5` alone does NOT error — the migration made the footnote MORE accurate and turned a bug-example into a showcase of the new sex-specific control. A 30-second `qcStudbook(ped, minSireAge=3.5, minDamAge=0)` vs `(…, minSireAge=0, minDamAge=3.5)` probe settled it. **(d) Verification asymmetry:** `check` rebuilds only real vignettes (`vignettes/*.Rmd`), NOT `vignettes/articles/*.qmd` (pkgdown articles are `.Rbuildignore`d), so 0/0/0-no-deprecation covers the `.Rmd`s; the articles are covered by the guard (no deprecated calls remain) + a quarto `--no-execute` render that validates markdown/tables (e.g. one param-table row split into two) WITHOUT needing the package installed — the execute path halts on `library(nprcgenekeepr)` when the working tree isn't installed, an env limitation, not a content defect. **(e) Side-effect + curated-file hygiene:** hand-add `minDamAge`/`minSireAge` to `inst/WORDLIST` in ASCII-sorted position (NEVER `spelling::update_wordlist` — [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]]); a stray quarto render drops `vignettes/articles/.gitignore` (`/.quarto/`) + a `.quarto/` dir — `rm` those render side-effects and re-check `git status` so the committed tree is EXACTLY the deliverable; keep issue#/"Slice N" out of rendered NEWS/vignette prose ([[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]]), batch multi-edit files bottom-up ([[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]]).

#### Learning 286 -- **When an issue asks for "*the* effective population size," the triage's highest-value move is to expose that "Ne" is not one number but a FAMILY of estimators, map each to the inputs the package already has vs. the one gap, and identify the display home from the existing sibling metrics -- then DEFER the estimator/population choice to the owner rather than picking one.** Triage of #118 ("Add the effective population size estimate"; body in full: "place to locate output has not been determined") -- analysis only, no code, mirroring the S301 #119-triage deliverable shape (`docs/audits/ISSUE_118_*_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md` + a condensed issue comment). **(a) A one-line metric request can be under-specified in the DOMAIN, not just the code:** "effective population size" names >=4 standard estimators for a managed pedigreed colony -- E1 founder-genome-equivalent / gene-diversity (`GD = 1 - 1/(2*FG)`), E2 demographic sex-ratio (`4*Nm*Nf/(Nm+Nf)`), E3 variance/reproductive-skew (`~(4N-4)/(Vk+2)`), E4 rate-of-coancestry (`1/(2*dCbar)`) -- which can differ by large factors on the same colony because each idealizes a different departure from Wright-Fisher. Naming and tabulating them (formula | answers | primary input) is the core deliverable; a PR that silently picks one answers a question the owner may not have asked (Dragon #1). The population is also unspecified (studbook / living / current breeders / per group). **(b) Ground the inventory in FIRSTHAND reads, and the closest existing metric is usually the strongest signal for BOTH feasibility and placement:** reading `reportGV.R` (the colony-level bundle already carries `fe`/`fg`/`fgSE`/founder counts), `makeFounderStatsTable.R` (renders exactly those as a one-row HTML table via `modFounderStats`), `calcFG`/`calcFE`/`calcFGSE`, `findOffspring` (per-animal offspring counts -> E3's Vk), `meanKinship`, `geneDrop`/`calcRetention`, and `findGeneration` (integer generation NUMBERS) showed most inputs already exist -- and that FG is itself the effective-founder-genome count (E1 is nearly free). The founder-stats table is the obvious answer to the issue's open "location" question: Ne is a colony-level scalar of the same kind as FE/FG, so one bundle field + one `<th>/<td>` pair is the idiomatic placement. **(c) Name the GAP precisely so E4 isn't accidentally scoped in:** `speciesGestation` stores gestation + min breeding ages, NOT a generation interval, and `findGeneration` gives integers not years, so the rate-of-coancestry Ne (the number a population geneticist most often means) cannot be computed rigorously without new bundled data or a stated assumption -- it is the highest-value / highest-cost estimator and belongs in its own later effort, not slice 1. **(d) DEFER the genetics-management decision (which estimator, which population) to the owner via a follow-up planning/`/grill-me` session -- do NOT force it in the triage** ([[observation-vs-decision]]): a triage enumerates options + recommends a DIRECTION (here: E2/E3 or a labeled set E1+E2+E3 as the cheapest honest win; E1 nearly free and worth including; E4 deferred) and seeds the plan's evidence-based inventory, exactly as S301 deferred #119's Option choice and the owner later ratified it in the planning session. **(e) Repo gotcha:** `gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` fail on this repo with the projectCards-deprecation GraphQL error ([[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]) -- fetch the issue body with `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/<N> --jq ...` instead. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (S301 triage precedent + the reportGV/founder-stats display path), [[observation-vs-decision]] (deferred the estimator choice, did not pick), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (grepped `#118`/"effective population" first -- confirmed genuinely new, no prior Ne work), [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (formulas stated plainly with what-each-answers), [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (No TDD phases: triage is an analysis deliverable, no production/test code -- Phase 3E runtime smoke N/A, nothing runtime changed; the doc's "build" is that it reads cleanly and every cited symbol/line was verified firsthand this session.)

#### Learning 287 -- **A planning session must re-verify that the surfaces a predecessor triage named are actually LIVE, and -- for a "compute a new metric" plan -- run the metric on real bundled data; both catch load-bearing errors that neither the triage nor a pure read surfaces.** Planning of #118 (effective population size) from the S308 triage seed -- owner ratified E1 (gene diversity `GD=1-1/(2*FG)`) + E2 (sex-ratio `4*Nm*Nf/(Nm+Nf)`) + E3 (variance) over current living breeders, E4 deferred (avenues saved as a #118 comment); plan at `docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md`, 4 slices (E1->E2->E3->publish). **(a) An inherited inventory can point at DEAD code.** The S308 triage named `makeFounderStatsTable()`/`modFounderStats` as the display home; firsthand this session, `makeFounderStatsTable()` has **no runtime caller** (only a `@seealso`) and `modFounderStats.R` does not exist -- the live founder-stats surface is `modSummaryStats.R:623-651` + `modGeneticValue.R:394-478`, fed from the `reportGV.R:264-276` bundle. A triage read confirms a symbol EXISTS; only a grep for runtime callers confirms it is LIVE. This is FM #21 (greenfield/assumption) applied to a predecessor's map: trust-but-verify the inventory before a plan tells an executor to edit a file no user sees (Dragon: editing only the dead helper changes nothing). **(b) Running the metric on real data during planning turns assumptions into evidence and catches DOMAIN bugs a read cannot.** Computing E2/E3 on `examplePedigree` this session (analysis only, no code) surfaced two corrections the triage's formulas assumed away: `sex` is a factor `{F,M,H,U}` with `NA->"U"` (`convertSexCodes.R:37-52`), so E2's `Nm`/`Nf` must count only `sex=="M"`/`sex=="F"`, not `!= "F"`; and `kbar=5.28 != 2` (colony growing), so the bare `(4N-4)/(Vk+2)` overstates Ne and the mean-adjusted Crow-Kimura form is needed. The worked numbers (`Nm=35,Nf=121 -> E2=108.6`; `N=156,Vk=16.5 -> E3=33.5`; E2 ~3.2x E3) also make the "cheap to compute now" claim concrete and vividly justify the owner's labeled-set choice (skew drags Ne far below the sex-ratio figure). A worked example is cheap in a planning session and directly closes the S308 self-assessed -0.5 ("did not numerically compute a sample Ne"). **(c) When the owner CLARIFIES mid-question with a design insight, engage it and separate its parts -- don't force it into scope.** The owner's E4 note ("generation length is arbitrary; set it by slider or derive from the sample") is a real design avenue; the right move was to split E4's two ingredients (generation length -- derivable from mean parent age at birth, or a slider -- vs. the coancestry-rate `dCbar` -- estimable cross-generationally from `findGeneration` cohorts), record both as a #118 future-work comment, and keep E4 out of the E1-E3 slices ([[observation-vs-decision]]). **(d) Repo gotcha (carried):** post issue comments via `gh api "repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/<N>/comments" -F body=@file` -- the REST route avoids the projectCards GraphQL deprecation that breaks `gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` ([[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]]). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (modeled the plan on `issue82-fg-se-plan.md`, the FG-SE precedent the triage named), [[observation-vs-decision]] (posed owner-only genetics decisions via `AskUserQuestion`; deferred the E3 formula-variant + display-labeling sub-decisions to the Slice-1 gate rather than deciding them), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (Explore-verified `GD`/`Ne` are genuinely new, no prior work), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (Slice/session refs kept to the plan + issue tracker, not the user-facing doc notes the plan schedules), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (No TDD phases: planning is an analysis deliverable, no production/test code -- declared N/A; Phase 3E runtime smoke N/A, nothing runtime changed; `docs/` is not in the R-package build so check/spelling are unaffected.)

#### Learning 288 -- **An implementation slice must (a) re-decide an inherited DISPLAY recommendation against the specific metric's actual population, (b) run every affected test with `NOT_CRAN=true` or a "passing" RED test is silently SKIPPED, and (c) run Phase 3E on REAL bundled data, not the unit fixture.** Issue #118 Slice 1 (E1 gene diversity, `calcGeneDiversity(fg)=1-1/(2*FG)`) under strict TDD -- new exported pure fn + additive `neGD` bundle field + GD cell in both live founder-stats surfaces; 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 3657 pass/0 fail/0 error, lint 0, spelling clean, `devtools::check()` 0/0/0. **(a) A plan's display recommendation can be over-general for the specific slice -- resolve it against the metric's OWN population at the ratification gate.** The #118 plan's D-a recommended a *separate* "Effective Population Size" block for all three metrics, motivated by Dragon D-1 (E1 vs E2/E3 use different populations). But firsthand, E1's GD is over the SAME population as FG (the analysis/proband set); the conflation risk applies only to E2/E3 (living breeders). So the honest Slice-1 placement is **GD beside FG** (a labeled founder-table cell + GV-tab row), deferring the separate block to Slice 2 (E2), the first living-breeder metric -- which the plan's own §2 E1 note already implied ("reports GD beside FG"). Before placing a metric, confirm which population it covers vs the surfaces around it, and pose the fork via `AskUserQuestion` rather than following the plan's headline recommendation literally ([[observation-vs-decision]]). **(b) `NOT_CRAN=true` is MANDATORY for the regression read, not optional -- a RED display test that reports 0 failures may have been SKIPPED, not passed.** The module test files carry a file-level `testthat::skip_on_cran()` (`test_modGeneticValue.R:6`; also `test_modInput*.R` per Learning 284(e)); under non-interactive `Rscript` `NOT_CRAN` is unset, so `testthat::test_file`/`test_dir` silently skips the WHOLE file and reports `fail=0`. My new RED display test appeared to "pass" pre-implementation -- but a direct `testServer` probe proved `grepl("Gene Diversity", html)` was `FALSE` (it *should* have failed); it was skipped. **The tell: check `skipped>0`, not just `failed==0`, and re-run with `NOT_CRAN=true` (which flips `fail=0`→`fail=1` for the right reason).** A skipped RED test masquerading as a valid one defeats the whole "fail for the right reason" gate -- so make the RED verification set `NOT_CRAN=true` and assert 0 skips on the files it touches. **(c) Phase 3E for a display change = render on REAL bundled data, not the unit fixture.** The module test used a fixture `neGD=0.9905`; the real smoke computed `reportGV(qcPed)` → `neGD=0.990528`, threaded it through the `founderStats` reactive, and rendered the actual founder table `... FG 52.79 +/- 0.04 | Gene Diversity (GD) 0.9905` -- exercising the whole `neGD`→reactive→`renderUI` path with real values (a fixture can hide a wiring/format mismatch). **(d) `commented_code_linter` + `implicit_integer_linter` are project-enforced (`.lintr` `best_practices`/`style` tags) -- match house style.** `1 - 1/(2*fg)` tripped `implicit_integer_linter`; use `1L - 1L/(2L*fg)` (as `calcFG.R:81` does; division still yields a double, so values/type are unchanged). A comment "gene diversity GD = 1 - 1/(2*FG) beside" tripped `commented_code_linter` (the `X = expr` fragment parses as an assignment -- the same trap as Learning 284's `reactives -- wired`); reword to plain prose and keep the formula in the roxygen only. Wrapping the formula in parens (`(GD = 1 - 1/(2*FG))`, as the `modGeneticValue` comment did) also escapes the linter, but plain prose is safest. Lint changed files with the package loaded; `object_usage`/`object_name` are artifacts ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]]). **(e) Additive golden-master mechanics:** inserting `neGD` after `fgSE` in the ORDER-SENSITIVE `expect_named` bundle list required updating BOTH assertions in `test_reportGV.R` -- the 2-space and 4-space-indented copies are NOT byte-identical, so two separate edits; the bundled-report `fg=52.75` value tests (`test_reportGV.R:425-457`) are the golden master proving `fe`/`fg` are unchanged. A new scalar rides beside `fg` exactly as `fgSE` did (#82 Slice 3) -- NOT a per-animal `$report`/`$gu` column; assert `expect_false("neGD" %in% names(gv$report))`. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (modeled the display wiring on the #82 `fgSE` precedent the plan named; plan §8 Slice-1 GREEN steps), [[observation-vs-decision]] (D-a/D-e/D-f + every phase transition posed via `AskUserQuestion`; resolved the E1-population nuance AT the gate, not unilaterally), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (`1L`/`2L` + comment reword, lint 0), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (`heterozygosity` hand-added to `inst/WORDLIST` in ASCII-sorted position, `update_wordlist` NOT run), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (issue/Slice refs live in source comments + CHANGELOG, not the roxygen/help prose -- `calcGeneDiversity`'s docs describe the metric, not the slice), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED firsthand reads + D-a/D-e/D-f scope gate → RED (1 new test file + 3 extended, all confirmed failing for the right reason, with the `NOT_CRAN=true` skip catch above) → GREEN (`calcGeneDiversity` + bundle + `@return` + both display surfaces + `document()`) → concluded no-refactor per owner (the GD cells mirror the existing `fgSE` N/A-guard, so a shared helper would reduce consistency); Phase 3E on real qcPed data.)

#### Learning 289 -- **A slice that (a) introduces a display element a PRIOR slice deferred must re-ratify it at THIS slice's gate, (b) wires a new helper into a widely-called function must honor that function's FULL input variety -- not just the happy-path fixture -- and (c) drives a `renderUI` in an ad-hoc Phase-3E probe must `session$flushReact()` first or the output reads empty.** Issue #118 Slice 2 (E2 demographic sex-ratio Ne, `calcNeSexRatio(ped)=4·Nm·Nf/(Nm+Nf)` over current living breeders) under strict TDD -- new `@noRd` `getLivingBreeders()` + exported `calcNeSexRatio()` + additive `neSexRatio` bundle field + a SEPARATE "Effective Population Size / current living breeders" block in `modSummaryStats` and a `Sex-Ratio Ne (living breeders)` row in the `modGeneticValue` GV-tab; 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (D-b/D-c/D-a scope + 3 phase gates), 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 0 fail/0 error, touched files 0 skipped under `NOT_CRAN=true`, lint 0, spelling clean, `devtools::check()` 0/0/0. **(a) A display decision a prior slice explicitly DEFERRED is a live decision at the gate, not an auto-applied given.** S310 ratified D-a as "GD beside FG; separate 'Effective Population Size' block deferred to Slice 2 (the first living-breeder metric)". This slice did NOT silently build the block from the plan -- it re-posed D-a's E2 portion (separate block vs append) alongside D-b/D-c in the pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`, because introducing the block IS the load-bearing Dragon-D-1 display choice ([[observation-vs-decision]]). Owner confirmed the separate block; GD stays beside FG. **(b) A helper wired into `reportGV()` inherits `reportGV()`'s ENTIRE input contract, including exit-less peds.** `reportGV` is called in tests on `makeOriginTestPed()`, a hand-built ped with NO `exit` column (`test_reportGV.R`), so `getLivingBreeders(ped)` doing a naive `is.na(ped$exit)` (→ `is.na(NULL)` = `logical(0)`, then a length-0 `living & isBreeder` mask) would have crashed those PRE-EXISTING passing tests the moment `neSexRatio=calcNeSexRatio(ped)` joined the bundle. The guard `living <- if (is.null(ped$exit)) rep(TRUE, nrow(ped)) else is.na(ped$exit)` (all animals living when no exit info) is mandatory, not defensive nicety. Lesson: before adding a new unconditional call into a shared function, grep its existing callers/fixtures for the columns your helper reads -- the well-formed bundled `qcPed` is not the only input. **(c) `as.character(output$fooUI)` in an ad-hoc `shiny::testServer` probe intermittently returns `character(0)` unless you `session$flushReact()` first** -- the same testServer flush/return quirk S310 hit. My real-data Phase-3E smoke first printed the block present (TRUE) then a stray all-FALSE line; a 3× loop then read empty every time -- NOT a render bug (the actual unit test, which flushes correctly, passed). Adding `session$flushReact()` before reading made it deterministic (block + "17.78" present, len=1704, all 3 runs). Distinguish a flush artifact from a real intermittent-render bug by looping with an explicit flush; trust the passing unit test over the ad-hoc probe. **(d) Shared-helper return shape chosen for cross-slice reuse:** `getLivingBreeders()` returns the living-breeder **ids** (not a subset data.frame or a logical mask) -- single-responsibility (it finds the population; each `calc*` applies its own formula), so E2 does `ped$sex[match(ids, ped$id)]` and Slice-3/E3 will do `findOffspring(ids, ped)` + `length(ids)`; ids are the most reusable contract. Degeneracy sentinel returns `0.0`/`4.0` (not `0`/`4`) so `implicit_integer_linter` is clean AND the type stays double (`expect_type(ne,"double")`). **(e) WORDLIST minimization + `document()` family cascade:** the roxygen phrase "proband/population selection" tripped spelling (`proband` singular absent; only `probands` plural is listed) -- reworded to "which animals are selected as probands" to reuse the listed plural rather than grow WORDLIST ([[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]]). `devtools::document()` after adding `@family genetic value analysis` re-touched EVERY sibling `.Rd` (calcA/calcFE/calcFG/... ) to append the `calcNeSexRatio()` cross-link -- verify those diffs are cross-link-only (one `\code{\link[=...]{...()}}` line each), not a roxygen-version whitespace churn, before committing. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (mirrored S310's `neGD` wiring + the `#82 fgSE` scalar-beside-fg precedent; plan §8 Slice-2 steps), [[observation-vs-decision]] (D-b/D-c/D-a + every phase transition via `AskUserQuestion`; re-ratified the deferred block rather than assuming it), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (`0.0`/`4.0`, comment length, lint 0 with package loaded), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (reworded to avoid a WORDLIST add; `update_wordlist` NOT run), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (`calcNeSexRatio` roxygen describes the estimator + its Wright-Fisher idealization, no issue/Slice refs; those live in source comments + CHANGELOG), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED firsthand reads + D-b/D-c/D-a scope gate → RED (2 new test files `test_getLivingBreeders.R`/`test_calcNeSexRatio.R` + 3 extended, all confirmed failing for the right reason, 0 skips under `NOT_CRAN=true`, golden-master intact) → GREEN (`getLivingBreeders` + `calcNeSexRatio` + bundle + `@return` + both display surfaces + `document()`) → concluded no-refactor (the E2 display guards mirror the existing `neGD`/`fgSE` N/A pattern; the separate block is genuinely new, no dead code to fold); Phase 3E on real qcPed data: 18 living breeders, Nm=8/Nf=10 → `neSexRatio=17.78` rendered on both surfaces.)

#### Learning 290 -- **A plan's stated RATIONALE for a load-bearing formula choice can be arithmetically INVERTED -- so before writing a single RED literal, compute EVERY candidate formula on real data, and if the direction contradicts the plan, present the correction with numbers and let the owner ratify (don't parrot the plan's headline OR its reasoning). And a crafted-pedigree RED test only PINS a formula variant if the fixture makes the ratified and rejected forms give DIFFERENT values.** Issue #118 Slice 3 (E3 variance effective size, `calcNeVariance(ped)`) under strict TDD -- exported pure fn reusing S311's `getLivingBreeders()`, additive `neVariance` bundle field, a SECOND "Variance Ne" column in the existing Effective Population Size block; 5 `AskUserQuestion` gates, 0 stakeholder corrections; full suite 3712 pass/0 fail/0 error, lint 0, spelling clean, `devtools::check()` 0/0/0, Phase 3E on real qcPed. **(a) The plan's recommendation AND its rationale were both wrong about DIRECTION -- worked numbers caught it.** The #118 plan (Dragon D-7 / D-d) recommended the "mean-adjusted Crow-Kimura form" *because "the bare `(4N-4)/(Vk+2)` overstates Ne."* Computing all three candidates on the real bundled pedigree (`N=156, kbar=5.28, Vk=16.54`) showed the opposite: **bare = 33.4 (the LOWEST), general Crow-Kimura = 110.9, rescale-to-replacement = 141.7.** The plan's whole headline narrative ("reproductive skew drags E3 far below E2") holds ONLY under the bare form -- which is dimensionally inconsistent here (it plugs a `kbar=5.28`-scale variance into a `kbar=2` formula), so the dramatic low number is an ARTIFACT, not a finding. I presented the three candidates + values + the inversion via `AskUserQuestion`; the owner ratified the general Crow-Kimura form `(N·kbar-1)/(kbar-1+Vk/kbar)` (the standard generalization; reduces to the classic `(4N-2)/(Vk+2)` at replacement) and `N<2 → NA`. Lesson: for a load-bearing formula decision, a worked-numbers gate turns a plan ASSUMPTION into evidence and is where an inverted rationale surfaces -- do it in PRE-RED, before the RED literals depend on it ([[observation-vs-decision]]). This is the S287(b) "run the metric on real data during planning" discipline applied at the IMPLEMENTATION gate. **(b) A crafted RED fixture pins a formula VARIANT only if the two candidate forms DISAGREE on it.** The discriminating test (`discPed`) used `kbar=3 (!=2)`: the ratified general form gives **1.875**, the bare form gives **1.0** -- so a wrong (bare) GREEN implementation FAILS. A `kbar=2` fixture would give the SAME value under both forms (the general form reduces to the bare at replacement), silently passing a bare implementation. Design at least one fixture where the ratified and rejected forms diverge, and VERIFY the exact expected literal by computing the formula directly with the existing helpers (`getLivingBreeders`/`findOffspring`) BEFORE writing it -- RED asserted a hand-verified 1.875/2.5, not a guess. **(c) A deterministic metric can legitimately produce Ne > census -- don't bake a "Ne < N" invariant into a test.** On qcPed the variance Ne (26.41) exceeds both E2 (17.78) AND the census (18 living breeders) because families are more even than Poisson (`Vk<kbar`) -- a real feature of the variance effective size. Computing qcPed's value during RED-DESIGN (not just relying on the plan's example) caught that a planned "expect Ne < N" assertion would have failed; the test instead asserts finite/positive + the exact pinned value 26.405868. **(d) A mid-session "record X for a future session" request = file a scoped tracker item NOW, do NOT start X.** The owner asked mid-work to "place an audit task for another session" (are there citations for each calculation?); the complete job of RECORDING it is one GitHub issue (#120, an AUDIT_WORKSTREAM task with the calculations enumerated) -- filed immediately, then straight back to the E3 gate (FM #2/#13: do the full job of the request without letting it become the session's work). **(e) Additive wiring mechanics (carried from S311, all held):** `neVariance` rides AFTER `neSexRatio` (bundle element #9) -- update BOTH `expect_named` copies (2-space + 4-space, NOT byte-identical, two edits); a SECOND `<th>/<td>` column in the EXISTING Effective Population Size block, not a third block; `1.0` literals (not `1`) for `implicit_integer_linter` (`.lintr` has `indentation_linter=NULL` + `line_length_linter(80)`, and `tests/` is lint-excluded -- so only R/*.R needs ≤80 cols); `Kimura` hand-added to `inst/WORDLIST` sorted (`update_wordlist` NOT run -- Crow/Wright/Fisher already recognized); `document()`'s `@family` cascade re-touched sibling `.Rd` cross-links (verify cross-link-only). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (mirrored S311's `neSexRatio` wiring + the plan §8 Slice-3 steps + the `#82 fgSE` scalar-beside precedent), [[observation-vs-decision]] (the D-d correction + all 5 phase/scope gates via `AskUserQuestion`; presented the inversion, did not decide it), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (`1.0` literals, lint 0 with package loaded), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]] (`Kimura` hand-added, `update_wordlist` NOT run), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (`calcNeVariance` roxygen describes the estimator + `@references Crow & Kimura (1970)` + the Wright-Fisher idealization, no issue/Slice refs -- those live in source comments + CHANGELOG), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (issue #120's future session should scan `docs/audits/` + CHANGELOG before re-running the citations sweep), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED firsthand reads + real-data computation of all candidate formulas + D-d/degeneracy scope gate → RED (new `test_calcNeVariance.R` + 3 extended, all failing for the right reason, 0 skips under `NOT_CRAN=true`, golden-master `fe`/`fg`/`fgSE`/`neGD`/`neSexRatio`/counts + #9/#73/#76/#2/#86/#82/#13 intact) → GREEN (`calcNeVariance` + bundle + `@return` + both display surfaces + `document()`) → concluded no-refactor per owner; Phase 3E on real qcPed: `neVariance=26.41` rendered beside `neSexRatio=17.78`.)

#### Learning 291 -- **A "publish/docs" slice under strict TDD is a real RED->GREEN cycle: the RED artifact is a static source-scanning `testthat` guard, the build-equivalent is `devtools::check()` (which builds the vignette) PLUS a Phase-3E probe that RENDERS the module UI, and `NEWS.Rmd` -- not `NEWS.md` -- is the source of truth.** (S313, Slice 4 of #118, closes #118; 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates -- surface scope, PRE-RED->RED, RED->GREEN, GREEN->REFACTOR; 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [RED for docs]** mirrored `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` (the S285 precedent): `system.file("extdata","ui_guidance",...)` + `readLines()` + `grepl()` (fixed for labels/formulas/citations, `ignore.case` regex for prose -- never both, per the grepl contract) over the 3 in-app HTML panels + the vignette child + `NEWS.Rmd`; RED = 19 phrase-absent failures WITH the `nzchar(path)` file-found checks PASSING (structurally sound, failing for the right reason). **(b) [build-equivalent for docs]** `devtools::check()` 0/0/0 builds `a3manual.Rmd`, which `child=`-includes `manual_components/_summary_statistics.Rmd` (`a3manual.Rmd:43`), so the render exercises the edit; the Phase-3E smoke went FURTHER and rendered `modSummaryStatsUI`/`modGeneticValueUI` to `as.character()` and asserted the new text is EMBEDDED (both panels load via `includeHTML(system.file(...))` at UI-construction, `modSummaryStats.R:44`/`:224`) -- proving the running app serves it, not merely that files exist. **(c) [NEWS source-of-truth]** `NEWS.Rmd` is the human-authored source and is BUILD-IGNORED (`.Rbuildignore` `^NEWS\.Rmd$`); `NEWS.md` is the github_document render that ships and is shown by pkgdown/`news()`. Owner directed "edit NEWS.Rmd, not NEWS.md" ([[edit-news-rmd-not-news-md]]). A trial render to a scratch file + `diff` vs `NEWS.md` revealed S307 had hand-added the #119 `minSireAge` bullet to `NEWS.md` ONLY, so a naive render would have SILENTLY DROPPED it -- so I backfilled #119 into `NEWS.Rmd` FIRST, then rendered, fixing the divergence AND leaving `NEWS.md` a faithful render. Lesson: before regenerating a generated file, diff it against its source and backfill whatever the source is missing. **(d) [carry the predecessor's warning into prose]** S312 warned NOT to repeat the plan's Section-4 "E3<<E2" narrative (it describes the REJECTED bare form); user docs describe the RATIFIED general Crow-Kimura form ("reflects family-size evenness; can exceed the census when reproduction is more even than random"). **(e) [verify WORDLIST empirically]** no WORDLIST change was needed -- `spell_check_package` scans `NEWS.md`/vignettes/`.Rd` but NOT `inst/extdata` HTML, and the only novel word ("coancestry") lives solely in the non-scanned HTML; run the check rather than pre-adding words. Carried: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] (investigated the NEWS render flow before acting on the directive rather than blindly re-rendering), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (no issue#/Slice/E-labels in rendered help or vignette prose; `(#118)`/`(#119)` only as NEWS issue-refs), [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]].

#### Learning 292 -- **A test-hygiene "make the suite WARN 0" task splits into two DIFFERENT TDD shapes -- a genuine RED->GREEN for the warning that is a real source defect, and a test-only assertion-tightening for the warning that is CORRECT deliberate behavior -- distinguished by asking "should the code emit this warning at all?" before deciding whether the fix lives in `R/` or in the test.** (S314, issue #121: the suite was `FAIL 0` but leaked 7 unasserted warnings; 1 scope + 3 phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`s, 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [root-cause the -Inf, don't patch the fixture]** All 5 `test_modPyramid` warnings ("no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf") traced to ONE line -- `getPedMaxAge()` (`R/getPedMaxAge.R:25`) `max(ped$age, na.rm=TRUE)` -- which fires when the ped has no `age` column OR all-NA ages; the module's `renderPlot` re-runs on each `session$setInputs`, so one defect leaked 5 copies. The test fixture (id/sex/birth, no `age`) merely EXPOSED a runtime-reachable defect (a colony import lacking birth dates -> all-NA ages -> the same `-Inf`, and `getPyramidPlot` would then derive a `-Inf` axis bound). Fix = guard the SOURCE to return `NA_real_` (its sole caller `getPyramidPlot.R:52` already maps `NA` -> `binWidth`), NOT hand an `age` column to the test. Confirming runtime-reachability (grep the single caller + confirm the recovery path already handles `NA`) is what justified the source fix over the test-only fallback the issue offered. **(b) [the other warning was CORRECT -- assert it, do NOT silence the source]** The 2 `test_gvaConvergence` warnings ("off-diagonal value(s) > 0.5") are the `checkKinshipOverrides` diagnostic firing correctly on a deliberately-invalid PSD-bound-exceeding override, TWICE -- once via `prepareKinshipOverrides`, once via `applyKinshipOverrides` (grep the message string to find BOTH call sites; the doubled count matters for the assertion). No source bug: the test asserted the resulting `expect_error` but let the warnings leak (`suppressMessages` does NOT catch warnings). Fix is test-only: nest `expect_warning(expect_error(suppressMessages(...), "above the maximum"), "off-diagonal value")`. **(c) [testthat edition-2 `expect_warning` consumes ALL warnings in the expression]** DESCRIPTION has no `Config/testthat/edition: 3`, so it is edition 2; empirically a single `expect_warning(...)` around the call swallowed BOTH ">0.5" warnings with no leak -- verified by re-running the file and seeing the summary reporter show dots not `W`. Don't assume one-warning-per-`expect_warning`; run it and count. **(d) [a "must NOT warn" RED assertion is `expect_warning(expr, NA)`]** The genuine RED for `getPedMaxAge` used `expect_warning(res <- getPedMaxAge(x), NA)` + `expect_true(is.na(res))` on both the no-`age`-column and all-NA cases -- 4 failures under the old source (warning emitted + returns `-Inf`), 0 after. This is the canonical way to pin "this must not warn". **(e) [monitor the warning column on EVERY run -- owner standing directive]** The owner reiterated mid-session: watch test warnings in ALL subsequent runs, not only the final read ([[regression-read-check-warnings]]). The clean regression read sums `failed`+`error`+`warning` (isolating the `test-app-`/`test-e2e-` baseline). Final: full suite `FAIL 0 · ERROR 0 · WARN 0`, 3740 pass/167 skip; `devtools::check()` 0/0/0; lint 0 on the changed source; Phase-3E rendered `getPyramidPlot()` on all-NA / no-age / real-age peds with 0 warnings. Carried: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the issue's own acceptance criteria drove the root-cause-vs-test decision), [[observation-vs-decision]] (scope + 3 phase gates via `AskUserQuestion`; presented, did not decide), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (lint 0 with the package loaded), [[edit-news-rmd-not-news-md]] (added the `(#121)` bullet to `NEWS.Rmd`, trial-rendered + diffed before writing `NEWS.md`), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]] (NEWS bullet = plain-language behavior + `(#121)` ref only), [[regression-read-check-warnings]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (Strict TDD: PRE-RED firsthand root-cause (empirically pinned `getPedMaxAge` as the sole `-Inf` source; grepped both ">0.5" call sites) + scope gate -> RED (new `test_getPedMaxAge.R` block, 4 failing right; gva test tightened, passes immediately as test-only hygiene) -> GREEN (`getPedMaxAge` guard + `@return` + `document()`) -> concluded no-refactor per owner.)

#### Learning 296 -- **A hand-rolled "roxygen block" line-scanner that treats a bare blank line as a block terminator UNDERCOUNTS titles -- roxygen2's own parser tolerates a genuine blank line INSIDE a comment run (title paragraph, blank, `@tag` paragraph) as ONE logical block, as long as no code intervenes; always parse with `roxygen2::parse_file()` itself, not a regex re-implementation of its grammar.** (S319, issue #103 CLOSED -- the internal `@noRd` title-voice sweep, Finding 3's last tail. REFACTOR-class, doc-only; TDD RED/GREEN N/A, matching the established #103-stage precedent. 1 `AskUserQuestion` scope/approach gate ("approve all as shown, apply now"), 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [the bug that would have undercounted the census]** A first-pass hand-rolled scanner (`grepl("^#'", line)` run-detection) found 72 `@noRd` blocks too, but 4 files (`addErrTxt.R`, `addGroupOfUnusedAnimals.R`, `colChange.R`, `fillBins.R`) came back with an EMPTY title -- because each has a REAL blank line (not `#'`) between its title paragraph and its `@param`/`@noRd` paragraph, which the scanner treated as ending the roxygen block entirely, splitting one logical block into two (an orphaned title-only "block" the `@noRd` filter correctly excluded, and a tag-only block whose "title" extraction hit `@param` on its first line and returned empty). Switching to `roxygen2::parse_file()` -- the actual parser, called with `env = new.env()` per file, reading `b$tags` for a `title` tag and testing `"noRd" %in%` the tag names -- recovered all 4 correct titles and confirmed the same 72/62 total S318 had already found, this time with zero blank titles. **(b) [the rule]** When a task needs to know what roxygen2 will actually parse (title text, tag presence, block boundaries), call `roxygen2::parse_file()` and read its `$tags` list -- never re-implement roxygen's comment-block grammar with a line-by-line regex scan for anything beyond a quick first-look grep. The one edge case (blank line tolerance inside a block) is exactly the kind of parser detail that's invisible until it silently drops data. **(c) [the "guarded bulk-edit + re-census" apply pattern from Stage 8a (Learning 243) generalizes cleanly to a second corpus]** 44 titles needed a rewrite (28 of 72 already conformed); applied via a script that extracts every "paragraph" (maximal run of non-blank `#'` content lines, using the SAME blank-line-tolerant logic as (a), broken only by a true blank line, a `#'`-only blank, or an `@tag` line) from each target file, matches by whitespace-normalized old-title text, and replaces the matched line-range with one new line -- 44/44 matched uniquely on the first (dry-run) pass, 0 skips, applied on the second. Post-apply re-census (same `parse_file()`-based script) confirmed 0/44 mismatches and the total block count held at 72. **(d) [`man/` diff is the correctness proof for an all-`@noRd` batch, not a coincidence to verify after the fact]** Because all 44 edited functions are `@noRd` (non-rendering), `devtools::document()` after the sweep produced an EXACTLY-empty `man/`/`NAMESPACE` diff (git status showed only the 41 edited `R/` files) -- predicted before running `document()`, then confirmed, mirroring Learning 246(2)'s "predict the empty diff, then prove it" pattern one level up (a whole-corpus sweep, not a handful of collapses). **(e) [line-length lint on a REWRITE, not just a new addition, needs the SAME wrap discipline as any other edit]** 3 of the 44 rewrites pushed a single `#'` line past 80 chars (a wordier imperative phrasing than the original wrap happened to need); `lintr::lint_dir()` scoped to the changed-file set caught all 3, each fixed by wrapping across 2 physical `#'` lines (roxygen2 still joins wrapped `#'` lines with no blank between them into ONE logical title tag -- verified by re-running the `parse_file()`-based census after the wrap and seeing 0 new mismatches). Re-lint after any title-length-increasing rewrite, not just after brand-new lines. **(f) [content-accuracy defects surfaced by a voice sweep are noted, not folded in]** Two adjacent defects were found and deliberately left for a future session, both flagged in the issue-closing comment/CHANGELOG rather than fixed here: `getIndianOriginStatus.R:14`'s `@return` has a mismatched Rd brace (`\code{color_index)`, pre-existing per an empty `git diff` on that untouched file) and fires a `roxygen2::parse_file()` parse warning on every census run; and `unknown2NA.R`'s title ("Remove IDs...") slightly overstates its own behavior (only the `id` column is filtered by row-removal, `sire`/`dam` are blanked to `NA` instead) -- both are Finding-1/content-accuracy class, not Finding-3/voice class, matching the precedent of leaving `getPedDirectRelatives.R`'s wrong description for its own session (Learning 244(9)). **(g) [issue closure itself, on THIS repo's `gh` quirk]** `gh issue comment` + `gh issue close` both worked cleanly (unlike `gh pr edit`, which fails on this repo's deprecated `projectCards` field per [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]] -- that workaround is PR-specific, issue commands are unaffected). Carried as applied (all held, no incidents): [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (re-read the S244 audit's own Finding 3 recommendation text, not just S318's summary of it), [[observation-vs-decision]] (posed the full 44-row proposal table for owner approval before touching any file, rather than applying then reporting), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (caught + fixed the 3 line-length hits before commit), [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]], [[regression-read-check-warnings]] (`devtools::check(cran=TRUE)` 0/0/0 + fast regression read 0 failed/error/warning, 3092 passed), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. **This was a REFACTOR-class documentation session -- TDD RED/GREEN N/A.** (S319, issue #103 -> direct commit to `master`; 44 `@noRd` title rewrites / 41 `R/` files, 0 `man/`/`NAMESPACE` diff, gate 0/0/0, lint 0, spell 0; issue #103 CLOSED, all 8 audit stages complete.)

#### Learning 293 -- **A concatenated grep-dump scratch file's line numbers are dump-position, not source-file position -- never cite one as a finding's `file:line` without a fresh `grep -n`/`Read` on the actual target file immediately before writing the citation.** (S315, issue #120 citation-coverage audit: an AUDIT_WORKSTREAM, TDD-N/A session -- pure findings report, no code/docs changed; 1 scope-gate `AskUserQuestion`, 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [the trap]** To triage 50 candidate files' roxygen blocks efficiently, `grep "^#'" file1 file2 ... >> dump.txt` concatenated every file's roxygen comments into one scratch file, then `Read` on the dump. The `Read` tool numbers lines IN THE DUMP -- e.g. line 1949 of the dump might be `R/modBreedingGroups.R`'s 16th roxygen line, not its 1949th. A finding drafted directly from that read (`R/modBreedingGroups.R` "line ~1949") looked like a real citation and was WRONG (the real location was lines 15-16) -- caught only because a pre-close-out self-check re-`grep -n`'d the actual file. **(b) [the rule]** Any line number reported in a deliverable must trace to either (i) a direct `Read`/`grep -n` of the ACTUAL target file run immediately before the citation is written, or (ii) an explicit re-verification pass against the real file before the deliverable is finalized -- a scratch dump is fine for browsing/triage/pattern-spotting across many files at once, but its line numbers are never citable. **(c) [scope of the risk]** This generalizes beyond citation audits to any session that batches multiple files into one scratch read for efficiency (e.g. a doc-error sweep, a multi-file grep triage) -- the efficiency win of one big read is real, but the line-number provenance must be re-established per-finding before the finding is written down, not assumed to carry over from the dump. **(d) [the audit's substantive finding, for context]** Cross-surface triangulation (roxygen `@references` vs. the built vignette manual vs. `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html`) found 5 statistics with a citation on NO surface at all (`calcNeSexRatio`, `calcGeneDiversity`, `meanKinship`, `rankSubjects`, `groupAddAssign`) despite sibling statistics in the SAME doc panel (`calcNeVariance`, `calcGU`) being correctly and fully cited -- the gap correlates with recency/first-citation-pass rather than centrality, i.e. the newest slice (issue #118) cited its own new metric (Variance Ne) but not the metric shipped alongside it (Sex-Ratio Ne) in the same slice. Report: `docs/audits/ISSUE_120_CITATION_COVERAGE_AUDIT_2026-07-08.md` (12 findings: 5 High, 5 Moderate, 2 Minor; 50/50 R files + 16/16 doc surfaces classified). Carried: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (fetched the actual issue #120 body via `gh api` before scoping, not just the title), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (S314's handoff pointed at `population_genetics_terms.html` as the delta-start, per S313's prior work -- avoided a full blind re-scope), [[observation-vs-decision]] (scope gate posed via `AskUserQuestion`, grounded in a real 44-file/4-cited count, not decided unilaterally), [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]] (`gh api repos/.../issues/120` to read the body). (No TDD phases -- pure audit/analysis-class deliverable, no `R/`/`vignettes/`/`inst/` file changed; the fix is an explicitly separate, owner-gated follow-on session per AUDIT_WORKSTREAM and the #109 precedent.)

#### Learning 294 -- **`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html` files mix tab and space indentation INCONSISTENTLY across siblings -- verify raw bytes (`cat -tv`/`od -c`) on the target region before the FIRST `Edit` on any doc-surface HTML file, not after a failed one.** (S316, issue #120 fix of audit §6, findings F1-F11 (11/12; F12 + Process recommendation explicitly deferred per owner scope-gate). REFACTOR-class, doc-only; TDD RED/GREEN N/A -- matches the S273/S274/S275/S278 precedent for audit-driven doc fixes. 2 `AskUserQuestion` gates (scope+3 owner decisions, then plan-vs-execute-now), 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [the trap]** `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/population_genetics_terms.html` is space-indented throughout; its sibling `gvAndBgDesc.html` is TAB-indented throughout (confirmed via `cat -tv` showing `^I`). The `Read` tool's line-numbered display renders both the same way visually, so copy-pasting a `Read`-displayed region into an `Edit` `old_string` works for the space-indented file but silently fails ("String to replace not found") for the tab-indented one -- the failure is indistinguishable at a glance from a genuine content mismatch. Two `Edit` calls failed this way before `sed -n '<range>p' file | cat -tv` (then `od -c` for a byte-level check) surfaced the tabs; re-typing the `old_string`/`new_string` with literal tabs matching the file fixed it in one try. **(b) [the rule]** Before the FIRST `Edit` on any HTML/legacy doc-surface file in this repo (R/Rmd sources are consistently space-indented; only the `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html` family is suspect), run one `cat -tv` or `od -c` probe on the target region to confirm tabs vs. spaces -- cheap up front, expensive as a silent no-op failure discovered only after the fact. Do not assume sibling files in the same directory share an indentation convention. **(c) [verification stack for an 11-finding, 19-source-file doc-only fix, beyond the S274 minimum]** `devtools::document()` (NAMESPACE diff empty) -> `tools::checkRd()` on all 13 regenerated `.Rd` (0 warnings) -> `spelling::spell_check_package()` (1 new flag "JW" from the newly-added MacCluer et al. 1986 co-citation; hand-added to `inst/WORDLIST` in sorted position, `update_wordlist()` NOT run per [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]]) -> `lintr::lint()` per changed R file (0, confirms <=80-col held) -> `tools::Rd2txt()` rendered 4 representative man pages to VISUALLY confirm citation prose and `\emph{}` italics render correctly (not just "no error") -> full `R CMD build` (renders the 3 changed vignette `.Rmd` children, confirms no knit break) -> `R CMD check --as-cran` (**2 NOTEs = the standing benign pair, 0 WARN/0 ERROR**; `testthat.R` 3112 PASS/0 FAIL/0 WARN, the S314 standing "monitor warnings" directive explicitly re-checked). This is a heavier bar than S274's `document()`+lint+spell+`--as-cran` because this fix's surface area spans 3 DIFFERENT doc formats (roxygen Rd, hand-written HTML, knitted Rmd) rather than roxygen alone, so each format's own failure mode (Rd rendering, HTML tab/space, Rmd knit) needed its own check. **(d) [citation text is assembled from in-repo sources, never re-composed from memory]** Every citation added this session was copied byte-for-byte from an existing in-repo source (Ballou & Lacy 1995 from `calcGU.R`, Crow & Kimura 1970 from `calcNeVariance.R`, the full Vinson & Raboin form from CLAUDE.md's own "Key References" section) rather than retyped from the audit report's prose, which is itself one step removed from the canonical text and could carry its own transcription drift. **(e) [an audit's per-finding "Location" list can be broader than its "Recommendation" actually requires -- re-verify by reading, don't blanket-edit every listed location]** F2's Location field named `genetic_value.html` as a doc surface lacking a GD citation; a direct read showed that paragraph explicitly delegates ("These...are defined in the Population Genetics Terms reference panel") rather than narrating the formula, so it makes no independent citable claim -- adding a citation there would have been redundant with the delegation, not a fix. Left untouched; the fix went to the two surfaces that actually assert the formula (`population_genetics_terms.html`, `_summary_statistics.Rmd`). Don't blanket-apply a finding's Location list; re-read each candidate location and edit only where an independent factual/citable claim is actually being made. Carried as applied (all held, no incidents): [[consult-project-source-of-truth]], [[observation-vs-decision]] (both `AskUserQuestion` gates presented options + the owner picked, including the mid-session plan-vs-execute fork the owner raised), [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]], [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]], [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]], [[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files]], [[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order]] (`population_genetics_terms.html` 3 edits, `gvAndBgDesc.html` 2 edits, `_summary_statistics.Rmd` 2 edits, all applied bottom-to-top), [[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs]], [[check-status-before-destructive-git]], [[regression-read-check-warnings]], [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]]. (No TDD RED/GREEN phases -- doc-only REFACTOR-class fix of an existing audit's findings, matching the S273/S274/S275/S278 precedent; #120 left OPEN, F12 + Process recommendation explicitly deferred per owner scope-gate.)

#### Learning 295 -- **A "may this be complete?" question is not answered by trusting a thorough changelog trail alone -- a well-documented multi-session effort can still have one small, genuine gap that never got a session of its own, because it fell between two other sessions' declared scopes.** (S318, issue #103 roxygen2 harmonization: owner asked whether #103 might be complete; verification-only + a 1-line fix, REFACTOR-class, TDD RED/GREEN N/A, 1 `AskUserQuestion` scope gate, 0 stakeholder corrections.) **(a) [the check that mattered]** ~15 prior sessions (S245-S269) each verified their OWN slice thoroughly (CI green, `--as-cran` 0/0/0, adversarial workflows) and the changelog read as "Stages 1-8b all landed" -- but that trail never re-confirmed Finding 1's own one-line recommendation ("fix the one `@returns`" in `set_seed.R`) because Stage 1 executed against the audit's separately-tabulated D1-D8 DEFECT table, not Finding 1's prose recommendation, and no later stage's scope happened to touch that file. `git log --follow -- R/set_seed.R` proved the tag predates the entire harmonization effort (commit `031ff714`) -- it was never regressed, just never picked up. **(b) [the rule]** When asked to verify "is X complete" against a documented multi-session effort, spend the few minutes on firsthand spot-checks BEFORE trusting the trail: `gh pr list` for open PRs referencing the tracker issue (none open = a strong but not sufficient signal), targeted greps against each of the ORIGINAL analysis document's specific named instances (not just its stage headings), and `git log --follow` on any single file the analysis called out by name. A stage-by-stage changelog read confirms the STAGES happened; it does not by itself confirm every INSTANCE within a stage's nominal scope was actually touched. **(c) [false positives are also worth chasing down, not just flagging]** A blind grep for the harmonization's own defect pattern (stray backtick markup, Finding 4) hit 2 files (`geneDrop.R`, `getGVGenotype.R`); reading the surrounding context showed both hits are inside `@examples` R-comment text (never markdown-processed), not the prose-backtick class the finding targeted -- confirming a grep hit is a genuine instance of the ORIGINAL finding's criteria (not just a textual pattern match) before reporting it, in either direction. **(d) [a real remaining scope tail is a sizing question for the owner, not a unilateral call]** The internal (`@noRd`) title-voice sweep (Finding 3) was explicitly scoped OUT of Stage 8a at S257/258 ("exported functions only") and never resumed; sized it (72 `@noRd` markers / 62 files, comparable to Stage 8a's original 100-file exported census) and posed the three live options (exempt it like the Shiny `mod*` carve-out already established / leave #103 open for a dedicated future session / attempt it now) via `AskUserQuestion` rather than picking one -- owner chose to leave it open. Carried as applied (all held, no incidents): [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (a refinement -- the changelog trail was necessary but not sufficient this time, worth the extra firsthand pass), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (re-read the original 2026-06-29 audit document's own Finding 1/§6 text, not just the changelog's summary of it), [[observation-vs-decision]] (posed the sizing/scope question, did not decide it), [[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround]] (`gh pr list`/`gh api` used to confirm PR state), [[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] (lint 0 on the 1-line change), [[regression-read-check-warnings]] (`devtools::check(cran=TRUE)` Status OK 0/0/0 + fast regression read 0/0/0 despite the change being a single `@noRd` tag), [[push-close-out-docs-to-origin]].
